title: Next-Generation Solid-State Transformers for Powering Artificial Intelligence Data Centers: A Survey of Emerging Topologies
authors: Jonas Huber, Johann W. Kolar
publish: IEEE Industrial Electronics Magazine
abstract: The rapid growth of AI datacenters and the transition toward megawatt scale power consumption of single server racks demand a fundamental redesign of datacenter power distribution architectures. Conventional low voltage ac (LVac) systems face increasing limitations in efficiency, scalability, and material usage. Solid state transformers (SSTs) offer a pathway to compact medium voltage ac to low voltage dc (MVac LVdc) conversion, enabling MVac distribution directly to the server racks. However, today’s industrial fully modular SST demonstra-tors show limited power density of roughly 0.2 MW/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>, which is insufficient for supporting the targeted high compute densities. This paper surveys emerging single-stage and quasi single-stage SST topologies with the potential for breaking through the power density barrier and achieving 1 MW/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> at 99% efficiency. Alternatives based on low-frequency transformers for near term adoption of LVdc distribution are also discussed. The paper highlights the need for a holistic comparative benchmarking of the different distribution architectures together with protection methods and SST concepts, especially including future MVdc distribution and MVdc-LVdc SSTs close to the server racks.

title: Orthogonal Approximate Message Passing for Double Linear Transformation Model
authors: Yufei Chen, Lei Liu, Yuhao Chi, Ying Li, Yong Liang Guan, Chau Yuen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract: The double linear transformation (DLT) model Y = AXB+N is widely used in signal processing and wireless communications, where X is estimated from the noisy observation Y with known matrices A and B. Most existing algorithms reformulate the DLT model into a vectorized linear form with the equivalent transformation matrix BT ⊗ A, upon which message passing principles are utilized to recover the signal. However, the Kronecker product causes numerous correlated submatrices in BT ⊗ A, which significantly increases the matrix dimension and obscures the original statistical distribution properties of A and B. To address this challenge, we propose a novel equivalent tri-decoupled double transformation (TDDT) framework that introduces an intermediate variable Z = XB to decouple the double linear constraint into two single linear constraints. This decoupling can preserve both the dimension and the statistical properties of A and B in the single linear problems. Building on the TDDT framework, a double linear orthogonal approximate message passing (DL-OAMP) algorithm is proposed, which incorporates three estimators for two decoupled single linear constraints and a more sophisticated nonlinear constraint on X beyond the independent and identically distributed assumption. Meanwhile, a state evolution analysis is established to accurately predict the asymptotic performance of DL-OAMP. Within the TDDT framework, we further apply the proposed DL-OAMP algorithm to compressed sensing reconstruction in signal processing and massive random access for near-field communications. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed DL-OAMP algorithm achieves superior performance compared with state-of-the-art methods in practical applications.

title: Robust Manipulation of Deformable Linear Objects
authors: Jimmy Envall, Stelian Coros
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Robotic manipulation is a fundamental challenge in the pursuit of automating household tasks and advancing robotic manufacturing. Manipulation planning for deformable objects is particularly challenging due to the associated large action spaces and complex dynamics. For deformable objects, some actions are sensitive to noise in the model, which can significantly degrade the accuracy of predicted trajectories. In this letter, we present a motion planning algorithm for arranging deformable linear objects (DLOs) into user-provided goal configurations. Using a novel problem formulation as well as a combination of sampling and gradient based optimization, the algorithm finds sequences of grasps and control inputs that are robust. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the new algorithm using numerical experiments and manipulation examples both in simulation and in real world environments.

title: Prior Knowledge-Guided Active Learning Network for Multi-Source Partial Discharge Diagnosis in Gas-Insulated Switchgear
authors: Y. Wang, J. Yan, Q. Jing, Z. Qi, Z. Liu, Y. Geng, J. Wang, D. Srinivasan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
abstract: The advancement of artificial intelligence presents both opportunities and significant challenges for multi-source partial discharge (PD) diagnosis in gas-insulated switchgear (GIS), but challenges remain due to insufficient incorporation of underlying physical mechanisms, limited labeled data, noise interference, class imbalance, and difficulties in decoupling mixed PD sources. To address these issues, this paper proposes a prior knowledge–guided active learning network (PKGALN) for multi-source PD diagnosis in GIS, in which physics-related PD characteristics are explicitly embedded into the model design under a limited-label regime. Specifically, a prior knowledge–guided U-former is constructed by incorporating physically interpretable PD descriptors (e.g., time–frequency statistical features reflecting discharge behavior) as global guidance for feature learning, while a scale-guided convolutional–wavelet encoder enhances denoising and multi-scale representation capability. In addition, a multi-label decoupling recognition module is designed to explicitly handle mixed PD sources, and a distribution-balanced loss is adopted to alleviate severe class imbalance. To further reduce annotation cost, an adaptive active learning strategy is introduced, dynamically balancing uncertainty and diversity during sample selection. Experimental results on both laboratory and field datasets demonstrate that PKGALN achieves diagnostic accuracies of 96.39% and 93.26%, respectively, using only 10% of labeled samples. By leveraging limited labeled data, the proposed approach enables robust diagnosis of both single-source and multi-source PDs.

title: Over-the-Air Diagnosis of Defective Elements in Intelligent Reflecting Surface
authors: Ziyi Zhao, Zhaorui Wang, Lin Zhou, Chunsong Sun, Shuowen Zhang, Naofal Al-Dhahir, Liang Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Due to circuit failures, defective elements that cannot adaptively adjust the phase shifts of their impinging signals in a desired manner may exist on an intelligent reflecting surface (IRS). Traditional way to locate these defective IRS elements requires a thorough diagnosis of all the circuits belonging to a huge number of IRS elements, which is practically challenging. In this paper, we will devise novel approaches under which a transmitter sends known pilot signals and a receiver localizes all the defective IRS elements just based on its over-the-air measurements reflected from the IRS. Specifically, given any set of IRS elements, we propose an efficient method to process the received signals to determine whether this cluster contains defective elements or not with a very high accuracy probability. Based on this method, we show that the over-the-air diagnosis problem belongs to the 20 questions problem, where we can adaptively change the query set at the IRS so as to localize all the defective elements as quickly as possible. Along this line, we first propose a sorted posterior matching (sortPM) based method according to the noisy 20 questions technique, which enables accurate diagnosis even if the answers about the existence of defective elements in some sets of interest are wrong at certain question and answer (Q&A) rounds due to the noisy received signals. Next, to reduce the complexity, we propose a bisection based method according to the noiseless 20 questions technique, which totally trusts the answer at each Q&A round and keeps removing half of the remaining region based on such answers. Via numerical results, we show that our proposed methods can exploit the over-the-air measurements to localize all the defective IRS elements quickly and accurately.

title: A Lightweight OBB-based SAR Ship Detection Network based on Feature Enhancement and Cross-Scale Fusion
authors: Fuqiang Zeng, Zhenkai Zhang, Boon-Chong Seet
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Ship detection in synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imagery plays an important role in maritime surveillance and security applications. However, in complex near-shore and port scenes, strong background clutter, significant variations in target scale, and the need for efficient feature representation make it challenging to achieve a good balance between detection accuracy and computational efficiency. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a lightweight oriented bounding box (OBB)-based SAR ship detection network based on feature enhancement and cross-scale fusion. By systematically redesigning key components, including feature enhancement, downsampling modeling, multi-scale fusion, and oriented target representation, the proposed method achieves coordinated optimization of accuracy and efficiency in complex scenarios. Specifically, a Multi-Scale Coordinate-Channel Attention mechanism (MSCCA) is constructed to enhance discriminative ship features while suppressing background interference. An Adaptive Dual-pooling Fusion Downsampling strategy (ADFD) is then introduced to reduce spatial resolution while alleviating fine-grained feature loss. In addition, a Lightweight Adaptive Multi-Scale Feature Enhancement module (LAMFE) is designed to improve multi-scale modeling capability under strict computational constraints. Finally, a Lightweight Oriented Bounding Box detection head (LwOBB) is employed to enable accurate localization of ship targets with arbitrary orientations. The experimental results on the publicly available R-SSDD and RSDD-SAR datasets show that the proposed detection network achieves higher detection accuracy while maintaining a lightweight structure. The model contains only 2.06 M parameters and has a computational complexity of 5.7 GFLOPs. Compared with several mainstream detection methods, the proposed method has achieved significant improvements in detection accuracy and effectively reduces the occurrence of missed detections and false alarms.

title: A Finite-Difference Time-Domain Approach for Conformal Electromagnetic-Thermal Coupled Analysis
authors: Zhonghui Li, Zhipeng Chen, Xianliang Wu, Minquan Li, Zhixiang Huang, Shuangqing Cao, Kaikun Niu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: To mitigate the staircase errors inherent in electromagnetic–thermal multiphysics coupling simulations, this work proposes a conformal electromagnetic-thermal coupling finite-difference time-domain (CETC-FDTD) method. For the electromagnetic field, conformality is achieved by weighting the constitutive parameters according to the fractional contributions of each medium along grid edges and faces. For the temperature field, the material parameters are conformally weighted by the volumetric fraction of each medium within the grid cell, thereby realizing consistent electromagnetic-thermal conformality. To achieve a more accurate and physically consistent temperature distribution within each grid cell, an interpolation scheme is developed to equivalently project the heat source onto the center of the Yee cell. This enables conformal treatment throughout the multiphysics electromagnetic-thermal coupling process. In addition, conformal considerations are also incorporated into the thermal convection boundary of the temperature field, where the corner temperatures are specially treated using directional weighting coefficients. The proposed method significantly improves the accuracy of electromagnetic–thermal coupling simulations while incurring virtually no additional memory overhead. Representative examples, including a cubic block, a sphere, a radome, and a three-dimensional packaging structure, are presented to validate the correctness and accuracy of the proposed CETC-FDTD method. The proposed approach exhibits strong potential for applications in radio-frequency and microwave engineering.

title: Three-Phase Hybrid Rectifier with Symmetric DC-bus for More Electric Aircrafts: Design and Validation using Controller Hardware-in-the-Loop
authors: Érico C. Guimarães, Marcus E. T. Souza Jr, Danillo B. Rodrigues, Luiz C. G. Freitas
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: Aircraft electrical power consumption has dramatically increased in recent years. A More Electric Aircraft (MEA) looks to replace many of the hydraulic and pneumatic systems with electrically driven motors and pumps. The key technology for realizing the MEA concept is power electronics. In this context, this work presents a proposal of a Three-phase Hybrid Rectifier with Symmetric DC-bus (TPHR-SDCB). The main goal is to replace the current Autotransformer Rectifier Unit (ATRU). The circuit topology derives from a simple Diode Bridge Rectifier Unit (DBRU) and a Four-Wire-Based Boost PFC converter associated with two Half-bridges resonant LLC converters (Boost PFC+LLC). The benefits of the proposed hybrid architecture are reduced system weight and higher efficiency, since the Boost PFC+LLC processes at most 15% of the rated power. Therefore, robustness and reliability can be increased due to the reduced thermal stresses to which the semiconductors are subjected. The proposed converter provides symmetric ±270V dc (540 V dc) from a three-phase 230V ac. A design example of 30 kW is presented, and the system's performance is evaluated using a using Controller Hardware-in the-Loop (C-HIL) platform.

title: Two-Port Quasi-Reflectionless Microwave Linear-Displacement Sensor Using a Balanced-Circuit Architecture
authors: Zekai Luo, Li Yang, Roberto Gómez-García
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: A microwave linear-displacement sensor with two-port quasi-reflectionless behavior is proposed. It is made up of a balanced-circuit architecture comprising the following parts: 1) a reflective-type RF-sensing network—constructed by a pair of identical bi-path transversal-signal-interference (TSI) sections serving as the stator and a set of duplicated open-circuit-ended stubs built on a common slider—and 2) two uniform wideband 3-dB quadrature couplers—each composed of three-stage parallel coupled lines and a terminating resistor at the isolated node—at the input and output ports of the overall circuit. In this manner, the frequency spacing between the notch bands of the RF-sensing network varies as a function of slider displacement. Meanwhile, the nontransmitted RF-signal energy coming from the RF-sensing network is dissipated by the resistors of the couplers, thus achieving quasi-reflectionless characteristics at both ports. As experimental validation, a 2-GHz microstrip prototype of the conceived RF sensor exhibiting a 20-mm dynamic range and an averaged sensitivity of 59.4 MHz/mm is manufactured and tested.

title: Development of a 4WD Omni Wheel Mobile Robot With Kinematics Control
authors: Mohammad Afif Ayob, Muhammad Hafizi Khairul Nazri, Nurasyeera Rohaziat, Hisyam Abdul Rahman, Azuwein Aida Bohari, Mohd Erdi Ayob
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: This paper presents the design and development of a 4WD omni-wheel mobile robot with kinematics control for enhanced precision and smooth movement in service applications. Built using 3D-printed components and controlled by an STM32 NUCLEO-H723ZG microcontroller, the robot utilizes SPG30E DC geared motors with encoders and MDD3A motor drivers. Its four omni-wheels enable seamless omnidirectional movement, maintaining orientation while navigating complex environments. Forward and inverse kinematics algorithms were implemented to ensure precise movement and accurate target positioning. Experimental results and MATLAB simulations demonstrated significant improvements in movement accuracy, with optimized wheel movement minimizing trajectory errors. The system is therefore suitable for service-oriented applications, particularly in hospitality and logistics environments. The findings highlight the robot's stability, efficiency, and suitability for automated delivery and service tasks in dynamic environments.

title: Zero-Shot Sentiment–Rating Consistency Analysis Using CLIP for Online Reviews
authors: Potla Pravallika, Pallavi Gairuboina, James Deva Koresh Hezekiah
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Product reviews are playing a major role in the decision of any individuals while doing their purchase online. Especially, the start ratings and the positive or negative reviews given over a product can change the decision of the purchase. In this paper, a CLIP based LLM model is proposed to provide a fresh perspective on reading the review text to estimate the sentiment of the review without providing any labelled data for the training process. Instead of that the system subsequently compares its estimation over the star rating in search of discrepancies, exaggerated evaluation, and special instances. This work is also providing an emotion based categorization on the product brands and their product for determining the level of satisfaction among the buyers. Experimental analysis indicates that CLIP embedding is effective at context based sentiment analysis and it explores a good scalability among the number of reviews and the customer behaviour without any training.

title: Constraint-Aware Multi-Objective Bayesian Optimization for Physical Connection Parameter Tuning in Brain–Computer Interfaces
authors: Hulin Jin, Zhiran Jin, Yong-Guk Kim
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Physical connectivity parameters have a decisive impact on the signal quality, synchronization stability, real-time performance, and safety compliance of Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI) systems. However, the optimization of these parameters is characterized by high dimensionality, strong coupling, multi-objective conflicts, and multiple safety constraints. This makes it difficult for traditional empirical or single-objective optimization methods to obtain globally feasible and performance-balanced solutions under finite sampling conditions. To address this, this paper proposes a Constraint-Aware Multi-Objective Bayesian Optimization (CA-MOBO) framework. This framework uses a multi-output Gaussian process to jointly model multiple performance indicators such as neural synchronization bias, decoding error, system latency, and energy consumption. As optimization, the model probabilistically characterizes physical and physiological constraints such as electrode impedance, stimulus intensity, feedback delay, and synchronization stability. The algorithm is based on Expected Hypervolume Improvement (EHVI) and introduces a feasibility probability modulation mechanism. This innovation constructs a unified acquisition function that integrates "performance gain— exploration and utilization—constraint satisfaction." Practical experiments were conducted on the BCI Competition IV-2a dataset, 32-channel SSVEP data, and a hardware-in-the-loop experimental platform for system evaluation. Results show that, compared to MOBO-EHVI, CA-MOBO improves hypervolume (HV) by approximately 11.6%, reduces IGD⁺ by approximately 24.3%, and increases feasibility by over 15% in 30 independent experiments. At the system level, decoding accuracy is improved by approximately 2.8 percentage points, synchronization stability index is improved by approximately 9.5%, and security violation rate is reduced to 2.7%. The proposed algorithm maintains stable performance across subjects, tasks (MI→SSVEP), and devices, and demonstrates superior overall efficiency in terms of system response latency and unit energy consumption.

title: Vibration Damping in Underactuated Cable-Suspended Artwork: Flying Belt Motion Control
authors: Martin Goubej, Lauria Clarke, Martin Hrabačka, David Tolar
publish: IEEE Robotics & Automation Magazine
abstract: This article presents a comprehensive refurbishment of the interactive robotic art installation Standards and Double Standards by Rafael Lozano-Hemmer. The installation features an array of belts suspended from the ceiling, each actuated by stepper motors and dynamically oriented by a vision-based tracking system that follows the movements of exhibition visitors. The original system was limited by oscillatory dynamics, resulting in torsional and pendulum-like vibrations that constrained rotational speed and reduced interactive responsiveness. To address these challenges, the refurbishment involved significant upgrades to both hardware and motion control algorithms. A detailed mathematical model of the flying belt system was developed to accurately capture its dynamic behavior, providing a foundation for advanced control design. An input shaping method, formulated as a convex optimization problem, was implemented to effectively suppress vibrations, enabling smoother and faster belt movements. Experimental results demonstrate substantial improvements in system performance and audience interaction. This work exemplifies the integration of robotics, control engineering, and interactive art, offering new solutions to technical challenges in real-time motion control and vibration damping for large-scale kinetic installations.

title: Numerical Analysis of Contact Resistivity Thresholds in Crystalline Silicon Solar Cells
authors: Ahathiyan G. S, H. Victor Du John, Harish Kathiravan M
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: Contact resistivity is a key factor which determine the electrical performance of high-efficiency crystalline silicon solar cells. In this proposed work, a comprehensive numerical analysis is perform to analyse the impact of front and rear contact resistivity using a physics-based device simulation in sentaurus TCAD. The specific contact resistivity (ρc) is varied from 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-9</sup> to 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup> while maintaining constant device geometry, material properties, and recombination parameters, enabling clear identification of the transition from ohmic to resistance-dominated transport. The results clearly indicate that the front contact is highly sensitive to resistivity, maintaining stable ohmic behavior for ρ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</inf>≤ 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-4</sup>Ω•cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and shifts to resistance-dominated transport within the range of 2 × 10⁻<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> to 4 × 10⁻<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. In this resistive range, the fill factor and power conversion efficiency degrades rapidly, this results in increased series resistance and current crowding at the front surface of solar cell, with numerical non-convergence appearing at higher resistivity levels. At rear contact, resistivity mainly contributes to the overall series resistance, which shows high tolerance to resistivity, with other electrical parameters remaining stable. These results define clear design limits and highlight the importance of front contact optimization in defining the resistive performance limits of crystalline silicon solar cells.

title: LegalAI: Automated Legal Document Analysis Platform
authors: Saurabh Mulik, Prathamesh Chvan Patil, Amey Kalsapnavar, Rudra Shete, Riddhi Mirajkar, Priya Jamdade
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The Smart Statement Reader is an AI-based system which uses Deep Learning and Natural Language Processing methods to ease the task of analyzing complex documents related to law and finance. The system has two major tasks: summarization of documents and highlighting at the clause level, both of which are helpful for end users in getting an overview of lengthy and complex documents. The framework leverages transformer-based networks, including Longformer for efficient long-text summarization, and LayoutLM for structural and spatial understanding of documents. Pooling these together allows the model to precisely locate and extract important provisions, such as duties, penalties, deadlines, and obligations, frequently embedded inside very lengthy legal documents. One of the main innovations of Smart Statement Reader is its ability to manage extended contextual dependencies-a problem for most traditional NLP systems-while maintaining semantic integrity and interpretability. By capturing textual and layout-based cues in the document, the model improves the accuracy of clause detection and summarization tasks with deeper contextual understanding. Furthermore, this approach also attempts to overcome the limitations of existing state-of-the-art models, particularly those that are afflicted with reduced interpretability, lesser transparency, and fragmented context in legal document analysis.

title: Hierarchical Uncertainty Aware Explainable Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Credit Default Risk Prediction
authors: Durga R, Vedala Naga Sailaja, Padmanaban K
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Financial institutions need credit default risk prediction to reduce losses to loans and have a stable portfolio management. Standard statistical models usually do not yield complex nonlinear relationships and feature interdependencies of large scale and imbalanced credit datasets. To circumvent these shortcomings, this paper suggests a Hierarchy Uncertainty Aware Explainable Hybrid Deep Learning Framework to predict credit default risk. The framework combines graph based hierarchical feature modelling based on a Graph Attention Network, a hybrid learning framework consisting of a combination of XGBoost and an attention based multilayer perceptron, and an attention based dynamic fusion mechanism. Monte Carlo dropout is also included that measures predictive uncertainty and SHAP based analysis offers model interpretability in a transparent manner. Large-scale real-world credit experimental test performance is much higher with an accuracy of 0.984 and a ROC AUC of 0.993 which is good minority default classification with an F1 score of 0.95 on the default class. The findings support that the suggested framework improves predictive accuracy, reliability, and transparency, thus suitable to be applied in real-life cases in the contemporary credit risk management systems.

title: Micro-Amplitude Wave Detection Based on Nonlinear Representation and Underdetermined Blind Reconstruction
authors: Lu Wang, Hao Li, Chunhui Zhao, P. Takis Mathiopoulos, Tomoaki Ohtsuki, Fumiyuki Adachi
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: In surface micro-vibration-based water–air cross-medium communication technology, existing research primarily focuses on idealized experimental settings involving single underwater sound sources and noise-free conditions. However, real-world ocean environments are often accompanied by multi-source interference, complex background noise, and nonlinear medium propagation, which pose significant challenges. To address these issues, this paper proposes an underdetermined blind source separation framework that combines a dual-feature-optimized generative adversarial network with subspace-based sparse feature extraction for surface micro-vibration signal detection. Specifically, a dual-feature-optimized U-Net GAN is designed to fuse temporal and time-frequency features, with blind source separation accuracy introduced as a feedback mechanism to guide adversarial training and improve the reconstruction of non-linear mixed signals. Subsequently, a subspace covariance-based mixing matrix estimation method is employed, coupled with a sparse-domain inverse transformation algorithm, to achieve accurate separation and reconstruction of source signals under underdetermined conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that the reconstructed source signals achieve 4.33% improvement in waveform similarity, 1.81 increase in source signal energy proportion, and 1.28 reduction in spectral distance compared to baseline algorithms.

title: Multi-pass Volume Reconstruction for Freehand Ultrasound: Demonstration in Simulation, Phantom, and In Vivo Kidney
authors: April Anlage, Qian Lin, Brian W. Anthony
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: Objective: This work describes the development and validation of an algorithm to improve ultrasound volume reconstruction from noisy initial scans taken using a tracked freehand ultrasound device. Methods: The algorithm was first tested and refined in simulation using an approximate kidney shape model re-sliced to represent various real life scan conditions. Next, an Intel RealSense D435i camera was used to track the pose of a commercial ultrasound probe. This device was used to scan a phantom with a known volume and shape. Finally, repeated scans were taken in vivo to test the repeatability of volume measurements. Results: The simulation experiments were used to refine algorithm hyperparameters. The volume of the phantom was measured to within 2.5-17% (1-11.5% with automatic pass finding) of ground truth for four different scan styles, compared to traditional methods that underestimate the volume by 17.5%. In 11 human subjects, right kidney volume was measured within between 3.6-14.6% (7.7-23.3% with automatic pass finding) variation between scans. Conclusion: The proposed algorithm was demonstrated to produce repeatable volume measurement results in both phantom and in vivo scans. Significance: The proposed algorithm improves the clinical translatability and repeatability of freehand volume ultrasound systems.

title: Early Stage Lung Cancer Diagnosis via Hybrid Learning Models on Histological Images
authors: T. M. Devi, Appala Harika, Amudalapalli Chandana Priya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The researchers in this study examine early-stage lung cancer diagnosis through hybrid learning models by applying three essential preprocessing steps to histopathological images which include normalization and augmentation and segmentation to enhance image quality and remove interference while extracting relevant tissue areas. The process of normalization standardizes staining and illumination methods throughout all samples while data augmentation produces additional variation in the dataset which helps improve model performance on new data and segmentation methods focus on extracting essential tissue structures that form the basis for cancer detection. The extracted features from Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) lead to the application of two traditional machine learning algorithms Support Vector Machines (SVM) and Random Forests (RF) for classification tasks. SVM creates an ideal decision boundary through high-dimensional CNN features which allows for exact distinction between benign and malignant tissue samples. RF operates as an ensemble classification method which combines multiple decision trees to create a more robust system that also enables users to understand which features have the biggest impact on the model’s performance. The different methods work together to improve classification results and decrease false alarms which leads to a more dependable hybrid diagnostic system for detecting lung cancer in its early stages.

title: FSMamba: A Frequency-Enhanced Spatial-Temporal Mamba Network for Local-Global Remote Sensing Image Change Detection_supp1-3691384.pdf
authors: Yan Xu
publish: N/A
abstract: Remote sensing change detection (RSCD) is challenged by complex scene variations, illumination differences, misregistration errors, and the need to jointly model global semantics and fine-grained local structures. To address these issues, this paper proposes FSMamba, a Frequency-enhanced Spatio-temporal Mamba network for local–global RSCD. FSMamba integrates frequency-domain analysis, spatiotemporal interaction, and efficient long-range dependency modeling within a unified framework. Specifically, a Frequency-Domain Enhancement Module (FDEM) is designed to capture global structural discrepancies via learnable spectral filtering, effectively suppressing pseudo-changes caused by illumination variations and noise. A Spatio-Temporal Perception Module (STPM) leverages channel-wise interaction and the linear-complexity visual state space model to enhance semantic consistency between bi-temporal features. In addition, a Global–Local Collaborative Decoder (GLCD) is introduced to adaptively fuse multi-scale features, preserving large-scale structural integrity while maintaining fine-grained boundary details. Extensive experiments on five public benchmark datasets demonstrate that FSMamba consistently outperforms state-of-the-art CNN, Transformer, and Mamba-based methods in terms of accuracy, robustness, and computational efficiency, validating its effectiveness under complex RSCD scenarios. Our source code is available on https://github.com/2419924948gyt/FSMamba.

title: Service-Oriented Robust Ship Fleet Planning Under Uncertainty_supp1-3689922.docx
authors: Tingsong Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: In the fiercely competitive and volatile shipping market, liner shipping companies face dual pressures to control costs and maintain service reliability. This study investigates a service-oriented robust ship fleet planning problem under uncertain container transportation demand, where the fill-rate is adopted as the service level indicator. The problem is formulated as a two-stage robust model that minimizes total costs while ensuring specified service levels for shippers. To align the decision-making process with business practice, an extended risk-bounded robust model is further developed, which captures the intrinsic relationship between nominal cost and worst-case cost and thereby helps managers make decisions according to their risk preferences. An enhanced column-and-constraint generation (EC&CG) algorithm is proposed to efficiently solve these models. To ensure research rigor, extensive out-of-sample tests and comprehensive sensitivity analyses are conducted to validate the robustness and applicability of the proposed models. The computational results demonstrate that the robust optimization approach exhibits excellent performance in both cost control and service level consistency, offering actionable insights and reliable decision support for ship fleet planning under uncertainty.

title: FruitTouch: A Perceptive Gripper for Gentle and Scalable Fruit Harvesting
authors: Ruohan Zhang, Mohammad Amin Mirzaee, Wenzhen Yuan
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: The automation of fruit harvesting has gained increasing significance in response to rising labor shortages. A sensorized gripper is a key component of this process, which must be compact enough for confined spaces, able to stably grasp diverse fruits, and provide reliable feedback on fruit conditions for efficient harvesting. To address this need, we propose FruitTouch, a compact gripper that integrates highresolution, vision-based tactile sensing through an optimized optical design. This configuration accommodates a wide range of fruit sizes while maintaining low cost and mechanical simplicity. Tactile images captured by an embedded camera provide rich information for real-time force estimation, slip detection, and softness prediction. We validate the gripper in real-world fruit harvesting experiments, demonstrating robust grasp stability and effective damage prevention. The hardware design files and simulation environment are open-sourced at project website: (https://fruittouch-dev.github.io/fruittouch/)

title: Enhancing Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Finance and Smart City Infrastructure: Development with Privacy Preservation using Blockchain-Enabled Federated Learning Frameworks
authors: Shobana D, M S Maharajan, V. Parimala, Zakaria Azzam, Ginni Nijhawan, M. Dinesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: As Artificial Intelligence (AI) becomes a more pervasive presence in high-value domains like healthcare, finance, and smart city infrastructure, it is fundamentally altering decision-making and service provision across society. But increased dependence on giant pools of sensitive data raises major issues about data privacy, data security, and regulatory compliance. We explore the integration of Federated Learning (FL) and Blockchain Technology as a strong framework to balance AI advancement and data privacy in distributed systems. In BEFL, each participant trains the model on their local data to avoid sending raw data as well as centralization and uses the blockchain mechanism to ensure the necessary transparency, traceability and trust of the other participants involved. For instance, in healthcare, where patient data confidentiality is of utmost importance, BEFL frameworks support institutions in joint model training for disease Diag, medical imaging, and treatment recommendation, etc., without compromising patient privacy, or breaking data sharing laws such as HIPAA or GDPR. Likewise, in the banking and finance industry, where data integrity and auditability are paramount, the combination of blockchain and federated learning can lead to creating fraud detection systems, risk assessment tools, and predictive analytics models, which can learn from a wide variety of datasets without revealing sensitive or proprietary financial information. This secure and distributed approach to AI development can be applied across smart city ecosystems, where a myriad of connected devices, sensors, and services— enabling intelligent and secure traffic optimization, energy management, and emergency response systems—can be created. In this paper, we propose an architectural design of the blockchain-based federated learning framework, introduce the main technical challenges, and present the corresponding mitigation strategies, including secure aggregation, differential privacy, and smart contract-based governance, etc.

title: Recall This Gesture? Investigating Gesture Memorability in Extended Reality_supp1-3678203.pdf
authors: zahra Borhani
publish: N/A
abstract: Gesture interaction is standard in modern extended reality head-mounted displays, e.g., Microsoft Hololens 2 and Apple Vision Pro. These gestures come from multiple sources, such as elicitation studies, the application designer, or an expert. However, it is unclear if the source of the gesture affects the memorability of gestures (i.e., level of recall). In a user study, 35 participants used the HoloLens 2 to test three gestures set from different sources: 1) expert-defined, whereby we asked four experts to define gestures; 2) user-defined, whereby each participant defined their gestures; and 3) elicitation-defined, whereby we used gestures proposed by previous work. Our results showed that expert-defined gestures (72.73% recall) were more likely to be remembered than elicitation-defined (59.87% recall) gestures. Further, user-defined gestures (88.75% recall) were the most memorable. These data suggest that the source of gestures has a substantial impact on their memorability.

title: Stochastic Analysis of Cramér-Rao Lower Bound for Positioning in mmWave-THz HetNets
authors: Jiajun He, Yiyong Sun, Feng Yin, Wenxin Xiong, Hing Cheung So, Hien Quoc Ngo, Hyundong Shin, Michail Matthaiou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: Terahertz (THz) frequency band has been widely studied and is recognized as a promising candidate for centimeter-level localization. However, the limited coverage of THz networks may result in localization failures, while a heterogeneous deployment of millimeter-wave (mmWave) and THz radio units (RUs) offers a viable solution to mitigate this issue. This paper presents a theoretical framework for evaluating the performance limits of localization systems in mmWave and THz heterogeneous networks. In this architecture, the mmWave RUs serve as macro base stations (BSs), while the THz RUs function as micro BSs distributed around each mmWave RU. By leveraging the standard tools of stochastic geometry to model the spatial distributions of the RUs and ambient obstacles, the localizability of a target is computed to evaluate the probability of achieving sufficient signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio for localization in both line-of-sight (LoS) and non-line-of-sight (NLoS) conditions. Furthermore, the Cramér-Rao lower bounds in both LoS and NLoS scenarios are analytically derived to characterize the overall positioning performance. Numerical results demonstrate that the hybrid deployment strategy significantly improves both the network coverage and localization accuracy compared to mmWave-only and THz-only networks.

title: Distributional Stability of Tangent-Linearized Gaussian Inference on Smooth Manifolds
authors: Junghoon Seo, Hakjin Lee, Jaehoon Sim
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Gaussian inference on smooth manifolds is central to robotics, but exact marginalization and conditioning are generally non-Gaussian and geometry-dependent. We study tangent-linearized Gaussian inference and derive explicit non-asymptotic <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$W\_{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> stability bounds for projection marginalization and surface-measure conditioning. The bounds separate local second-order geometric distortion from nonlocal tail leakage and, for Gaussian inputs, yield closed-form diagnostics from <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$(\mu,\Sigma)$</tex-math></inline-formula> and curvature/reach surrogates. Circle and planar-pushing experiments validate the predicted calibration transition near <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sqrt{\Vert \Sigma \Vert \_{\text{op}}}/R\approx 1/6$</tex-math></inline-formula> and indicate that normal-direction uncertainty is the dominant failure mode when locality breaks. These diagnostics provide practical triggers for switching from single-chart linearization to multi-chart or sample-based manifold inference. Code and Jupyter notebooks are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/mikigom/StabilityTLGaussian</uri>.

title: A Calibration-Free Multi-Expert System for Automated Diopter Measurement: Overcoming Noise-Induced Binocular Inconsistency
authors: Thimaporn Phetkaew, Wattanapong Kurdthongmee, Wei-Jou Duh
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Estimating ocular alignment in unconstrained settings faces challenges due to Noise-Induced Binocular Inconsistency (NIBI) which results from random events like blinking, head motion and tracking noise leading to temporary inter-ocular inconsistencies. Existing solutions typically follow either a framebased or a classification-based pipeline which often fail to yield reliable results especially when required to produce continuous measurements for clinical interpretation. In this work, we introduce a calibration-free deep multi-expert architecture that is capable of estimating the required prism diopters in a matter of seconds, by analyzing a short monocular video clip in real-time. Unlike prior methods, we tackle ocular alignment as a temporal problem, which results in improved robustness. Our approach consists of a Geometric Expert (Living Ruler) which outputs iris-normalized and scale invariant features. These are followed by a Trajectory Expert that encodes both eye movement temporal dynamics and deep image features, which are then passed through a Stability Supervisor. The Supervisor — a fully convolutional Transformer encoder — outputs frame-wise reliability scores which are subsequently used to suppress any noise-induced inconsistencies. Due to the lack of publicly available datasets with corresponding prism diopter annotations, we simulate geometry-based reference deviation values for eyes with real human eye geometry from the Columbia Gaze Dataset. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms other solutions in terms of both accuracy (MAE) of 1.3038Δ and R2 of 0.9963 for the range 0–50Δ and stability where a temporal fusion strategy leads to better results compared to frame-wise approaches especially when affected by transient noise. These findings indicate that continuous, calibration-free estimation of ocular deviation from standard video input is feasible, supporting potential applications in tele-screening and longitudinal monitoring.

title: Capturability as Controlled-Invariant Sets: Recursive Feasibility for Variable-Stepping Time S2S NMPC
authors: Chao Song, Xizhe Zang, Boyang Chen, Yan Liu, Le Qi, Jie Zhao
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Capturability characterizes a safe region of states for humanoid walking and is most commonly constructed by analyzing the one-dimensional divergent component of motion (DCM) of the center of mass. In this work, by exploiting the mathematical structure of the step-to-step (S2S) dynamics, we characterize capturability directly at the S2S level using the notion of a controlled positive invariant set (CPIS), which is computed via backward reachable sets (BRS). Although the inclusion of step time as a control input makes the S2S dynamics bilinear and renders these BRS difficult to compute directly, we show that, thanks to the specific mathematical structure of the S2S model, the capturable CPIS of the original nonlinear system can be exactly represented by the controlled invariant set of a linear system obtained at the minimum step time $T\_{\min }$. Building on this set-valued characterization, we first propose a variable-step-time ALIP-based NMPC that employs the CPIS as a terminal set to guarantee recursive feasibility. We validated our approach through numerical simulations of the ALIP model, showing that an NMPC formulation equipped with a CPIS terminal set increases the feasibility (success) rate for challenging initial conditions near the boundary of the capturable set. We further demonstrated the effectiveness of the proposed method via both simulation and real-world hardware experiments on the Bruce humanoid robot, as well as simulation experiments on the Unitree G1.

title: Behavior Aware Recovery and Closed Form Fisher Adaptation for Few Shot Electricity Theft Detection
authors: Yuting Ding, Jianyong Zheng, Fei Mei, Chen Chen
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Electricity theft poses serious risks to utility revenue and distribution grid stability, making robust Electricity Theft Detection (ETD) a critical priority for modern smart grids. Existing ETD methods often smooth out consumption behaviors during data recovery and show unstable performance under imbalanced and shifted deployment conditions. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an integrated Multi Metric Adaptive Recovery and Meta-Learned Fisher Discriminant (MMAR–MLFD) framework tailored for practical smart grid deployments. First, the Multi-Metric Adaptive Recovery (MMAR) module integrates multiple complementary autoencoder objectives and adaptively fuses multiple reconstruction outputs to preserve theft related load irregularities while suppressing noise under missing measurements. Second, the Meta-Learned Fisher Discriminant (MLFD) performs episodic meta learning to learn a shared encoder. Each task then derives a Fisher decision boundary in closed form from a few labeled users, avoiding inner-loop updates. Validated on a national scale smart grid dataset, MMAR–MLFD reduces recovery error by up to 55.3% and improves detection accuracy by 7–14%. The proposed method also lowers false-positive rates by more than one-third, reducing unnecessary field inspections and enhancing the reliability of revenue-protection operations.

title: A Fast Differentially Private Distributed Algorithm Over Unbalanced Directed Networks
authors: Mengxue Ran, Zheng Wang, Yan Lei, Hongyi Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal and Information Processing over Networks
abstract: This paper develops a novel distributed optimization algorithm which combines the differentially private technique with the Barzilai-Borwein method (DPBB) for unbalanced directed networks. The algorithm protects the gradient data of agents by adding Gaussian noise to a gradient tracking process, achieving <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$(\varepsilon,\delta)$</tex-math></inline-formula>-differential privacy with an explicit expression. Then, it analyses the trade-off between convergence accuracy and privacy level. In order to accelerate convergence rate, it incorporates the Barzilai-Borwein method for dynamically adjusting the step size. DPBB is ultimately able to reach the neighborhood of optimal solution in a linearly convergent manner. Two distributed logistic regression examples demonstrate the algorithms' effectiveness for fast convergence speed, low optimal mean square error and high model classification accuracy.

title:
A Phase Change Coupled Model for Plasma Arc Erosion on SF
                    <sub>6</sub>
                    Circuit Breaker Electrodes under Turbulent Flow
authors: Xinyuan Hong, Youyuan Wang, Yuxiang Leng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Electrode erosion caused by plasma arcs is a major factor limiting the electrical lifetime of high-voltage switching equipment. Accurate modeling of this process is therefore essential. However, experimental observations reveal diffuse and nearly uniform erosion on electrodes after current interruption, in contrast to the localized crater morphology simulated by established arc models. This study indicates the physical origin of this discrepancy is turbulent flow during the arcing process. To examine this hypothesis, a multi-physics simulation framework is developed, which coupled magnetohydrodynamics, a k–ε turbulence model, and phase-change of the electrode material. Comparisons under laminar and turbulent conditions demonstrate that strong turbulence acts as an important macroscopic factor affecting the erosion morphology and material evaporation. Experimental validation is conducted on a 252 kV self-blast SF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sub> circuit breaker. The proposed model estimates the evaporated mass and length loss with errors below 3.43% and 3.56%, respectively. These results provide a reliable numerical tool and theoretical basis for electrical lifetime assessment and structural optimization of high-voltage switching equipment.

title: CrossTrack-UAV: A Real-World Dataset and Baseline for Cross-View Multi-Object Tracking using Fixed-Wing UAVs
authors: Chungsu Jang, Youngjung Kim, Sebin Lee, Wonsuk Kwon, Taehyun Kim, Yong-Duk Kim, Sung-Eui Yoon
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Cross-view multi-object tracking (CVMOT) aims to simultaneously track a group of objects over time in each view and to associate the same objects across different views. This task is essential for swarm monitoring and has attracted considerable attention in the literature. However, most existing datasets focus on static cameras or cameras with stable motion in low-altitude environments, which hinders progress in developing methods for fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). To fill the gap, this work presents CrossTrack-UAV: the first real-world and large-scale CVMOT dataset using fixed-wing UAV swarms equipped with 2-axis gimbal cameras. CrossTrack-UAV was collected by four UAVs flying manually, covering five different cities in South Korea. The resulting dataset comprises 53 distinct scenarios and 1,272 cross-view tracks with 1,059,408 annotated 2D bounding boxes for 3 classes. It also contains synchronized metadata from UAVs, including GPS position, altitude, and gimbal orientations essential for extracting 3D geometric features. We introduce a CVMOT baseline built on the multi-sensor joint probabilistic data association filter (M-JPDAF). We extend M-JPDAF with spatial coherence processing to filter noisy and inconsistent data. Extensive experiments on CrossTrack-UAV demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed baseline and the value of the benchmark.

title: A Multi-Modal Machine Learning Framework Integrating Medical Records and Wearable Sensor Data for Early Diabetes Prediction
authors: Divya. M, D. Shunmuga Kumari, P.Anusha, Angeline Ranjithamani, A. Angel Cerli, V. Divya
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Diabetes mellitus is a rapidly emerging health challenge globally and delayed diagnosis of the disease tends to lead to severe complications, besides increasing the healthcare cost. Traditional screening methods are mainly based on periodic clinical tests and static medical records, limiting the ability of obtaining physiological changes in early stage states. Existent prediction models often use a single source of data which results in poorly accurate prediction in generalization across different populations. This study is to develop a multi-modal machine learning framework which consists of a combination of electronic medical records (EMRs) and continuous wearable sensor data that would enable prediction of diabetes as early as possible and with high accuracy. Some feature engineering techniques, temporal aggregation, and data normalization were applied prior to training the model. Ensemble and deep learning models, such as Random Forest, XGBoost and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks were tested based on stratified cross-validation. The integrated multi-modal model achieved an accuracy of 94.2%, precision of 93.1%, recall of 95.4%, F1-score of 94.2% and an AUC of 0.97 which are 8-12% better than single modality-baselines. The results show that integration of medical records with wearable sensor data is able to improve prediction of diabetes early significantly in favor of proactive intervention and personalized healthcare delivery.

title: EffcientNetB0-based Deep Learning Approach for Early Plant Disease Detection using Leaf Image Classification
authors: M. Poojitha, U. Sai Ragini, James Deva Koresh Hezekiah
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Plant diseases can damage crops and reduce their growth, which often results in economic problems in agriculture. Detecting these diseases at an early stage is essential to protect crop health and improve productivity. In this work, we developed an automated plant disease identification system using deep learning and leaf images from crops such as corn, grapes, and apples. Our introduced model is based on EfficientNetB0, which showed outstanding performance in classifying multiple disease categories. To make the system more reliable, data augmentation techniques were used to manage different lighting condition in lighting, angles, and backgrounds. The model obtained an accuracy of 97.92%, outperforming other architectures like InceptionV3, MobileNetV2, DenseNet121, Xception, and VGG16 with a lesser accuracy of 83.33%, 78.33%, 77.08%, 75%, 73.33% respectively. Performance measures like precision, recall, and F1-score confirmed its strong performance. The confusion matrix further showed that the model effectively distinguishes between healthy and diseased leaves. This approach provides a fast and accurate solution for real-time disease detection. Overall, the proposed system can support farmers in taking timely action and promoting sustainable agricultural practices.

title: Axial-Field Switched-Reluctance Machine for In-Wheel Motor Applications: Hybrid Analytical Modeling (Reluctance Network and formal Analytical solution) and Inductance Measurements
authors: Imane Belmahi, Salim Asfirane, Georges Barakat, Ferhat Chabour, Yacine Amara
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: The aim of this paper is to study the possibilities of an axial-field switched reluctance machine. The analytical model established from the solution of Maxwell’s equations is developed and combined with a lumped parameter electromagnetic equivalent circuit model (expert reluctance network) to account for the analytical model’s lack of consideration of magnetic saturation of ferromagnetic material. This is based on the correction of air-gap flux density computed via the analytical model with the reluctance network, which significantly improves the results. A prototype has been built, and the tests allow for the evaluation of the capabilities of switched reluctance axial-field structures for in-wheel motor applications. Results on self-inductance estimation show that the Hybrid analytical model allows for corrections of the Analytical model showing a better agreement with measurements and three-dimensional finite element method model.

title: Capacity-Optimized Design of Reconfigurable Antenna Patterns for MU-MIMO Systems
authors: Cuicui Zhang, Ming Zhang, Xiaoming Chen, Xiaobo Liu, Shitao Zhu, Anxue Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Reconfigurable antennas have emerged as a promising solution in wireless communication systems to adapt to dynamic environments and enhance system performance. Pattern reconfigurability plays a crucial role in improving antenna adaptability to spatially diverse multi-cluster channels, thus maximizing channel capacity. Existing works primarily address either the mode selection problem from a pre-designed finite pattern set which adjusts only beamwidth and scanning direction and thus can not offer optimal channel adaptation, or the mode design problem of computing continuously adjustable patterns under the assumation of an infinite pattern number but without considering the limit on the pattern number in real-world antennas. To address this gap, this paper proposes a practical design methodology that jointly accounts for the finite number of reconfigurable patterns and their precise optimization over diverse channel conditions. Specifically, channels are grouped based on user location distribution, and a joint optimization approach combining alternating optimization, auxiliary variables, and bisection is employed to design the optimal pattern set under the constraint of limited reconfigurable states. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the optimized patterns and show that the achieved capacity exhibits an approximately logarithmic relationship with the number of reconfigurable patterns, thereby providing a practical validation of existing theoretical analyses. This work bridges the gap between theoretical mode design and practical implementation by offering a concrete and implementable solution for designing a finite set of reconfigurable antenna patterns tailored to realistic system constraints.

title: Automated Park & Charge: Architecture and Evaluation of Coordinated EV Charging in Automated Parking Facilities
authors: Bassem Hichri, Mohamed Amine Mejri, Fabian Dürkop, Axel Sturm
publish: 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Factory Communication Systems (WFCS)
abstract: The increasing adoption of electric vehicles introduces challenges for charging infrastructure, including limited charger availability and inefficient utilization due to long parking durations. Automated Valet Park & Charge (AVPC) systems combine automated valet parking with coordinated charging management and have the potential to improve charging infrastructure utilization by enabling vehicles to be autonomously relocated between parking and charging positions. This paper presents a system architecture for AVPC that defines communication interfaces between user services, vehicle systems, facility infrastructure, and external information services. Preliminary results indicate that models trained on previous charging sessions can be used to improve the accuracy of parking duration and energy demand compared to user inputs and that automated parking combined with coordinated charging scheduling can increase the ratio of satisfied charging sessions under high charging demand. Future work will investigate the integration of smart grid information into the charging scheduling.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Drug Delivery Systems: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Shraddha Zade, Sachin Dagadkar, Bansod Sneha Bharat, Chinmaya Khadse, Priya Kate, Khushi Tulankar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is revolutionizing pharmaceutical sciences, particularly drug delivery systems (DDS), by enhancing precision, efficiency, and personalization. This review explores AI applications in optimizing formulation design, predicting drug release kinetics, and enabling smart, targeted delivery. Key methodologies include machine learning models such as neural networks, support vector machines, and reinforcement learning. Advantages encompass accelerated development, reduced costs, and improved therapeutic outcomes, while disadvantages involve data dependency and model interpretability issues. Limitations and challenges, including ethical concerns and regulatory hurdles, are discussed. Future prospects highlight AI’s potential in personalized medicine .

title: A Real-Time Pixel-Streaming Architecture for Accurate Fabric Defect Detection
authors: Thigaram Divya Teja, Vangara Sai Navaneeth, P Nithish Kumar, Sunkesula Alisha Babu, T V V Satyanarayana
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Automatic detection of defects on fabric images is one of the requirements of fabric inspection systems today, and the accuracy and efficiency of the processing are critical. The current methods that are founded on low-rank decomposition are mostly iterative optimization and high memory consuming which do not fit real-time image processing systems. This paper introduces a completely streaming fabric defect detection architecture, which is modeled and described at the register-transfer level in Verilog HDL and tested through simulation, which is appropriate on FPGAs. The proposed architecture does not require frame buffering and iteration since frame processing occurs per clock cycle in a pipelined architecture with specific modules like Memory Ladder, CNSIU, DCFD, Texture Fusion, HAMM and Loop-Hole Rectification. Verilog simulation of the design is tested in Xilinx simulation tools and resource estimation is done by synthesis in Yosys which shows an estimated hardware resource usage of approximately 20k logic elements, 1.3k LUTs, 900 flip-flops and one DSP block. Experimental evaluation shows a detection accuracy of 92.4%, precision of 91%, recall of 90% and F1-score of 90.5%. Unlike decomposition-based methods, the proposed architecture is deterministic and has low memory demands and can be run at a high processing rate, an aspect that qualifies it to be used in future FPGA -based real-time textile inspection systems.

title: Design Hybrid System for Secure Cloud Storage based on Advanced Homomorphic Encryption
authors: Gireesh Kambala
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Cloud computing has innovated data processing and storage with its scalability, flexibility, and cost-effectiveness. Classic encryption methods guarantee the confidentiality of data, but these methods necessitate decryption for processing, thus revealing the data to possible breaches. The SecCloud-HHE (Secure Cloud Hybrid Homomorphic Encryption) is the subject of this paper, and it is a hybrid encryption architecture designed for secure and efficient cloud data storage. The combination of homomorphic encryption for encrypted computation and attribute-based encryption for fine-grained access control gives SecCloud-HHE confidentiality, scalability and controlled access. The encryption and decryption times for the smallest files (24 bytes) were very low and measured at 4.13 ms and 0.90 ms, respectively. The timings for the biggest files (8248 bytes) were still 1319.34 ms for encryption and 311.21 ms for decryption, which are very long but still practical, thus showing the algorithm's scalability. Cipher text representing a linear growth, rising from fragments of 28 bits to 8270 bits, assures the stable overweightness of storage. The time for key generation and verification also increased linearly. When compared to the current methods such as ECC (Elliptic Curve Cryptography), KP-ABE (Key-Policy Attribute-Based Encryption), and VFHE-DWOA (Verifiable Fully Homomorphic Encryption with Dynamic Weighted Optimization Algorithm), SecCloud-HHE lagged behind all with no noticeable delay. The findings back the framework's appropriateness for live and limited-resource cloud settings, thus providing a safe, effective, and scalable option for encrypted data processing, as well as for access control.

title: An Integrated Deep Learning and Texture-based Approach for Brain Aneurysm Segmentation and Classification
authors: T V S Gowtham Prasad, Yatham Srinivas, Banda Niranjan, Pathuri Sreekanth, Yalamanchili Karthik
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Identifying the brain aneurysms at an early stage and accurately is essential to prevent morbidity and mortality due to rupture. An automated segmentation of brain aneurysm and classification system is developed using Deep Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) that was implemented in MATLAB 2021. This proposed project contains a structured pipeline consisting of image preprocessing, handcrafted features extraction, classification using Deep Learning based method to improve diagnosis. RGB images of brain angiographic dataset obtained from Kaggle are changed to locate aneurysms using grayscale and denoised with hybrid median filtering and Wiener filtering methods to preserve the vascular edges. The Local Binary Pattern (LBP) and a fuzzy LBP segmentation scheme were used to accurately locate the aneurysms considering their intensity. The refined segmentations were accomplished by using morphological operations and adaptive thresholding methods and GLCM features are used to define the spatial texture of the segmented aneurysms, providing a detailed quantitative analysis of the localized tissue, they are classified into 3 types (saccular, fusiform, and ruptured) using a deep CNN model. The experimental results of the 80/20 train/test dataset for image segmentation demonstrate that proposed system provides higher performance than existing traditional ML approaches (Thresholding + SVM) and region-based ANN models. The developed system achieved an accuracy of 94.49% and specificity of 91.42%. These findings metrics shows that, the framework's efficiency as a clinical decision-support tool and highlight its suitability for future deployment in edge computing environments.

title: AI-Driven Learning Analytics for STEM Education in Primary School Admission
authors: Dina Fitria Murad, Deryan Everestha Maured, Bambang Dwi Wijanarko, Silvia Ayunda Murad, Siti Maryam, Shahnorbanun Sahran
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) education is increasingly emphasized as a foundation for preparing students to adapt to a rapidly advancing technological world. While numerous studies have explored the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in quiz generation, assessment, and personalized learning, most applications have been developed for secondary or higher education contexts. Limited attention has been given to primary school admission, where early assessment can play a pivotal role in identifying learning readiness and promoting equitable access to STEM education. This study proposes an AI-driven web-based system that integrates quiz generation, learning analytics, and personalized recommendations for primary school admission. The system leverages the Groq API to automatically generate STEM-oriented quizzes tailored to students’ age, analyzes their responses to detect strengths and weaknesses, and suggests targeted learning materials to support readiness. An experimental implementation demonstrates the feasibility of producing diverse quiz items through Groq’s high-performance inference, providing meaningful diagnostic insights, and offering adaptive recommendations. The novelty of this research lies in applying AI-powered assessment to the admission stage of primary education, bridging a gap overlooked in prior studies. By unifying quiz creation, analytics, and recommendation in one framework, the system highlights both challenges and opportunities for enhancing early STEM education, with implications for improving equity and personalized learning pathways.

title: Online Meteorological Forecasting Model Using Multi-feature Information Gain PSO and Evolving Fuzzy Systems
authors: Lei Hu, Xinghan Xu, Xingyi Miao, Jianwei Liu, Min Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Evolutionary Computation
abstract: Reliable meteorological forecasting is crucial for climate-resilient cities, renewable-energy integration, aviation and maritime operations, and public-health early warning. Modern sensor networks and reanalysis products generate multi-variate streams at fine temporal resolutions, where seasonal transitions, synoptic regimes, and extreme events can continuously reshape cross-variable dependencies and predictive relevance. Such nonstationarity and concept drift may inflate model complexity and degrade adaptability in evolving fuzzy systems (EFSs). To address this, we propose MIGPSO-EFS, an online forecasting framework that tightly couples a drift-aware binary feature optimizer with an interpretable EFS under a leakage-free sliding-window protocol. Specifically, an improved binary particle swarm optimizer periodically reselects informative lagged covariates via information-gain ranking, multi-group weighted sampling, importance-aware mutation, and adaptive threshold gating, while the downstream EFS evolves a compact rule base using density-based structure adaptation and updates consequents through fuzzy weighted recursive least squares with activation-ratio pruning for low-latency inference. Experiments on real-world datasets from five cities across minute-, hour-, and day-level resolutions, together with multi-horizon prediction and extreme-event level-grading evaluation, show consistent accuracy–efficiency advantages over representative evolving, deep-learning, and classical/hybrid baselines. Wilcoxon signed-rank tests further confirm that the improvements are statistically significant, demonstrating robust and practical online forecasting under drifting meteorological regimes.

title: Enhanced Speed Control of SPMSMs via Physics-Informed Neural Network-Based Active Disturbance Rejection Control
authors: Jialiang Dai, Zhaoyi Wang, Rongrui Lin, Li Liu, Jungho Ahn, Ju Lee, Hyunwoo Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion
abstract: This paper presents a novel Active Disturbance Rejection Control (ADRC) strategy enhanced by a Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN), designed to compensate for the rotor speed derivative-i.e., speed acceleration, which is typically neglected in conventional ADRC implementations. Owing to the difficulty in accurately measuring speed acceleration and its inherent nonlinear characteristics, the motor's dynamic equations are embedded within the neural network, and automatic differentiation is utilized to estimate acceleration. A physics-guided loss function is constructed to ensure the convergence of the network output. The PINN architecture is specifically designed to address complex nonlinear mappings while operating within the computational constraints of microcontrollers. In comparison with two representative ADRC configurations, the proposed method demonstrates improved dynamic performance. Its effectiveness is experimentally verified on a 1-kW permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) drive platform.

title: Variable Selection-Based Recursive Method for Adaptive Quality-Related Fault Detection_supp1-3689128.pdf
authors: Yan Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Fault detection (process monitoring) for dynamic and nonlinear industrial processes, which can be viewed as complex systems, has become increasingly important in recent years. Under complex and dynamic operating conditions, traditional multivariate statistical process monitoring (MSPM) methods struggle to effectively capture the evolving behaviors of quality indicators at the system level. In this article, a novel variable selection-based adaptive MSPM method is proposed for quality-related process monitoring. First, an improved part mutual information (PMI) method is proposed for variable selection, categorizing process variables into quality-related and quality-unrelated groups, addressing the shortcomings of the original PMI being able to measure the correlation between only two variables. Second, a novel recursive kernel principal component regression (RKPCR) incorporating the hierarchical model order-reduction strategy is proposed to effectively track quality indicators. This method screens high-quality data for model updates based on distance-based classification and linear approximation, which mitigates model degradation caused by faulty data. In addition, a recursive kernel principal component analysis (RKPCA) method similar to RKPCR is proposed to monitor quality-unrelated faults. Finally, a numerical example and two industrial processes are used to demonstrate the performance of the proposed method.
