title: Parallel Nonlinear Dynamic Control of Microgravity Active Vibration Isolation
authors: Song Wang, Wenbo Dong, Mengxi Yu, Zongfeng Li, Shuquan Wang, Ruinan Mu, Ke Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article proposes a coupled nonlinear dynamic model and a novel parallel control strategy for microgravity active vibration isolation systems, aiming to address the critical technical limitations of existing methods in practical satellite payload applications. Current dynamic models and linear/nonlinear control approaches fail to fully capture the strong dynamic coupling effects and inherent nonlinear characteristics of active vibration isolation systems for actual satellite payloads, leading to their inability to meet the stringent microgravity environment requirements of high-precision space science experiments. To tackle this challenge, we derive the coupled nonlinear dynamic model of the spacecraft-umbilical cable-payload platform via the Newton–Euler method. On this basis, a dynamic feedforward compensation controller based on the super-twisting algorithm is designed for the spacecraft, while a parallel controller integrating relative pose constraints, acceleration feedforward and feedback is developed for satellite payloads. This three-layer parallel control architecture ensures both the operational safety of the payload and superior vibration isolation performance. Finally, the effectiveness and engineering feasibility of the proposed model and control strategy are comprehensively verified through numerical simulations and physical experiments based on a ground-based air-float test system, providing a reliable technical solution for high-precision microgravity active vibration isolation of satellite payloads.

title: A Quantum Computing-Based Neural Network Approach for Modeling Electrical Discharge Machining
authors: A. Karunyan, Raja Das
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Electrical discharge machining (EDM) is a stochastic procedure used for machining difficultto- cut materials such as AISI D2 steel; yet, precisely forecasting surface roughness (Ra) is problematic due to the non-linear interactions among the process parameters. Although data-driven models like the Backpropagation Neural Networks (BPNNs) are extensively utilized, enhancing their generalization ability on a constrained experimental dataset continues to be a prominent research focus. This study assesses a hybrid Quantum Neural Network (QNN) framework for predictive modelling. The architecture utilizes Angle Encoding through parameterized Rx rotation gates to transform classical inputs such as (Peak Current, Pulse-on Time, Duty Factor, Voltage) into quantum states, subsequently employing a variational circuit with linear entanglement to capture complex feature correlations. The suggested model was executed and verified in the MATLAB environment to illustrate the algorithmic benefits of the quantum processes. Experimental validation was performed utilizing a dataset including 90 observations obtained from the machining trials. The QNN’s performance was evaluated in comparison to standard Artificial Neural Networks (ANN), Radial Basis Function Networks (RBFN), and Support Vector Regression (SVR). The QNNmodel attained a Correlation Coefficient (R) of 0.9643 and a Mean Squared Error (MSE) of 0.1642. The results demonstrate that the suggested quantum architecture provides enhanced predictive accuracy for this application, outperforming the conventional classical baselines in modelling the intricate surface roughness profile.

title: Application of the Inverse of Equivalent Conductance Method for Eliminating Crosstalk in Capacitive and Incremental Sensor Arrays
authors: Carlos Medrano-Sanchez, Sergio Dominguez-Gimeno, Javier Martinez-Cesteros
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Sensor arrays can be measured by selecting a row-column pair with multiplexers. However, the basic circuit configuration does not extract the desired sensor value but the equivalent sensor value between that pair. In this paper, we prove that the Inverse of Equivalent Conductance Method, which was originally tested with piezoresistive sensor arrays, is also valid regardless of the sensor type (in particular for capacitive sensors). Moreover, a new linear approximation is presented for incremental sensors in which the admittance takes the form y = y<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub> + Δy. The error of the numerical algorithms for estimating sensor values depends on the array size, the sensor tolerance, the span, and the measurement uncertainty. The influence of these factors is analyzed by means of simulations. The original fixed-point solution of the method performs better in general, but the error of the linear approximation becomes very similar if the measurement uncertainty or the array size increase. For a large 16x16 array, the error of the proposed approaches is better than 1% for a tolerance of 10%, an admittance span of 40%, and a measurement uncertainty lower than 0.01%. Besides, a typical pressure image is shown and post-processed resulting in a very effective ghost object removal. In addition, capacitive and resistive physical prototypes with sizes up to 12x12 are also tested, for which the average error is less than 2%. The proposed approaches are based on a rigorous theory and make it possible to eliminate crosstalk using fast algorithms, while keeping simple and affordable DAQ circuits.

title: Fast Multi-Frequency Spherical Antenna Measurement via Time Domain Representation
authors: Nicolas Mézières, Laurent Le Coq, Gwenn Le Fur
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: The characterization of radiation patterns of antennas are necessary but challenging and thus the reduction of field sampling sizes have been hugely investigated. The recent advances in compressive sensing led to significant data reduction along numerous studies of sampling distributions over the scanning surface. This work proposes an efficient and reliable method to foster the information contained into the frequency axis to improve the reconstruction accuracy from undersampled data. This technique is based on the Least Angle RegresSion algorithm coupled with a Fourier Transform and is validated on both simulation and experimental antenna measurement cases of various topologies and complexities.

title: E-Link: A 256-ch Mini-Pedestal Connector for High-Density Soft Neural Interfaces
authors: Tianyu Bai, Gen Li, Yongli Qi, Hui Fang
publish: IEEE Journal on Flexible Electronics
abstract: High-density thin-film neural interfaces face persistent packaging challenges at the electrode–electronics interface, where conventional rigid connectors impose high insertion forces, strict alignment requirements, and limited reusability. We present E-Link (Elastomeric Link), a modular 256-channel mini-pedestal connector designed to facilitate simplified, user-friendly assembly through an insertion-free, alignment-tolerant interface utilizing an elastomeric conductive interposer and a compliant threaded compression mechanism. Finite element analysis and experimental validation confirm uniform contact stress across the 16 × 16 interconnect array. The system achieves stable electrical performance, exhibiting contact impedances of 0.3-0.4 kΩ, an RMS noise floor of 2.68 ± 0.46 μV, and a connection yield exceeding 97% over 100 mating cycles. The detachable architecture reduces chronic head-mounted mass from 6.6 g to 2.8 g while maintaining safe thermal operation (30.5 °C steady-state) under a full 20 kHz sampling bandwidth. Design analysis further indicates scalability toward 1024-channel integration within the same 25-mm-diameter footprint by leveraging fine-pitch elastomeric conductive pillars. This work provides a robust and scalable connection solution for high-density flexible neural interfaces with broad application for bioelectronics.

title: Lighting-Robust Behavioral Cloning via Bayesian Visual Encoding for Robot Manipulation
authors: Zhenyuan Dong, Dongxu Han, Zhi Wang, Zhiyi Yan, Qian Lin, Ziyi Zhang, Peng Zhang
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Behavioral cloning (BC) has demonstrated strong performance in robot manipulation by learning visuomotor policies directly from expert demonstrations. However, under illumination-induced distribution shifts, vision-based BC policies often become sensitive to spurious visual variations, leading to degraded reliability and generalization. To address this issue, we propose a lighting-robust BC framework that incorporates Bayesian deep learning into the visual encoder. Specifically, we model a posterior distribution over convolutional weights and optimize the evidence lower bound, where a Kullback–Leibler regularization term constrains feature learning under illumination variability. In simulation, the proposed method achieves higher success rates and shorter rollout lengths than deterministic baselines, while maintaining strong sample efficiency with a single Monte Carlo sample. In real-world experiments across seven illumination regimes, including distribution-shifted conditions, our method consistently outperforms BC baselines and remains stable under severe lighting changes. These results indicate that Bayesian regularization provides an effective inductive bias for learning visual representations that improve robustness and generalization in vision-based robot manipulation.

title: A Compact Thermal Model and a New Cooling Factor Metric for Active Antennas
authors: Feza Turgay Celik, Alexander Yarovoy, Yanki Aslan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper introduces a compact thermal model (CTM) for antenna-level analysis of the PCB structures that enables fast and accurate prediction of the chip temperature (Tchip) under varying structural and environmental conditions. The CTM represents conduction within the antenna system and convection and radiation to the environment through an equivalent lumped-element network, providing a computationally efficient alternative to full thermal simulations. A principal contribution is the definition of the Antenna Cooling Factor (ACF), a new parameter that quantifies a cooling capability of the antenna and its influence on the resulting Tchip. The ACF offers designers a direct means to assess thermal behavior during the antenna-design process, thereby supporting integrated electro-thermal co-design of antenna systems. Using the proposed model, designers can simultaneously assess electromagnetic characteristics (e.g., bandwidth, maximum realized gain), structural thermal constraints (e.g., chip or antenna temperature), and joint parameters such as antenna noise temperature. The proposed CTM and ACF framework is validated experimentally using a dedicated thermal measurement setup. Nine antenna prototypes resonating at 3.5 GHz with different dielectric materials, element sizes, and antenna types are fabricated, connectorized, and characterized through combined electromagnetic and thermal measurements. The strong agreement between measurements and model predictions confirms the accuracy and practical relevance of the approach. The results establish a systematic methodology for incorporating thermal performance into antenna design with minimal computational load.

title: ReCoTR: Reducing Semantic Cognitive Shift via Dual-Consensus Token Compression for Remote Sensing Image-Text Retrieval
authors: Jirui Huang, Yaxiong Chen, Chuang Du, Shengwu Xiong, Xiaoqiang Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: With the rapid advancement of vision-language models (VLMs) in general-purpose settings, their application to cross-modal retrieval and semantic understanding of large-scale multimodal remote sensing (RS) data is emerging as a key enabler for urban governance, environmental monitoring, and disaster response. However, the pervasive issue of semantic shift in RS image poses a significant challenge to the transferability of pre-trained VLMs. To address this limitation, we propose ReCoTR, an enhanced CLIP-based cross-modal retrieval framework tailored for remote sensing applications. ReCoTR tackles region-level granularity bias and contextual semantic drift through a Dual Consensus Token Evaluation (DCTE) module, which leverages a mixture-of-experts strategy to fuse inter-modal semantic consensus with intra-modal structural consistency, enabling fine-grained estimation of semantic confidence for visual tokens. Moreover, to mitigate representational contamination caused by background noise, we introduce the Semantic Confidence Token Compression (SCTC) module. This module selectively filters and aggregates tokens with high semantic relevance, thus reducing redundancy and alleviating the noise amplification inherent in CLIP’s average pooling. Experimental results on three benchmark RS cross-modal retrieval datasets demonstrate that ReCoTR consistently outperforms existing methods on bidirectional image-text retrieval tasks, validating its effectiveness and robustness in remote sensing semantic alignment scenarios. Our source codes are available at: https://github.com/Jerry710/ReCoTR.git.

title: Microwave Photonics Empowered Minimalist Base Station for Integrated Sensing and Communication via Cascaded Intensity and Phase Modulation
authors: Shengkun Chen, Zhenglin Tang, Lingjie Zhang, Yaowen Zhang, Mengke Wang, Zhen Zeng, Heping Li, Zhiyao Zhang, Yong Liu
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) is a pivotal enabling technology for next-generation wireless networks, yet its deployment is impeded by system complexity. While microwave photonics offers a viable route to ISAC and aligns well with the present cloud radio-access-network (C-RAN) architectures, existing approaches still face challenges such as bulky base station designs and spectrum competition between sensing and communication. Here, we propose and demonstrate a microwave photonic-empowered ISAC scheme based on cascaded intensity and phase modulation, which enables an ultra-simplified base station using a photodiode-driven antenna configuration. This scheme not only overcomes group-velocity dispersion-induced power fading in long-haul fiber transmission by leveraging complementary frequency responses of cascaded intensity and phase modulation, but also expands usable bandwidth and mitigates spectrum contention between sensing and communication. Additionally, chirp-based phase modulation is adopted to suppress Brillouin-scattering-induced signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) degradation, achieving an 11-dB improvement in the Brillouin threshold, supporting high-power transmission in fiber. In the experiment, wireless communication with an equivalent common-public-radio-interface (CPRI) data rate of 331.776 Gbit/s over a 20-km analog radio-over-fiber (A-RoF) link and 3-m wireless transmission is demonstrated, alongside centimeter-accuracy wireless ranging (9.81 cm resolution). This work provides critical hardware and system-level foundations for ubiquitous ISAC deployment in next-generation networks.

title: PGM: Physics-Informed Generative Meta-model for Few-shot SAR Target Recognition
authors: Ruoxue Li, Shuyuan Yang, Zhixi Feng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Few-shot target recognition in synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imagery remains challenging due to limited labeled data and complex scattering characteristics. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel Physics-informed Generative Meta-model (PGM) framework that effectively integrates SAR physical principles into a unified self-supervised meta-learning paradigm. Specifically, PGM combines the backscatter relative phase image (BRPI) and the backscatter amplitude image (BAI) to cooperatively learn comprehensive target representations. First, a modality bidirectional guided encoder (MBGE) is introduced to interactively extract complementary features between BRPI and BAI. To effectively leverage unlabeled data, we develop a physics-informed generative pre-training approach utilizing a hierarchical amphi-masking strategy (HAMS). This strategy operates at both the image and feature levels and is optimized via a novel physics-informed triple loss, which incorporates BRPI into the spatial-frequency reconstruction of BAI. Following pre-training, the model undergoes meta-level discrimination adapted for few-shot scenarios. Extensive experiments on the MSTAR-SOC, MSTAR-22, and GF3-Aircraft datasets demonstrate that PGM significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both 1-shot and 5-shot settings, achieving accuracy improvements of up to 4.91% on the standard MSTAR-SOC dataset. The code is available at https://github.com/SYLee0212/PGM.

title: HiPAN: Hierarchical Posture-Adaptive Navigation for Quadruped Robots in Unstructured 3D Environments
authors: Jeil Jeong, Minsung Yoon, Seokryun Choi, Heechan Shin, Taegeun Yang, Sung-eui Yoon
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Navigating quadruped robots in unstructured 3D environments poses significant challenges, requiring goal-directed motion, effective exploration to escape from local minima, and posture adaptation to traverse narrow, height-constrained spaces. Conventional approaches employ a sequential mapping-planning pipeline but suffer from accumulated perception errors and high computational overhead, restricting their applicability on resource-constrained platforms. To address these challenges, we propose Hierarchical Posture-Adaptive Navigation (HiPAN), a framework that operates directly on onboard depth images at deployment. HiPAN adopts a hierarchical design: a high-level policy generates strategic navigation commands—planar velocity and body posture—which are executed by a low-level, posture-adaptive locomotion controller. To mitigate myopic behaviors and facilitate long-horizon navigation, we introduce Path-Guided Curriculum Learning, which progressively extends the navigation horizon from reactive obstacle avoidance to strategic navigation. In simulation, HiPAN achieves higher navigation success rates and greater path efficiency than classical reactive planners and end-to-end baselines, while real-world experiments further validate its applicability across diverse, unstructured 3D environments.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Secure Transformer–CNN Hybrid Framework for Privacy-Preserving Epileptic Seizure Prediction using EEG Signals
authors: C.Gethara Gowri, M Amanullah, Sathiya Priya.S, Devi P P
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Epileptic seizure prediction using electroencephalogram (EEG) data has become an essential research focus in improving patient safety through early intervention. However, centralized deep learning models often expose patient data to privacy risks and security breaches. This paper proposes a Blockchain-Enabled Secure Transformer–CNN Hybrid (BSTCH) framework for accurate and privacy-preserving epileptic seizure prediction. In the proposed architecture, EEG data collected from multiple healthcare institutions are locally processed and transformed into encrypted feature embeddings using deep neural networks. These embeddings are securely stored and verified through blockchain-based smart contracts, ensuring data authenticity and tamper resistance. A Transformer–CNN hybrid model is employed for spatiotemporal feature extraction and seizure classification, combining the local feature learning strength of CNNs with the temporal attention capabilities of Transformers. The blockchain layer guarantees transparent and immutable logging of model activities, thereby enhancing trust and accountability among participants. Experiments conducted on CHB-MIT and TUH EEG datasets demonstrate that BSTCH achieves 97.1% accuracy, 96.4% sensitivity, 96.7% specificity, and an AUC of 0.98, outperforming traditional CNN, LSTM, and centralized deep learning models. The results confirm that integrating blockchain-based security with advanced hybrid deep architectures provides a scalable, interpretable, and privacy-compliant solution for real-world epileptic seizure prediction systems.

title: An Orthogonal-Axis Spherical Parallel Mechanism With Large Workspace for Humanoid Hip Joints: Design, Optimization, and Experimental Validation
authors: Haotian Liu, Zhaoming Liu, Hongwei Wang, Long Cui
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Conventional serial-chain configurations for humanoid robot lower limbs exhibit issues such as concentrated driving torques, inertial coupling, and error accumulation, leading to inadequate joint stiffness, limited control precision, and reduced dynamic stability. To overcome these drawbacks, this study proposes a novel Orthogonal-axis Spherical Parallel Mechanism, inspired by the ball-and-socket structure of the human hip joint. The kinematic model of the mechanism is established. A multi-objective optimization framework is then constructed, considering workspace coverage, dexterity, and maximum joint torque, and optimal parameters are obtained through weighted decision-making. Simulations and experiments verify that the optimized mechanism fully covers the common range of motion of the human hip joint, demonstrates improved dexterity within common motion ranges, and supports a single-leg payload of 40 kg. Integrating high stiffness, a large workspace, and superior load capability, this design offers a comprehensive solution for humanoid robotic legs.

title: A Hybrid SRU-Transformer-Conv1d Architecture for High-Speed Signal Transient Prediction With Uncertainty Quantification
authors: Haining Jiang, Yinhang Zhang, Liyin Wu, Xi Yang, Yongzheng Zhan, Renmin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electromagnetic Compatibility
abstract: Time domain transient simulation is crucial in signal integrity analysis of high-speed serial link design. In this article, we propose a novel hybrid neural architecture combining simple recurrent unit (SRU), Transformer, and 1-D Convolution (Conv1d) layers for accurate and uncertainty-aware transient waveform prediction in high-speed serial links. In this hybrid neural architecture, SRU effectively models local temporal dependencies, the Transformer encoder captures global context, and the Conv1d module enhances fine-grained pattern extraction in the output stage. Compared to other time-series prediction models, this method performs better in waveform fitting accuracy and shape preservation. To estimate prediction uncertainty, Monte Carlo dropout is applied during inference, offering reliable confidence intervals of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm 2\sigma$</tex-math></inline-formula>. In addition, the experiments shows that it also has high practical value in eye diagram prediction.

title: Colorectal Polyp Detection Using SAM-HQ: A Zero-Shot Learning Approach for Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Sthuthi M. S., Sneha, T. H. Manoj, Siddhartha T. C., M. Farida Begam
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Colorectal cancer (CRC) is a leading cause of cancer-related mortality worldwide, frequently originating from precancerous polyps that remain undetected during routine colonoscopy. Manual inspection by endoscopists is tedious, subjective, and inconsistent, with adenoma detection rates ranging as low as 15% due to polyps’ morphological variability and low contrast with surrounding mucosa. This paper proposes a hybrid zero-shot semantic segmentation framework that integrates the Segment Anything Model-High Quality (SAM-HQ) for precise, boundary-aware global feature extraction with a Swin-Small transformer for domain-specific local contextual learning. A learned fusion module employing cross-attention, contrastive alignment (InfoNCE loss), and a combined Dice-Focal objective adapts foundation model outputs to colonoscopy images without requiring large-scale pixel-level annotations. The model is trained on an augmented dataset drawn from Kvasir-SEG, CVC-ClinicDB, and CVC-ColonDB benchmarks. Experimental results demonstrate a Dice coefficient of 0.9182, IoU of 0.8654, precision of 0.9290, recall of 0.9248, specificity of 0.9910, and accuracy of 98.25%, outperforming the SAM-HQ baseline and supervised methods such as TransUNet. The proposed approach advances annotation-efficient polyp detection and supports real-time clinical decision assistance in CRC screening workflows.

title: Scalable Association of Users in CF-mMIMO: A Synergy of Communication, Sensing, and ISAC
authors: Ahmed Naeem, Anastassia Gharib, El Mehdi Amhoud, Hüseyin Arslan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: Cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output (CF-mMIMO) is a key enabler for sixth-generation (6G) wireless systems, offering enhanced spectral efficiency and ubiquitous coverage. In such systems, the association of user equipments (UEs) to access points (APs) is a critical challenge, as it directly impacts scalability, interference suppression, and overall system performance. Conventional user association (UA) methods optimize communication throughput but overlook emerging 6G requirements from sensing and integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) applications. To address this, we propose a scalable user association (SUA) scheme for CF-mMIMO networks that explicitly considers heterogeneous UE service needs, including communication, sensing, and ISAC. The proposed SUA scheme integrates AP masking, link prioritization, and optimization-driven AP selection to balance system load and enhance service quality. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly reduces interference and computational runtime, while improving symbol error rate for communication UEs and probability of detection for sensing UEs.

title: A Novel Fault Diagnosis Method Based on Current Behavior for SRD System Considering Multifault Features
authors: Lefei Ge, Delong Wang, Nan Du, Qianrao Fu, Dianxun Xiao, Shoujun Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: To enhance the reliability of switched reluctance machine drive (SRD) systems, this article proposes a novel fault diagnosis method focusing on current behavior analysis to identify faults in power switches, diodes, windings, and current sensors. By employing a novel sensor placement strategy and a precise current reconstruction algorithm, this method enables real-time monitoring of each component within the SRD system without additional hardware. The proposed method features a simple structure that is independent of control methods, allowing for comprehensive diagnosis of 12 fault types across six categories within as few as one to four control cycles. Through experimental verification, the proposed method exhibits its robustness and fast response, demonstrating the applicability in various SRD environments.

title: Machine Learning Techniques for Wind Turbine Predictive Maintenance Using SCADA Data
authors: Leonardo M. S. Xavier, Caio Sóter D. B. Mota, José Filho C. Castro, Pedro Rosas
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper proposes a data-driven framework for early anomaly detection in critical wind turbine components using only Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) data, eliminating the need for additional high-cost Condition Monitoring Systems (CMS). The specific aim of this study is to develop and comparatively evaluate machine-learning models capable of accurately predicting the temperature behavior of gearbox bearings and generator drive-end bearings, enabling early identification of abnormal operating conditions. The proposed methodology includes SCADAdata preprocessing and filtering to represent normal turbine operation, feature selection based on correlation analysis, min–max normalization, and chronological data splitting to avoid information leakage. Three regression models are implemented and compared: Multiple Linear Regression (MLR), eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural networks. Hyperparameters for XGBoost are optimized using Bayesian optimization, while LSTM architecture is configured with early stopping to mitigate overfitting. Model performance is evaluated using R², RMSE, MAE, and MAPE metrics, and anomaly detection is performed through statistical process control applied to prediction residuals (±3σ control limits). Results obtained from real SCADA data of three 2.1 MW wind turbines located in northeastern Brazil demonstrate that XGBoost provides the most consistent overall performance, achieving R² values up to 0.92 and MAPE below 2.2% for generator bearing prediction, while LSTM achieved R² up to 0.99 and MAPE below 0.5% in specific gearbox cases, albeit with significantly higher computational cost. The findings indicate that SCADA-based machine-learning models, particularly XGBoost, constitute a scalable, low-CAPEX and low-OPEX alternative for wind turbine condition monitoring, contributing to reduced operational and maintenance costs and improved wind farm reliability.

title: Energy-Efficient Multi-Robot Coverage Path Planning of Non-Convex Regions of Interests
authors: Sourav Raxit, Jose Fuentes, Paulo Padrao, Abdullah Al Redwan Newaz, Md Tamjidul Hoque, Mark Kulp, Leonardo Bobadilla
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: This letter presents an energy-efficient multi-robot coverage path planning (MRCPP) framework for large, nonconvex Regions of Interest (ROI) containing obstacles and no-fly zones (NFZ). Existing minimum-energy coverage planning algorithms utilize meta-heuristic boustrophedon workspace decomposition. Therefore, even with minimum energy objectives and energy consumption constraints, they cannot achieve optimal energy efficiency. Moreover, most existing frameworks support only a single type of robotic platform. MRCPP overcomes these limitations by: generating globally-informed swath generation, creating parallel sweeping paths with minimal turns, calculating safety buffers to ensure safe turning clearance, using an efficient mTSP solver to balance workloads and minimize mission time, and connecting disjoint segments via a modified visibility graph that tracks heading angles while maintaining transitions within safe regions. The efficacy of the proposed MRCPP framework is demonstrated through real-world experiments involving autonomous aerial vehicles (AAVs) and autonomous surface vehicles (ASVs). Evaluations demonstrate that the proposed MRCPP consistently outperforms state-of-the-art planners, reducing average total energy consumption by 3% to 40% for a team of 3 robots and computation time by an order of magnitude, while maintaining balanced workload distribution and strong scalability across increasing fleet sizes. The MRCPP framework is released as an open-source package and videos of real-world and simulated experiments are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://mrc-pp.github.io/</uri>.

title: TransACO: Transformer-Guided Ant Colony Algorithm for Combinatorial Optimization
authors: Bo Sun, Peixi Peng, Guang Tan, Yanjie Song, Yonghong Tian, Witold Pedrycz
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) is a heuristic algorithm that has been widely used to solve combinatorial optimization problems. However, determining heuristic measures and initial pheromones levels in an ACO is problem-specific and often requires expert intervention, posing a significant challenge for the users. We investigate how the Transformer, a powerful learning model based on a self-attention mechanism, can be used to enable the automatic design of heuristic measures and pheromones. We present Transformer-Guided ACO (TransACO), a framework that combines neural methods with heuristic algorithms. The Transformer is designed as a learner that encodes problem instances, captures individual interactions, and generates heuristic measures and pheromone matrix. Additionally, a candidate point perturbation local search technique is proposed to enhance exploration. We conduct extensive experiments on four representative combinatorial optimization problems—traveling salesman problem, capacitated vehicle routing problem, orienteering problem, and multiple knapsack problem—across diverse instance sizes. Notably, in cross-scale generalization experiments, a model trained solely on small instances successfully transfers to 10k-node problems: on TSP-10 k, TransACO achieves a 4.12% improvement, and on CVRP-10 k, it yields a 1.16% gain over strong baselines. These findings demonstrate the robustness, adaptability, and scalability of TransACO.

title: TinyVBS: Design and Implementation of a Palm-Sized Pneumatic Variable Buoyancy System for 100-Meter-Depth Operation in Compact Marine Robot
authors: Hexiong Zhou, Zheng Zeng, Lian Lian
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: This paper presents the TinyVBS, a compact pneumatic variable buoyancy system designed for underwater robots operating at depths of up to 100 meters. The palm-sized device, weighing less than 500 grams, features a structurally simplified architecture, facilitating low-cost fabrication and effective compensation for depth-dependent elastic bladder deformation under hydrostatic pressure. By exploiting the inherent connection between bladder expansion volume and wall stress, the device obviates the need for flow or volumetric sensors. A novel pressure-adaptive control strategy dynamically modifies the behavior of the pneumatic components across variable environmental pressures. Laboratory hydrostatic environmental emulations exhibited steady closed-loop performance throughout five consecutive buoyancy adjustment cycles, achieving a total vertical movement of 700 m with a depth resolution of 0.5 m. Sea trials confirmed operational robustness to a maximum depth of 112 m, preserving functionality despite environmental disturbances including currents and thermal gradients. This advancement enables economical miniaturization of buoyancy control systems for coastal robotic applications with strict size and weight constraints.

title: Application of Dual-mode Density Peak Clustering Algorithm in PD Detection Devices
authors: Tao Han, Dabiao Nie, Yu Gao, Jin He, Chunlin Hao, Boxue Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: The essence of pulse separation is decoupling multi-source pulses. The performance of pulse separation directly constrains the accuracy and engineering application value of partial discharge (PD) detection and diagnosis. Recent algorithms suffer from insufficient robustness when dealing with pulse clusters in overlapping separation regions. Moreover, when deployed at the edge, the algorithm struggles to balance operational efficiency and computational resource consumption. To address these issues, we improve and optimize a dual-mode density peak clustering algorithm for edge deployment. First, the algorithm can automatically select between the Manhattan distance mode and the cross-correlation distance mode according to the overlapping degree of pulse clusters, thereby achieving optimal separation. Meanwhile, an edge deployment optimization scheme is designed to address the efficient embedding of the algorithm in PD detection devices. Experimental results indicate that the cross-correlation distance mode excels at separating overlapping clusters, while the Manhattan distance mode suits non-overlapping ones. In complex three-source scenarios, the optimized algorithm achieves an accuracy of 98.7% with an overall separation duration of 11.837 s. Its precision and computational efficiency are significantly higher than those of classical methods. The algorithm provides an effective solution for detecting complex insulation defects.

title: In Silico Study of Hemagglutinin Gene of AIVs H5N1 Subtype Indonesian Isolates: Sequence-Based Phylogeny Analysis and Receptor Binding Prediction
authors: Ryan Septa Kurnia, Christian Marco Nugroho, Otto Martua Silaen, Muhammad Ade Putra
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 12th International Conference on Biomedical and Bioinformatics Engineering
abstract: None

title: Optical Characteristics and Systemization of Personal Glasses-Free 3D Display Using Visual Perception and Retroreflective Sheet
authors: Kaoruko Yuge, Hideaki Takada
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: We present a personal glasses-free 3D display system utilizing retroreflection and linear blending. This study aims to develop a simpler, more compact configuration suitable for desktop use. We evaluated the optical characteristics of a retroreflective sheet and narrow-angle diffusers to determine design conditions. Luminance distribution and image overlap between adjacent projectors were quantitatively analyzed. An ultra-short-focus projector improved luminance under desktop settings. Projector height adjustment ensured sufficient overlap of luminance distributions, improving motion parallax continuity. System synchronization was implemented in Unity, enabling consistent operation between host and client devices. This work demonstrates a practical personal, glasses-free 3D display system providing a technical foundation for its application.

title: Optimization of Electric Bus Fleet Size in Urban Public Transport: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Abhishek Chauhan, Amardeep Singh, Paurav Goel
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The shift to electric bus (EB) systems is crucial for sustainable urban transport. Yet, optimizing EB fleet size and operations is a tough challenge. It’s shaped by economic, technical, and environmental factors. This review looks at recent studies (2017–2025) on optimizing electric bus fleets. It highlights methods, models, and trends. Evidence shows optimized scheduling can cut fleet size by up to 38.2%. Planning charging infrastructure well can lower costs by 13.38%. However, poor planning can lead to trip losses and higher costs. This highlights the need for better optimization frameworks. The study sorts approaches into deterministic, stochastic, and robust methods. It includes graph-theoretic, mixed-integer, and multi-objective models. New methods use big data and machine learning. They tackle real-world uncertainty and help make better decisions. Despite these advancements, gaps still exist. Key issues include limited modeling of battery wear, lack of focus on demand uncertainty, and equity in infrastructure deployment. This review highlights recent developments, notes key limitations, and suggests future research. It seeks to improve optimization strategies. These strategies balance cost, reliability, and sustainability in urban electric bus operations.

title: Graph-Aware Policy Learning Via Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks in Continuous Control
authors: Aurélien Moine, Naeem Ul Islam, Dharnish Raja, Syed Humayoon Shah, Muhammad Khalid
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: Learning control policies in high-dimensional continuous environments with complex physical structure is a challenging problem, as standard reinforcement learning approaches often rely on unstructured function approximators that fail to capture relational dependencies. Although recent approaches incorporate expressive models such as Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) or graph-based representations, they often lack a unified framework that jointly models structural connectivity and an interpretable function approximation. In this work, we propose Graph Convolutional Knowledge-Aware Networks based Proximal Policy Optimization(GCNKAN-PPO), which integrate Graph Convolutional Neural Networks (GCNs) with KANs within the Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) framework. The GCN component explicitly model the physical connectivity, while- KAN layers enhance expressiveness and interpretability. Structured observation encoding and task-specific rewards further align learning with environment dynamics. Experimental evaluation on MuJoCo Ant-v5 shows that GCNKAN significantly outperforms the baseline PPO variants, particularly in Ant-v5, where graph-structured reasoning is essential. The results highlight the importance of model-environment alignment and show that combining graph-based inductive biases with KAN-based function approximation improves learning efficiency and performance in continuous control tasks.

title: eStream 2026 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 Open Conference of Electrical, Electronic and Information Sciences (eStream)

title: Multi-Factor Predictive Modelling of Cylindrical Shell Buckling Under Compressive Load via an Integrated Finite Element Analysis and Artificial Neural Network Framework
authors: Mohd Nor Aiman Ab Patar, Mohd Shahrom Ismail, Ilyani Akmar Abu Bakar, Jamaluddin Mahmud
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: Predicting the axial buckling capacity of thin-walled cylindrical shells is challenging due to their sensitivity to geometric imperfections and nonlinear elastoplastic behaviour. While finite element analysis (FEA) offers accuracy, it is computationally expensive for extensive parametric studies. To address this, this study introduces a novel integrated FEA–Artificial Neural Network (ANN) framework. A six-factor Box–Behnken Design generated 54 nonlinear FE simulations varying radius, thickness, length, and material properties to train a feedforward ANN using the Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm. The model demonstrated excellent accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(R=0.9844)$</tex>, with prediction errors largely below 5 %. Sensitivity analysis identified wall thickness <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(44.9 \%)$</tex>, radius <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(35.1 \%)$</tex>, and yield strength (12.8 %) as dominant parameters, while length and elastic properties contributed minimally. Ultimately, the FEA–ANN framework delivers a rapid, accurate, and physically interpretable surrogate model that mitigates the computational burden of traditional FEA, offering a powerful tool for efficient design optimization and reliability evaluation of shell structures.

title: Predictive Local Multiple Attention For Real-time Risk Assessment in Autonomous Driving
authors: Jinlai Zhang, Xinwang Tao, Lecu Wang, Kai Gao, Zhizhen Liu, Lin Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: In autonomous driving, accurate and real-time assessment of driving risks is critical to ensuring road safety and promoting passenger trust. This paper proposes an innovative attention mechanism framework, Predictive Local Multiple Attention (PLMA), to improve risk assessment capabilities in autonomous driving systems. PLMA framework introduces Local Refinement Attention Module (LRAM) and Multidimensional Focused Attention Fusion Module (MFAFM) to achieve efficient feature capture, significantly reducing processing overhead and improving the accuracy of risk assessment. Both LRAM and MFAFM are designed to address specific challenges in real-time risk assessment for autonomous driving systems. We experimentally validate our approach with a high-fidelity psychophysical driving dataset and demonstrate that it goes beyond existing methods in terms of risk prediction accuracy, computational efficiency and real-time performance. The PLMA model achieved a significant improvement in risk prediction accuracy with its 0.97 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> score and demonstrated strong adaptability across diverse individualized operator profiles. This research not only promotes the development of autonomous driving technology but also provides a robust foundational proof-of-concept and practical guidance for the construction of intelligent transportation systems in the future.

title: FAST-Calib: LiDAR-Camera Extrinsic Calibration in One Second
authors: Chunran Zheng, Fu Zhang
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Practice
abstract: This paper proposes FAST-Calib, a fast and user-friendly LiDAR-camera extrinsic calibration tool based on a custom-made 3D target. FAST-Calib supports both mechanical and solid-state LiDARs by leveraging an efficient and reliable edge extraction algorithm that is agnostic to LiDAR scan patterns. It also compensates for edge dilation artifacts caused by LiDAR spot spread through ellipse fitting, and supports joint optimization across multiple scenes. We validate FAST-Calib on three LiDAR models (Ouster, Avia, and Mid360), each paired with a wide-angle camera. Experimental results demonstrate superior accuracy and robustness compared to existing methods. With point-to-point registration errors consistently below 6.5 mm and total processing time under 0.7 s, FAST-Calib provides an efficient, accurate, and target-based automatic calibration pipeline. We have open-sourced our code and dataset on GitHub to benefit the robotics community.

title: Qualification of the ITER In-Vessel Magnetic Diagnostics and Instrumentation: Mirnov Coils and Integrators
authors: P. Moreau, F. P. Pellissier, M. de Combarieu, T. Alarcon, S. Meriaux, Y. Ma, G. Golluccio, L. Capellà, A. Neto
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: Magnetic diagnostics play a key role in any tokamak operation. Accurate measurements are mandatory to ensure precise magnetic reconstruction of the plasma and, as a result, to perform plasma control and wall protection. On the International Thermonuclear Experimental Reactor (ITER) tokamak, 450 Mirnov coils (inductive magnetic sensors) will be installed on the inner skin of the vacuum vessel. They constitute the main magnetic systems to recover the plasma magnetic parameters. The full set of sensors was calibrated with a dedicated testbed in the frequency range of 20 Hz up to 2 MHz by measuring their response in terms of amplitude and phase shift. In addition, the integrator associated with any inductive magnetic sensor is a crucial element in the magnetic signal measurement chain because, being prone to drift, it may compromise the accuracy of the plasma reconstruction accuracy. One prototype of the ITER integrator was qualified on WEST during plasma experiments (800 pulses with a duration up to 6’04”) and compared to WEST measurements to identify any drift.

title: Automated Data Quality Scoring using Statistical Drift Detection and Rule-based Semantic Constraints
authors: T.R. Nisha Dayana, Jeyashree R, Arivazhagan.P, S.Prathiba, Keerthana K
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: High-quality data is impractical when it comes to analytics, machine learning and operational decisions. However, traditional data-quality frameworks have up to now been constrained in the ingenuity of representing changes in data distributions through time and inconsistencies of term interpretations. Existing solutions usually look at structural profiling or univariate checks which create holes in finding evolving drift or rule violations which can lead to compromising downstream applications. This study is aimed to develop a hybrid framework to combine the statistical drift detection to the rule-based semantic constraint to provide the data quality scoring that is interpretable and adaptive. The methodology proposed a multi-step approach, including baseline profiling, statistical drift monitoring, rule based semantic, integrity and composite-score with adaptive-feedback. Experiments were performed on a semi-synthetic healthcare claims data set with injected covariate, label and concept drift scenarios. The proposed StatDrift + RuleFusion method achieved better performance with an AUROC of 0.94, AUPR of 0.88, F1 score of 0.86, Brier score of 0.045, FNR of 0.07, and FDR of 0.05 and outperformed five state-of-the-art methods. The developed framework is able to bring together the results of statistical and semantic evaluation in order to effectively detect, quantify and communicate quality issues in the data in real time, which provides a scalable and interpretable solution for modern data governance.

title: Sketch-Based Rust Texture Generation Using Diffusion Model
authors: Yongming Zhang, Tianyu Zhang, Kazunori Miyata, Haoran Xie
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: Generating realistic and controllable rust textures remains a challenging problem in computer graphics, vital for applications such as model texturing and digital image editing. This paper proposes a two-stage, sketch-based framework for synthesizing photorealistic rust textures. In the first stage, a conditional adversarial network converts user sketches into multi-level corrosion control maps representing hierarchical rust intensities. In the second stage, control maps guide a conditional diffusion model to produce high-fidelity textures that reflect user intent. To enable training without manual annotation, we introduce an unsupervised segmentation algorithm that extracts multi-level control maps from real rust images using color saturation and local texture features. Quantitative and qualitative evaluations show that our framework surpasses existing methods in realism, structural fidelity, and controllability. The generated textures effectively capture the complex and diverse characteristics of real rust surfaces.

title: An Explainable and Lightweight CKD Prediction Framework using Crow Search-based Feature Selection and LightGBM
authors: Bellamgubba Anoch, Karuna Kumar Valicharla, M Jaganmohan Reddy, B. Nikhila, Ediga Amarnath Goud, Sai Prasad Baswoju
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) is a major global health problem that requires early detection to prevent disease progression. Machine learning techniques have recently been applied for automated CKD diagnosis; however, many models suffer from feature redundancy and limited interpretability. This study proposes an efficient and interpretable CKD prediction framework that integrates the Crow Search Algorithm (CSA) for feature selection, Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) for classification, and SHAP for model explainability. The CSA method reduces the original 24 clinical features to 14 important attributes, improving computational efficiency while maintaining high predictive performance. Experiments were conducted using the UCI CKD dataset, consisting of 400 patient records. The proposed CSA–LightGBM model achieved 100% accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. SHAP analysis further explains the contribution of important clinical variables such as hemoglobin, albumin, and serum creatinine. The results demonstrate that the proposed framework provides accurate, interpretable, and computationally efficient predictions, making it suitable for clinical decision support and mobile healthcare applications.

title: A Novel FOPI-Controlled Energy Management Framework for DC Microgrids with Integrated EV Fast Charging Infrastructure
authors: Kartik Mallick, D.Krishna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Over the next ten years, it is expected that more electric cars will cause fast progress in how EVs are made, which will lower how much batteries cost. Because it takes a long time to charge batteries, which makes people not want to buy EVs, fast charging places are a much better and faster way to charge them. This writing explains a method for handling a small DC power network, created to discover the impact of quick charging spots for electric cars and local power sources, along with the degree to which they alter the network’s operation, notably when employing technology that allows cars to send power back to the grid. The primary aim of this study is to utilize the EV battery to provide DC power for a distribution static compensator, with the expectation of reducing the impact of voltage fluctuations. The results of simulations done with MATLAB Simulink show possible improvements, both for the person using it, like not needing as many places to charge, and for how well the distribution system works, like making the voltage better.

title: A Spatio-Temporal Hybrid Intelligence System for Unauthorized Land Use Detection and Flood Impact Quantification from Satellite Data
authors: Haarish Ragavendra R, Guruprakash R, Logeshwari S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The paper describes a hybrid deep learning based system to detect illegal land occupation and estimate the extent of flood damages through the use of satellite image. The model is a composite of VGG16 and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks used to extract spatial and analyze temporal changes over multi-temporal satellite data respectively. VGG16 model learns the spatial patterns at detail level whereas LSTM learns the time-sequence variations in order to detect unauthorized encroachment of the land and flood prone areas. A fuzzy logic module is used to determine changing degrees of a flood damage using derived features to overcome challenges encountered in satellite observations. The system handles both pre- and post-event imagery and creates automated notifications to authorities to assist in the fast process of making decisions. Data handling and preprocessing are done using geospatial libraries GDAL, OpenCV. The results of the experimental studies show that the land-use change is high precision of land-use change detection and dependability in flood damage assessment, which implies the effectiveness of the framework in real-time tracking and sustainable land management.

title: Smart Hospital Connectivity and Integrated Service Management System
authors: P.Phani Sai Raghavendra, B.Nimith, R.Suguna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The digital transformation of hospital operations has become a need that arises from the growing need for effective healthcare provision. Most conventional hospital systems rely on stand-alone processes and paper-based records, leading to data duplication, inefficiency and interoperability problems between departments. Limitations of this kind can be overcome by a proposed integrated web-based multi-hospital management system that aims to simplify interactions among patients, physicians, laboratory staff, pharmacists, and hospital administrators. Accordingly, this paper presents an integrated web-based multi-hospital management system, targeting the simplification of interaction among all the above-mentioned stakeholders of a hospital. Therefore, the Smart Hospital Connectivity and Integrated Service Management System facilitates the tracking and management of patient records from multiple hospitals on a centralized online platform. Consequently, the system integrates features such as online scheduling, making payments for laboratory tests, and live doctor–patient communication.The Smart Hospital Connectivity and Integrated Service Management System provides for tracking, sharing, and managing patient’ information in multiple hospitals in a single centralized online platform. The key features of the system include online scheduling, paying for laboratory tests, and live doctor–patient communication. Experimental testing has proven the effectiveness of the platform in handling multiple users simultaneously, making secure online payments, and exchanging trusted messages between patients and physicians. The proposed Smart Hospital Connectivity and Integrated Service Management System contributes to a modern health infrastructure by promoting digitalization and evidence-based decision-making in the Management of Hospitals.

title: Multimodal Conversational Emotion Recognition in Education with Uncertain Missing Modalities: A Graph Fusion Network Approach
authors: Juan Yang, Ruinan Shi, Xu Du, Yiqian Xie, Weiwei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Learning Technologies
abstract: Multimodal Emotion Recognition in Conversations (MERC) under uncertain missing modalities remains a critical challenge, especially in educational contexts. Significant research efforts have been dedicated to developing novel solutions to tackle this problem. Nevertheless, existing methods are difficult to fully utilize available information for high-quality data reconstruction, and fail to learn discriminative multimodal representations for handling uncertain missing modalities. Furthermore, the generalization of such methods in educational contexts remains largely overlooked. To address these challenges, this study proposes a novel graph fusion network approach for achieving MERC tasks in education with uncertain missing modalities. Our method first reconstructs missing modalities by using adaptive fusion weights to aggregate information from the most similar neighbors in the available modalities. The reconstructed data is processed via a mask-aware graph structure to explicitly exploit masked cues to enhance the robustness of intra-modal representations. Initial multimodal fusion representations derived from single-modal features are employed to build a dual-graph structure for further refining the multimodal representation learning of each utterance. During the training process, our method also introduces the boundary-aware contrastive learning to enable the accurate identification of samples near decision boundaries. Experiments on three multimodal datasets, including one self-collected educational dataset, CMU-MOSI, and CMU-MOSEI, demonstrates its superiority, effectiveness, and generalizability.

title: LearnSign: AI-Powered Real-Time Indian Sign Language Learning Platform for Children Aged 1–15 Using MediaPipe and LSTM
authors: Amith K M, Prajwal K, Adarsh K R, Bindu B N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Indian Sign Language (ISL) is the primary communication mode for the Deaf and Hard-of-Hearing community in India. A major challenge is the lack of affordable, interactive, and child-friendly learning platforms that support early communication development. This paper presents LearnSign, an AI-powered hybrid learning system designed specifically for children aged 1–15. The platform uses MediaPipe for real-time hand landmark detection and an LSTM-based classifier to recognize six ISL signs (A, B, C, 1, 2, 3) with 94% accuracy and less than 100 ms latency. LearnSign integrates interactive learning modules, an age-segmented curriculum, a real-time sign translator, and gamified progress dashboards. A user study with 10 learners demonstrated a 26% improvement in gesture accuracy within one session. The system has received independent external validation through national level competitive evaluations. Results show that LearnSign can significantly assist early learners in acquiring foundational sign language skills and has strong potential for large-scale deployment in accessible education.

title: Fast Rendering of Spotlight Beams with Complex Luminous Intensity Distributions
authors: Sogo Inagaki, Yoshinori Dobashi
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: Realistic lighting effects, such as spotlight beams, are widely used in games and visual production. However, rendering them is costly because scattered light must be integrated along each viewing ray. We propose a fast method that precomputes the accumulated intensity as a 2D texture, enabling efficient and visually plausible rendering.

title: Incipient Inter-Turn Short-Circuit Fault Diagnosis Based on Spatiotemporal Multibranch Graph Convolutional Network
authors: Xusong Bai, Qian Chen, Xiangjin Song, Guohai Liu, Zhengmeng Liu, Weihang Hong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Permanent magnet synchronous motors are widely used in various industrial drive systems. Accurately diagnosing inter-turn short-circuit (ITSC) faults in windings is a crucial prerequisite for ensuring the safe and stable operation of the drive system. Hence, this article proposes a spatiotemporal multibranch graph convolutional network (ST-MGCN) for the diagnosis of incipient ITSC faults. To address the challenge of weak fault features under complex conditions, ST-MGCN employs a three-branch architecture to simultaneously extract spatial, temporal, and global features from multichannel current data, thereby enriching the representation of fault features. A novel causality-driven fusion module adaptively reweights branch contributions and feature significances, preserving subtle incipient fault representations. Experimental validations on two distinct datasets exhibit the superior diagnostic accuracy and noise robustness of the proposed ST-MGCN. It demonstrates exceptional sensitivity to the weak features of the incipient ITSC fault. It maintains high performance under complex conditions, with varying speeds and load levels, outperforming the state-of-the-art methods.

title: CrossTongueGuard: Detecting Cyberbullying in Code-Mixed Conversations
authors: Kadaganchi Sudhakar, Nakka Upender Goud, Bhogolu Denesh Kumar, Karamkonda Srinadh, Pathlavath Bhaskar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Cyberbullying has emerged as a recent concern in the global online communication platform, causing emotional distress and psychological trauma to individuals. The detection of cyberbullying is particularly challenging in the multilingual code-mixed online social media communication platform, where individuals communicate in multiple languages in a single message and use informal language, transliteration, and regional slang. These language complexities reduce the efficiency of traditional text classification techniques. To address this challenge, this paper proposes Cross Tongue Guard, a systematic framework for the detection of cyberbullying in multilingual code-mixed communication platforms. The framework employs systematic preprocessing techniques, semantic context-based annotation, and multi-aspect analysis based on intent, harm, and aggression. The framework integrates traditional machine learning models and multilingual transformer models to efficiently extract both statistical and contextual features. The primary aim of this research is to develop a scalable and language-agnostic cyberbullying detection framework that can handle real-world multilingual social media data efficiently and establish a safe online communication environment.

title: Breast Cancer Biomarker Discovery Using an Enhanced Quantum-Based Avian Navigation Optimizer and Ensemble Learning Model
authors: Morteza Rakhshaninejad, Mohammad Fathian, Navid Yazdanjue, Amir H. Gandomi, Farnaz Barzinpour
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: Breast cancer remains a global health challenge, and early detection is crucial for improving survival rates. However, traditional biomarker detection methods in machine learning face challenges such as high false positives, large gene datasets, and limited sample sizes. These challenges are exacerbated by the limitations of traditional differential evolution techniques, which struggle with scalability and effectiveness in high-dimensional, complex problems. This study introduces Ensemble-Based Logical Binary QANA (LBQANA_En), an improved differential evolution variant tailored for large-scale global optimization in gene expression analysis. By integrating six gene expression datasets, LBQANA_En overcomes the constraints of small sample sizes and the complexities inherent in gene expression data. Inspired by the quantum navigation of migratory birds and leveraging logical operators like XOR and OR, LBQANA_En outperforms other binary QANA variants in biomarker detection. The algorithm successfully identifies key biomarkers, including LPL, LEP, CD36, CDC20, TOP2A, and EZH2, significantly improving breast cancer detection accuracy and reducing false positives, achieving an impressive F1 score of 98.958%. These biomarkers provide critical insights into important pathways, such as AMPK and PPAR signaling, setting a new benchmark in computational biology and bioinformatics research, and paving the way for advancements in diagnostic techniques and medical science.

title: MedRoboLegalNet: An AI Framework for Evaluating Medical Negligence and Legal Compliance in Robotic Surgeries
authors: S.Thangamayan, Laura Rose Selvakumar, Prabhakharan. R, Nethra Shree G, Yuvaraaj Madhan, R.Sudharshan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The increased application of robotic devices in surgical procedures not only results in an elevated level of accuracy, but also raises efficiency. The growing applicability of robotic surgical systems has created medical malpractice issues and the agencies are faced with the responsibility of ensuring legal compliance requirements. We present the MedRoboLegalNet that is a system of artificial intelligence integrated into the evaluation system and can examine robotic surgical practice and legal compliance within the healthcare complex. The hybrid model combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to analyze surgical images with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks that analyze time-series surgical data along with transformer-based Natural Language Processing (NLP) models to analyze legal documents. The testing data of the framework was real surgical logs and patient outcomes as well as the actual medical malpractice legal case files. MedRoboLegalNet was found to have accuracy of 97.22% when identifying surgical errors along with law breaches. Legal compliance was detected with a 96.85% accuracy and yielded a higher precision and recall rate of above 94% in a variety of legal cases. The developers as a constituent of the framework incorporated a negligence scoring system, which quantifies the risk levels in surgical practices. The analysis depicts a high degree of solidity of the framework that provides practical data to the healthcare administrators and law experts to improve patient safety and accountability in the legal context. The study has proven that MedRoboLegalNet is a new solution operating at the interface between medical technology and the law.

title: Agent Mode: A Minimalist, Machine-Readable Web Protocol for Enhancing AI Agent Efficiency and Search Engine Optimization
authors: Ashirvad Singh, Arnod Mukhopadhyay, Madan Lal Saini, Amarnath Kumar, Sourav Kumar Verma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: In today’s evolving world, AI agents have stepped into the world of the web, built for human eyes, made up of flashy dynamic content, complex Document Object Models (DOMs), lucrative ads, and security mechanisms like captchas which make agents slow, clumsy and error prone. This paper introduces "Agent Mode", a novel web protocol to solve this mismatch between human-centric web design and machine efficiency. "Agent Mode" activates a minimalist, lightweight machine readable interface via simple HTTP header or meta tags. Drawing parallels to "dark mode", Agent Mode is a simple addon to existing websites delivering indexing, stripped-down HTML with embedded JSON-LD structures, semantic search APIs, and direct action endpoints. Agent Mode results in sub-100ms load times and reduces error rates by up to 85%. We have discussed protocol’s specifications, implementation guidelines and ethical considerations. In the future, when AI agents will surf, "Agent-Ready" badge will guide AI agents for best suitable websites and prioritize them over other complex webs acting as a search engine optimization for efficient error-free task completion.

title: Integrated ECG IoT-Based Health Monitoring Using Machine Learning
authors: Gurusigaamani Ayyanar Muthulingam, Kardeepa Ponnuchamy, Bojjireddy Prathyusha, Nanubala Sanjeeva Sai Kumar, Jonnalagadda Kavayasasanka, Anand A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: An electrocardiogram (ECG) is widely used in medical practice to capture the electrical activity of the heart. While it serves as a reliable tool for early detection of cardiac abnormalities, conventional hospital-based machines are expensive, bulky, and not designed for continuous or home-based monitoring. In this work, we describe a simple monitoring setup built with Internet of Things (IoT) hardware, mainly Arduino and Raspberry Pi, which is able to capture ECG signals and process them directly. The system applies a band-pass filter to clean the data and then uses R-peak detection to calculate the heart rate, R-R intervals, and heart rate variability (HRV). These values were further examined using basic machine learning models to check for irregular patterns, such as tachycardia, bradycardia, or unstable rhythm. The framework also provides an estimate of anxiety levels and hypertension risk. Experiments on real-time patient data showed that the system produced reliable results, with more than 95 accuracy in heart rate estimation and in the detection of irregular conditions. Overall, this design shows that a small and low-cost IoT device, when combined with simple machine learning methods, can provide practical support for continuous monitoring and can also help in telemedicine. This work also relates to the United Nations Sustainable Development Goal (SDG 3), which focuses on good health and well-being.

title: Effective Visual Feedback in Natural Virtual Grasping for Complex Tasks
authors: Kazuki Narita, Mie Sato
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: Hand tracking enables grasping operations in virtual space without controllers, and various visual feedback methods such as color changes at fingertips or contact points have been proposed. However, their effectiveness in complex, everyday-like tasks and for objects of diverse shapes remains insufficiently verified. To address the unnatural grasping motions often seen in previous studies, this study proposed a natural grasping algorithm that reproduced more realistic finger–object interactions. Six types of visual feedback were implemented, and their effectiveness was evaluated through quantitative and qualitative analyses.

title: Generative Artificial Intelligence for Synthetic Data Creation and Advanced Data Augmentation in Analytical Software Pipelines
authors: Govinda RaviRaj Mulasa, Santhosh Deep Moturi, Sailaja Songa
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Reliable analytical software pipelines often suffer from limited, sparse, and imbalanced tabular datasets, which can negatively impact predictive performance and generalization. To address this challenge, this study proposes an end-to-end analytical framework that integrates Conditional Tabular Generative Adversarial Network (CTGAN)-based synthetic data generation with controlled augmentation strategies to enhance data diversity and learning robustness. The generated synthetic samples are combined with real data using predefined augmentation ratios, followed by evaluation using Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and LightGBM classifiers. Experimental results on the Criteo Attribution Modeling dataset demonstrate that the proposed pipeline significantly improves predictive performance, achieving optimal results with LightGBM, including an accuracy of 0.93, recall of 0.61, precision of 0.48, F1-score of 0.54, and ROC-AUC of 0.91. The findings further confirm that moderate synthetic enrichment effectively balances data diversity and redundancy, leading to improved detection of minority class instances. The proposed framework is scalable, dataset-agnostic, and suitable for real-world applications across domains such as healthcare, finance, and digital marketing. Overall, this study demonstrates that combining generative artificial intelligence with controlled data augmentation enhances the reliability and effectiveness of analytical software pipelines.

title: Electric Vehicle Remaining Driving Range Prediction via Gated Recurrent Units and Kolmogorov-Arnold Network Model
authors: Lianpo Li, Lin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: When driving electric vehicles, the remaining driving range shows different decreasing trends as the battery status and driving condition change. Accurately predicting the remaining driving range of the vehicle can alleviate the driver’s range anxiety and improve the driving experience. This study designs a hybrid model combining Gated Recurrent Units (GRU) and Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) to predict the remaining driving range. The model obtains 30 sets of sampling data as input from the recent driving process during which the battery state of charge decreases by 1%. Each set includes two types of data: battery status and driving environment. The GRU network is utilized to extract relevant temporal information, followed by the KAN network performing regression prediction to output the remaining driving range online. Comparing the prediction results of using GRU, Long Short-Term Memory Network (LSTM), LightGBM, XGBoost and KAN alone under the same conditions, the designed GRU-KAN model has the lowest mean absolute error. The results of this research can be applied to the battery management system to improve the intelligent management of electric vehicles.

title: Preparing the Stage for Ambient Internet of Things: A Study of Positioning Protocols in 5G Networks
authors: Nico Kalis, Muhammad Shahrukh, Benjamin Rother, Sven Pawletta, Christian Haubelt, Frank Golatowski
publish: 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Factory Communication Systems (WFCS)
abstract: The number of Ambient Internet of Things (A-IoT) devices, which are ultra-low power and often battery-free, is steadily increasing. For these devices, the positioning in fifth generation (5G) networks is challenging. Therefore, this paper presents the planning of an initial study that addresses the overhead related to the reliable transport of position-related data using the LTE Positioning Protocol (LPP) and the Sidelink Positioning Protocol (SLPP), respectively. More specifically, the memory usage and time behavior of Abstract Syntax Notation One (ASN.1) encoding and decoding of corresponding LPP and SLPP messages should be investigated by comparing different ASN.1 codec solutions on user equipment (UE) side in current 5G networks. Based on the outcomes of this study, several approaches should be discussed to answer the question, whether the LPP and SLPP protocols are suitable for the newly introduced Ambient Internet of Things devices to handle position-related data.

title: GLRT-Based Deep Metric Learning for Robust Remote Sensing Object Retrieval
authors: Linping Zhang, Xueqian Wang, Zhizhuo Jiang, Yu Liu, Yong-Qiang Mao, Xian Sun, Gang Li, You He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Remote sensing object retrieval (RSOR) aims to identify images of the same object from large remote sensing image databases. However, existing RSOR methods often rely on simple and distribution-agnostic distance metrics, for example, Euclidean distance, that treat all the feature dimensions equally, making them susceptible to the influence of spurious features and vulnerable to variations such as noise, resolution degradation, and rotation variations. Inspired by the Neyman–Pearson theorem, we propose a generalized likelihood ratio test-based deep metric learning (GLRT-DML) approach for RSOR. GLRT-DML is a distribution-aware metric learning framework that leverages distribution information from feature embeddings across the dataset to suppress the influence of spurious features while emphasizing informative ones. This process facilitates the construction of a robust metric space for RSOR. Extensive experiments on ship, aircraft, and vehicle retrieval tasks demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed GLRT-DML approach compared with state-of-the-art RSOR methods.

title: Optimizing Mobile Food Ordering Experience via UX-Oriented Sequential Interaction Design and Usability Evaluation
authors: Pisit Chansuek
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Mobile food ordering services have rapidly expanded, yet many applications still struggle to deliver seamless and intuitive user experiences that support efficient decision-making. This study presents a UX-oriented approach to optimizing mobile food ordering through a structured sequential interaction design that reduces cognitive load and enhances usability. The proposed system, CrispyClick, was developed following user-centered design principles, emphasizing clear navigation flow, simplified ordering steps, and visually coherent interfaces. A total of 147 participants engaged with the application in real-world ordering scenarios, and their experiences were evaluated using a five-point Likert-scale questionnaire covering usability, process clarity, interface aesthetics, and overall satisfaction. The results indicate a high level of user satisfaction (mean <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=4.41, \text{SD}=0.52$</tex>), with ease of use emerging as the most influential factor, followed by the clarity of interaction steps and interface design. These findings suggest that structuring user interaction in a sequential and cognitively efficient manner can significantly improve the overall food ordering experience. The study contributes practical design insights and a scalable UX framework that can be applied to mobile commerce systems, particularly in supporting small and community-based food businesses.

title: A Hybrid Multi-Modal Framework for Fake Social Media Account Detection using BERT, ResNet, and Social Network Analysis
authors: Santosh R S, Suhas M S, Viresh S Alagawadi, Chetana Srinivas
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: The proliferation of sophisticated fake accounts on social media platforms poses significant threats to cybersecurity, information integrity, and digital trust. Traditional detection methods relying on single-modal analysis struggle against increasingly deceptive account creation strategies. This paper introduces a novel hybrid multi-modal framework that synergistically combines BERT-based natural language processing for textual analysis, ResNet convolutional neural networks for visual feature extraction, and ensemble learning through XGBoost for robust classification.
