Our approach processes three critical data dimensions semantic patterns in user generated text (usernames and biographies), visual characteristics of profile images, and behavioral metadata including follower-to-following ratios. The framework was validated on a balanced dataset of 5,130 Instagram profiles (2,565 authentic and 2,565 fake accounts), demonstrating exceptional performance with XGBoost achieving 99.02 % accuracy and SVM reaching 99.41% accuracy Comprehensive evaluation using precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC metrics confirms superior performance compared to traditional single-modal approaches. The key innovation lies in the fusion of complementary feature spaces 768-dimensional BERT embeddings, 256-dimensional ResNet features, and numerical behavioral indicators creating a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1, 0 2 5}$</tex>-dimensional representation that captures the multi-faceted nature of account authenticity. This robust framework addresses the limitations of existing approaches and provides a scalable solution adaptable across major social media platforms including Twitter, Facebook, and Instagram.

title: A Comprehensive Benchmark and Comparative Analysis of Pre-processing Methods for Deep Learning-Based Skin Lesion Detection
authors: Sakib Imtiaz, Emrana Kabir Hashi, Md. Ashikuzzaman Ashik, Zahara Tasnim
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Deep learning models have achieved remarkable success in automated skin lesion detection. However, the impact of diverse image pre-processing techniques on classification performance remains insufficiently explored. Our study presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of several pre-processing strategies applied to deep learning-based skin lesion classification using the MultiCancer dataset. We systematically evaluated the effectiveness of classical and advanced image enhancement techniques, including hair removal, bilateral filtering, and random erasing, both individually and in combination across ten state-of-the-art convolutional neural networks (CNNs), such as ResNet50, DenseNet121, and EfficientNetB0, alongside a customdesigned CNN architecture. Experimental results, measured with metrics including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, specificity, and Matthews correlation coefficient, indicate that while certain pre-processing combinations yield marginal improvements, high-performing deep learning models like ResNet50 and EfficientNetB0 demonstrate robust accuracy irrespective of specific pre-processing steps. Notably, the highest validation accuracy achieved in these experiments was 92%, consistently obtained by ResNet50 across several pre-processing configurations. These findings suggest that, with sufficiently expressive architectures and a diverse dataset, the overall benefit of elaborate preprocessing may be limited, streamlining the path towards effective and reproducible skin cancer classification workflows. The implications for clinical deployment stress efficiency and simplicity, without significant compromise in diagnostic accuracy.

title: Big Data & Analytics, Data Automation Recent Trends: Smart Memory Offloading for Enhanced Data Processing Throughput
authors: Aravind Narayanan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Large-scale data processing frameworks, such as Apache Spark, frequently encounter severe memory constraints that degrade system throughput due to excessive memory pressure and extended garbage collection cycles. This work introduces a smart memory offloading mechanism that strategically transfers data from primary memory to high-speed storage tiers, thereby releasing heap memory and mitigating memory bottlenecks. The approach minimizes performance degradation from storage latencies while enabling higher processor utilization. Implemented within a widely adopted Java runtime environment, the proposed method demonstrates significant improvements in server throughput for managed data processing workloads. These results establish a practical and scalable solution for enhancing pipeline efficiency in modern big data ecosystems. In the insurance sector, where real-time fraud detection, claims processing, and risk modeling require rapid access to large-scale policyholder and transactional data, memory constraints can severely impact operational efficiency and customer experience.

title: An Optimized Technique for Iris Spoof Detection in a Biometrics System through a Siamese Network
authors: Pooja Rai, Priyesh Kanungo
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: To maintain the integrity of iris identification systems, iris liveness detection is essential.In this paper, we present a Siamese Network approach with a convolutional layer for a real-time iris PAD system to increase robustness and generalization. The proposed formulation compares an incoming probe against genuine prototypes and flags a presentation attack via distance between them and calculates the loss function. Considering model quantization, the Siamese technique maintains CPU throughput providing strong generalization.The research compares the Siamese approach with previous handcrafted methods based on Local Binary Patterns and Histograms of Oriented Gradients. Siamese Network has shown great performance in other biometric modalities like Signature analysis making it open to be applied on Iris Spoof Detection.With the accuracy of 97.2 percent and AUC of 0.99 , the superiority of CNN + Siamese approach has phenomenally surpassed the One-Class SVM approach with 85.6 percent accuracy and Traditional CNN approach with 91.3 percent accuracy . Convolving it with deep learning techniques open its way for real time application programs.

title: Human-in-the-Loop Automation Patterns for Financial Services Using Camunda + Modern Java UIs
authors: Saravanan Manikandan, Gurinder Pal Singh, Vijaya Rama Raju Gottimukkala, P S L Narasimharao Davuluri, Renganayaki Sadagopan, T. Venish Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: HITL automation is increasingly used in finance to enhance the accuracy of decisions, operational efficiency, and regulation compliance. The use case scenarios include loan approval and fraud detection, implemented through Camunda BPM and Java-based UIs. The system has automated rule execution, DMN-based decision logic, and specific human verification; thus machine intelligence collaborates with human intelligence. Once we took it for a test drive, we realised it could save a lot of process time and human effort. It managed to automate 60-65% of the routine tasks while the accuracy for complex cases really improved with human moderation. In HITL workflows decisions are 15-20% better, with less errors compared to a fully automated process. Scalability testing shows a steady performance with high concurrency loads. The audit trail visualization offers a full traceability dimension in support of compliance. The results show that HITL automation is effective in doing important financial tasks by creating automation with human intervention.

title: AI-Driven Decision Support System for Sustainable Multi-Modal Transportation Management under Uncertainty and Time-Window Constraints
authors: Saurabh Jhureley, Ashish Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The proposed Hybrid Cognitive Swarm Reinforcement Framework (HCSRF) is an intelligent and agile solution framework that is used to control complex, dynamic and uncertain transportation networks. The model combines the capabilities of deep reinforcement learning and swarm intelligence to allow autonomous, collaborative and sustainable decision making in the many transport modalities. All system agents act based on a context-based policy network where they learn the best routing and timescales plans by exploring a cognitive environment model that represents the dynamism of the real time demand, travel time, and availability of resources. Swarm coordination mechanism is a self-balancing mechanism that provides collective intelligence by enabling collective sharing of experiences and coordinating strategies of the agents so that the computational overhead is reduced and resultant optimal globally. A two-fold scale sustainability optimizer does not only reduce emissions but also time changes, maintaining an environmentally friendly approach as well as operational efficiency. Adaptive feedback loop continually improves the policy changes according to the forecasting analytics and the performance indicators, providing the system with an opportunity to deal with the uncertainty and time-sensitive limitations. Simulations show that HCSRF shows an excellent scalability, less congestion and less energy consumption in contrast to the traditional optimization and heuristic-based methods and can be viewed as a powerful and visionary model in the next generation transportation control. The suggested HCSRF technique attains an overall accuracy of 98 percent, exhibiting higher performance relative to all current models.

title: Explainability First Parkinson’s Analytics: SHAP Driven Attributions from Telemonitoring and Voice Signals
authors: V Susan Mano Derry, S Lakshmi, A G Danish
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: The existing analytics for Parkinson’s disease are dominantly focused on maximizing prediction accuracy using only voice or telemonitoring data, using intricate black-box models with little insight into why or how the predictions are made. Such an approach lacks clinician-readable interpretability, does not support longitudinal tracking, and often uses random data splits that risk leakage and overly optimistic results. These limitations make clinical trust difficult and hinder real-world deployment. This research study presents a new explainability-first framework that prioritises transparent decision rationales over headline accuracy metrics, ensuring that a clinically meaningful justification accompanies every prediction. The approach in this work overcomes these gaps by adopting an explainability-first design using SHAP, with the goal not only of PD detection but also of providing stable, pathophysiology-consistent, auditable feature attributions. It integrates voice and telemonitoring signals while allowing subject-aware splits of voice recordings and temporal, patient-wise partitioning of telemonitoring sessions, ensuring leak-free insights suitable for progressive follow-ups. The framework provides SHAP summary and bar rankings, dependence and interaction visualisations, force and decision-path diagrams per patient, and temporal drift traces that allow clinicians to track how the contributions of features, such as age, jitter-based instability, sex, and test time, evolve across repeated visits. By forthrightly addressing the limitations of accuracy-centric PD models, the current study adopts a clear, transparent, governance-ready, and reproducible explainability-first architecture that provides a practical pathway for trustworthy screening and severity monitoring in real-world clinical environments that depend on human oversight and interpretability.

title: Randomized Masked HRM-RSA Encryption and Decryption with Hidden Exponentiation
authors: Mathumita S, Sai Lakshmi R, Medhaa M, Siva Priya MS
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: As technology advancement is continuously supporting digital business, security issues have become the key concern over the last years. While RSA and other public-key cryptosystems are usually considered to be secure, they are also susceptible to some ways of attacks; in particular, attacks based on the predictability of ciphertext. To create a more secure RSA which will lessen the chance of various cryptographic attacks this work comes with the latest version of RSA called HRM-RSA (Hidden Real Modulus RSA) that by the use of randomization techniques and masking.HRM-RSA employs hash-based masking and Diffie-Hellman modular exponentiation to generate more ciphertexts and hence, protection against known Plaintext and Ciphertext Analysis attacks. The paper demonstrates the superiority of HRM-RSA over the normal RSA by the use of encryption simulations and a comparison of the variations in ciphertexts. Moreover, we present the concept of using HRM-RSA for steganography as the addition of randomness guarantees the privacy of the concealed message

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Fake News Detection Using LSTM, BiLSTM, and CNN-BiLSTM Models
authors: Lavanya Ravilla, Suneetha Manne, Varalakshmi Maddireddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Recently, The swift increase in social media over the last few years has made it much easier to spread misinformation and misleading news coverage, which has led to some grave problems in society. Models that are sensitive to the context of words and those that are sensitive to the sequential interdependencies in text are essential to identify fake news. We suggest addressing this problem by implementing a hybrid deep learning model based on Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM), and a Convolutional Neural Network with BiLSTM (CNN-BiLSTM) to identify fake news. We tested and trained the models on benchmark data sets and contrasted their performance based on accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score, and we found that CNN-BiLSTM model is better than single LSTM and BiLSTM models in identifying local text patterns and long-range dependencies, which result in more accurate recognition of fake news. In addition, we developed a live-time Web site, which uses the trained model, whereby users can enter news articles or headline and predictions are made on the fly on whether they are authentic or not.

title: An AI-Driven Framework for Threat Detection and Traffic Flow Optimization in Smart City IoT Networks
authors: Kailasam Muthukumarasamy, Nivethitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: In smart cities, the integration of Internet of Things (IoT) technologies and machine learning is transforming infrastructure management, transportation efficiency, and public safety. Extensive interconnectivity within intelligent traffic systems introduces significant cybersecurity challenges, which can be mitigated by applying predictive analytics. This study examines IoT-based urban mobility systems alongside machine-learning-driven cybersecurity enhancements. By leveraging predictive analytics, the framework helps prevent cyberattacks targeting traffic control mechanisms while also reducing congestion, minimising accidents, and protecting critical infrastructure. Predictive analytics supports fault detection, hazard prevention, and overall system resilience by enabling continuous analysis of traffic and network data. The findings indicate that IoT communication channels can be secured effectively through encryption, blockchain-based mechanisms, and AI-enabled network monitoring. The proposed framework integrates cybersecurity measures with intelligent traffic management to improve the efficiency, safety, and reliability of transportation networks in smart city environments. The findings of this study address a deficiency in the field of smart city cybersecurity by proposing an innovative, scalable, and adaptable approach to protecting urban transportation infrastructure components from cyber threats. One of the objectives of this solution is to facilitate traffic flow in next-generation smart cities in a way that is both secure and frictionless.

title: Lateral Automation Techniques and Technologies for Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Tejashri Kolhe, Amol Shinde, Chavez Anthony, Ayush Gupta, Krishna Dongre, Sumedh Gadpayle
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Autonomous driving systems rely on precise steering mechanisms to maintain stability and ensure safe vehicle movement. Among the various control components, estimating the steering angle is essential for functions such as lane alignment, trajectory management, and obstacle response. Conventional rule-based and control-theory approaches have been applied for years, but their effectiveness decreases in unpredictable and highly dynamic road conditions. As a result, data-driven techniques, particularly those based on machine learning, have become a preferred solution. This paper explores different steering angle prediction methods, including regression-based models, convolutional neural networks, and reinforcement learning strategies, as well as vision-oriented, sensor-driven, and integrated hybrid frameworks. The study also discusses how IoT-enabled devices such as cameras, inertial measurement units, GPS modules, and on board processor support continuous data acquisition and real-time decision-making. Existing research is analysed with respect to model structures, datasets, hardware implementations, and evaluation criteria. Additionally, ongoing challenges such as computational efficiency, robustness across diverse driving scenarios, sensor inaccuracies, and safety considerations are addressed, followed by potential future directions emphasizing advanced sensor fusion, edge intelligence, and affordable autonomous steering solutions.

title: A Comprehensive Review of AI-Driven Approaches for Crop Disease Detection and Management
authors: Ojasvi Raj, Ankur Jain, Aryan Pahari, Nanda K, Anand Motwani, Ilma Khan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Agriculture faces a persistent challenge from crop diseases, resulting in significant economic losses and posing a serious threat to global food security. Traditional disease diagnosis methods rely heavily on manual visual inspection, which is time-consuming, subjective, and prone to error, particularly in large-scale agricultural settings. Addressing these limitations, this paper presents a comprehensive review and comparative analysis of AI-driven approaches for crop disease detection and management. The study synthesizes insights from more than 20 peer-reviewed research works published between 2020 and 2024, examining architectural trends and performance metrics across state-of-the-art models including VGG-19, ResNet-50, EfficientNet, Inception variants, and Swin Transformers. In addition to the literature review, an experimental evaluation is conducted on a standardized potato disease dataset to compare multiple deep learning architectures under identical conditions. The findings highlight a clear shift toward advanced deep learning-based solutions, with several models achieving classification accuracies in the range of 98% to 99%, demonstrating the strong potential of AI technologies in enabling accurate, scalable, and efficient crop disease diagnosis for precision agriculture.

title: Innovations in AI with Machine Learning for Smart Retail: Analysing Consumer Behaviour and Forecasting Demand from Video and Transaction Data
authors: G Deepa, T. Kamaleshwar, V. C. Ranganayaki, L. Dharani, V Samuthirapandi, S. Jaanaa Rubavathy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: More automation, increased productivity, and personalised consumer experiences have all resulted from recent advances in AI and ML in the retail sector. This study tests new artificial intelligence methods for consumer behaviour and demand forecasting using video analytics and transactional data. While static consumer data and sales records are useful for traditional retail analytics, they can miss intricate behavioural patterns that affect purchases in real time. Using state-of-the-art computer vision, deep learning, and multimodal learning frameworks, store video streams may be mined for data such as dwell length, frequency of product interactions, consumer trajectories, and emotion recognition. When paired with transactional data, behavioural signals reveal shifts in customer intent and demand. Using video data, RNNs, and transformer-based models, this paper outlines a state-of-the-art retail analytics solution. In addition to transaction data, video analytics provide buying cycles, seasonal patterns, and price elasticity. Better demand forecasts and customer segmentation may result from using these features in multimodal fusion. Adaptive store design and dynamic pricing are enabled by reinforcement learning for predicting consumer behaviour.

title: MSCSE-YOLO: A Small Object Detection Network for UAV Imagery Based on Multi-Scale Spatial Context and Semantic-Guided Enhancement
authors: Kun Yuan, Qing Zhu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: In complex UAV aerial imagery, small object detection is significantly challenged by complex background interference and inadequate coordination between spatial and semantic information, which substantially degrades detection performance. To address these issues, this paper proposes a multiscale spatial context and semantic-guided enhancement network for object detection, termed MSCSE-YOLO. Specifically, a MultiScale Spatial Context Enhancement Module (MS-SCEM) is introduced to capture spatial contextual information across multiple receptive fields, thereby enriching feature representations. In addition, a Semantic-Guided ForegroundBackground Decoupling Module (SG-FBDM) is designed to leverage deep semantic information to guide region-discriminative enhancement of shallow fine-grained features, effectively improving foreground-background discrimination. Experimental results on the VisDrone2019 dataset demonstrate that, while maintaining a favorable balance between model complexity and detection accuracy, MSCSE-YOLO achieves superior small object detection performance in complex UAV scenarios compared with the baseline model and several existing methods.

title: Research on Embedded Network Security Defense Mechanism Based on the Synergy of Cortex-M MPU and LwIP
authors: Liye Zhang, Haifeng Luan, Chaoqiong Fan, Tiancheng Jiang, Haoliang Guo, Chuanbo Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: As the Internet of Things (IoT) and the Industrial Internet deeply integrate, numerous resource-constrained devices based on the Cortex-M core connect to networks via the lightweight TCP/IP protocol stack (LwIP). However, the native LwIP stack, designed primarily for communication efficiency and code compactness, severely lacks defense mechanisms against common network threats such as IP spoofing, fragmentation reassembly attacks, and buffer overflows. Traditional pure software firewall solutions consume significant CPU resources, while general operating system-level isolation schemes are difficult to adapt to embedded bare-metal or lightweight RTOS environments. This paper proposes a “hardware-software synergistic” in-depth defense system: at the software level, the LwIP core is improved to implement bitmap-based fast source IP authentication and dynamic fragmentation control; at the hardware level, the Memory Protection Unit (MPU) of Cortex-M is utilized to construct a fine-grained memory isolation fence. Experimental results show that this mechanism effectively resists IP spoofing and DoS attacks while restricting buffer overflows to specific physical areas, keeping the impact on network throughput within 5%, thereby achieving a balance between security and real-time performance.

title: Real-Time Currency Identification for Visually Impaired Users Using a Lightweight YOLOv5-Based Object Detection System
authors: Nethi Sathwik, Talasila Balaji, M. Chandralekha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: The blind people have a common problem of identifying the currency denominations when conducting their daily monetary activities. This paper introduces an identification system of real time Indian currency on a lightweight deep learning object detection approach optimized for edge devices. The proposed system employs the YOLOv5 Nano architecture to identify various Indian currency denominations from live camera feeds under different lighting conditions and backgrounds. The Experimental evaluation shows that the YOLOv5 Nano model achieves high detection performance with a mAP of 99.5% at an IoU threshold of 0.5, while maintaining significantly lower inference latency compared to other YOLOv5 variants. To enhance accessibility, the detected currency denomination is communicated to the user through an audio feedback mechanism, enabling visually impaired users to independently and accurately recognize currency notes.

title: Detection and Classification of Cracks on Ship Hulls Using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Anis Charrada, Chiheb Zaoui, Habib Dallagi
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: In this paper, a novel approach utilizing artificial intelligence (AI) for the detection and classification of cracks on ship hulls is investigated. The work uses YOLOv8-based algorithm to improve the accuracy and efficiency of crack detection. The analysis shows that the algorithm could be used for image processing of ship hulls, providing a detailed report of found cracks. The results indicate substantial enhancement in the safety and maintenance of marine services.

title: A Unified Machine Learning Framework for Vehicle Insurance Claim Fraud Detection
authors: Adithya Jakkaraju, Atharva Mangeshkumar Agrawal
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Insurance fraud remains a major concern for the global insurance industry, resulting in substantial financial losses each year. Detecting fraudulent vehicle insurance claims is particularly challenging because of high dimensional data, imbalanced class distributions, and subtle behavioral patterns associated with fraud. This paper presents a unified machine learning framework designed to improve the reliability and consistency of fraud detection across heterogeneous datasets. Two publicly available sources, the Fraud Oracle dataset and the Car Insurance Claim dataset, are used to evaluate the generalization capability of the proposed framework. The methodology integrates a standardized preprocessing pipeline that includes data cleaning, encoding, outlier correction, and correlation based feature selection. Five algorithms, namely Logistic Regression, Random Forest, C5.0, Gradient Boosting, and XGBoost, are trained using identical settings and optimized through five fold cross validation. Experimental results show that Gradient Boosting and XGBoost consistently achieve the highest accuracy, recall, and area under the ROC curve. Cross dataset evaluation further confirms their strong generalization ability. A SHAP based interpretability analysis highlights policy related and behavioral variables as the most influential predictors of fraud. The results demonstrate that a unified and reproducible machine learning workflow can significantly improve real world vehicle insurance fraud detection.

title: Ethical AI Governance Models for Federated Multi-Agent Ecosystems
authors: Tanvi Mittal, Mallikarjun Gouru, Harsh Ranjan, Chandra Shekhar Pareek, Chaitanya Kumar Nelavayi, Rishith Reddy Bandaru
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The fast proliferation of federated multi-agent systems in fields like smart healthcare, autonomous systems, edge-cloud intelligence, and cyber-physical systems has further raised the issue of ethical responsibility, openness, and regulation in the decentralized artificial intelligence (AI). In contrast to centralized AI systems, federated multi-agent systems use autonomous agents to learn and operate together in heterogeneous and untrusted environments, and traditional governance mechanisms are insufficient. The paper suggests a federation-specific Ethical AI Governance Model, and it is based on the principles of fairness, explainable, privacy protection, accountability, and regulatory compliance. The suggested model proposes a multi-layer governance architecture to include the ethical policy orchestration, compliance enforcement at the agent level, safe federated auditing, and ongoing assessment of ethical risk with the help of trust-aware metrics. The framework also guarantees accountable decision-making by integrating governance controls both in the local agent and global federation levels without affecting the system scalability and learning efficiency. The widespread conceptual analysis and illustrative examples reveal the effectiveness of the proposed model of governance in the reduction of ethical risks, including the spread of bias, the lack of transparency in decision making, and the development of malicious agents. The research provides the groundwork of federated intelligence based on ethics-by-design, which allows the reliance on reliable, transparent, and socially responsible implementation of the large-scale multi-agent AI systems.

title: MO-DEEF: A Novel Fuzzy-Hybrid Metaheuristic Optimization Algorithm for Internet of Things-Enabled Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Aryan Baibaswata, Shyam Kumar, Anubhav Singhal, Faraj Chishti, Abhimanyu Mittal
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The widespread adoption of IoT devices in everyday life has necessitated a need for network systems to have improved capabilities in terms of high throughput and minimal latency, as well as extensive network coverage and connectivity. However, due to the numerous conflicting objectives in these network systems, optimizing these networks is quite the complex challenge. This research introduces a novel approach, "Fuzzy Logic Driven Multi-Objective DOA-EEFO (MO-DEEF)," a hybrid algorithm which combines the existing algorithms, Electric Eel Foraging Optimization Algorithm (EEFO) and Dream Optimization Algorithm (DOA), as well as Mamdani-based Fuzzy Logic Controller for parameter tuning to address this issue. It leverages the population-based search techniques of EEFO along with the exploitation capabilities provided by DOA, assisted by the FLC to switch between the two when required. The primary objective of this paper is to identify the Pareto-optimal front that expresses the complex trade-offs among the various conflicting objectives. Extensive simulation outcomes demonstrate the significant efficacy of the proposed MO-DEEF algorithm on an IoT network in terms of its multi-objective optimization capabilities as compared to existing algorithms.

title: Evaluation of Whisper-based Kannada ASR: Challenges, Analysis, and Future Directions
authors: Samrudha Narendra, Samved Suresh Jain, Ronith H U, Chandravva Hebbi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Kannada, a Dravidian language with limited resources, creates difficulties for speech recognition because of varied sounds, regional accents, and scarce well-labeled audio. Despite efforts to increase the accuracy of the Vaani ASR model, the results did not yield a measurable improvement. Therefore, the study redirected its focus toward systematic Word Error Rate (WER) analysis to better understand the model’s errors. This work examines why the ARTPARK–IISc Whisper-medium-Vaani-Kannada system struggles with accurate transcriptions. Even after several tuning attempts using controlled adjustments and stopping rules, gains remained small, unstable. Testing across 2,199 clips resulted in a word error rate of 0.3318; character errors reached 0.0741. The results show pronunciation differences, uneven domain representation in training data, or misaligned audio-text pairs still pose major challenges. Such outcomes highlight the importance of phonetically diverse datasets, reliable cleaning methods, yet targeted adjustments designed for under-resourced Indian languages like Kannada.

title: Boundary-Aware Clause Segmentation for Zero-Shot Legal Clause Discovery Using Natural Language Inference
authors: K Ramanan, R Gokul Krishna, M Gopika Shree
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Identifying the basic legal clauses in a contract is one of the main areas of focus when it comes to analyzing contracts for regulatory compliance; this has been addressed to date through supervised learning methods that require extensive domain-specific training in order to perform successfully. Whilst models such as LegalBERT have demonstrated an impressive ability to identify basic legal clauses within contracts, their reliance on pre-labelled training sets limits their subsequent ability to scale across many jurisdictions and contract types (e.g., leases, purchase agreements, employment contracts, etc.). In this paper, we propose a framework that explicitly identifies the clause boundaries of a contract prior to identifying its basic clauses. Our results provide further support to the need for structural pre-processing when using zero-shot legal potential natural language processing (NLP).

title: Transformer-Based Sentiment Classification for Innovative Customer Feedback Analysis in Thai Cosmetic Industry
authors: Theerawut Tantiathimongkhon, Tanintorn Limpisiri, Thanakit Saengpan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: This research aims to develop an effective sentiment analysis system for classifying Thai cosmetic reviews, which present unique linguistic challenges such as the lack of word boundaries, the use of domain-specific terminology, and the prevalence of sarcasm in online discourse. We propose a hybrid approach that combines domain-specific pre-processing with machine learning and deep learning architectures, constructing a specialized beauty corpus and extracting sentiment-bearing keywords. We compare the performance of three models: Support Vector Machine (SVM), Bidirectional LSTM (Bi-LSTM), and fine-tuned WangchanBERTa. The experimental results demonstrate that WangchanBERTa significantly outperforms both traditional approaches, achieving an F1-score of 0.9450, compared to 0.8652 for SVM and 0.7600 for Bi-LSTM. Error analysis of the SVM model reveals specific challenges of the Thai language, such as complex negation usage and temporal sentiment shifts, which are effectively addressed by the Transformer-based architecture. The proposed system offers scalable solutions for both large e-commerce platforms and small enterprises, enabling smart customer feedback analysis that enhances business productivity and supports data-driven resource management. This study showcases the effectiveness of integrating domain-specific natural language processing techniques with pre-trained language models, providing a robust benchmark for sentiment analysis in the beauty industry and contributing to technological innovation and economic growth in Thailand's digital economy.

title: Dynamic Adaptive Prediction of RFA Pump Parameters and Wideband Gain Optimization Based on Deep Neural Networks
authors: Tianyi Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: As optical communication systems expand into the Super <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C}+\mathbf{L}$</tex> band, Raman fiber amplifiers (RFAs) have emerged as pivotal components due to their flexible, wideband amplification capabilities <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">[1]</sup>. Aiming at the bottlenecks of prolonged computation time and proneness to local optima in traditional nonlinear inverse design under multi-pump configurations, this paper proposes an intelligent RFA pump parameter configuration scheme deeply integrated with Deep Residual Neural Networks (Res-DNN) and Adaptive Weight Particle Swarm Optimization (AWPSO). Firstly, a dataset with engineering robustness is constructed by introducing a Gaussian noise data augmentation strategy, and a Res-DNN is designed as a surrogate model to achieve efficient and accurate prediction of gain spectra and noise indicators from pump parameters. On this basis, an AWPSO multi-objective optimization algorithm adopting a cosine nonlinear strategy is proposed for global optimization. System-level long-distance transmission verifications demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly improves computational efficiency. While realizing precise net gain compensation and excellent gain flatness, it remarkably enhances the received signal quality and reduces the system bit error rate (BER). This research provides a practical and feasible technical pathway for the efficient and automated parameter configuration of next-generation ultra-wideband optical communication systems.

title: Machine Learning Based Hotel Booking Decision Prediction and Recommendation Framework
authors: Niraj Kumar, Pankaj Kumar Tyagi, Shaina Arora
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The proposed study presents a new machine-learning-based model of hotel booking decision prediction and personalized recommendation, specifically aimed at supporting the dynamic requirements of the contemporary hospitality management. Contrary to the current system which is either based on cancellation prediction or single source suggestion, this framework uses a multi-source information such as customer profiles, behavioral trends, real-time preferences and contextual hotel factors. It is an amalgamation of superior ensemble learning algorithms and multi-criteria decision analytics that allow one to accurately foresee the booking results and customized hotel recommendations. The framework assumes a hybrid style whereby there is collaborative filtering, content-based recommendation, as well as sentiment analysis of user-generated interactions that is done to increase the decision support functions. The area under the ROC, MCC, accuracy, and computational efficiency on large and heterogeneous datasets are strictly considered as the performance. This technology enables hotel management and customers with usable insights, enabling them to best make their booking and to customize their experience and forms a major step forward in returning to traditional solutions of hotel recommendations and forecasting.

title: AI-Powered Predictive Marketing: Enhancing Customer Engagement Through Data Intelligence
authors: Noor Ahmad Alkhudierat
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: The rise of AI tools and their increasing usage is altering the way in which marketing communications and customer engagement will be enhanced throughout the enterprise. This paper is also intended to propose an overall predictive marketing methodological framework which can be introduced on the ground of companies' actual data obtained from digital campaign records. Following an approach of machine learning similar to the expedition estimation hypothesis, it's possible to capture patterns of online consumers and forecast their unique gunning response to advertising stimuli. The empirical everything you need bit creates digital traces from various platforms which allow to represent a user-content-brand interaction structure. Models are also employed to generalize about predictability of decision and decision-making determinants, and nowcasting of longer-lasting audience appeal. It thus makes the above researchers' call for more empirical evidence in studies of marketers' use of intelligent analysis systems to improve relevance levels in communication and offers, as well as some indication on how marketers might approach designing their communication strategies with (or based on) data mined results or predictions. There is not only theoretic meaning but also practical meaning with the proposed model for marketing strategy, according to which we could establish structure of automated marketing and CRM in digital interaction channel; enlarge company customer loyalty and competitive power by using of digital economy.

title: Privacy-Aware Prompt Masking Interface for Secure Large Language Model Interactions
authors: Hemprasath Sankar, M Pio Godwin, Amogh Vijay, F Smiley Hana Cladis, Subashree Prakasam, M Sri Harshavarth
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: The growing deployment of LLMs in real-world applications such as customer service, productivity assistants and decision support tools necessitates attention to privacy and security considerations since various user studies highlight a significant risk of exposing sensitive personal and organisational data while interacting with LLMs through conversational interfaces, primarily due to the absence of real-time protections against such disclosures in traditional interfaces. As a step towards mitigating these risks, we develop a novel privacy-aware prompt masking interface (PAPMI). PAPMI is a lightweight middleware that acts as an intermediary between the user and the LLM, intercepting the user’s prompt, identifying sensitive entities, calculating a context-aware risk score, masking highly sensitive information from the prompt, and forwarding a privacy-preserving prompt to the LLM. This approach is model-agnostic and runs entirely at the device level, enforcing privacy-by-design. The analytical assessment demonstrates that the proposed prompt masking technique achieves reductions of more than $85 \%$ and $92 \%$ in leakage of sensitive data and conversational response utility, respectively; both highly promising results that demonstrate the effectiveness of prompt masking at a practical scale.

title: Enhancement Of Image Watermarking Using White Box Cryptography
authors: Sonam Ahirwar, Piyush Kumar Shukla, Abhishek Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Digital image watermarking is a crucial technique for protecting intellectual property and verifying the authenticity and integrity of images in the digital era. This thesis presents a robust and secure image watermarking system that integrates frequency-domain transforms (Discrete Cosine Transform, DWT, and Haar Discrete Wavelet Transform, Haar-DWT) with cryptographic embedding using a randomly generated substitution-box (S-box) in a white-box cryptography framework. The proposed method embeds a secret watermark (such as a logo or signature) into the host image in an imperceptible way, providing copyright protection, authentication, and tamper detection capabilities. We first discuss the problem statement and objectives, highlighting the need for a watermarking scheme that is both robust against common image manipulations (e.g., compression, noise, cropping, rotation) and secure against attackers who might have full knowledge of the algorithm (white-box threat model). A comprehensive literature survey is provided, covering classical and state-of-the-art watermarking techniques, the use of DWT and Haar wavelet transforms for resilient embedding, cryptographic approaches to watermarking (including chaotic maps and S-box usage), and the principles of white-box cryptography. We then formalize the problem and present our proposed methodology in detail. The core idea is to perform a one-level Haar wavelet decomposition on the image, apply block-wise DWT to selected subbands (in particular, the mid-frequency Haar subband), and embed the encrypted watermark bits into the DWT coefficients. The encryption of the watermark is achieved via a randomly generated S-box, which provides confusion (as per Shannon’s criteria) and ensures the watermark cannot be deciphered without the secret key. Block diagrams and flowcharts are included to illustrate the embedding and extraction processes of the watermarking system. We also describe our MATLAB-based implementation, including the code structure and functions for embedding, extraction, and evaluation.

title: Efficient Hybrid Metaheuristic Model for Early Detection of Breast Cancer in Mammograms
authors: S Koushalya, S. Baulkani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Breast cancer detection through mammography depends on proper feature extraction and classification, where biologically-inspired Metaheuristic optimization-based approaches significantly improve diagnostic accuracy. In this study, we proposed a feature selection and optimisation technique using the CSA and a feature optimisation method, RFO, to enhance the performance of microcalcification classification in the DDSM database. Contrast-Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalisation (CLAHE) for contrast enhancement. We enhance feature extraction by leveraging crowd intelligence to select features. And NOA, inspired by the foraging habit of nutcracker birds, to optimise feature combination. RFO utilises the red fox foraging to improve the classification accuracy. RFO can refine the classification boundaries of machine learning models. The NOA-RFO model hybridisation provides a good compromise between precision and efficiency. It has a rich heritage as one of the most efficient methods for detecting breast cancer from mammograms. It includes an excellent Computer-Aided Diagnosis (CAD) system for early detection.

title: Predicting the Unpredictable: A Big Data Approach to Airline Delay Analysis
authors: Linta Islam, Syada Tasmia Alvi, Nazim Uddin, Chowdhury Abida Anjum Era
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Airline delays play a very significant role in influencing the global economy and also in individual carrier performance. Thus, delay prediction is an important area of research as minimizing delay can efficiently solve these issues. We used the time series dataset provided by U.S. Department of Transportation and conducted classification and regression analysis. In this study, we employed only PySpark for our data processing and analysis, based on the Apache Spark technology of the big data framework. We used five machinelearning models for regression analysis and achieved an RMSE of 20.30. Additionally, we used seven machine-learning models for classification with an accuracy of 94.39%. We further compare the performance measures of several machine learning models for different types of delay.

title: Solar-Powered NPK Detection System for Precision Agriculture
authors: Eliganti Ramalakshmi, Vijay Reddy Goli, Venkata Sushma Chinta, Y Nagini, Srujana Inturi, Sowmya Kethi Reddi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This paper presents a low cost solar powered NPK detection system for real time soil nutrient monitoring in precision agriculture. The prototype combines an ion selective electrochemical NPK sensor, an Arduino Nano microcontroller, and RS485 based Modbus RTU communication, providing on site measurements of nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium. Nutrient values are displayed on a compact OLED interface, while a small photovoltaic array with lithium-ion storage enables fully off grid operation. Field experiments across multiple soil types show an average agreement of about 93% with laboratory analyses. A detailed component cost analysis indicates a total hardware cost between 2500 and 3500 INR, making the system accessible to small scale farmers. By reducing dependence on laboratory testing and providing immediate feedback at the field, the proposed platform supports more informed fertilizer management and improved crop productivity in resource constrained environments.

title: Optimal NER systems: BenchMarking Transformer Based models and Traditional Models on BioMedical Corpus
authors: Maram Harsha Vardhan, B Balaji Naik, Maram Koteswara Rao, Guntu Nooka Raju
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Recent advancements in technology, particularly in the medical domain, have enabled efficient analysis and processing of unstructured data. In medical Named Entity Recognition (NER), various biomedical corpora exist and many researchers work to extract the concealed information within them. The growth of Natural Language Processing (NLP) has made it easier to extract drug names, chemical names, disease terms, symptoms, dosage forms, outcomes, and diagnoses. In this study, an extensive heuristic comparison of both traditional models and state-of-the-art transformer-based models for biomedical NER is conducted. Traditional models considered include CRF, MEMM, and APT, while transformer-based models include BERT and its variants. The SpaCy machine learning library is used for training annotated entities. The main objective of this study is to identify and extract diverse medical entities accurately. This study considers two different datasets and the evaluation focuses on the precision, recall, and F1 score of the models. The Best F1 score was achieved on BC5CDR dataset by PubMedBERT model and with the F1 score of 93.20%. In addition, the structured data obtained can be used in Innumerable application such as Medical Decision Systems, Biomedical research, Patient health Data Management.

title: Tomato Ripeness Detection Using YOLOv8 and Edge-Aware Texture Mapping for Smart Agriculture
authors: Manoj Chandru P M, Praveen Kumar P, Venkatesan S, M Revathi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Prediction of fruit ripeness is important in the quest to maximize agricultural productivity but the existing techniques have been characterized by issues related to subjectivity and constraints in a real world set up. In the current paper, the author presents a new computer vision system that can accurately detect tomato ripeness in smart agriculture. We are one of the first projects to introduce a state-of-the-art real-time object detector (YOLOv8) and an Edge-Aware Texture Mapping together in a creative way. This synergy empowers the system to strongly localize tomatoes and detect the minute textural machinations, and when the problem of inconsistent lighting and occlusions arise it makes an optimal resolution to solve it. We diligently selected and groomed a varied data set and critically assessed the integrity of the YOLOv8 model when placed under a simulated real condition and perform a comparison with YOLOv7. Our results have shown great accuracy and reliability which herald great potential in reducing post harvest losses, enhancing efficiency in the supply chain and a huge improvement in sustainable agricultural practices.

title: Research on Leather Defect Detection Algorithm Based on GND-YOLOv11
authors: Yixuan Lin, Meilin Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: To address the challenges of data acquisition, slow detection, and low accuracy for small objects in leather defect detection, this paper proposes a lightweight algorithm based on an improved YOLOv11, termed GDN (Ghost-DFC-NWD-YOLOv11). First, Albumentations and Mosaic augmentation are combined to enrich sample diversity. Second, the Ghost module is introduced to replace C2f in the Backbone and Neck, significantly reducing parameters and computational overhead. Furthermore, the DFC Attention mechanism is integrated to enhance feature extraction, and the Normalized Wasserstein Distance (NWD) is adopted to replace traditional IoU for better small object detection. Experimental results show that compared to the baseline YOLOv11, GDN reduces FLOPs and parameters by 18.3% and 16.3% respectively, while achieving an AP of 94.5% for small objects and an overall mAP of 94.6%, effectively balancing high precision with a lightweight design.

title: An Empirical Study on Rough Set Theory for Feature Selection and Classification Model Optimization
authors: Amit Dandriyal, Pooja Sahu, Udutala Mahender, Anubhav Rai, B Mamatha, Suman Punia
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Researchers face an important medical challenge in seizure prediction where machine learning methods provide promising solutions for proper classification systems. The research puts forth a rough set theory (RST)-based mechanism for selecting features that enhances categorization performance. A three-step analytical process includes data pre-processing as well as statistical feature selection with mean, median and mode calculation and multiple machine learning algorithm classification. Medical data obtained from UCI repository consists of 178 attributes. The data normalization process starts with applying Min-max discretization techniques while the dependency analysis of rough sets performs feature reduction operations. The performance evaluation utilizes decision tree (DT) within a set of algorithms that also includes JRip, J48, random forest (RF), Naïve Bayes (NB), multilayer perceptron (MLP), and k-nearest neighbours (KNN). The integration of mean-based feature selection into RST produces the most accurate results enabling RF to deliver the optimal classification outcomes at 0.883 F-measure. J48 ranks among the top classifiers as it stands as the second-best classification model. The proposed approach performs effectively according to comparative assessments with WASRA, NRSC, and KNRS along with other rough set-based models. Real-world seizure prediction applications would benefit from introducing rough set-based feature selection because it results in enhanced classification accuracy.

title: Prediction and Analysis of Indoor NO2 Concentration Based on PCA-ANN Model
authors: Zhenheng Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{NO}_{2}$</tex>, as one of the primary indoor pollutants, can easily increase health risks even for low-risk populations. This study addresses the challenge of predicting and analyzing indoor <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{NO}_{2}$</tex> concentrations based on outdoor <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{NO}_{2}$</tex> levels in a tropical coastal city, focusing on the multi-factor nonlinear influences. An integrated prediction model combining Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and Artificial Neural Network (ANN), termed PCA-ANN, was developed. The research first employs PCA to reduce the dimensionality of multi-source environmental data and extract core influencing factors. Subsequently, ANN is used to establish a nonlinear mapping relationship between these factors and indoor/outdoor <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{NO}_{2}$</tex> concentrations. Compared with models such as multiple linear regression, the PCA-ANN model demonstrates significant advantages in prediction accuracy. The results indicate that this model effectively handles high-dimensional data and accurately predicts dynamic indoor <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{NO}_{2}$</tex> concentrations from outdoor <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{NO}_{2}$</tex> levels, providing a superior tool for intelligent indoor air quality management in tropical regions.

title: Multi-Scale Cross-Modal Alignment for Automatic Modulation Classification
authors: Guocheng Ma, Liansheng Zhuang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Automatic Modulation Classification (AMC) is essential for identifying modulation schemes in non-cooperative communication systems. Recent deep learning-based AMC methods have explored various strategies to improve recognition performance, such as multi-scale time-frequency representations and multimodal input fusion. In principle, integrating multi-scale and multimodal approaches offers complementary benefits—multiscale perception captures signal details across different granularities, while cross-modal alignment enhances feature robustness under channel distortions. However, most existing studies still focus on only one of these directions in isolation. To bridge this gap, this paper proposes MPCA, a novel AMC framework that integrates multi-scale feature perception with cross-modal alignment and employs supervised contrastive learning. MPCA first tokenizes multi-resolution spectrograms and fuses them via a token-wise cross-scale attention mechanism. It then aligns spectral tokens with raw IQ sequences through a cross-modal attention module, followed by a hybrid convolutional-transformer backbone for joint feature learning. Experiments on benchmark datasets (RML2016.10a/b, RML2018.01a) show that MPCA consistently outperforms existing methods across Signal-to-Noise Ratio(SNR) regimes, confirming the effectiveness of its multi-scale and multi-modal design.

title: Multi-Resolution Feature Learning for Traffic Light Recognition via Wavelet-Enhanced CNN
authors: Rim Trabelsi, Khaled Nouri
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Robust traffic light recognition (TLR) is essential for intelligent transportation and autonomous driving systems operating under challenging real-world conditions. This paper presents a lightweight hybrid framework combining discrete wavelet transform (DWT) based multi-resolution feature extraction with a convolutional neural network (CNN) for accurate and efficient traffic light detection and classification. The proposed method decomposes RGB images into wavelet sub-bands to enhance discriminative structural features while reducing noise and computational cost, then feeds the resulting representation into a compact CNN for real-time inference. Experiments conducted on the Bosch Small Traffic Light Dataset demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves high accuracy and strong robustness under varying illumination and environmental conditions, while maintaining low computational complexity suitable for embedded applications.

title: Automatic Hybrid Extractive-Abstractive News Headline Generation Using a BiLSTM-Attention-LSTM Sequence-to-Sequence Architecture
authors: Ishwari Shivaji Jamadade, Amruta Veeresh Kasbegoudar, Harshitha M Banakar, Manjunath S M, Sumaiya P
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This work presents a hybrid approach that combines extractive and abstractive headline generation to address the limitations of both methods. Two benefits are the addition of more semantic context through lead-based extraction and a solution to problems with copy repetition, fact accuracy and costs of training the extractive model on large datasets. As a prototype, lead based extraction and an attention-based sequence-to-sequence (Seq2Seq) architecture were combined to yield an encoder using a bidirectional long short term memory (LSTM) with a decoder driven by an attentional mechanism, following standard neural sequence modeling practice in deep learning. The resulting system was trained on the CNN/DailyMail dataset containing over 35,000 vocabulary words yielding ROUGE-1=30.40%, ROUGE-2=12.17%, ROUGE-L=20.18% when evaluated on 200 validation samples, outperforming the lead-3 baseline. Thus the hybridization of these approaches appears to yield an efficient and effective hybrid extractive/abstractive model for headline generation. Future work may include copying mechanisms and coverage modeling to improve the model’s accuracy in relation to the training dataset.

title: HPNA: A Hybrid PSO–NSGA-II Optimization Framework with Adaptive Diversity Preservation for Multi-Objective UAV Three-Dimensional Path Planning
authors: Meiyang Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Multi-objective UAV three-dimensional path planning demands simultaneous optimisation of path length, energy consumption, and flight safety under dense obstacle environments—a challenge that exposes three critical limitations in existing methods: (1) single-objective metaheuristics (e.g., vanilla PSO, GWO) sacrifice solution diversity for convergence speed and cannot represent Pareto-optimal trade-offs; (2) evolutionary multi-objective algorithms (e.g., NSGA-II) provide well-spread fronts yet suffer from slow convergence, limiting real-time applicability as fleet size grows; (3) static initialisation and fixed operator parameters cause premature stagnation, degrading robustness in high-density obstacle scenarios. To address these limitations, we propose HPNA (Hybrid PSO-NSGA-II Algorithm), a unified optimisation framework that integrates (1) Pareto-guided PSO velocity updates driven by crowding-distance tournament selection from an elite non-dominated archive, (2) an adaptive cosine-modulated inertia weight that dynamically balances exploration and exploitation throughout the search horizon, and (3) an entropy-monitored chaotic re-initialisation mechanism that injects structured stochasticity whenever population diversity collapses. Extensive experiments on six standard multi-objective benchmarks (ZDT1-6, DTLZ2) and a three-dimensional UAV path-planning simulator with up to 16 agents confirm that HPNA consistently outperforms PSO, NSGA-II, MOPSO, and GWO across hypervolume, IGD, success rate, and computational scalability metrics.

title: User Risk Perceptions and Protective Actions in FemTech in the UK and Iran
authors: Maryam Mehrnezhad, Teresa Almeida, Ehsan Toreini
publish: ACM Transactions on Social Computing
abstract: None

title: GoT-HCS: Graph-of-Thoughts Enhanced Hierarchical Reasoning for Automated Clinical Hospital Course Summarization
authors: Asma A. Alhashmi, Khalid N. R. Alharbi, Ahmed Badi Alshammari, Monir Abdullah, Abed Saif Ahmed Alghawli, Chams Sallami, Shouki A. Ebad, Abdulbasit A. Darem
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Brief Hospital Course (BHC) summarization is a critical yet cognitively demanding clinical documentation task that synthesizes voluminous electronic health records into concise discharge narratives. While recent large language models have demonstrated promise for automated clinical summarization, existing approaches exhibit significant limitations: single-pass generation lacks mechanisms for iterative refinement, leading to factual hallucinations; sequential architectures fail to model complex temporal and causal dependencies between clinical events; and black-box generation impedes interpretability essential for clinical adoption. We introduce a new method named Graph-of-Thoughts Hierarchical Clinical Summarization, or GOT-HCS. This method solves all these problems by formalizing the process and enhancing the knowledge. It comprises a directed graph of thoughts in which every node implies one of the specific steps of the clinical reasoning process. The node edges are time relationships, cause and logical relationships. Graph attention networks and knowledge graphs are also included in the approach.Amulti-stage reasoning engine is developed by generating, aggregating and refining candidate summaries in an iterative way based on learned quality scoring, and hierarchical distillation structures thoughts into coherent narrative generative clinical themes. Extensive analysis of three clinical benchmarks MIMIC-IV-BHC, MTS-Dialog, MIMIC-III shows that GoT-HCS can expect to outperform GPT-4 by 6.5 % increase on hospital course summarization and 3.8 % decrease on MEDCON, as well as retain the competitive computational performance. The systematic ablations confirm the role of graph-based reasoning, knowledge buildup and refinement as significant aspects of performance and a qualitative examination indicates better preservation of information and fewer hallucinations than sequential generation methods.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Organizations: A Systematic Review of Operationalizing Generative Artificial Intelligence Capabilities for Organizational Performance
authors: Badr Machkour, Ahmed Abriane
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Between 2018 and 2025, artificial intelligence expanded alongside major market shifts, while generative artificial intelligence began to reshape organizational strategy and value creation across functions and sectors. This article offers an integrated account of organizational uses of generative models in marketing, operations, human resources, finance and governance and identifies four core capabilities of generative artificial intelligence, namely personalization, decision augmentation, automation and ecosystem orchestration. It links these capabilities to the adoption and, above all, to the intensity and quality of use. Following PRISMA 2020 and PRISMA-S, we conducted a systematic review of Scopus and Web of Science in English, with critical appraisal through the Mixed Methods Appraisal Tool and a SWiM narrative synthesis. From 1,284 records, 83 peer reviewed studies were retained. Findings converge on a coherent architecture of mediating mechanisms. Dynamic capabilities, absorptive capacity, process and organizational reconfiguration and multi-level innovation form an organizational infrastructure that translates generative artificial intelligence into organizational performance outcomes. Responsible governance of artificial intelligence and data, grounded in transparency, human oversight, risk management and environmental vigilance, acts as a moderating buffer that aligns use with strategic objectives, amplifies benefits and contains risks. We also find a transversal role of this governance layer across functions and sectors through standard setting, supervision and accountability arrangements. The study proposes an operationalized integrative framework with fourteen testable hypotheses and outlines avenues for future longitudinal and quasi-experimental research, together with managerial implications for turning local experimentation into durable strategic advantage.

title: A Hybrid End-to-End Neural Network for Accurate Global Natural Gas Trade Forecast
authors: Yu Yan, Gang Lu, Xiaoqing Yan, Peng Xia, Jia Chen, Jie Yang, Di Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Accurate forecast of global natural gas trade is crucial for a country to promptly optimize and adjust its energy strategies for energy security. Recently, a variety of machine learning-based approaches have been adopted to address this issue. However, global natural gas trade is influenced by various complex (e.g., reserves of natural gas, energy imports and exports, economy, population, etc.) and uncertain factors (e.g., climate change, international turmoil, and global pandemics). Existing approaches either partially or loosely explore these factors by nonend-to-end learning, resulting in suboptimal feature extraction and temporal integration for forecasting. To fill this gap, this article proposes a novel hybrid end-to-end neural network (HENN) with three components: graph attentional network (GAT), temporal transformer (TT), and multilayer perceptron (MLP). First, GAT learns the latent features of annual trade network among various countries year by year. Second, TT integrates these annual latent features with temporal information to forecast the trade network's representations for the upcoming year. Third, MLP employs the forecasted representations to forecast the trade network of next year. The three components are trained by end-to-end learning, ensuring the feature extraction and the integration of temporal information are both comprehensive and optimal for forecasting. Extensive experiments with global natural gas trade data from 2000 to 2023 show that the proposed HENN significantly outperforms advanced models in forecasting the global natural gas trade.

title: Inductance Identification Method for Dual Active Bridge Converters Considering Sampling Noise Over the Full Power Range
authors: Yun Zhang, Mingqi Xu, Qinghan Wei, Zhen Huang, Bin Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: The dual active bridge (DAB) converter is widely used in energy storage systems (ESS) due to its bidirectional power flow capability and high efficiency. Extensive studies on advanced modulation and control strategies have been conducted to enhance the performance of DAB converters. However, these methods strongly rely on accurate inductance parameters. Existing parameter identification methods exhibit limited capability in suppressing sampling noise, making it difficult to achieve accurate identification over the entire power range. To address this issue, this article proposes a Sage-Husa inductance parameter identification method. First, the measurement noise variance is approximately estimated using a small number of samples, enabling the evaluation of sampling noise levels under different operating conditions. Then, the process noise variance is adaptively updated based on the variance of the prior error, thereby achieving accurate inductance identification over the full power range. In addition, considering the insufficient accuracy and operational complexity of conventional LCR meter, an inductance measurement method is proposed to provide a reliable reference for the identification results. Finally, to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method, a 200 W DAB converter prototype is built, and full power range inductance identification is implemented under an advanced extended dual-side asymmetric phase-shift modulation (EDAPSM) scheme. Experimental results verify the correctness and effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Enhanced Weighted Target Approximation Using an Improved Particle Swarm Optimization Approach
authors: Gabriel R. Dos Santos, Guilherme C. Machado, Alexandre C. B. Ramos, Douglas D. Bueno, Douglas B. Riffel, Alexandre C. R. Ramos
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This article proposes a two-layer tactical decision architecture for the autonomous allocation of Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) swarms in Search-and-Rescue (SAR) missions under severe resource constraints. The approach addresses the triage and distributed delivery challenge by integrating a Weighted Target Approximation protocol enhanced by Particle Swarm Optimization (WTA–PSO) with a Spatial Tactical Evaluation heuristic (Spatial Clustering). In the first layer, WTA–PSO replaces the conventional proximity-based target choice with a low-cost one-dimensional optimization that jointly accounts for flight distance, the victim’s medical urgency, and local drone-to-drone conflict, while preserving WTA’s intention exchange and distributed conflict resolution. In the second layer, spatial clustering enables UAVs, each constrained to a single rescue payload (single-payload drop) to evaluate the disaster topology and focus on victim clusters that lie within the payload coverage radius. Experiments were conducted in a dynamic two-dimensional simulation environment with mobile targets categorized into three urgency levels (green, orange, gray). The results reveal complementary gain profiles: under strict one-to-one engagement, WTA–PSO significantly increases the global Humanitarian Impact (Total_Value) exclusively through intelligent prioritization (emphasizing the critical gray level), while keeping the service volume near the fleet’s physical limit. However, when spatial clustering is enabled (Layer 2), swarm intelligence enables multiple simultaneous rescues with a single payload, breaking the logistical ceiling imposed by the fleet size (Nuav) and dramatically maximizing mission efficiency. The architecture’s robustness is validated over 50 independent runs (seeds) with high statistical significance (Kruskal–Wallis and paired Wilcoxon tests, p < 0.01), along with scalability analyses for reduced (Nuav = 15) and supersaturated (Nuav = 60) fleets. The findings show that coupling WTA–PSO’s predictive conflict-mitigation capability with the geometric exploitation of victim distributions consistently maximizes the operational effectiveness of robotic swarms across disasters of different scales.

title: Improved Localization of Partial Discharge Sources Using Powered Superlets
authors: Soumik Ghosh, Debangshu Dey, Sovan Dalai, Biswendu Chatterjee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Reliable detection and localization of partial discharges (PDs) in transformers are critical for ensuring insulation integrity and extending equipment lifetime. Conventional acoustic and time–frequency (TF) analysis methods such as the Short-Time Fourier Transform, Continuous Wavelet Transform, Stockwell Transform often face limitations under low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). This paper introduces the Powered Superlet Transform (PST), a novel TF representation that employs a powered growth schedule for the number of cycles in wavelet sets, resulting in increased inter location distance in TF plots and thus increased accuracy in localization. The PST is applied to acoustic signals measured from multiple PD sources in a controlled transformer tank setup. Subsequently, feature extraction through TF methods and classification through Support Vector Machine is performed. Comparative analysis with established TF techniques and acoustic localization methods demonstrates that PST is the most effective technique in terms of accuracy while both PST and Adaptive Superlet Transform (ASLT) provides superior noise robustness in terms of reduced localization error. Experimental results under varying SNR conditions show that PST can reliably localize PD sources with accuracy of 78.8% and approximate RMS localization errors 11.857 cm at SNRs as low as 2.5 dB, while established methods are less accurate. These findings highlight PST as a promising tool for advanced acoustic PD monitoring and condition assessment of high-voltage transformers.

title: Joint Cyber-Physical Data-Driven Enhanced Cyber Attack Detection for Power Systems
authors: Luyu Wang, Kaifeng Zhang, Ying Wang, Kun Yuan, Biplab Sikdar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: Rapid, early, and accurate detection of cyber intrusions is increasingly vital for ensuring the security and operational resilience of modern power systems, as cyber-attacks grow in both frequency and sophistication. However, existing approaches primarily focus on either cyber or physical data independently, ignoring critical interactions across these two layers. To bridge this gap, this paper proposes a novel Cross-Layer Graph Attention-Transformer Network (CL-GTN), which simultaneously incorporates information from both the cyber and physical layers, and effectively integrates them through a learnable physics-informed point-to-bus projection mechanism. Specifically, the projection matrix constructed from power system sensitivity factors explicitly captures the fine-grained mapping from cyber data points in communication packets to affected physical grid nodes, providing a robust cross-layer fusion basis. Graph attention networks (GAT) are employed separately at the cyber and physical layers to extract relevant local and topological features, followed by a Transformer module that dynamically aggregates cross-layer temporal dependencies for accurate attack identification. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmark systems, including the IEEE 118-bus and large-scale ACTIVSg2000 systems, confirm that CL-GTN achieves high detection accuracy (over 0.98) and strong early-warning capability, successfully detecting up to 90% of attacks before execution with an average lead of 2.4 time steps. Comprehensive ablation and robustness studies further validate the model’s stability and component effectiveness. In addition, interpretability analyses show that the proposed projection-matrix mechanism accurately identifies critical cyber–physical coupling points, thereby enhancing situational awareness and defense capability.

title: HRTF2MESH: 3D Human Ear Mesh Reconstruction using Commodity Acoustic Devices
authors: Navid H. Zandi, Bodee Quansah, Rong Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Capturing fine-grained 3D shapes and anthropometric features of human ears is a non-trivial and yet important task in a variety of applications. Existing approaches relying on specialized equipment such as high-definition 3D scanners or photogrammetry from RGB(-D) cameras are laborious in data acquisition and post-processing, and may perform poorly in case of occlusions from face and head covering, accessories, and the complex geometry of pinnae. Recognizing the strong dependency of acoustic anatomical transfer functions called Head-Related Transfer Functions (HRTFs) on the morphological features of pinnae, we develop HRTF2Mesh, a novel acoustic-based method for 3D human ear mesh reconstruction using commodity devices – a pair of earphone microphones and a smartphone device. HRTF2Mesh takes sparse acoustic measurement data from a subject, estimates and encodes the HRTFs as low-dimension vectors and then generates 3D mesh representation of their ear with the help of 3D morphable ear models. HRTF2Mesh has been evaluated using both public datasets and data collected from a diverse set of volunteers. Experiment results show that HRTF2Mesh achieves an average ear mesh estimation errors of 0.92 mm for test subjects in public datasets, and 1.70 mm amongst the volunteers. The data acquisition process of HRTF2Mesh can be completed under 5 minutes by a single user.

title: Adaptive TSCH Scheduling for Emergency Packets in High-Density Low-Power and Lossy Networks
authors: Jianan Zhang, Jiamei Lv, Hailang Zhang, Yi Gao, Wei Dong
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: High-density low-power and lossy networks (LLNs) for IoT applications (e.g., smart cities) urgently require low latency in emergencies like fires. However, existing hash-based or traffic-aware Time Slotted Channel Hopping (TSCH) scheduling methods fall short in such emergency scenarios.
