These methods lack both dedicated scheduling mechanisms for emergency packets and effective conflict resolution strategies, thereby resulting in frequent collisions and urgent traffic latency that fails to meet response demands. While reinforcement learning (RL)-based schemes can mitigate these issues through multi-objective optimization, their high computational and energy overheads place stringent demands on the capabilities of edge nodes. To address these challenges, We propose ATSE, an adaptive TSCH scheduling for emergency packets in high-density LLNs. TSE employs subtree-segregated time-slot allocation to assign regular and emergency packets to independent subtree resources for precise scheduling, combined with temporal emergency time-slot adjustment to dynamically reallocate slots for non-real-time emergency packets, ensuring low-latency delivery. For conflict avoidance, we design an Nhash-based conflict avoidance method via selective time-slot segment partitioning, allowing conflicting packets to request resources from sinks in dedicated segments, thereby reducing collisions and control overhead. Additionally, ATSE integrates multi-sink load balancing to evenly distribute dynamic network traffic, improving overall transmission performance and resource utilization. We implement ATSE on Contiki OS and evaluate it in large-scale, high-density scenarios. Experimental results show that ATSE outperforms baseline methods, reducing latency by 18.6%, improving reliability by 10.5%, and lowering energy consumption by 10.6%.

title: Guest Editorial: Large AI Models for Communications: Part 2
authors: Feibo Jiang, Kezhi Wang, Merouane Debbah, Zhu Han
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: We are honored to present this special issue of IEEE Communications Magazine on Large AI Models for Communications. As 5G continues to advance in terms of low latency, massive connectivity, and high data rates, the 6G vision of ubiquitous intelligence is accelerating the deep integration of communication technologies and artificial intelligence. Empowered by capabilities in perception, analogy, and reasoning, the new generation of Large AI Models (LAMs) can efficiently characterize complex communication environments, enhance system generalization and adaptation to previously unseen scenarios, and support more personalized services for end users. Nevertheless, the further development of this emerging paradigm still faces several critical bottlenecks, including the computational and energy burden caused by the deep coupling of LAMs and communication systems, the lack of effective collaboration mechanisms between large and lightweight models, and the relative scarcity of domain-specific knowledge bases, datasets, and evaluation benchmarks for communications. This special issue brings together sixteen representative contributions and systematically examines the key issues in the convergence and evolution of LAMs and communication systems from the perspectives of theoretical foundations, system design, and open challenges, with the aim of offering useful insights to both academia and industry.

title: A Dual-Slope Adaptive Feedback Strategy for Dynamic Voltage Balancing of Series-Connected SiC MOSFETs
authors: Yuanxin Li, Zhangyong Chen, Yunfeng Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Silicon carbide metal-oxide-semiconductor field-effect transistors (SiC MOSFETs) can enhance the voltage withstand capability when operated in series. However, dynamic voltage imbalance caused by mismatches in parasitic parameters of devices or circuits is an inherent issue in series operation, which significantly degrades the service life of series-connected devices. Existing active schemes suffer from either a long voltage balancing period or a significant degradation of the inherent fast switching speed of SiC MOSFETs. In contrast, existing passive schemes are plagued by either considerable energy loss introduction or complex structures that are difficult to implement. To address these issues, this article proposes a novel passive voltage balancing strategy for dual-slope drain-source voltage (vds) of SiC MOSFETs. By employing simple components to enforce equal vds slew rates and controlling the circuit’s operation time, the proposed scheme minimizes the impact on switching speed as much as possible while achieving effective voltage balancing. Comparisons with the conventional gate drive (CGD) through experiments and with the existing best active scheme have verified that the proposed method can effectively achieve dual-slope vds driving and significantly reduce the impact of the voltage balancing circuit on switching speed (restricting the turn-off time to ∼120 ns compared to the ∼80 ns baseline, substantially outperforming the ∼300 ns required by the benchmark active scheme).

title: Method for Quantifying Cooperation Scores in Tent Dismantling Operations
authors: Kotaro Yamatogi, Koji Kinoshita, Tsuyoshi Hatori, Atsushi Sugita, Masaharu Isshiki, Yoshinobu Higami
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: Cooperative tasks involving multiple individuals occur across diverse fields, and analyzing group behavior is essential for understanding overall group function. Each agent within a group acts according to its individual intentions, and in cooperative tasks, agents implicitly collaborate by discerning others' intentions. In this study, we extracted behavioral feature vectors for each agent from their temporal dynamic structures and tracking data using graph neural networks, then quantified cooperation scores between agents based on their similarity. Furthermore, using these scores, we demonstrated the temporal evolution of cooperation scores among agents in the tent dismantling operation videos and showed the cooperation scores for each working group.

title: A Mobile-Based Food Ordering System with Enhanced User Experience and Usability: Design and Evaluation of JETFOOD
authors: Pisit Chansuek, Jukkapun Sawatdee, Phanintorn Suaprae
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Mobile-based food ordering systems have become essential in modern digital lifestyles, where user experience (UX) and usability significantly influence user satisfaction and continued usage behavior. This study presents the design and evaluation of JETFOOD, a mobile-based food ordering system developed using a user-centered design approach to enhance usability and interaction quality. The system design integrates UX principles, including simplified navigation, intuitive interface layout, and efficient interaction flow. A total of 50 participants were recruited to evaluate the system using a fivepoint Likert-scale questionnaire. Both descriptive and inferential analyses were conducted, including mean, standard deviation, and correlation analysis to examine relationships between usability, interface design, and user satisfaction. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system achieves a high level of user satisfaction (Mean = 4.62, S.D. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$= \mathbf{0. 5 0}$</tex>). Furthermore, usability and interface design exhibit strong positive correlations with overall user satisfaction, confirming the effectiveness of the UX-driven design approach. The findings highlight the importance of integrating user-centered UX principles into mobile service systems and provide practical guidelines for developing efficient and user-friendly food ordering applications.

title: Two-Stage Signal Reconstruction for Amplitude-Phase-Time Block Modulation-based Communications
authors: Meidong Xia, Min Fan, Wei Xu, Haiming Wang, Xiaohu You
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: Operating power amplifiers (PAs) at lower input back-off (IBO) levels is an effective way to improve PA efficiency, but often introduces severe nonlinear distortion that degrades transmission performance. Amplitude-phase-time block modulation (APTBM) has recently emerged as an effective solution to this problem. The intrinsic amplitude and phase constraints of each APTBM block can be leveraged to mitigate PA-induced nonlinear distortion via constraint-guided signal reconstruction. However, existing reconstruction methods apply these constraints only heuristically and statistically, limiting the achievable IBO reduction and PA efficiency improvement. This paper addresses this limitation by decomposing the nonlinear distortion into dominant and residual components, and accordingly develops a novel two-stage signal reconstruction algorithm consisting of coarse and fine reconstruction stages. The coarse reconstruction stage eliminates the dominant distortion by jointly exploiting the APTBM block structure and PA nonlinear characteristics. Subsequently, the fine reconstruction stage minimizes the residual distortion by casting it as a nonconvex optimization problem subject to explicit APTBM constraints, for which a closed-form solution is derived. The proposed algorithm is validated through comprehensive numerical simulations and testbed experiments. Results show that, without compromising transmission quality, the proposed algorithm enables an additional IBO reduction of approximately 5 dB in simulations and 2 dB in experiments over baseline methods, yielding relative PA efficiency improvements of 77.8% and 30.9%, respectively.

title: Solar PV Powered DC Charger for Electric Vehicle
authors: Nikita C Kambalimath, A B Raju, Vinod S Patil
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Solar photovoltaic (PV) powered DC charging architectures provide an effective alternative to grid-dependent EV chargers, particularly where reliability, efficiency, and renewable energy usage are priorities. To understand the operation of such systems, this work examines the behavior of a DC charging setup based on a reconfigurable PV–battery energy storage system (BESS) and explains the power management strategy used to utilize solar energy efficiently. The charging system integrates a buck converter for maximum power extraction from the PV array and a bidirectional buck–boost converter to regulate BESS charging and discharging. Through relay-based reconfiguration, the system supports multiple operating modes that enable flexible power flow among the PV source, EV battery, BESS, and auxiliary loads. The discussion highlights how these configurable interfaces improve PV utilization, maintain uninterrupted EV charging, and improve overall system efficiency. The insights provided offer a clear understanding of the operational logic and performance of renewable-powered DC charging systems.

title: Real-Time IoT-based Torque Estimation in Induction Motors using Adaptive Kalman Filters
authors: Keerthi Kumar N, Anjana Joshi, Priya Dongare Jadhav, Girish Jadhav, Sivaprasad Lebaka, S.SriRagavi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: real-time torque estimation in the induction motors is critical in the high-performance control of the electric drive, fault detection, and predictive maintenance in Industry 4.0 settings. Traditional model based estimation methods tend to be inaccurate in the event of change of parameters, variation in loads, or nonlinear operating conditions. The present paper will endeavor to address these issues by using an IoT-based adaptive Kalman filtering system to estimate the actual dynamic torque. The system is integrated with high frequency stator current and voltage as well as sensing, an ESP32 based IoT acquisition layer and real-time online monitoring through an MQTT supported cloud dashboard. Adaptive Kalman Filter (AKF) is a real time algorithm used to update covariance parameters that corrects noise and inverter switching disturbance and temperature induced changes in resistance. A steady-state, transient, and load-perturbed experiment of a 1.5 kW, 4 poles induction motor demonstrates that the AKF can be used to achieve a 18-25% improvement in the accuracy of torque estimation relative to traditional Kalman and MRAS observers and achieve lower estimation latency of less than 12 ms. An AKF estimator incorporating the IoT is highly adapted to smart industrial drives that demand constant monitoring and adaptive control.

title: Comparison of Analytical Method and AI Based Method to Estimate Frequency Nadir / Frequency Zenith
authors: Megha Fernandes, Bindu S.
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: A sudden imbalance of generation and load in a power system affects the frequency nadir / frequency zenith. The rotating mass of the conventional generators and load contributes to inertia of the system, which supports the frequency stability during disturbance. The replacement of conventional generation by the renewable energy sources (RES) leads to a decline in inertia and affects the frequency stability of the system. This paper shows the estimation of frequency nadir / zenith by analytical method and Artificial Intelligence (AI) based method. An analytical method based prediction of frequency nadir with known inertia and disturbance size is compared with Artificial Neural Network (ANN) based online prediction of frequency nadir. It is found that the estimation of frequency nadir / zenith by proposed ANN method is the most accurate. ANN model is used to estimate frequency nadir / zenith on MATLAB Simulink platform for a system with conventional generation and solar PV.

title: AI-Assisted Bacteria Detection and Growth Estimation Methodology using Deep Learning
authors: Chirag R Rai, Akshar Rai, Ashith Rai, Athul M R, Akshaya D. Shetty, Usha Desai
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: To analyze the bacteria present in the sample, the growth percentage calculation plays a very important role. This paper aims to identify and analyze the bacterial colonies in laboratory-captured images, using the Deep Learning (DL) methodology, which reduces the time and human effort required as compared to traditional manual examination. In this study, training dataset from Roboflow open source which consists of labeled classes namely, E. coli, Staphylococcus, and Acylostoma and tested against the Laboratory collected data. The bacteria image dataset, prepared in YOLOv8 format and this model is trained and tested to recognize bacterial colonies with 0.9131 precision and 0.9036 recall score. Also, mean Average Precision (mAP) @.5, and @.5:.95 ranges from 0.9 to 0.7 indicating precise localization and benchmark performance. During analysis of DL model, trained for 50-60 epochs with less than one second detection speed. The detected and undetected cases visualized using the user interface window. This methodology helps for effective bacteria detection which is very supportive for risk analysis conditions.

title: EduGen: AI powered Interactive visual learning for Enhanced Understanding
authors: Mithra Venkatesan, Sheetal Pawar, Nilakshi Rajule
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Teaching Learning methodologies have undergone rapid changes in comparison with traditional methods. There are various resources available from which students can acquire knowledge. Teachers can also utilize these resources to augment the teaching learning practices. Different online portals like YouTube provide various educational videos. However, these videos are not completely in line with the user’s requirements. AI can be used for gaining knowledge. These video models available are generic and do not cater to specific needs of the users. Hence there is need for a platform which can offer personalized educational videos in accordance with the user’s needs and specifications. This work proposes a model based on multimodal approach which combines various text to image, text to speech models to generate a user defined video. Different techniques such as General Adversial Network, Variational Autoencoders based models and Natural Language Processing have been utilized to build a heuristic video of a particular educational concept. Such personalized learning platforms will greatly facilitate and enhance learnings in future generations.

title: Blockchain-Based Identity and Access Management in IoT Networks
authors: Abdul Hadi, Quazi Taif Sadat, Senthilnathan Balasubramaniam, Laxman Rao Vattam, Ravi Teja Thutari, Ponnarasan Krishnan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The accelerated growth of Internet of Things (IoT) devices in the smart city, healthcare, industrial automation, and critical infrastructures present a major challenge in managing identities and access control because of the heterogeneity of the devices, the need to scale, and because device identities are usually centralized on a single trusting authority. Commonly, traditional identity and access management (IAM) systems are not sufficient to combat the security vulnerabilities of single points of failure, unauthorized access, and identity spoofing in large-scale IoT systems. In order to address these constraints, in this paper, a blockchain-based identity and access management system has been developed that will offer decentralized, secure, and tamper-resistant authentication and authorization to IoT networks. The proposed solution uses the distributed ledger technology and smart contracts to make self-sovereign device identities, fine-grained access control policies and transparent auditability all without resorting to centralized authorities. There is the inclusion of lightweight cryptographic primitives to fit the resource limits of the IoT devices and off-chain storage mechanisms are employed to increase system scalability and performance. Security analysis proves that the proposed framework works well in preventing the typical attacks such as identity forgery, replay attacks and unauthorized privilege escalation. The results of experimental evaluation show the better access latency, the increased system reliability, and the decreased administrative overhead of experimental IAM solutions in comparison with traditional centralized IAM solutions. The identified blockchain-based IAM architecture will provide a scalable and reliable platform of the secure IoT implementations in the next-generation cyber-physical systems.

title: Skin Cancer Detection: Synergistic Integration of Deep Features with Ensemble Classifiers
authors: Vikas Narlakanti, Vaishnavi Prashanth, Dasari Veera Venkata Nooka Surya Saran, Suresh Kaswan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The mounting incidence of skin cancer across the world shows the need for the efficient, reliable computer-aided diagnosis system. But constructing these computer-aided systems often encounters problems like the unbalanced medical datasets and the struggle in understanding and deriving the discriminative features from the lesion types that look alike. To handle these issues, we present a hybrid integrated framework in this study. This framework is developed by combining the powers of deep learning, machine learning and ensemble learning. The widely recognised HAM10000 dataset is used in this research, which was balanced using the RandomOverSampler technique to ensure fair modelling. Multiple models are evaluated on the preprocessed HAM10000 dataset, where the DenseNet121 emerged as the best performer, so we selected it as the feature extractor. We trained multiple machine learning classifiers on these extracted features, and they showed significant improvement in the performance. To further optimise the performance, we tested different combinations of voting ensembles on these classifiers. Out of all the soft and hard voting ensembles explored, a hard voting ensemble of logistic regression and light-boost gradient boosting machine obtained a superior classification accuracy of 99.21% along with excellent precision, recall and F1-measures, significantly outperforming the baseline approaches. The notable results showcase the workflow’s reliability as a decision-support tool for the dermatologists. Its capability to improve the diagnostic precision, enable early detection and ultimately strengthen patient outcomes makes it highly beneficial for clinical applications.

title: Efficient CNN-LSTM-Based Hybrid Model-Predictive Control for Real-Time Maximum Power Point Tracking Under Partial Shading Conditions
authors: Pandikumar M, Mohammad Irshad, Guna G, Srihari T
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: This study introduces a computationally efficient CNN–LSTM hybrid framework for real-time maximum power point tracking (MPPT) in photovoltaic (PV) systems operating under partial shading conditions. Traditional model-predictive control (MPC) schemes are accurate but often limited by high computational demands, restricting their use in high-frequency converter applications. To overcome this challenge, the proposed approach employs depthwise separable convolutions for extracting spatial features from voltage–current data, combined with a pruned LSTM network enhanced with an attention mechanism for temporal sequence modeling. This integration substantially lowers computational complexity while preserving high predictive precision. By structuring voltage and current measurements as a two-dimensional grid, the framework supports inference with execution times below 500 μs, demonstrating suitability for embedded real-time control. The attention strategy further strengthens the quadratic programming optimizer by emphasizing critical temporal states, leading to improved adaptability under dynamic shading scenarios. Experimental validation using a TI C2000 Delfino microcontroller with 8-bit quantization confirms that the method achieves 99.2% tracking accuracy of the global maximum power point, consistently outperforming conventional perturb-and-observe and incremental conductance algorithms. Hardware implementation on a GaN-based DC–DC converter, coupled with precise power monitoring, highlights the practical feasibility of the system and evidences a 50-fold acceleration compared to unoptimized MPC. The work contributes toward bridging advanced machine learning with practical power electronics, offering a scalable and real-time solution for enhancing the performance of renewable energy conversion systems.

title: Self-Powered Narrowband Perovskite Photodetector Using Photon Field Modulation Layer
authors: Sampati Rao Sridhar, Mohammad Adil Afroz, Ajay Kumar Sahu, Ankita Sengupta, Nilesh Kumar Jangir, Soumitra Satapathi, Brijesh Kumar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Blue narrowband photodetectors are crucial for next-generation sensing applications such as imaging, health monitoring, and environmental detection. Outstanding optoelectronic characteristics and bandgap tunability make perovskites prime candidates for realizing narrowband photodetection. In this work, we fabricated a self-powered blue narrowband photodetector based on a mixed-cation perovskite (FA<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{{0}.{9}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>Cs<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{{0}.{1}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> PbI <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{{3}}\text {)}_{~ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>using a photon-field modulation approach. The detector exhibited a response peak at 490 nm, with a full-width at half-maximum of 77nm, along with a detectivity of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{{10}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> Jones. The detector also showed a rise time (t<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{r}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of 318 ms and a fall time (t<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{f}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of 286 ms under 490-nm illumination, and stable performance up to 38 days without encapsulation. This study demonstrates stable blue narrowband photodetection suitable for low-power integration into imaging and optoelectronic systems.

title: From Pairwise to Higher-Order Interactions: Stability Switching of Fractional-Order BAM Neural Networks
authors: Jiajin He, Yang Liu, Min Xiao, Peng Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: When modeled as complex networked dynamical systems, conventional neural networks, which predominantly rely on pairwise (first-order) interactions, often prove inadequate in capturing complex group dynamics. As these systems are extended to incorporate higher-order interactions, their dynamical behaviors become increasingly intricate. Consequently, within the scope of systems engineering, analyzing the stability boundaries is crucial for ensuring operational reliability. This article investigates the stability switching induced by bifurcation in a fractional-order bidirectional associative memory (BAM) neural network system with higher-order interactions. First, a generalized system is formulated, featuring a complete two-layer structure comprising <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n+m$</tex-math> </inline-formula> neurons. Analytical conditions for local stability and the onset of Hopf bifurcation are derived, which are applicable to large-scale networked systems of arbitrary size. Numerical simulations validate the theoretical results and reveal rich dynamical behaviors, demonstrating that system stability and bifurcation are critically influenced by time delay, higher-order interaction strength, fractional order, and network scale. A key finding is that the proper incorporation of weak higher-order interactions can raise the bifurcation threshold and suppress oscillation amplitudes, providing theoretical guidelines for the design and optimization of complex networked systems.

title: Wireless Enabled Situationally Aware Robot Collaboration in Industrial Environments
authors: Alexander Raab, Wolfgang Pointner, Damir Hamidovic, Hans-Peter Bernhard
publish: 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Factory Communication Systems (WFCS)
abstract: Dynamic factory floors require autonomous mobile robot fleets to operate efficiently and reliably in a coordinated fashion. Therefore, this paper presents a decentralized coordination concept that combines local perception with selective, structured information exchange among robots, infrastructure, and supervisory systems. By maintaining a shared situational view, the approach supports adaptive navigation and coordinated task execution while keeping communication overhead low. A live demonstration further illustrates integration with an external dashboard for monitoring and control. The results emphasize the importance of reliable wireless communication as a key enabler for scalable and resilient smart factory operations.

title: A Strategic Assessment Framework for Digital Maturity in Government-funded Research Institutes
authors: Jinho Choi, Su Han Lee, Jung Su Kim, Jong In Moon, Kyoeng Yoen Min, Nina Shin
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In this study, we developed a quantitative assessment model that measures the digital maturity of South Korea’s government-funded research institutes (GRIs) and provide practical guidance for advancing digital transformation in the public sector. We proposed a digital maturity assessment (DMA) model tailored for GRIs and demonstrated its enhancement through implementation in real-world public sector cases. We employed a Design Science Research (DSR) approach to develop and validate a DMA model for GRIs, which was initially derived from an interview-based qualitative study. The resulting GRI-specific model incorporates six core dimensions: strategy, organization, operations, users, technology, and data. The proposed model was applied to 122 respondents from 21 institutes. To justify the aggregation of individual responses to the institutional level, we validated the consensus among respondents using the within-group agreement index rWG(J), which yielded a high average value of 0.85. The results indicate that the overall digital maturity of the GRIs remains at the “Emerging” level, with notable strengths in user engagement and data usage. However, there are critical gaps in strategy, organization, and technology. The proposed DMA model serves as a robust tool for benchmarking digital capabilities and provides a foundation for customized roadmaps, governance, and policy alignment to advance transformation.

title: SPARK- Speech Pattern Analysis for Recognizing Parkinson’s Disease
authors: Mahe Mubeen Akhtar D, Jahnavi U, Ilaaram, Kanthashree S N, Manisha N
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Early diagnosis of Parkinson's disease (PD), a chronic neurodegenerative disorder, can improve patient care and allow for the utilization of disease-modifying treatments. Speech degradation is also one of the quickest detectable mundane symptoms of PD and provides a viable means of detection that is non-invasive. In this paper, The SPARK (Speech Pattern Analysis for Recognizing Parkinson's disease), a novel machine learning based framework to detect PD from speech signals. Mel Frequency Coefficient Cepstral (MFCC) are extracted to represent discriminative vocal cues. Several classifiers are employed and tested such as different kernels of Support Vector Machine (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbour (KNN), Random Forest and XG boost. For enhancing stability and decreasing over fitting, ensemble methods such as voting and stacking are applied. Experimental results show that these stacking generalization ensemble models outperform all other algorithms and obtain a best accuracy of 91.23% with balanced precision and recall. The entire system is then implemented and an interface is designed using Flask such that voice can be analyzed at any time for easy screening. These results demonstrate the promise of speech-based ensemble learning as an inexpensive and scalable clinical decision support instrument for early Parkinson’s disease detection.

title: A fusion method of GNSS-IR sea surface height using the Carrier and SNR
authors: Fei Yang, Weicong Chen, Ruixian Hao, Yao Du, Zhicai Li, Sichen Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Sea surface height (SSH) can be retrieved from reflected signals of Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) using the interferometric reflectometry technique, in which the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and the carrier phase could be utilized for this inversion. How to combine them to improve the accuracy of the SSH inversion remains a key research focus, therefore, this study proposes a Bayesian-based multi-source data fusion framework that effectively combines SNR and dual-frequency carrier phase. The experiment was conducted at station AT01 in Alaska, USA, using SNR signals of S5Q and S7Q and carrier phase of L5 and L7Q from the Galileo satellite navigation system. Three types of combinations were employed in the Bayesian fusion, including Fused-1 combining S5Q and L5Q-L7Q, Fused-2 combining S7Q and L5Q-L7Q, and Fused-3 combining S5Q, S7Q and L5Q-L7Q. Numerical results show that single inversions with S5Q, S7Q, and L5Q–L7Q yield RMSEs of 19.83, 19.45, and 19.50 cm, respectively, while the proposed fusion method greatly improves accuracy. Specifically, the RMSE are 10.79, 11.29, and 9.23 cm for Fused-1, Fused-2 and Fused-3, respectively. The residual and Bland-Altman analyses also show that the proposed fusion method has a better performance in terms of consistency and bias. The statistics of daily RMSE show that the proposed fusion method can effectively solve the problem of large fluctuations in single signal inversion, and can stabilize the fluctuation between 5 and 15cm. Moreover, the proposed fusion method still achieved good results in special scenarios of flood and ebb tide. Experiments at the SC02 station demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method even when the single inversion already achieves high accuracy.

title: Enhancing Torque Response During Overmodulation via Dynamic Flux-Trajectory Optimization in DTC-SVM SPMSM Drives
authors: Xin Luo, Peihe Li, Qipeng Tang, Pan Luo, Yongqi Wang, Anwen Shen, Jinbang Xu
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Direct-torque-control cooperating with space-vector-modulation (DTC-SVM) are attracting lots of attentions in surface-mounted permanent magnet synchronous motor (SPMSM) drives due to excellent dynamic performance. However, its dynamic performance may deteriorate in overmodulation region because several switching periods may be spent to achieve the desired torque that is physically infeasible in one step when operating near the voltage limit. Although conventional overmodulation methods have considered the voltage hexagon as the limit of the output voltage to maximize the voltage utilization, they don’t focus on the whole torque dynamic response over multiple periods. Thus, a torque response enhancement strategy based on dynamic flux-trajectory optimization is proposed in this paper. The proposed strategy programs a dynamic stator-flux optimization trajectory based on the minimum distance between the initial and desired flux/torque states, which can effectively enhance the torque response to fast transients throughout the entire overmodulation operation. Furthermore, a binary iterative searching algorithm is presented at voltage and current limits, which can not only determine the optimal transition time and corresponding applied voltage vector, but also significantly reduce the computational intensity of dynamic trajectory searching. Finally, experimental results verify the effectiveness and feasibility of proposed torque response enhancement scheme.

title: Remote Payment-Triggered Fast Power Restoration Mechanism: A Real-Time Data Collection Approach
authors: Jing Zhang, Xiaoruo Chen, Dandan Qi, Shan Cha
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: Due to the physical isolation between the payment status of power users and the power supply network, how to achieve millisecond level fault identification and closed-loop power restoration control through data fusion technology while ensuring real-time and accurate data has become the core technical bottleneck faced by the rapid power restoration system in remote payment mode. Therefore, a fast power restoration technology based on data collection under remote payment is proposed. Build a multi-channel collaborative data collection network and secure authentication message mechanism, and achieve accurate recognition of payment status through multi-dimensional data classification algorithms and redundant processing technology. By combining deep first state detection with distributed parallel computing, a dynamic restoration decision model based on node voltage constraints and power flow is established to achieve fast restoration. The experimental results show that the proposed scheme has demonstrated excellent performance in various tests, with a high success rate of power restoration and accuracy in identifying abnormal operating conditions. Moreover, the security authentication message mechanism maintains low latency even under high-frequency data streams, ensuring real-time performance in rapid power restoration control.

title: Defect Detection in Space Craft and Launch Vehicle Sensors
authors: Likitha M, Kumkum A, Ambika, Krishna Kumar P R, Usha Desai
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Reliable sensor operation is indispensable for safety and success of spacecraft and launch vehicle missions. The Liquid Propulsion Systems Centre (LPSC–B) of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), which deploys pressure transducers, thermocouples, accelerometers, and flow meters within liquid propulsion stages to monitor critical parameters throughout ground testing and flight. Sensor defects ranging from gradual bias drift to complete dropout can produce erroneous telemetry that compromises control decisions and jeopardizes mission safety. This paper presents a machine learning (ML)-based defect detection framework for spacecraft and launch vehicle sensors, developed at LPSC–B, ISRO. A Random Forest ensemble classifier is trained on eight time-domain and frequency-domain features extracted via a sliding window from raw sensor telemetry to detect five fault categories: Normal, Bias Drift, Fixed-Value Fault, Noise Spike, and Sensor Dropout. Evaluated on a 90,000-sample dataset of real LPSC telemetry and fault-injected simulation data, the system achieves 96.4% accuracy and macro F1-score of 0.963, outperforming Decision Tree, Naïve Bayes, KNN, and SVM baselines. ROC-AUC scores exceed 0.98 for all classes. The system is deployed as a real-time monitoring plug-in within LPSC ground support equipment achieving sub-50 msec inference latency.

title: Skin Abnormality Detection from Dermoscopy Images with Deep Learning Architecture
authors: Thejadharsan Thiyagaraja Nagendaran, Ramesh Kumar Sivagurunathan, T. Babu, Muhammad Attique Khan, S. Prabha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Cancer is a harsh disease and needs appropriate detection and management. Skin-cancer (Sc) is mainly due to frequent exposure of the skin to sun and its condition needs to be detected and trusted early. Dermoscpy is widely adopted clinically to analyse SC and manual examination is complex and time consuming. Hence, this research proposed deep-learning (DL) tool for analysing benign/malignant SC. This work considered 1500 images for the evaluation and the outcome achieved is evaluated to confirm the merit of the developed system. Initially, individual features based evaluation is performed using 1x1x1000 features with softmax classifier and it achieved a result close to 90%. Later, fused-feature is generated and the investigation is repeated with softmax. Proposed tool help to get >99% accuracy during benign/malignant SC detection process. The result of this study confirms that the developed system provides a significant result with the considered image database.

title: A Learning-based Framework for Hierarchical Encryption and Collaborative Verification of Sensitive Contract Content
authors: Wu Hong
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: With the widespread application of the digital contracts across organizations and platforms, contract content exhibits significant hierarchical differences in semantic sensitivity, access permissions, and collaborative verification. Therefore, addressing the problem that existing encryption schemes generally employ a "peer protection, peer access" strategy, which struggles to balance security and system efficiency, this paper proposes a learning-driven hierarchical encryption and collaborative verification unified framework (LB-HECV). This framework enables refined protection and multiparty trusted verification of sensitive contract content. This framework is based on lattice cryptography. By constructing nested lattice structures, it achieves a strict mapping relationship between different semantic levels of contracts and decryption difficulty at the cryptographic level. Simultaneously, this research introduces a deep learning model to perform semantic embedding modeling of the contract text. This model utilizes semantic features to drive dynamic key perturbation and adaptive noise adjustment. This innovation can enhance the attack complexity of high-sensitivity levels while satisfying decryptability constraints. In the collaborative verification stage, this paper constructs a multi-party consistency constraint model. Furthermore, the model incorporates game theory methods to analyze the consistency stability of the system under the condition that rationally deviating participants exist. Experimental results show that, at the high-sensitivity layer (L3), the proposed method maintains a decryption success rate above 98%. Numerically, the ciphertext information entropy fluctuation range is controlled within ±0.006, and the Runs Test pass rate reaches 98.7%. Under the BKZ-β model, the learningdriven perturbation mechanism effectively improves the security strength of the high-level equivalent SVP. Even with the introduction of 20% validator deviation behavior, system consistency remains at 74.9%.

title: Intelligent Code Analysis and Automated Testing Technologies for Power Grid Software Systems
authors: Jingyi Zhao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: Power grid software used in dispatching, substation monitoring, metering, and distribution management must satisfy stronger safety, traceability, and release-governance requirements than generic enterprise applications. Although recent studies on Large Language Model (lLM)-assisted code review and automated test generation have improved developer productivity, their direct adoption in grid software remains limited by weak domain grounding, inadequate risk prioritization, and insufficient coupling between review evidence and Continuous Integration (CI) / Continuous Delivery (CD) execution. This paper proposes a lifecycle-aware intelligent engineering framework for power grid software systems. A bounded re-trieval-augmented semantic review module first extracts structured change intent from commits, requirement notes, and defect tickets, then retrieves grid rules, historical incidents, and CI evidence, and finally invokes a locally deployed code LLM only within a controlled context window to produce evidence-linked review comments and test intents. A multi-source risk model then integrates static severity, historical defect density, semantic change scope, business criticality, and test-gap signals to allocate validation budget and trigger staged execution. Generated tests are retained only after deterministic compilation, oracle checking, and sandbox validation, while high-risk changes additionally activate digital-twin replay. Experiments on $\mathbf{1 8 6}$ change sets from 12 grid-related repositories show that precision improves from 0.74 to 0.87, recall from 0.68 to 0.82, and effective coverage from 11% to 29%, while median review time is reduced by 34%. Paired statistical tests confirm that the improvements in precision, recall, and effective coverage are significant ($\mathbf{p}\lt0.01$).

title: World Navigation Hat: Development of a Wearable Navigation Aid Using Aiot for the Visually Impaired
authors: Jason C. D'Souza, Ethel Herna C. Pabito, ChenLin Wang, Glenda Guanzon, Adha Theza R. Naputol, Vince Ginno B. Daywan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: Over 2.2 billion people worldwide suffer from visual impairment. Conventional sensory substitution systems are often ineffective, providing either too little navigational information or causing sensory overload. To overcome this limitation, this study introduces STESIS, an AIoT wearable navigation hat powered by a Raspberry Pi 4 and dual stereo cameras. STESIS upgrades a baseline proximity algorithm into a multi-pinging system, dividing the environment into overlapping spatial bins (left, center, right). To prevent sensory flooding, auditory feedback is systematically organized using a Time-Division Multiplexing (TDM) sequencer. Testing on 40 blindfolded participants and one blind individual demonstrated a sub- 100 ms processing latency and yielded a 25.0% improvement in multi-obstacle identification over the baseline system. While mental demand slightly increased, the structured TDM sonification eliminated the need for continuous head-scanning, drastically reducing physical demand and frustration, and ultimately decreasing the overall cognitive workload by 27.8%. STESIS confirms that time-sequenced spatial sonification successfully prevents sensory masking, providing a low-fatigue method for environmental awareness. Furthermore, its modular framework supports future multimodal sensor fusion, advancing assistive technology initiatives.

title: Precise Indoor Positioning Using Hybrid Fine Time Measurements Over Radio and Light Waves
authors: Dennis Goodson, Christoph Kottke, Volker Jungnickel
publish: 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Factory Communication Systems (WFCS)
abstract: Precise indoor localization is a sought-after feature in industrial networks. For in-building applications in robotics and automation, currently, precision is limited to the meter range by multi-path scattering and penetration through objects and walls. Light communication, also denoted as LiFi, uses light waves as a new wireless medium for which propagation is based on the line-of-sight. For precise positioning, we apply distance measurements according to IEEE 802.11 fine-time measurements over LiFi and conduct experiments in typical indoor scenarios. We demonstrate that centimeter precision can be reached using LiFi and observe that the multiple signal representation (MUSIC) algorithm is more robust at all distances compared to a previously used linear regression approach in the phase-frequency response. To overcome coverage limitations of LiFi, we propose a hybrid approach, using fine-time measurements at radio frequencies for coarse positioning everywhere in the factory hall and the same method adapted to light waves in regions of interest where the precise positioning is actually needed.

title: Mechanism Analysis and Control Technology for Dot Enlargement and Registration Accuracy in Flexographic Printing Systems
authors: Zhenhai Song
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: This study addresses two basic quality issues typically encountered in flexographic printing: dot gain and registration mistake. It looks into these problems from the viewpoints of collaborative control and mechanistic modeling. The study methodically creates a multi-scale unified modeling and adaptive control framework. Mesoscopic contact mechanics and ink supply dynamics, macroscopic tension coupling and transmission errors, and microscopic ink layer rheology and interface wetting are all modeled. A unified dot gain model is created, incorporating physical diffusion, substrate absorption, and optical scattering effects. This model is then integrated with a strongly coupled registration error dynamic model of multi-color units. Furthermore, this study proposes a collaborative control method that integrates physical prediction compensation, adaptive parameter updating, and visual intelligent recognition. This method achieves bidirectional coupling suppression of dot gain and registration error. By introducing model feedforward compensation terms and an online parameter identification mechanism, the control structure effectively improves the system's stability and accuracy under various disturbances and operating conditions. Systematic experimental verification was conducted on an industrial-grade six-color flexographic printing platform. Experimental results show that, under different substrate and process parameters, the proposed unified dot gain model reduces the prediction error in the mid-tone region by 38.6%–54.2% compared to the traditional Murray–Davies model. Under speed fluctuation and tension disturbance conditions, the steady-state mean square error of the proposed control strategy is significantly lower than that of traditional PID and robust control methods. The dynamic prediction accuracy of registration error is improved by an average of approximately 41.5%, and both steady-state RMS error and maximum instantaneous error are significantly suppressed. Under coupled disturbance scenarios, the system's combined energy function value for both types of errors decreases significantly. A 72-hour continuous production run test shows that the dot gain pass rate reaches 97.8%, and the registration error pass rate reaches 98.6%.

title: Multimodal Video Summarization with Modality-Attention Fusion and Hierarchical Transformer Reasoning
authors: Nidhi Passi, Nitin Arvind Shelke
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The accessibility of faster and cheaper internet and the popularity of digital content have drastically shorten the gap of data availability in multimedia formats. Further, the ease of human understanding of visual sources based on cognitive theory has further accelerated the creation of digital content. This study proposes a unified framework that adapts preprocessing, feature extraction and multimodal fusion strategies to the specific characteristics of different video types. The proposed framework introduces a modular video summarization pipeline that combines transformer based visual encoding, vision language captioning with BLIP and ASR-based audio transcription, allowing flexible integration of multimodal cues across varied video types. The architecture further introduces a modality-attention fusion module and a hierarchical local-global Transformer to capture both short-range event dynamics and long-range narrative structure. Experiments on the SumMe and TVSum datasets demonstrate consistent improvements over classical LSTM- and GAN-based baselines, validating the benefit of multimodal semantic cues. The objective of our work is to identify optimal architectural choices that enhance the accuracy and generalization of video summarization across varying structural and semantic characteristics widely available.

title: AI Enabled Industrial Internet of Things (AI-IIoT) Systems: An Overview
authors: S. Vishnu, Vishnuvarthan Rajagopal, A. Alfred Kirubaraj, Praveena Sirimella, Adnan M. Abu-Mahfouz, S. R. Jino Ramson
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: The Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) plays a key role in transforming the traditional industrial infrastructure into a smart integrated framework through the amalgamation of physical entities and virtual entities. The integration of AI into the IIoT systems opened a new paradigm named AI-IIoT that enhanced the performance of IIoT systems through data centric insights, prognosis, adaptive actuation, and context aware-automation. However, there are many critical challenges that prevents the large scale adoption of the AI-IIoT systems. This paper presents an overview of AI-IIoT systems focusing on its architecture, applications, challenges and future directions.

title: A Reconfigurable IEEE-754 FP32 Fused Multiply Accumulate Architecture with Hybrid Multiplier and Prefix Adder Optimization
authors: Krishnananda K R, Subramanya G. Nayak
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The proposed work presents the design, verification, and full RTL-to-GDSII implementation of a single-precision fused multiply-accumulate (FMA) unit that can be changed or reconfigured and follows the IEEE-754 standard. The unit is defined by y = a × b + c. The design incorporates selectable multiplier and adder architectures, such as Booth + CSA, Array, and Vedic hybrid multipliers with Kogge-Stone and Han-Carlson adders. It also has an optional approximate arithmetic mode to minimize dynamic power. The proposed five-stage pipelined datapath delivers high throughput with a balanced latency profile while keeping IEEE-754 rounding accuracy and special-case handling. Using Cadence tools, we carried out functional and physical verification, which showed that the power and area were being used efficiently. Using Cadence Genus-Innovus flow, the design was tested in a 90 nm CMOS technology node. It worked at a maximum frequency of 175 MHz with positive slack and a total power of 33.55 mW in a layout footprint of 48,197 μm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. A comparative evaluation shows that the reconfigurable architecture has a better balance of performance, power, and flexibility than traditional fixed datapaths. The proposed work addresses Sustainable Developmental Goals 4 and 9.

title: Demonstration of Device Discovery on Device-Side 5G-TSN Bridge Translator
authors: Christopher Lehmann, Dominik Welte, Manuel Schappacher, Axel Sikora, Thomas H৶schele, Frank H. P. Fitzek
publish: 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Factory Communication Systems (WFCS)
abstract: The integration of Time-Sensitive Networking (TSN) and private 5G systems enables flexible and deterministic communication for industrial applications. While core 5G-TSN bridge integration mechanisms have been widely discussed, the practical realization of internal bridge management procedures remain a key implementation challenge. This demo paper presents an implementation-focused demonstration of a standard-compatible device discovery workflow for a 5G-TSN bridge, concentrating on device attachment to the Ethernet port of the Device-Side TSN Translator (DS-TT). The architecture follows a YANG modelbased design and reuses the reference components introduced in prior work, including a Port- and Bridge Management Handler (PBMH), PMIC Parser and Generators (PPGs), local YANGbacked datastore, and NETCONF-based access. The NetworkSide TSN Translator (NW-TT) is explicitly out of scope for this demonstration. Particular emphasis is placed on the transport of Port Management Information Containers (PMICs) through 3GPP-specified 5G Core mechanisms, including NAS encapsulation, SMF processing, interaction between PCF and TSN Application Function (TSN AF), and acknowledgement handling. The demonstrated workflow shows how neighbor information advertised through Link Layer Discovery Protocol (LLDP) can be detected at the DS-TT, converted into PMIC-based management notifications, and made available to the TSN AF through the 5G system in a standards-aligned manner.

title: Real-Time Mobile Object Recognition for Semi-Autonomous Driving Assistance Using YOLO
authors: Woan Ning Lim, Kelvin Chuen Zhuan Chua, Muhammed Basheer Jasser, Hui Na Chua, Ghassan Saleh ALDharhani
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: Semi-autonomous driving systems require reliable perception capabilities to enhance road safety and driver situational awareness. This paper presents the design, development, and evaluation of a mobile-based object recognition system intended to support semi-autonomous driving applications by detecting critical road objects, including vehicles, traffic lights, and pedestrians. The proposed system leverages the YOLOv8 deep learning architecture and is trained on a curated and balanced dataset compiled from multiple open-source repositories. Extensive experiments are conducted to analyze the impact of key training configurations, including epochs, warmup epochs, dataset splitting ratios, optimizers, learning rates, model variants, and continued fine-tuning strategies. The optimized YOLOv8-Small model is deployed within a mobile application using TensorFlow Lite, enabling real-time inference on resource-constrained devices. Experimental results demonstrate strong detection performance, achieving a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 0.908 at an IoU threshold of 0.5. The findings confirm the feasibility and effectiveness of mobile-based deep learning perception systems as a cost-effective alternative for enhancing road safety in semi-autonomous driving scenarios.

title: Blockchain Driven Supply Security
authors: Nitin Kumar Thakur, Avinash Maurya, Varun Kumar, Parma Nand, Rani Astya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The current paper introduces a blockchain-based supply chain management system that will improve the level of data integrity, authentication, and cybersecurity in the contemporary logistics processes. Decentralized structure is made through combining a web-based application to the Ethereum blockchain with the help of Ganache, Metamask, and Solidity smart contracts. The system can record the supply chain events in a secure manner and provide tamper-proof traceability and can validate the transactions automatically without involving centralized intermediaries. The effect of the proposed model on the results of the experiment shows that the model enhances transparency, minimizes the risks of manipulating data, and builds trust among the stakeholders in comparison with the traditional supply chain systems. One of the practical blueprints of implementation offered by the study based on the literature findings is the future development of the blockchain-driven supply chain security solutions.

title: Machine Learning Approaches for Student Effort Optimisation in Digital Learning Systems
authors: Olga Ovtšarenko, Tarvo Mill
publish: 2026 Open Conference of Electrical, Electronic and Information Sciences (eStream)
abstract: Student learning effectiveness relies not only on the time dedicated to studying but also on how students regulate their cognitive effort, respond to task complexity, and manage academic pressure. Increasing academic workloads in higher education have resulted in a rise in perceived procrastination, which often causes inefficient effort distribution and lower academic performance. Recent advances in computing, learning analytics, and artificial intelligence (AI) present new opportunities to analyse and support effort regulation on a large scale. This study investigates the feasibility of predicting student dropout risk using AI and the Moodle platform learning environment, based on activity recordings, to explore ways to optimise student engagement and minimise dropout risk. Effective effort regulation is closely associated with student motivation and their ability to interpret performance information. The findings of this study emphasise the importance of incorporating intelligent computing systems into the design and adaptive implementation of curricula to support student effort, reduce procrastination, and enhance academic outcomes.

title: Majdoor Digital Trust-Ledger (MDTL): A Decentralized Framework for Bridging Informal Labor to Formal Economic Inclusion
authors: Shivam Sharma, Sakshi Singh, Manish Pal, Sumit Paswan, Kamna Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Millions of informal workers are able to work in the economy without the ability to be subjected to formal fiscal recognition or empirical employment history. The Majdoor Digital Trust-Ledger (MDTL) is a decentralized and privacy-sensitive framework according to which evidence of informal labor is converted into self-sovereign digital assets. MDTL combines Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs), Verifiable Credentials (VCs), escrows based on smart contracts, and Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs) to implement verifiable, but confidential proof-of-work. The system proposes a 5-layer architecture, i.e. Identity, Credential, Ledger, Privacy, and Verification, which is designed to be scalable, interoperable, and it can be run on resource-constrained deployment. MDTL progresses the agency of workers by creating portable and tamper-evident labor records to allow financial inclusion without degrading privacy.

title: Wireless Smart Stent for Real-Time In-Stent Pressure Monitoring and Endoleak Detection
authors: Jun Ying Tan, Subhrodeep Ray, Haijun Liu, Albert Kim, Jungkwun JK Kim
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This paper presents an implantable smart stent system for continuous in-stent blood pressure monitoring aimed at post–endovascular aneurysm repair (EVAR) surveillance. The proposed system employs a cylindrical piezoelectric polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF) membrane that functions simultaneously as a pressure sensor and mechanical stent structure. The PVDF membrane is engineered with a perforated design to enhance pressure sensitivity. An active 433 MHz wireless transmitter module, implemented on a custom flexible printed circuit board, is integrated with the stent to enable real-time wireless pressure data transmission. The system is evaluated using an in vitro physical model of an abdominal aortic aneurysm (AAA). Experimental results demonstrate a wireless interrogation range of up to 100 cm, with an average diastolic pressure sensitivity of 3.35 mV/mmHg and a pulse pressure sensitivity ranging from 0.37 to 0.45 mV/mmHg. In addition, proof-of-concept endoleak conditions simulated in the physical model produce distinct voltage signatures, indicating the capability of the proposed smart stent to differentiate endoleak scenarios. These results demonstrate the feasibility of an actively powered, wireless smart stent as a sensing platform for post-EVAR pressure monitoring.

title: Toward Efficient Smart Waste Management: ResNet50 based Transfer Learning for Imbalanced Garbage Classification
authors: Narinder Kaur, Nisha Jaswal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The swift escalation of waste production globally poses a significant challenge to both the economy and the environment. To address this issue, we require effective and scalable techniques for automating waste classification. This study examines the application of deep learning, namely the ResNet50 architecture, for imbalanced trash classification. Two methodologies were evaluated: (i) a baseline ResNet50 model constructed from the ground up with random weight initialization, and (ii) a transfer learning approach employing ImageNet pretrained weights with fine-tuned layers. We utilized a discarded photographic collection of 2,527 samples categorized into six groups: cardboard, glass, metal, paper, plastic, and refuse. To enhance generalization, we employed preprocessing techniques including normalization, augmentation, and stratified partitioning. To rectify the class imbalance, weighted loss functions were employed, facilitating the identification of the minority class. The experimental findings indicate that the transfer learning model surpasses the baseline performance. The overall accuracy is 95%, which is better than 88%. There have been big improvements in precision, recall, and F1-scores across all categories, but especially in the underrepresented garbage class. The results show that transfer learning works well in real-world waste management situations since it speeds up convergence, cuts down on training time, and improves classification accuracy. This study shows that intelligent trash sorting systems that use deep learning can help the environment and improve recycling efforts.

title: Energy-Efficient Hybrid Cache Memory Design for Edge-Based AI Inference Using SRAM and ReRAM
authors: Navaneeth Krishnan, Ram Krishna, Rajesh Kannan Megalingam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Edge devices are becoming more burdened with the task of executing AI inference in real-time, although they have limited energy and efficient computing capabilities. The memory access and data movement is a very important part of the overall energy consumption and effective memory design is an important factor in development of low-power, high-performance AI applications. This Paper offers the design and simulation of an energy-efficient hybrid cache memory system based on SRAM and ReRAM energy-efficient edge-based AI inference. The hybrid architecture employs the high-speed access speed of SRAM to the data required to be accessed frequently, and the non-volatility and low leakage power of ReRAM to less frequently used data. The entire system was coded in Verilog HDL and several verification of functionality and performance was conducted in ModelSim at a hardware level. It incorporates SRAM and ReRAM cache banks in the architecture, an LRU replacement unit, and frequency-based data placement mechanism to provide energy and access latency optimization. The results of the simulation are proven to be the correct work of a cache with the overall hit rate of 63.33% and the decreased energy use in comparison with the DRAM-only case. The experimental results of an analytical estimation of energy consumption show that in 60 accesses the hybrid cache uses a total of 223.8 pJ of energy, which shows that the hybrid cache has the potential of being used in a low-power high-performance edge computing system.

title: Emoception: Selective Affective Layer Fine-Tuning of Video Vision Transformers for Player Arousal Change Recognition from Gameplay Footage
authors: Yi Xia, Ibrahim Khan, Mury Fajar Dewantoro, Wenwen Ouyang, Ruck Thawonmas
publish: IEEE Transactions on Games
abstract: This paper proposes Selective Affective Layer Fine-Tuning (SALFT), an efficient adaptation framework for Video Vision Transformers in player arousal recognition from gameplay. To bypass computationally expensive full fine-tuning, SALFT introduces a selection criterion based on the L2-norm change in layer parameters after brief adaptation, directly measuring representational shifts and providing a more stable basis than gradient-based alternatives. Evaluated via five-fold cross-validation on the Arousal Video Game AnnotatIoN dataset, SALFT achieves performance comparable to full fine-tuning across all games without statistically significant degradation (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$p > 0.05$</tex-math></inline-formula>), while updating only <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\approx$</tex-math></inline-formula>8% of parameters (over 92% reduction). Notably, in one game, SALFT consistently outperforms both full fine-tuning and the best baseline across all metrics and folds, reaching the theoretical minimum <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$p$</tex-math></inline-formula>-value (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$p=0.0625$</tex-math></inline-formula>, exact two-sided Wilcoxon signed-rank test). Additionally, we introduce an interpretability method to trace attention patterns, enhancing model transparency. These results establish SALFT as an effective and efficient approach for affective game computing. GitHub repo for code, data, and supplementary materials: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/Yi-Xia-2010/Emoception-SALFT</uri>

title: Machine Learning with Hybrid Quantum-Classical Approaches: Exploring the Limits of Quantum Computing in Artificial Intelligence
authors: Mosses A, Abdelhalim Mohammad Jubran, Sorabh Lakhanpal, R. Babitha Lincy, Veena K, Guma Ali
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The entwining of quantum computing with classical machine learning has proven to be one of the most promising frontiers of artificial intelligence, as it possesses the potential to speed up intricate computations in ways that classical systems will never be able to achieve. This study delves into the theoretical and practical implications of hybrid quantum-classical approaches in machine learning, while also examining their advantages, limitations, and applicability in different domains. Utilizing quantum entanglement and parallelism, hybrid solutions also have the potential to process high-dimensional data, optimize complex functions, and facilitate pattern recognition tasks more efficiently. However, noise, decoherence, and limited qubit connectivity on current hardware pose considerable barriers to practical implementations. The authors discuss hybrid architectures like quantum-enhanced optimizers and variational quantum circuits which include classical pre- and post-processing to alleviate quantum limitations. Moreover, a comparative study is performed to show the effectiveness of hybrid models comparing with classical machine learning state-of-the-art in natural language processing, computer vision and financial modeling. Results indicate that although hybrid quantum–classical approaches provide significant gains in optimization and computational efficiency; these benefits remain limited by hardware limitations and the scalability of quantum algorithms. With these considerations in mind, the paper offers guidance for targeting future research directions in quantum machine learning, including advances in quantum error correction, quantum hardware scalability, and new algorithmic approaches. Additional advancements needed and notably, the study focused on a smaller representation of data Since quantum computing is relatively new and researchers are still exploring its application, it is still early days in terms of machine learning models that are solely, intrinsically quantum.

title: AoI-Constrained Integrated Communication and Control for VLC-Based UAV Formations with Interference Mitigation
authors: Ruifeng Chen, Fang Yang, Nan An, Jintao Wang, Zhu Han
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: In this letter, integrated communication and control for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) formations enabled by visible light communication is investigated. A three-dimensionally decoupled distributed linear controller for leader–follower formation keeping under multi-hop motion status information exchange is designed. To explicitly capture information freshness, the age of information (AoI) is incorporated into a control-oriented stability analysis, through which the admissible AoI threshold is characterized by tractable stability conditions in the form of solvable linear matrix inequalities. Accordingly, a freshness disparity policy is developed to satisfy the specified AoI constraint. Moreover, frequency-division multiplexing is employed to mitigate co-channel interference and meet the target signal-to-interference-ratio requirement. Finally, numerical results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves stable formation keeping.

title: A Federated Learning Framework for Early Detection of Osteonecrosis of the Jaw using Dental Imaging
authors: S. Murugeswari, K. Muthulakshmi, J. Relin Francis Raj, S.T. Bharathi, D. Yamini, S. Balamurugan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Early detection of osteonecrosis of the jaw remains clinically challenging due to low disease prevalence, subtle radiographic manifestations, and fragmented availability of diagnostic imaging data across institutions. Existing diagnostic systems operate within isolated clinical environments, resulting in limited exposure to rare pathological patterns and reduced generalization capability. Severe class imbalance, heterogeneity in imaging protocols, and strict privacy regulations further restrict centralized data utilization, impeding large-scale analysis and collaborative learning for rare maxillofacial disorders. To address these challenges, a privacy-preserving federated learning framework is presented for early osteonecrosis of the jaw detection using distributed dental imaging data. The proposed methodology enables collaborative model training across multiple institutions while maintaining complete data locality, thereby overcoming regulatory and ethical barriers associated with centralized data sharing. Local preprocessing and training ensure adaptability to heterogeneous imaging sources, while parameter aggregation facilitates collective learning from geographically dispersed rare disease samples. This collaborative strategy improves sensitivity to early-stage necrotic bone changes that are often overlooked in conventional isolated systems. Key features of the proposed work include distributed data utilization without raw data exchange, robustness to non-identically distributed imaging environments, and improved diagnostic performance under data-scarce conditions. The framework effectively mitigates limitations arising from institutional data silos, enhances learning from rare cases, and supports reliable cross-site generalization. By combining privacy preservation with collaborative intelligence, the proposed approach establishes an efficient and scalable solution for rare disease detection in dental and maxillofacial imaging workflows.

title: Development and Comparative Validation of an Explainable AI-based Multisource Cancer Survival Prediction Model
authors: K. Ramakrishna Reddy, Shanker Chandre
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Accurate cancer survival prediction remains challenging due to heterogeneous patient profiles and fragmented clinical data sources. This study presents an explainable artificial intelligence framework for survival prediction using multisource cancer data integrating clinical variables, molecular profiles, and treatment records. The proposed model was developed and evaluated on 4,567 patient records aggregated from publicly available oncology repositories, comprising 1,284 mortality events and 3,283 right-censored cases. A multimodal deep survival architecture was trained and comparatively validated against Cox Proportional Hazards, Random Survival Forests, and DeepSurv models. The proposed approach achieved a concordance index of 0.7814, outperforming Cox Proportional Hazards (0.6942) and DeepSurv (0.7426), corresponding to 401 additional correctly ordered survival pairs in the validation cohort. Explainability analysis using SHAP identified 23 clinically consistent prognostic variables, enhancing model transparency. These results demonstrate that explainable multisource AI models can substantially improve survival prediction accuracy while maintaining clinical interpretability.

title: Development of an IoT + Centralized Control System for Hydropower, Wind-Solar Hybrid Power Plant Clusters and Multi-Protocol Unified Access Technology
authors: Youping Li, Xiaojun Hua, Liangliang Chen, Rui Lv, Lei Wang, Longjun Zou
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: The goal of “Double Carbon” is to manage hydropower, wind-solar hybrid clusters of power plants against the main development of renewable energy. However, existing systems suffer from three major problems: heterogeneous protocols (e.g., Modbus, IEC 61850 and DL/T 645), severe data strengths, and weak centralized control and coordination capabilities. This article first identifies the pain points in monitoring power plant clusters and protocol access, and then develops a four-layer management architecture for the centralized Internet of Things: “Imagination - Transmission - Platform - Application.” Finally, system performance is tested through experiments. The specific steps are as follows: 1) This paper completes the system architecture design and hardware selection; 2) It develops protocol partitioning, data normalization and gateway hardware to achieve multi-protocol compatibility; 3) it develops centralized control functions such as real-time monitoring and coordinated planning; 4) This paper tests a system in a 150MW cluster consisting of a medium-sized hydropower plant, two wind farms, and three solar power plants. Experimental results show that peaks of $15.3 \%, 14.8 \%$, and $13.9 \%$ were observed on 3, 7, and 10 days of testing. The coordinated reduction rate has remained consistently low, ranging from $2.3 \%$ to $5.2 \%$, with minimal fluctuations, effectively addressing the challenges of centralized control over multi-source renewable power plants.

title: Towards Scalable Parallel Verification of Multi-Robot Systems In ROS 2 Using Reduced Distributed State Space
authors: Sawsen Khlifa, Chiheb Ameur Abid, Asma Ben Letaifa, Belhassen Zouari
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: One of the persistent challenges in formally verifying distributed and concurrent systems is the combinatorial growth of the state space, a phenomenon known as the state explosion problem. The number of potential configurations for these systems increases significantly with scale and interconnection, rendering exhaustive analysis computationally costly or even infeasible. To address this problem, researchers have been resorting to modular design approaches that divide an intricate system into smaller, independently verifiable components. Based on an in-depth examination of the strengths and limitations of current works in the literature, we suggest parallelizing the construction of the Reduced Distributed State Space (RDSS), which is an abstract and condensed representation that leverages the subdivision of the system into separate modules. To this end, we propose a generalized distributed architecture that defines the roles of a central coordinator and multiple worker modules, along with their communication points for synchronizing the RDSS building. This architecture is then concretely implemented using the Robot Operating System (ROS 2) framework, which provides robust primitives such as topics and services for inter-process communication, making the distributed execution both scalable and maintainable. Our method ensures stuttering equivalence with the global system and enables the scalable verification of local LTL\X properties using each module’s metagraph, without the need to explore the full RDSS. This implementation lays the foundation for modular model checking in distributed environments. Its effectiveness is demonstrated through a cooperative patrolling case study, which serves not only to validate the correctness of the proposed algorithm but also to observe its distributed behavior through simulated executions.

title: Exploring the Impact of RIS-Scattering on NOMA-Enabled ISABC System
authors: Kollanandy Rojith, Raviteja Allu, Keshav Singh, Chih-Peng Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: This paper investigates the integration of a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-assisted backscattering radio frequency identification (RFID) tag, referred to as RIS-Scatter, within an integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) framework. This system enhances tag sensing, backscatter transmission, and multiuser communication by enabling a dual-functional base station (DFBS) to provide downlink services while supporting RIS-Scatter parameter estimation, with accuracy quantified by the Cram´er–Rao bound (CRB).
