To further improve spectral efficiency and support massive connectivity, non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) is employed, allowing multiple users and RIS-Scatter links to share the same time–frequency resources via power-domain multiplexing. The objective is to maximize the communication sum-rate while satisfying quality-of-service (QoS) requirements of both RIS-Scatter and user links, subject to transmit power, RIS reflection, NOMA, and CRB constraints. The resulting non-convex problem is solved using an iterative algorithm that integrates fractional programming (FP), majorization– minimization (MM), the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM), and semidefinite programming (SDP). Numerical results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed approach, demonstrate the performance gains achieved through NOMA, and reveal the influence of key system parameters, including DFBS antennas, transmit power, tag SINR, RIS elements, modulation efficiency, CRB threshold, and DFBS–RIS-Scatter distance.

title: Proceedings
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)

title: Explainable Employee Churn Prediction using SHAP-based Interpretability Analysis
authors: V. Murugan, Lakshmi Sowmya, J. A. Bhavya Reddy
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The problem of employee turnover is critical to organizations both in terms of operations and finance. In turn, such a requirement of human resource (HR) analysis as valid and reliable predictive models is of paramount importance in the modern context of contemporary human resource (HR) analysis. Even though machine learning models have demonstrated excellent predictive power regarding employee turnover, a lack of explainability renders them less desirable in real-life situations that involve the human resources department. The current paper presents a comparative study of the application of explainable machine learning models to predict employee turnover utilizing demographic, occupation-related, compensation variables, and employee satisfaction data in the human resource dataset of an organization. This paper discusses some predictive models, which are both explainable and inherently explainable, in the context of predictive accuracy and explainability. These diverse frameworks ensure that the various stakeholders within the HR sector are able to not only point out employees at risk of leaving their workplace but also the causes of employee attrition. Besides, the paper reflects the use of Responsible AI Guidelines through equity evaluation, bias testing in sensitive variables, and transparent results in the research. The performance of the models through experiments justifies their competencies in that the models can perform equally well with more desirable explanations for employee retention strategies.

title: EssentCell: Discovering Essential Evolutionary Relations in Noisy Single-Cell Data_supp1-3691222.pdf
authors: Brendan Mumey
publish: N/A
abstract: Single-cell sequencing (SCS) enables the study of tumor evolution at the resolution of a single cell. SCS data can be represented as a binary matrix, where the $ij$-th entry indicates whether cell $i$ has mutation $j$. There is a simple characterization of when the data is compatible with a perfect phylogeny based on the absence of a special “conflict” submatrix. In practice, SCS data are noisy, which raises the natural question of the minimum number of entries that must be flipped in the data matrix to make it conflict-free and thus compatible with a perfect phylogeny. Furthermore, the likelihood of a false positive is several orders of magnitude smaller than that of a false negative rate. We consider a variation of the minimum-flip problem parameterized by the number of false positives. Restricting the false positive rate to a small range, often multiple optimal solutions can arise. While previous work has focused on reconstructing a single optimal phylogenetic tree, we are interested in the relations that are present among all optimal solutions; we call such relations essential. In this work, we propose an efficient algorithm based on integer linear programming to determine the essential relation on the cells given an SCS data matrix. We test our tool, ${\sf EssentCell}$, on several data sets and discuss the results found.

title: Locality-Preserving Graph Laplacian Manifold Learning Based Model Predictive Control for Three-Phase Inverters
authors: Jianwu Zeng, Lizheng Cheng, Vincent Winstead, Cristian Garcia, Jose Rodriguez
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: This paper presents a model predictive control (MPC) strategy for three-phase inverters based on locality preserving projections (LPP). Unlike conventional machine learning (ML)–based MPC approaches that rely on predefined or high-dimensional input features, the proposed LPP-MPC automatically extracts compact, informative representations by preserving the data's intrinsic geometric structure. This dimensionality reduction enables fast linear control-law evaluation with computational complexity O(1), making the controller well-suited for real-time implementation. Experimental results demonstrate that the LPP-MPC achieves lower total harmonic distortion (THD) and reduced tracking error compared to quadratic-programming (QP) MPC under both linear and nonlinear load conditions, and the proposed controller maintains consistently lower THD throughout load transients than other methods such as two-degree-of-freedom (2DOF) MPC. Compared to existing model-free (MF) MPC and deep learning neural network (DLNN), the LPP-MPC has the lowest THD and root mean square error (RMSE) with the least computational time owing to its efficient linear structure and strong generalization capability.

title: 3D Printed All-Metal Huygens Antenna Array with Wide-Angle Scanning and Low Cross-Polarization Level
authors: Yuqi He, Wei Lin, Liang Hua Ye
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This communication proposes an all-metal Huygens antenna array with exceptional wide-angle scanning capability and ultralow cross-polarization level. This work represents the first demonstration of wide-angle scanning realized with a Huygens antenna configuration. The fundamental principles enabling wide-angle scanning are comprehensively analyzed, demonstrating the Huygens source approximates the ideal element pattern necessary for achieving scan-independent impedance characteristics. To validate this concept, a 1×7-element array (overall dimensions: 0.93λ₀ × 2.59λ₀ × 0.34λ₀ at 10.8 GHz) is developed. The prototype, fabricated via 3D metal printing technology, ensures structural robustness and cost efficiency. Measurement results reveal 2-dB gain drop from peak realized gain from -82° to +80° and 3-dB beam coverage spanning from an exceptional -95° to +93° range, while maintaining cross-polarization discrimination greater than 30 dB throughout the scanned range. This work establishes a new solution framework for high-reliability wireless system requiring ultrawide scanning of antenna array, e.g., wireless communication with moving objects.

title: A Distributed Surrogate-Assisted Evolutionary Algorithm for Heterogeneously Expensive Constrained Optimization
authors: Jin Wang, Xilu Wang, Handing Wang, Yaochu Jin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Evolutionary Computation
abstract: Real-world constrained optimization problems are often driven by separate expensive simulations to evaluate constraints, where the computational costs of different simulation tools vary significantly. We refer to such problems as heterogeneously expensive constrained optimization problems (HE-COPs). Many surrogate-assisted evolutionary algorithms have been developed to solve expensive constrained optimization problems, but these methods have not considered the heterogeneous property of expensive constraints. To address this issue, we propose an efficient distributed surrogate-assisted evolutionary algorithm, termed DSAEA. The proposed algorithm achieves an asynchronous parallel invocation of constraint simulations within a distributed framework via an asynchronous sampling mechanism. This mechanism adaptively allocates time slots based on the heterogeneous evaluation times of constraint simulations and evaluates the samples prioritizing the feasibility of more expensive constraints to mitigate search bias caused by uneven evaluation resources. An extended version, DSAEA-AUX, further improves the performance and robustness by incorporating an auxiliary computing worker agent. To facilitate fair assessment, an HE-COP benchmark test suite is proposed, which introduces heterogeneous evaluation time costs. Extensive experiments on this benchmark demonstrate the superiority of DSAEA and DSAEA-AUX, both algorithms achieve satisfactory performance within a limited time and outperform six state-of-the-art algorithms.

title: Aggregation Method for EV Clusters Based on Confidence-Robust Polytope Inner Approximation
authors: Hongpeng Liu, Yirui Sun, Wei Zhang, Jianwei Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: This paper proposes an electric vehicle (EV) cluster aggregation method utilizing confidence-robust polyhedral inner approximation to fully leverage the flexible scheduling potential of EV clusters, while addressing the challenges of inadequate robustness and the complexities of parameter heterogeneity associated with traditional polyhedral inner approximation methods. Initially, controllable EVs are discerned via controllability criteria, and the FCM algorithm is utilized to cluster these controllable EVs, employing polyhedra to delineate their viable regions. Subsequently, dynamic tuning parameters are incorporated to create confidence-robust polyhedra, and an inner approximation optimization model that accounts for parameter uncertainty is established to achieve high-precision approximate viable regions. The Lagrangian duality theorem and Chebyshev inequality are employed to streamline and resolve the inner approximation model, thus deriving the comprehensive aggregated feasible region of the EV cluster. The results indicate that, in contrast to conventional polyhedral inner approximation methods, the confidence-robust polyhedron enhances both robustness during the inner approximation process and the accuracy of the feasible region. Additionally, the simplified inner approximation model markedly improves computational efficiency, offering effective and practical technical support for large-scale EV cluster aggregation.

title: Hybrid Neural Network with Implementation Efficiency for Joint Dispersion and Nonlinear Compensation in High-speed and Long-haul Transmission Systems
authors: Luhan Jiang, Ying Wu, Ying Wang, Jianyu Long, Yifan Chen, Wen Zhou, Kaihui Wang, Jianjun Yu
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: In high-speed and long-haul coherent optical fiber transmission systems, compensation of chromatic dispersion is one of the most computationally intensive components. Meanwhile, fiber nonlinear effects further limit the performance. To address these challenges, a hybrid neural network scheme is proposed to jointly compensate for dispersion and nonlinearity. The structure features a stacked dilated convolutional module that enables streaming-friendly dispersion compensation with reduced parameters, and an autoencoder-assisted dual-branch nonlinear equalizer module to suppress nonlinear impairments with robustness. The scheme is experimentally validated in an 896-Gb/s single-channel high-speed coherent system and a long-haul WDM system of 640-Gb/s QPSK signals extending to 15360 km, achieving up to 1.47-dB and 2.25-dB SNR gains over conventional DSP, respectively. Upon the performance improvement, the scheme shows relatively low computational complexity while maintaining memory friendliness, showing potential for real-time deployment on edge-computing platforms.

title: Intrinsic Bayesian Errors-in-Variables Under Anisotropic Noise
authors: Dominik Friml, Jakub Kunčák, Pavel Václavek
publish: IEEE Control Systems Letters
abstract: We present an intrinsic Bayesian formulation of the linear errors-in-variables problem under anisotropic Gaussian noise using a homogeneous representation. By analytically marginalizing the latent noise-free data, we obtain a closed-form marginal likelihood in arbitrary dimension. Under ellipsoidal normalization, the resulting maximum likelihood estimator coincides with generalized total least squares. We develop Bayesian inference on an affine chart via homogenization, yielding an explicit posterior decomposition into a heavy-tailed radial component and an affine Gaussian term with a chart-dependent correction factor, and ensuring posterior properness under a Gaussian prior. A conditionally conjugate variational approximation enables efficient inference and admits a recursive implementation with exponential forgetting, resulting in a practical online variational EIV algorithm with closed-form updates. We illustrate the method on an application-motivated melt-flow-rate identification problem from plastic recycling, with anisotropic measurement noise and direct comparison to a numerically computed reference posterior. The example assesses recursive tracking and posterior-approximation quality under slight model misspecification.

title: The Impact of Generative AI-Supported Experiential Learning on Secondary School Teachers' AI Competence Self-Efficacy in Mainland China: A Quasi-Experimental Study
authors: Xueliang Zhang, Alex Wing Cheung Tse
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Education and E-Learning

title: Design and Analysis of MEMS Accelerometer for Bridge Structural Health Monitoring (SHM)
authors: Shashank Singh, Gargi Khanna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: For ultra-low-frequency structural health monitoring (SHM) applications, where precise measurement of low-amplitude ambient vibrations is crucial, a MEMS capacitive accelerometer is improved in this work. Full three-dimensional finite element method (FEM) analysis in COMSOL Multiphysics is employed to systematically optimize the design for low-frequency operation by modifying a reference baseline accelerometer geometry reported in the literature. The baseline accelerometer geometry exhibits a first-mode eigen frequency of approximately 99.46 Hz. Through FEM-based parametric optimization of the suspension spring length and proof-mass width, the fundamental eigenfrequency is further reduced to 79.691 Hz. The optimized accelerometer achieves a displacement sensitivity of 23.811 μm/g and a capacitive sensitivity of approximately 2.2516 fF/g, demonstrating its suitability for low-frequency vibration monitoring for bridge structural health systems.

title: Predictive Maintenance and Operational Efficiency in Wind Energy using Machine Learning
authors: Piyush Anand, Mohd Sadique, Priyanshu Kushwaha, Meenakshi Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Due to the increased reliance on renewable energy, wind power production requires increased reliability and cost-efficiency. Unforeseen failures of wind turbine parts including the generator, gearbox and blades are very costly to maintain, and also reduces energy generation. This paper presents a predictive maintenance model that can be used in detecting any form of machine failure using the operational data that is collected by sensors. The sample has 40 predictors that consist of 40,000 training and 10,000 testing observations that represent the real-life conditions of the turbines. A number of classification models are developed and optimized, minimizing both the false negatives and false positives, the performance of which is measured using a new cost-sensitive metric that rates the ratio of the minimum possible maintenance cost to the costs caused by the model. According to experimental results, the proposed approach can not only increase the accuracy of failure detection, but also reduce total maintenance costs, which will reduce operational efficiency and reliability in wind energy systems.

title: A MERN Stack Framework for Real Estate Platforms Leveraging Conversational AI and Predictive Analytics
authors: Jahnvi Tiwari, Praveen Raj, Anushka Garg, Mahendra Pratap Yadav, Priyanka Singh, Dheeraj Dubey
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: SmartEstate is an AI-augmented real estate platform developed with the MERN stack to rectify inefficiencies in online property search and management. The system offers secure, role-based access for buyers, sellers, and administrators through JWT and Google OAuth, and incorporates an NLP-driven conversational chatbot for natural language property discovery. A rental price prediction engine utilising ensemble learning (Random Forest Regressor) was developed using a dataset of 3,335 properties in Bengaluru, achieving great predicted accuracy (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.94 for training, 0.80 for testing). The platform features an administrative dashboard, real-time listing management, and scalable REST APIs hosted on cloud infrastructure. Load-testing outcomes validate dependable responsiveness during simultaneous usage, indicating practicality for real-world implementation. SmartEstate enhances transparency, personalisation, and decision-making in property searches, while offering a modular framework for future enhancements, like virtual tours, regulatory API connection, and language support.

title: Advanced Optimization and Load Forecasting in Smart Industrial Grids: Employ AI for Enhancing Energy Efficiency in IIoT
authors: C Senthilkumar, S. Sujatha, A Jeyamurugan, S. Chitra Selvi, Liya Alias, M Agash Chozhan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: A new breed of intelligent, networked manufacturing complexes is springing up due to the Industrial Internet of Things. Smart industrial networks see increased energy efficiency as a result of this upgrade. Scheduling and time-based reactive load management cannot keep up with dynamic industrial activity. Carbon emissions, operational expenses, and energy waste all increase. For proactive energy management, AI can precisely optimise and predict loads. Using IIoT infrastructure and AI, this research presents a smart industrial grid energy-optimisation and load-demand prediction system. Using state-of-the-art ML and DL models such as GBM, Reinforcement Learning, and LSTM networks, the platform reliably forecasts future load patterns. Using historical energy consumption data, IIoT sensor data, and current environmental conditions, our optimistic rule optimises grid scheduling, demand response, and energy spending. Industrial process demand can be levelled, peak load reduced, and load anticipated using PSO and GA optimisation techniques. With the help of artificial intelligence (AI) power control, the grid control system can adapt to load-specific gross demand, renewable energy input, and market electricity pricing. Costs are reduced, and energy spikes are prevented during operations. Compared with conventional scheduling algorithms, AI-driven optimisation in smart industrial grid simulation systems achieves a $20 \%$ increase in energy efficiency and a $95 \%$ improvement in load prediction accuracy. These innovations lessen the load on the grid, operational expenses, and ecological footprint.

title: Automated Berg Balance Scale Evaluation System Based on OpenPose Pose Estimation and Real-Time Keypoint Analysis
authors: Chang Yu, Wei Deng, Xiaoqiang Cao, Bin Hu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: To address the subjective bias, inter-rater variability, and inefficiency of traditional manual Berg Balance Scale (BBS) scoring, this study develops the first intelligent BBS assessment system in China using OpenPose keypoint detection. The system deeply integrates the 14 BBS clinical tasks with OpenPose's real-time multi-person 2D pose estimation framework to create a fully engineered automated scoring solution. For realtime pose estimation, the first 10 layers of VGG-19 serve as the backbone to extract high-dimensional feature maps. Multi-stage CNN branches then predict keypoint confidence maps and Part Affinity Fields (PAFs) in parallel. PAFs - OpenPose's core nonparametric representation - encode limb position and direction as 2D vector fields, enabling accurate multi-person association. A bipartite matching algorithm finally assembles keypoints into complete skeletons, effectively handling occlusion and interference while delivering robust, high-precision coordinates. Using the 25 BODY_25 keypoints, the system derives four quantitative dimensions: joint angles, positional/distance relationships, duration statistics, and assistance detection. These features, combined with subject-specific calibration, dynamic thresholds, multi-frame validation, and finite state machine tracking, enable objective automated scoring of all 14 BBS items. This work fills a key technical gap in intelligent BBS assessment in China, greatly reducing subjective errors and time costs. It provides an efficient, objective, and traceable tool suitable for home, community, and clinical rehabilitation, shifting balance evaluation from experience-based to data-driven and intelligent methods. Experiments confirm high keypoint accuracy, strong PAF robustness, and stable real-time performance, offering a solid foundation for advancing intelligent rehabilitation medicine.

title: Non-Invasive Blood Pressure Monitoring and Estimation using Dual-Wavelength PPG and Machine Learning
authors: Sanneboyina Anil, M Naresh, Joginaidu Kuriti, Sreedhar Kollem, Samineni Peddakrishna, Chenna Kesavulu Gaddam, Modugu Krishnaiah
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This study introduces a photoplethysmography (PPG)-based method for monitoring blood pressure(BP) using finger-tip signals. This approach enables the real-time, non-invasive estimation of systolic and diastolic blood pressure through the analysis of pulse wave signals. The comprehensive framework presents for non-invasive BP estimation utilizing dual-wavelength PPG signals in conjunction with machine learning (ML) algorithms. In the proposed sys-tem, an embedded pulse oximetry and heart-rate sensing module is built using the MAX30101 optical sensor and the MAX32664 ultra-low-power biometric sensor hub to facilitate the synchronized PPG signals at 660 nm and 880 nm wavelengths. The acquired signals are then processed in real-time by a microcontroller executing a Blood Pressure Tracking (BPT) algorithm, which estimates systolic and diastolic BP. Multiple regression models are trained and tested with quantitative output driven by derived features based on PPG combined with demographic data of 575 individuals. The predictive accuracy of the proposed models was compared to PPM reference BP values and the linear regression model had the best predictive accuracy with Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and 3.25 mmHg and a Mean Squared Error (MSE) 16.47 mmHg<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. This metrics suggest that the proposed system has a robust, scalable solution to the continuous, non-invasive BP monitoring, appropriate in healthcare applications.

title: Evaluating the Portability of BERT-based Misinformation Detection from Twitter to Bluesky
authors: Swayam Prakash Panda, Ajay Kumar Phulre
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The rise of social media has led to a parallel rise in misinformation, which remains a serious and widespread issue still not addressed properly. While existing detection models exist for mainstream platforms like Twitter, as we see newer decentralized social media platforms like Bluesky emerge, the transferability of these models is an unaddressed and significant question. Our study conducts a three-step controlled experiment to evaluate the portability of a standard BERT-based model trained for identifying misinformation from Twitter to Bluesky. The first part of the experiment achieves a baseline F1-score of 0.64 on the public LIAR dataset. Then, when this model, which was trained on Twitter, is tested on a heuristically labelled Bluesky dataset, it achieves a relatively high zero-shot F1-score of 0.73. After some further fine-tuning using a subset of the Bluesky dataset, the study observes that the performance of the model is almost perfect - reaching an F1-score of 0.997. This study infers that while foundational misinformation signals are highly portable, achieving the best possible results still requires platform-specific fine-tuning and adaptation.

title: Few-Shot Learning Towards Voice Cloning
authors: Shrisha M Sakri, Priya M Karikatti, Ravi N Khokale, Kaushik Mallibhat
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Voice cloning model aims to generate and synthesise speech while preserving the speaker’s identity along with prosody characteristics using short-duration audio samples. The proposed pipeline includes audio preprocessing of the input audio, followed by the XTTS model used as the main reference-conditioned TTS model, and YourTTS serves as the secondary synthesiser for enhancing variations based on the expressions, along with the fallback mechanism for obtaining stable output. The generated speech is enhanced using DSP techniques and adaptive prosody correction. Obtaining pitch, energy along with the duration matrix shows the errors with reduced and accurate results. High-quality cross-lingual voice cloning is supported. Thus the proposed model has a robust voice-cloning framework that supports multiple languages, improves quality of sound, fixes rhythm and pronunciation, and adjusts the style of speaking.

title: Herbal RX: Herbal Remedy Extraction and Recommendation Using Deep Learning and Image Processing
authors: Srikakulapu Subhakar, Kinthali Harishit, Singothu Durga Prasad, Poluri Jaya Chandra
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Medicinal plants are used as primary healthcare facilities around the world but its proper utilization and accurate identification is a difficult task to non-experts. This paper will introduce a complex mobile based system named Herbal Rx that provides the transition between the established knowledge of herbs and the latest technology by using three modes of interaction: plant-name search, image-based identification and symptom-based recommendation. It uses a lightweight convolutional neural network based on MobileNetV2 trained with the help of the TensorFlow Lite optimizer to execute efficient inference on the device, without relying on continuous internet connectivity. These include a localized and structured database of scientifically proven medicinal data, such as botanical names, medical uses, methods of preparation, treating ailments, and safety guidelines, to ensure a uniform offline access. Firebase services are optional to use in authentication and history management. Classification accuracy of more than 90% is experimentally proven to be possible on diverse medicinal plant species in different environmental conditions. The system has inference times of less than 6 seconds on the mid-range smartphones, thereby being usable in real time in the constraints of resources.

title: Integrating U-Net and LSTM for Precise Multichannel EEG-Based Emotion Recognition via Differential Entropy Features
authors: Ketha Dhatri, Yemparala Lalith, M.Jeyaselvi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The issue that will be investigated in this study is the possibility of precise emotion recognition based on multichannel electroencephalography (EEG) signals, which is a key element of affective computing and human-computer interaction. We suggest a hybrid system with U-Net and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks in order to obtain both spatial and time differences in the EEG data. U-Net is applied to identify complex spatial features in a multichannel setting, whereas the LSTM models highlight the temporal dynamics of emotion changes. Differential Entropy (DE) is the most important feature, which works as the best discriminatory feature and it has lower computational cost. It is done using the framework in MATLAB and it is compatible with an efficient preprocessing, feature extraction and model training. The test analysis on normal EEGs with experiments represents good accuracy levels, ability to resist inter-subject variability, and capacity to withstand noise. The suggested approach provides a real-time and accurate emotional recognition system, which will be used to develop adaptive technologies and neurofeedback systems.

title: A Multi-Level Alerting Framework for Detecting Driver Drowsiness
authors: R Pandimeena, Bn Vignesh, T Sampath
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Driver drowsiness is a major cause of road accidents and requires reliable real-time detection. This work presents a low-cost, privacy-preserving driver-monitoring system that uses infrared (IR) eye-state detection and IMUbased head-orientation tracking. Unlike conventional camerabased methods, the proposed system avoids vision and cloud processing by employing a lightweight TinyML model for real-time edge inference. A multi-level alert mechanism is implemented to provide vibration, audio, and emergency warnings, including external alerts to nearby vehicles, based on the duration of drowsiness. The system achieves approximately 93& accuracy with minimal false alarms under simulated conditions. Overall, the proposed approach offers an energyefficient and practical solution for real-time embedded automotive safety applications. Keywords-Motion perception, edge computing, internet of things, real-time alarms, driver sleepiness and low-power embedded machines.

title: LeafLife: An Explainable Deep Learning Framework with Robustness for Grape Leaf Disease Recognition
authors: B. M. ShahriaAlam, Md. Nasim Ahmed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Plant disease diagnosis is essential to farmers’ management choices because plant diseases frequently lower crop yield and product quality. For harvests to flourish and agricultural productivity to boost, grape leaf disease detection is important. The plant disease dataset contains grape leaf diseases total of 9,032 images of four classes, among them three classes are leaf diseases, and the other one is healthy leaves. After rigorous pre-processing dataset was split (70% training, 20% validation, 10% testing), and two pre-trained models were deployed: InceptionV3 and Xception. Xception shows a promising result of 96.23% accuracy, which is remarkable than InceptionV3. Adversarial Training is used for robustness, along with more transparency. Grad-CAM is integrated to confirm the leaf disease. Finally deployed a web application using Streamlit with a heatmap visualization and prediction with confidence level for robust grape leaf disease classification.

title: Development of AI-Powered Voice Agent System for Business Communication: A Latency Optimization Approach
authors: D. Lakshmi Padmaja, Deepthi Kalwa, Lavanya Gurrala, Jitesh Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Voice-based artificial intelligence systems show promise for automating business communications, but current implementations suffer from high latency that disrupts natural conversation flow. This paper presents our ongoing work developing a voice AI agent system specifically designed for startup and real estate sectors, with primary focus on latency reduction and multilingual conversation support.We developed a prototype system integrating natural language processing, voice synthesis, and telephony APIs to create an automated lead qualification agent. Through systematic optimization of connection pooling, asynchronous processing, and WebSocket streaming, we reduced system response latency from an initial 15-25 seconds to 3-6 seconds in local testing—representing a 76-88% improvement. The system supports Hindi-English code-switching, a critical requirement for Indian market applications.Our prototype demonstrates human-like voice quality and maintains conversational coherence during test interactions. We present the technical architecture, optimization strategies employed, and preliminary performance results from controlled testing. While full production deployment and business impact validation remain future work, our findings suggest that sub-6-second response times are achievable with current technologies, making voice AI practical for real-world business applications. The system implementation is available as an open-source project, and demonstration videos document the current capabilities.

title: Multi-Architectural Deep-Learning and Hybrid Ensemble Approaches for Early Alzheimer’s Classification
authors: Zarin Rafah Chowdhury, Jannatul Mawa, Nurul Momin Resat, Abir Hasan Imran, Ilham Bari Chowdhury, Abhishek Sushil
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease is a progressive neurodegenerative condition marked by the decline of memory and higher-order cognitive functions, making early detection a critical priority. This study explores the automated identification and classification of Alzheimer’s disease from MRI scans through state-of-the-art deep learning methodologies. A dataset comprising 12,020 MRI images was utilized, categorized into four diagnostic groups: non-demented, very mild, mild, and moderate. To optimize both accuracy and computational efficiency, the images underwent systematic preprocessing—standardized resizing and normalization, along with Contrast-Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) and a range of geometric and photometric augmentations to enhance feature clarity and strengthen model generalizability. The analysis encompassed a comprehensive evaluation of multiple convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures, including ResNet-50, VGG-16, VGG-19, DenseNet201, Inception-V3, MobileNet-V2, Xception, and EfficientNetV2. Furthermore, hybrid ensemble frameworks were developed by combining ResNet-50 with VGG-16, VGG-19, Inception-V3, and MobileNet-V2 backbones, and further integrating these with machine-learning classifiers such as XGBoost, k-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN), and Random Forest. By synthesizing deep, hybrid, and ensemble approaches, this work seeks to capture subtle discriminative patterns across MRI sequences, thereby improving the predictive performance of Alzheimer’s disease classification.

title: Cybersecurity and IT Technologies as a Driver of Digital Marketing Sustainability
authors: Hassan Ali Al-Ababneh, Rahmeh Salman Haddadin, Fayez M. Al-Khawaldeh, Akef Yousef Alzyadat, Noor Ahmad Alkhudierat, Farah Hanna Zawaideh
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Modern digital marketing ecosystems operate in an environment of constant cyberthreats, requiring a shift from reactive security measures to strategic digital resilience management. The purpose of this study is to substantiate the role of cybersecurity and IT technologies as key factors in the long-term stability and competitiveness of digital marketing. The methodology is based on a systems-process approach, including an analysis of corporate IT system architectures, organizational data protection procedures, and digital risk management practices. The study was conducted using five international companies (Microsoft, Google, Meta, IBM, and Cisco) to assess the relationship between cybersecurity maturity and the resilience of marketing operations. Benchmarking, business process modeling, and the development of a Marketing Cybersecurity Maturity Index (MCMI) were used. The results showed that integrating data protection technologies into a digital marketing strategy helps reduce reputational and operational losses, increases user trust, and creates a sustainable competitive advantage. The practical value of the study lies in the proposal of a methodology for assessing the impact of cybersecurity on brand sustainability and the effectiveness of digital communications.

title: Hybrid Multi-Stage Clustering for the General Pickup and Delivery Problem
authors: Hanen Aydi, Sameh Chtourou, Adnen El-Amraoui, Ahmed Frikha, François Delmotte
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: This paper addresses the General Pickup and Delivery Problem (GPDP), a complex combinatorial optimization challenge characterized by strict operational constraints. It introduces a hybrid clustering framework that combines capacitated K-Medoids for feasibility, Basic Variable Neighborhood Search (BVNS) to escape local optima, and Ward's algorithm to enhance spatial compactness and separation. The proposed approach is evaluated on the Li & Lim benchmark (56 instances with 50 pickup–delivery requests (100 nodes) each) across Location-Clustered (LC), Location-Random (LR), and Location-Clustered-Random (LRC) topologies. Clustering quality is assessed through a multi-criteria evaluation using the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP). The results show that BVNS + Ward's consistently outperforms K-Medoids, yielding higher explained variance, improved compactness, and better load balance, thus demonstrating its robustness and practical value for real-world logistics applications. The optimal threshold <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R_{T}^{2}$</tex> refers to the target explained variance used in Ward's algorithm to determine when to stop merging clusters, ensuring both compactness and operational feasibility of the solution.

title: Computer-Aided Diagnosis for Breast Cancer Classification Using Deep Learning on Ultrasound Images
authors: Vedant Nakshane, Dhiraj Mundane, Ganesh Atre, Ankit Kumar, Pramod Dharmadhikari
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Breast cancer remains one of the most prevalent and deadly diseases affecting women worldwide, with over 2.3 million diagnoses and 685,000 deaths reported in 2020. Early detection and accurate diagnosis are critical for improving patient outcomes and survival rates. This study presents a comprehensive deep learning-based system for automated breast cancer classification using ultrasound images. We implement and compare three state-of-the-art Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architectures—ResNet50, DenseNet121, and EfficientNetB0—to classify breast ultrasound images into three categories: normal (healthy tissue), benign lesions, and malignant tumors. Using the publicly available Breast Ultrasound Images (BUSI) dataset containing 780 images, we employ transfer learning, data augmentation, and advanced training techniques including early stopping and learning rate scheduling. The system achieves approximately 91% accuracy with DenseNet121, demonstrating robust performance across multiple evaluation metrics including precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC. To enhance clinical interpretability and trust, we integrate Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) for visual explanation of model predictions. Additionally, a user-friendly Streamlit-based web application is developed for practical deployment, enabling healthcare professionals to upload ultrasound images and receive real-time diagnostic predictions with visual explanations. This work demonstrates that modern CNN architectures can effectively distinguish between malignant, benign, and normal breast tissue, providing a practical diagnostic aid that could supplement radiologists and improve screening efficiency in clinical workflows.

title: Blood Bank Demand Forecasting and Management Using AI Approaches
authors: N. Rajkumar, Kunchapu Laxman Raj, V. Sai Manideep
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The management system of blood banks today is moving towards Artificial Intelligence to provide the use of intelligent automation and predictive healthcare. Models such as Support Vector Machines, Gradient Boosting, and Transformer-based architectures are used to improve demand forecasting accuracy which offer precision in demand forecasts and accurate match of donor and recipients. The adaptive learning technologies of AI analytics will be able to monitor trends in the donation, anticipate deficit, and optimize blood stocks. The model can also evaluate blood quality, pathogen identification, and compatibility assurance and automation to reduce the risks of transfusion. Chat modules in natural language processing assist in donor interaction and communication with the donors and predictive models advise on future blood supply requirements, using seasonal/demographic data. It is worth nothing that reinforcement learning and federated models will remain the same in supporting privacy-preserving training on and in the dynamic scheduling in real-time circumstance. Creatively the technologies are utilized to achieve better decision-making, waste reduction and trustworthiness of blood chains. AI is an essential technology of innovation in revolutionizing blood management of a socio-culturally supporting proactive and data intelligent lifelong ecosystem.

title: Responsive Virtual Apparel Try-On through Pose Detection, Geometric Warping, and Deterministic Size Matching
authors: Harish Kunder, Abhijit S. Patil, Hrishikesh Bhosle, Vaishnavi Praveen Kamble, H C Manya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: As online retail continues to grow, especially in the fashion sector, consumers increasingly seek more interactive and personalized shopping experiences. One of the most innovative solutions emerging in this space is the virtual trial room-a tool that allows users to visualize how garments would look on them without physically trying them on. This paper examines several approaches to virtual fitting systems developed with the help of computer vision, augmented reality, and artificial intelligence. Some models rely on simple frameworks like OpenCV and Flask to create web-based interfaces where 2D outfits are projected onto the user’s image in real time, adjusting to body posture and dimensions to improve fit accuracy. Others make use of Kinect sensors combined with the Processing environment to deliver a richer, three-dimensional experience through real-time body tracking and spatial mapping. Additionally, there are solutions that operate using basic webcams, offering features like garment color customization and logo overlay through techniques such as contour recognition and pixel-level image adjustments. These technologies not only enhance customer engagement but also offer retailers practical benefits by reducing return rates and enabling more efficient online operations

title: Structure-Aware Wavelet Diffusion for Low-Light Image Enhancement with YCbCr Guidance
authors: Lixin Zou, Zhenyu Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Restoring visibility in low-light conditions without compromising structural integrity remains a critical challenge in computer vision. Existing diffusion-based enhancement techniques are frequently hindered by color shifts stemming from entangled RGB conditioning, as well as structural distortions caused by isolated frequency processing. To address these limitations, a novel Structure-Aware Wavelet Diffusion Framework is proposed. Diverging from conventional RGB conditioning, a YCbCr-Guided Spatial Feature Transform (SFT) is introduced to explicitly decouple and modulate luminance and chrominance, ensuring high color fidelity. Furthermore, an Inter-Subband Interaction (ISIA) module is developed. By leveraging cross-attention mechanisms, it transfers essential structural cues from low-frequency components to high-frequency textures, effectively suppressing visual artifacts. Comprehensive evaluations on the benchmark LOL-v1 dataset demonstrate that the presented framework achieves state-of-the-art structural preservation (SSIM of 0.87) and perceptual quality (LPIPS of 0.13), significantly outperforming recent regression and generative models.

title: RIDS: RPi based clinical image-denoising using modified diffusion and spatial filters
authors: Himanshu Gupta, Anil Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The outcome of a clinical procedure, administered by a medical practitioner based on a non-invasive diagnostic technique, is dependent on the quality of data obtained during the process. However, in opposition to such ideal scenarios, disturbances or noise components get added to these images, thereby degrading the information content. In that context, a denoising algorithm capable of preserving the edges and simultaneously enhancing the features of the images is envisioned. This intention leads to the development of a denoising algorithm involving mode functions in variational mode decomposition and spatial filters, namely the Wiener filter, bilateral filter, and modified non-linear diffusion filter. Here, the fine and coarse details of an image are smoothened in such a way that the structural features are also preserved. Moreover, the performance controlling parameters of the algorithm are adaptively selected using optimization techniques. Finally, the resultant optimal performance metric scores are evaluated based on the peak-signal-to-noise-ratio (PSNR), mean square error (MSE), feature-similarity-index (FSIM), and structural-similarity index (SSIM) metrics, and are verified for cross-platform dependence using the Raspberry-Pi-5 hardware. The quantitative and qualitative analysis of the proposed method with other denoising methods concludes in favor of the technique with a significant 80% average decrease in MSE with 3.9% deviation, a 51% average increase in PSNR with 0.8% deviation, a 53% increase and 1.3% deviation in FSIM, and a 386% average increase in SSIM having 5.3% deviation in the performance when involving the RPi hardware for varying levels of noise. In all, this work provides an improved and superior noise suppression method that has an efficient performance and also preserves the visual characteristics of an image.

title: GFD-Net: A Gated Feature-Alignment Network with Dynamic Sampling for Retinal Vessel Segmentation
authors: Zhiling Zhu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Automatic retinal vessel segmentation faces challenges such as loss of high-frequency details, semantic information deficiency, and imprecise boundary reconstruction. To address these issues, we propose the GFD-Net model, which integrates a gating mechanism, frequency-aware mechanism, and dynamic alignment mechanism. Specifically, frequency-aware deformable downsampling (FDD) effectively preserves micro-vessel details through the synergistic action of wavelet transform and deformable convolution. The Spatial Gated Alignment Unit (SGAU) optimizes skip connections through decoder-guided semantic mechanisms to suppress noise. Meanwhile the Dynamic Alignment Sampling Module (DASM) utilizes learned pixel-level offsets to enhance upscaling, enabling precise morphological reconstruction. Experimental results on DRIVE and STARE datasets demonstrate that GFD-Net achieves a balance between global topology awareness and local detail preservation, reaching outstanding performance.

title: An IoT-Enabled Smart Universal Charger for Modern Devices with Adaptive Device Recognition and Safety Control
authors: K.Sheikdavood, Akshaya N. S., Bharathi S., Dhaarani T.
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This paper explores the design and build of a smart universal charger with IoT features, built to power and monitor all sorts of modern gadgets. The setup works off a single input—you can plug in either a 19 V / 3.3 A barrel jack adapter or a 20 V USB-C Power Delivery (PD) adapter, both through the same cable. There are three interchangeable output options: USB-C, USB-A, or a DC barrel jack. Swapping out the output connectors is easy, so you can charge pretty much any device you throw at it.A buck-boost converter (LTC3780) keeps the voltage steady, no matter what you’re charging. An INA219 sensor tracks current, voltage, and power. The ESP32 microcontroller pulls it all together, showing live stats on an OLED screen and sending the data to Blynk and ThingSpeak for remote monitoring and analysis. With real-time power tracking and cloud-based data logging, this system is safe, efficient, and flexible. By blending power electronics with IoT, it gives you a smart, universal charging solution that fits the way we use portable devices today.

title: An Efficient Hybrid CNN-LSTM Framework for Network Intrusion Detection Using UNSW Data
authors: Kistam Gopi, Kanakala Raja Sekhar, G.Narsamma, B.Venkata Varma, B.S.S.Ganesh Pardhu, A.Lakshmanarao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Network intrusion detection is a critical component of cybersecurity systems, aimed at identifying malicious activities within network traffic. With the increasing scale and complexity of modern networks, traditional security mechanisms face limitations in detecting diverse and evolving attack patterns. Learning-based approaches have therefore gained prominence for analyzing network traffic data and distinguishing normal behavior from intrusions. However, achieving consistent detection performance remains challenging due to high-dimensional features and variability in attack characteristics. This paper presents a learning-based intrusion detection framework using the UNSW-NB15 dataset, formulated as a binary classification problem separating normal and attack traffic. The study evaluates classical machine learning models, a deep learning model based on a multilayer perceptron, and hybrid ensemble strategies that combine neural and tree-based learners. Performance is assessed using standard evaluation metrics, including accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. Experimental results demonstrate that hybrid ensemble models outperform individual learning approaches by effectively leveraging complementary decision patterns. The findings highlight the effectiveness of combining machine learning and deep learning techniques for robust intrusion detection in contemporary network environments.

title: A Blockchain-Based ECDSA Framework for Drug Authentication and Traceability in Bangladeshi Healthcare
authors: Sri Apurbo Saha, Mst Shimla Sinthia, Shamim Ahmad, Istiaque Ahmed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Counterfeit pharmaceuticals pose a critical public health threat in Bangladesh and globally. According to the World Health Organization (WHO), around 10% of medical products in low- and middle-income countries are substandard or falsified, with some regions exceeding 30%. In Bangladesh, industry reports indicate that nearly 40% of medicines are counterfeit or substandard, accounting for over BDT 1,500 crore in illegal sales annually. These fake drugs lead to therapeutic failure, adverse reactions, antimicrobial resistance, and preventable deaths-eroding trust in the healthcare system. To combat this, we propose a hybrid blockchain-based authentication and traceability framework tailored for the Bangladeshi pharmaceutical supply chain. It integrates a permissioned Hyperledger Fabric network for immutable drug lifecycle logging and a two-layered cryptographic scheme. Certificateless aggregate signatures enable efficient participant authentication without traditional certificates, while ECDSA-signed QR codes on packaging allow users to verify authenticity using public blockchain records. The system marks a product as “Sold” upon first verification to prevent reuse, with zero cost for subsequent checks. Smart contracts manage identity, shipment, and sale updates while ensuring privacy and throughput. This model offers a scalable, transparent, and secure approach to mitigating drug counterfeiting in Bangladesh.

title: A Hybrid EfficientNet-XGBoost Framework for Multi-Class Lung Disease Classification
authors: S Kavitha, Vani Rajasekar, T Vanaja, K S Pagalavan, S Sachinsandron, Roshni Magesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: The issue of lung diseases is a major health challenge in the world and therefore needs effective and dependable diagnostic tools. Traditional chest X-ray assessments are manually interpreted and are often slow and prone to limited precision. The present paper suggests an automated deep learning model with $\mathbf{5 6, 0 0 0}$ chest X-ray images for the classification of lung diseases in multiple classes. In this study, we evaluated three CNN models, which were based on transfer learning: ResNet, DenseNet, and EfficientNet. Among the three, EfficientNet was the best at the individual model accuracy. In a bid to get even better results, we designed a hybrid model that comprises EfficientNet to extract deep features and XGBoost to classify the extracted features to achieve improved performance and accuracy and achieved an AUC of 0.96. This showed that the model had formed CNNbased representation using gradient boosting thereby enhancing the robustness of the model and accuracy in representing a valuable scalable add to the initiation of lung disease classification to support early and accurate diagnosis.

title: Research on PPG Signal Denoising Based on Improved Grey Wolf Optimizer and Variational Mode Decomposition
authors: Hao Yang, Mei-lin Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This study aims to develop an adaptive denoising framework for photoplethysmography (PPG) signals to improve waveform fidelity under complex noise conditions. An IGWO-VMD-WT method is proposed, in which an improved grey wolf optimizer (IGWO) is employed to jointly optimize the mode number and penalty factor of variational mode decomposition (VMD) using minimum envelope entropy as the fitness function. Based on the optimized VMD results, noise-dominated and signal-related modes are identified and reconstructed through wavelet refinement to suppress electromyographic interference and baseline drift. The method is evaluated on both simulated PPG signals and real clinical data. Quantitative results show that the proposed method achieves an SNR of 20.74 dB, RMSE of 0.0504, and NCC of 0.9958 on simulated data, outperforming WT, EMD, and conventional VMD. The results demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively enhances denoising performance while preserving pulse waveform morphology.

title: Detecting Fetal Health Conditions Using a Federated Learning Approach with an Incentive-Based Reward System
authors: Humayra Ahmed, Muhammad Nazrul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: During pregnancy, fetal health monitoring is essential for early detection of complications. Cardiotocography (CTG) is a widely used method to assess fetal well-being. Centralized machine learning approaches that are used for interpreting CTG data pose privacy risks due to the sensitive nature of medical records. Thus, this study proposes a reward-driven Federated Learning (FL) framework for fetal health classification by enabling collaborative model training. This framework does not require sharing raw patient data. We simulate a realistic non-IID setting using a Dirichlet distribution to partition the CTG dataset across clients. We also introduce a reward-based aggregation mechanism that measures client contributions based on both data quality and quantity. Clients that have a negative impact are dynamically excluded to enhance model robustness. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed FL model achieves 92.15% accuracy, exhibits strong generalization across clients, and has a low execution time of 2.12 seconds. The model performs well across all the fetal health classes while achieving high AUC values and clear class differentiation. Overall, the proposed framework offers a privacy-preserving, adaptive, and efficient solution for real-world medical applications. The framework is lightweight and scalable to larger hospital networks, as only model weights are exchanged, not patient data, which minimizes communication cost.

title: A Lightweight CNN Transformer Framework for Low-Resource Multilingual Speaker Verification in Indian Conditions
authors: Parikshith Patil, Atharva Jeevannavar, Satish Chikkmath, Nirmala S. R.
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Speaker verification is a critical component of biometric security systems, enabling the authentication of a speaker’s identity regardless of spoken content. This study proposes a robust deep learning-based speaker verification framework designed to address challenges posed by real-world variability in language, speaking style, and recording conditions. The system is trained on a multi-variability speech dataset (IITG-MV/IMSV) collected from 100 speakers across five sensors, two languages (English and a native language), and both conversational and reading styles. Acoustic features including log-Mel spectrograms, MFCCs, and Chroma are extracted from preprocessed audio, which undergoes speech segmentation, noise reduction, and normalization. The architecture employs residual convolutional blocks, SpecAugment for data augmentation, and a lightweight transformer to capture speaker-discriminative patterns. Evaluation using Equal Error Rate (EER) demonstrates the model’s effectiveness across linguistic and sensor variations. The proposed system achieves a notable EER of 2.8% in English settings, outperforming the baseline 3.82%, and shows improved generalization in cross-lingual scenarios (EER of 4.7% vs. 5.01%). These results highlight the system’s potential for scalable and accurate speaker verification in diverse and multilingual environments.

title: Service Robotics for Automated Tourist Assistance in Smart Destinations
authors: Maher Odeh Falah Alshamaileh, Lama'a Al-Orainat, Hassan Ali Al-Ababneh, Fatin A. Elhaj, Ahmed Ameen, Mhran Alzoubi, Ahmed Farhan Al-Amayreh
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: As tourist contexts become more complex and the need for personalized, real-time requests grows within smart destinations, such needs call for innovative technology solutions. Service robotics is a creative way to do so by using autonomous navigation, contextual awareness and interactive services. This paper applies a Design Science Research methodology to design and analyze a service robotics framework for automated tourist assistance in smart destination ecosystems. The research begins with the identification of operational and informational gaps in existing digital tourist services, followed by the definition of design objectives focused on adaptability, accessibility, and scalability. Based on these objectives, a modular robotic assistance artifact is developed, integrating mobile robotic agents, cloudbased knowledge management, and context-aware interaction mechanisms. The proposed artifact is demonstrated through representative tourism scenarios, including urban navigation, transport hub assistance, and event-based visitor support. The evaluation is conducted using scenario-based analysis, focusing on functional coverage, service responsiveness, and system integration capabilities. The evaluation uses quantitative serviceperformance indicators (SCR, RTRI, CSQI, SLRC) to compare human-only versus robotic-assisted assistance across representative scenarios. The results show improved coverage, reduced response time, extended availability, and measurable workload redistribution, providing an actionable KPI toolkit for smartdestination planning. Abbreviations and notation - SCR - Service Coverage Ratio; RTRI - Response Time Reduction Index; CSQI - Composite Service Quality Index; SLRC - Service Load Redistribution Coefficient; Vd - daily assistance requests; Th - mean duration of human-assisted service; Ch - cost per human interaction; Lh - number of supported languages (human-based); Ah - service availability (hours/day); Tr - average robotic assistance time; Ar - robotic service availability; Cr - cost per robotic interaction; Ns - successfully serviced requests; Nh - requests handled exclusively by human staff.

title: A Lightweight Detection Method for Transmission Line Bird Hazards Based on Bird-YOLO
authors: Shaocheng Hu, Xiren Miao, Xinyu Liu, Youxin Hu, Jinhao Su
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Bird perching and nesting on transmission lines may induce severe electrical faults and threaten grid stability. To address the challenges of small target scales, complex backgrounds, and insufficient localization accuracy in UAV inspection images, this paper proposes Bird-YOLO, a lightweight bird hazard detection method. Built upon YOLO26, the proposed method introduces the Efficient Channel Attention module into the neck network to enhance feature fusion under complex backgrounds, employs Focal Loss in the classification branch to alleviate sample imbalance and improve hard-sample learning, and adopts the SIoU loss function to optimize bounding box regression in terms of position, direction, and shape. Experimental results show that Bird-YOLO achieves an mAP of 73.6% and an mAR of 78.0%, with 9.9 M parameters and 50.6 G FLOPs. Compared with the YOLO26 baseline, the proposed method improves detection accuracy while maintaining a lightweight architecture, demonstrating its effectiveness and practicality for bird hazard detection in UAV-based transmission line inspection.

title: Implementation of Machine Learning (ML) in Industrial IoT Systems: Smart Manufacturing Lines Fault Detection and Maintenance Prediction in Real Time
authors: Shiva Shankar Mummidi, Safeyah Tawil, Naga Sekhar Madala, D. Kirubakaran, P. Hareesh, Y Krishna Bhargavi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Since Industry 4.0 is growing rapidly, IIoT technologies are being incorporated into smart industrial environments. IIoT allows real-time data collection, networked gear, and intelligent automation, but unexpected equipment failures, unscheduled downtime, and inefficient maintenance plans still plague manufacturing systems. Rule-based monitoring and reactive maintenance cannot handle the amount, speed, and complexity of sensor data provided by modern manufacturing lines. A framework for smart production lines enabled by the Industrial Internet of Things (IoT) that employ Machine Learning to detect defects and estimate maintenance needs in real time is presented in this paper. Supervised, unsupervised, and hybrid ML methods are used to analyse operating logs, machine performance measures, and high-frequency sensor data. Discovering early concerns and estimating the lifespan of industrial assets are made possible through predictive modeling, anomaly detection, and better feature extraction. System optimization in dynamic operating settings is made possible by adaptive learning and rapid data streaming. When compared to traditional monitoring systems, ML-based defect detection significantly improves detection accuracy, reduces the number of false alarms, and allows for proactive scheduling of maintenance. This strategy is applicable to many different businesses since it decreases equipment downtime, saves time, and increases asset life.

title: Genet: A Hybrid Congestion Control Framework with a Dynamic Tenure System and Learned Inference
authors: Wenjie Xi, Yifan Su, Xiaowei Wang, Tong Xin
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: In this paper, we propose Genet to address the structural inefficiencies of static coordination in hybrid congestion control. Genet operates as a modular meta-controller that orchestrates a diverse portfolio of candidate algorithms; standard rate regulation is delegated to the active candidate. To optimize the exploration-exploitation trade-off, Genet introduces a Dynamic Tenure System (DTS) that dynamically scales execution tenure based on component confidence, thereby amortizing probing overhead. The Physically-Gated Learned Rate Inference Engine (LRIE) intervenes only during “heuristic collapse” to resolve signal ambiguity via dimensionless invariants, strictly anchored by empirical capacity. Experimental results on commercial 4G/5G traces show that, compared to state-of-the-art hybrids like Anole, Genet not only improves throughput by 9.5% and reduces tail latency by 15%, but also reduces throughput jitter by 75%.

title: Gradient Descent Method for Solving Low-Tubal-Rank Tensor Factorization Problem
authors: Xinyi Li, Ze Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Low-rank tensor modeling is fundamental in multi-dimensional data analysis. Tubal rank based methods are effective but often rely on computationally expensive T-SVD. We propose a low-tubal-rank tensor factorization model and develop a gradient descent algorithm for solving the resulting non-convex problem efficiently. By working in the Fourier domain, the computation reduces to independent matrix multiplications. We prove global convergence under random initialization and a sufficiently small step size. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method, as well as its superior computational efficiency compared with T-SVD method.

title: Federated Learning Based Integrated Infrastructure Resilience Framework for Privacy-Preserving and Distributed Intelligence in Smart City Networks
authors: Ankita Fouzdar, Y. Geetha, Vandna Batra, Arun Kukreti, Deepak Patidar, Prashant Kumar Shukla
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This study suggests a new Federated Learning-Based Integrated Infrastructure Resilience Framework (FLIIRF) that can be used to realize privacy-preserving, distributed intelligence to increase the resilience of smart city networks. The framework incorporates edge-enabled federated learning, and decentralized infrastructure monitoring, adaptive fault-tolerant decision-making mechanisms. It also enables real-time coordination of heterogenous city subsystems. Transport, energy, communication, and emergency systems without compromising on sensitive data by conducting local training of models. A resilience-aware optimization module reread predictive intelligence as part of continuous update to guarantee a proactive approach to failures management and provision of effective resilient service maintenance. Simulation-based experimentation shows 28.4 % improvement in anomaly detection performance, 50 % improvement in fault recovery latency, and 64 % lowered data transmission overhead as compared to centralized machine learning models. Also, the risk of privacy leakage is decreased by 63.3 % due to the non-centralized training paradigm. By showing the possibility to construct future proof, self-adaptive, and privacy preserving smart city infrastructures, the proposed FLIIRF contributes to resilience and decentralized coordination of urban complex systems extensively.

title: Enhancing Connectivity, Security, and Energy Efficiency in Contemporary Residential Settings using AI with Li-Fi Technology for Smart Homes
authors: V Vijayalakshmi, V.C. Ranganayaki, S. Saranya, Sami Askar, Pallavi Giri, G. Karthikeyan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Smart homes offer unparalleled convenience, automation, and connectivity, but they also pose security, energy efficiency, and network dependability issues. We investigate the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) with Light Fidelity (Li-Fi) technology as a cutting-edge solution to improve household connectivity, security, and energy efficiency. Li-Fi, which transmits data via visible light, provides high-speed internet while reducing RF interference and congestion. AI-driven systems improve data transmission, resource allocation, and real-time decision-making in smart homes, making them more efficient and safer. Our approach leverages AI algorithms to intelligently manage the allocation of network resources in Li-Fi-enabled smart homes, ensuring seamless connectivity even as the number of devices and data demands increase. Moreover, AI-driven security systems are employed to detect and mitigate potential threats, providing robust protection against cyberattacks and unauthorized access. In terms of energy efficiency, AI models predict usage patterns and dynamically adjust lighting, appliances, and Li-Fi-based communication to reduce energy consumption. Not only can the combination of artificial intelligence with $\mathbf{L i}$-Fi improve the user experience, but it also helps to promote environmentally responsible energy habits and strengthens network security. The findings of this study reveal that artificial intelligence and lithium-ion batteries have the potential to transform smart home infrastructure by providing a solution that is both scalable and energy-efficient, thereby satisfying the ever-increasing desires of contemporary residential settings.

title: Fuel Cell Power Conversion by Using Fuzzy Logic and ANFIS Controllers with Quadratic Converter: An Experimental Analysis
authors: CH Hussaian Basha, Nagaraju Dharavat, Subhashree Choudhury, Kotakonda Chakravarthi, Malgireddy Srikar Reddy, Hema Rasala
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cells (PEMFCs) are becoming the fuel cell of interest with their high efficiency, low emissions, and suitability with current renewable energy systems. Nevertheless, a difficulty in extracting the maximum power is the non-linear current-voltage (I-V) properties of these cells. In order to address this, Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) methods are used to maximize power output at different operating conditions. This research introduces an adaptive fuzzy MPPT controller to improve the speed and precision of the tracking of the maximum power point of PEMFC systems. Also, to overcome the confined high output current, which is commonly related to the fuel cells, a Switched Capacitor Boost Converter (SCBC) is presented. This converter is effective in increasing the output voltage with a small size and weight of the entire system. The main benefits of the SCBC are a larger voltage conversion ratio, less voltage stress, a wide input voltage range, and constant output voltage. Modeling of the entire system is done in MATLAB/Simulink, and the performance of the converter is tested experimentally through a programmable power source.

title: Joint Resource Orchestration for LEO Satellite Networks Based on SFC-Constrained TEG
authors: Xuan Li, Junxiang Qin, Xiaotian Ma
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite networks have become a core component of 6G infrastructure, enabling timely space-based services for global users. Service Function Chain (SFC), as an application-driven service technology, is widely used in differentiated service scenarios of space-based systems. The introduction of SFC poses critical challenges for dynamic resource orchestration in satellite networks. To address these issues, we propose an SFC-Constrained Time-Expanded Graph (SC-TEG) model for joint resource orchestration in LEO satellite networks. This model unifies communication, computation, and storage resource modeling, introduces a functional layer dimension to support sequential VNF processing in SFCs, and incorporates a graph pruning strategy based on link establishment constraints. Three benchmark resource orchestration algorithms are designed based on SC-TEG to accommodate diverse user demand scenarios. Simulation results based on Walker constellations of different scales verify that SC-TEG reduces space complexity by 73.7% compared with traditional multi-layer TEG. Among the three benchmark algorithms, Shortest-Path Orchestration effectively limits the average task path length to within 4 in small-scale task scenarios. Load-Balancing Orchestration improves the task orchestration success rate by up to 57.6% in large-scale task scenarios.

title: Smart and Decentralized Security: AI and Blockchain Have a Synergistic to Future IoT
authors: Gurpreet Singh, Kapil Bhardwaj, Gurpreet Kaur, Chetna Pandey, Bhawna Sharma, Balraj Preet Kaur
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The convergence of Artificial Intelligence (AI), blockchain, and the Internet of Things (IoT) promises a robust, decentralized security paradigm that addresses pressing concerns over data integrity, authentication, privacy. This paper first surveys Security Challenges in AI, IoT, and Blockchain, highlighting the vulnerabilities that arise from distributed sensor networks, model drift, and ledger tampering, then explore AI Technologies in IoT Cybersecurity, detailing anomaly detection, predictive maintenance, and autonomous threat mitigation, while underscoring the need for transparent, explainable models. Next, the role of Blockchain in IoT Security is examined: immutable ledgers, smart contracts, and consensus protocols enhance data integrity and provide auditable audit trails, yet they introduce latency and scalability concerns. In final section discusses challenges and limitations, including computational overhead, energy consumption, regulatory compliance, and the difficulty of integrating heterogeneous devices. By synthesizing these insights, the study demonstrates that a synergistic combination of AI and blockchain can create a self-healing, trust-based IoT ecosystem, but achieving this vision requires overcoming technical, economic, and policy barriers.

title: KNN-Assisted Graphene -Based THz Biosensor for Liver Cancer Detection
authors: Hamza Wertani, Aymen Hlali, Hamza Ben Krid, Hassen Zairi
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Early detection of liver cancer remains challenging due to the weak dielectric contrast between healthy and malignant tissues in the terahertz (THz) frequency range. This paper presents a compact, highly sensitive, and reconfigurable THz biosensor based on a hybrid grapheme-gold metasurface for liver cancer biomarker detection. The proposed structure exploits strong plasmonic field confinement generated by the graphene-gold metasurface patterned on a dielectric substrate, thereby enhancing light-matter interaction. To further improve sensing performance, a machine-learning framework based on the K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) algorithm is employed to optimize the combined effects of graphene chemical potential, relaxation time, and operating temperature. Full-wave electromagnetic simulations integrated with KNN predictions demonstrate high sensing performance, achieving sensitivities of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$76.36 \text{GHz} /$</tex> RIU for healthy cell and 187.38 GHz/RIU for cancer cell. The biosensor attains a maximum quality factor of 38.85 and a peak figure of merit of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.69 \text{RIU}^{-1}$</tex>. These results confirm the potential of the proposed THz biosensor as an effective platform for real-time, label-free liver cancer detection, suitable for future intelligent biomedical sensing applications.

title: Emotion-Based Music Recommendation System: Enhancing Personalized Listening Experiences
authors: SK. Sumayya, T. L. Surekha, S. Hima Bindu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Emotion-aware music recommendation systems have received considerable attention owing to their potential in improving the engagement and emotional well-being of users. This paper proposes an automatic music recommendation system that identifies human emotions through facial expression analysis using images and live webcam input. Face detection is performed using YOLOv5, while emotion classification is achieved using a fine-tuned EfficientNetB4 model trained on the AffectNet dataset, capable of detecting six basic expressions of humans. Then, according to the detected expression, the appropriate songs can be recommended using the Spotify API based on the audio features of the songs, such as valence, energy, and tempo. The proposed system has been tested, and the overall accuracy of the system in the classification of emotions stands at 95%, which proves the efficiency of the proposed system on a difficult dataset. In contrast to traditional music recommendation systems that rely mainly on listening history or user preferences, the proposed system can change the suggestions based on the facial expressions of the users.

title: Deep Learning-Based Brain Tumor Detection in MRI Using a Hybrid YOLOv8 and InceptionV3 Model
authors: Asem Khmag, Mawada Shhob, Shymaa Khmag
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Brain tumor detection and localization from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scans represent a critical task in medical image analysis, where accuracy and robustness are essential for clinical decision support. This study proposes an intelligent deep learning-based framework for automated brain tumor detection using a hybrid architecture that integrates YOLOv8 with InceptionV3.
