In order to reduce noise and maintain image information, the system starts with preprocessing employing an adaptive Wiener filter. For classification, a hybrid LSTM–Vision Transformer (ViT) model is used, producing reliable image embeddings. In parallel, a transformer-based text feature extractor processes text queries or captions to generate high-quality text embeddings. Following that, both modalities are projected into a common embedding space with semantic alignment imposed. Filtering removes superfluous or deceptive information, whereas attention correction highlights essential cross-modal correlations to further improve the embedding quality. Moreover, cross-model retrieval makes precise text-to-image matching possible, facilitating uses like environmental study, disaster relief, and land cover interpretation with the highest accuracy of 94.56%. The proposed approach validation from Python software shows how well filtering and attention-based correction combined to provide dependable and semantically consistent retrieval in challenging remote sensing situations.

title: A Design Thinking Approach to Developing Songtun for Student Assignment Management in Digital Learning Environments
authors: Thanakrit Janchidfah, Thanayos JindaKaew, Bunyalak Ruamsap, Pisit Chansuek
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Task management has become a critical challenge for university students in the digital era, particularly in relation to missed deadlines and delayed assignment submissions, which can negatively impact learning performance and time management skills. Although various digital tools are available to support task organization, many existing solutions are not fully developed through genuinely learner-centered approaches, especially within the context of Thai higher education. This study aims to design and develop a mobile application named “Songtun” to assist students in recording, tracking, and managing academic assignments by adopting the Design Thinking methodology as a human-centered innovation framework. The research involved 125 undergraduate students who participated in a real classroom implementation of the Songtun application. The research instruments included the developed application and a five-point Likert-scale satisfaction questionnaire. The development and evaluation process followed the five stages of Design Thinking: empathizing with users' needs, defining key problems, generating solution ideas, prototyping the application, and testing its usability in authentic learning environments. Participants used the application continuously over a two-week period, after which both quantitative and qualitative data were collected to assess system effectiveness and user experience. The findings indicate that the Songtun application effectively supports students in organizing academic tasks, reducing the likelihood of forgetting assignments, and encouraging more timely submission behaviors. Overall user satisfaction was reported at a high level <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(M=4.36, S D=0.52)$</tex>, with usability receiving the highest ratings among evaluated dimensions. These results suggest that integrating Design Thinking principles into the development of educational digital tools can significantly enhance learner experience and promote self-regulated learning practices. The study highlights the potential of the Songtun application as an innovative academic support solution tailored to the needs of students in Thai higher education institutions.

title: Enhancing Battery Efficiency and Driving Safety: Advanced State of Charge Estimation Using the Extended Kalman Filter Algorithm
authors: Ashraf A. Shanaq, Subramaniam Ganesan, Debatosh Debnath, Mohamed A. Zohdy
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Accurately estimating the battery module's state of charge (SOC) is critical in electric vehicle design. The process is essential in providing operators with precise and continuous mileage information. The accuracy of SOC estimation also helps mitigate the risk of overcharging or discharging the battery, ensuring overall driving safety. A fundamental aspect of SOC estimation methodology involves constructing a suitable battery model to achieve heightened response accuracy. This study proposes an electric vehicle SOC model that utilizes the extended Kalman filter algorithm. The research focuses on lithium-ion power batteries and uses real-world data to conduct SOC estimation investigations. The study's findings demonstrate that the proposed model exhibits accuracy when the internal battery temperature aligns with the ambient temperature at 25°C. The model has minimal terminal voltage differences, and the average voltage difference is 9mV. Applying the extended Kalman filter algorithm at room temperature significantly improves the recovery percentage of SOC voltage, surpassing an average of 73%, thereby demonstrating high accuracy. The study employs the extended Kalman algorithm to estimate SOC current waveforms, specifically emphasizing discharge currents of 4A. The simulation results showcase exceptional estimation accuracy and robust applicability in estimating SOC under these conditions. The proposed SOC model utilizing the extended Kalman filter algorithm enhances accuracy and reliability in estimating SOC for lithium-ion power batteries. The results of this study have significant implications for the design and development of electric vehicles, particularly in optimizing battery performance and ensuring driving safety.

title: Revisiting 1998 Torque Data: a Machine Learning Analysis of Time Series Data for Tapping Experiments
authors: Alaa Al Najjar, Nakul Havaldar, Valentin Plenk, Marco Linß
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of real-time tool condition monitoring in tapping processes using machine learning techniques, with a focus on cross-material generalization and robust fault detection. The study leverages a historical dataset from 1988, comprising 2,195 tapping experiments on two steel alloys- 16 MnCr 5 and 42 CrMo 4 -monitoring torque (Mz) signals to predict binary quality outcomes (good/bad) based on defined quality criteria. To overcome limitations in prior work, the authors introduce a feature extraction method that captures both amplitude and duration characteristics across distinct phases of the torque signal,. The evaluation framework includes increasingly challenging train/test splits: random, run-wise holdout, and cross-material (training on 16 MnCr 5, testing on 42 CrMo 4), enabling assessment of real-world generalizability. Multiple machine learning models are tested using both raw time-series data (after cleaning and normalization) and engineered features. Results show Matthews Correlation Coefficients (MCC) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.40-0.41$</tex> under cross-material testing-indicating moderate but meaningful generalization across materials with different mechanical properties. This performance level suggests that fundamental physical regularities in successful tapping produce consistent torque signatures, enabling transferable detection of anomalies without retraining. Findings support the feasibility of plug-and-play monitoring systems in agile manufacturing environments, where minimal setup and broad applicability are essential.

title: Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning and Multimodal Architectures for Zero-Shot Rare Disaster Recognition
authors: Amrutavarshini Beernalli, Pooja Pattar, Vaishnavi Pagad, Rohan Dixit, Uday Kulkarni, Veena Hunasimarad
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Natural disasters have increasingly required rapid and reliable automated recognition because of their far-reaching effect on human life, infrastructure, and environmental safety. With the increasing availability of visual data from satellites, drones, and online platforms, Machine Learning and Deep Learning Methods have played an important role in image based disaster identification. However, conventional supervised models have faced difficulty when rare disaster categories contain limited or no labeled data. Multiple architectures were evaluated in this study for zero shot learning setting and compared Pure CLIP RN50, EfficientNetB0+B1 combined and fine tuned, ResNet50, and a hybrid ResNet50 paired with CLIP RN50 embeddings. The models were trained in four seen disaster classes and evaluated in three unseen disaster types. The ResNet50 + CLIP RN50 hybrid model achieved the highest performance, achieving 98% accuracy on seen classes and 99.92% accuracy on unseen categories, outperforming the other approaches. The findings reflected that multimodal fusion significantly improved zero-shot generalization and contributed to a more reliably recognized disaster of rarity.

title: A Compact Multi-Band Microstrip Antenna with π–H Radiator and Plus-Shaped DGS for mmWave 5G/6G Applications
authors: Kuldip Kumar, Basit Mohi Ud Din Naikoo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This Research paper describes a plus-shaped defected ground structure (DGS) based high-gain triple-band π– H slotted microstrip patch antenna, and is simulated and designed for millimeter-wave (mmWave) applications in 5G and 6G communication. The design idea is motivated by the bandwidth enhancement techniques explained in [1], wherein the optimization of a π-slotted dual-band rectangular microstrip antenna was made with response surface methodology (RSM).Here, another H-slot is incorporated within the π-slot structure to enhance impedance matching further and create another resonance, and a plus-shaped DGS is implemented in the ground plane to increase bandwidth and gain.The antenna is proposed employing Rogers RT6002 substrate having a dielectric constant of 2.94 and thickness of 1.6 mm, simulated in CST Microwave Studio. The designed antenna has triple-band operation at 53 GHz, 59 GHz, and 71 GHz with reflection coefficients of −33 dB, −50 dB, and −37 dB, respectively. The VSWR values are 1.049, 1.0084, and 1.0286, which are good impedance matches in all the bands. The antenna offers gains of 5.45 dBi, 4.13 dBi, and 6.10 dBi and directivities of 6.9 dBi, 6.09 dBi, and 8.02 dBi, respectively. The introduction of the π–H slot combination and plus-shaped DGS greatly increases radiation efficiency and multiband behavior, rendering the antenna a good choice for compact, high-gain 5G and mmWave devices.

title: Port-Hamiltonian Modeling and IDA-PBC Control of an Extended SEPIC Converter for Bidirectional V2X Applications
authors: Hatem SGHIRI, Marouan MARZOUG, Chaouki Mnasri, Faouzi BACHA
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: This paper proposes a rigorous modeling and control strategy based on the port-Hamiltonian (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex>) framework applied to a bidirectional DC-DC extended SEPIC converter intended for Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) applications. Unlike commonly used idealized averaged models, the proposed formulation explicitly incorporates dissipative effects arising from parasitic resistances of inductors, equivalent series resistances of capacitors, and switching component losses. The system is described using canonical energy variables, ensuring intrinsic consistency of power exchanges and dissipation. A Lyapunov based stability analysis is carried out, and an Interconnection and Damping Assignment Passivity-Based Control (IDA-PBC) law is developed to guaranty output voltage regulation under bidirectional power flow operation. The desired equilibrium point is analytically computed from the dissipative steady-state equations, avoiding the use of iterative numerical methods. MATLAB/Simulink simulation results confirm the theoretical validity of the proposed approach and demonstrate robust regulation under load variations.

title: A Data-Driven–Expert Hybrid Model for Real-Time Battery State of Health Diagnostics
authors: Ibrahim Zafar, Chodagam Srinivas, Regadi Madhurima, Kenchappa Gari Yogananda, Tanguturi Mohammad Sadiq, Gopireddy Sindhusree
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Accurate estimation of a battery’s State of Health (SOH) is essential for the safety and longevity of energy storage systems used in electric vehicles and renewable energy applications. This paper presents a hybrid intelligent battery management framework that fuses fuzzy logic inference with support vector machine (SVM) regression to improve SOH prediction under diverse operating conditions. Multi-sensor data capacity fade, internal resistance, temperature, vibration, and humidity were synthesized in MATLAB to evaluate realistic degradation behavior over 100 charge-discharge cycles. A Mamdani fuzzy inference system first encodes expert knowledge to generate a preliminary SOH estimate under uncertain or noisy conditions. This estimate is then combined with the original sensor features and processed by an SVM regressor with optimized hyper-parameters, enabling the model to capture complex nonlinear relationships. Comparative evaluations show that the hybrid method reduces estimation bias, achieves lower RMSE, and provides higher R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values than standalone FIS or SVM approaches. The results confirm that integrating expert rules with machine learning improves robustness and interpretability, offering a practical solution for real-time SOH monitoring in advanced battery management systems.

title: Design of Wireless Charging Unmanned Vehicle Based on Electromagnetic Compatibility
authors: Haiyan Wu, Liqiang Sheng, Xicheng Zhu, Haoyu Cui
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: To enhance the system stability, charging efficiency, and operational safety of wirelessly charged autonomous vehicles in complex electromagnetic environments, this paper conducts a systematic design study addressing electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) issues. By analyzing the coupling mechanism between the high-frequency electromagnetic fields of wireless charging systems and the sensitive electronic equipment in autonomous vehicles, an interference propagation model is established, and a comprehensive EMC design approach is proposed, encompassing interference source suppression, propagation path control, and protection of disturbed devices. The paper elaborates in detail on the electromagnetic optimization of the wireless charging system' s circuit topology, layout and shielding strategies for onboard electronic equipment, vehicle-level grounding and filtering network design, as well as interference mitigation techniques based on software algorithms. Research results demonstrate that by establishing a multi-level EMC protection framework, internal and external electromagnetic interference can be significantly reduced, achieving highly reliable coordination between wireless charging and autonomous driving functions. Real-vehicle test data indicate that the optimized vehicle meets the requirements of international standards such as ISO 11451 and CISPR 25 in terms of both radiated emissions and immunity, with peak radiated emissions reduced by up to 16 dB, the proportion of time exceeding the threshold for radar ranging error decreased by 94.9%, and GNSS positioning loss-of-lock phenomena completely eliminated. Under dynamic charging conditions, the vehicle's path-tracking lateral error is reduced by 65.6%, validating the feasibility and superiority of the proposed solution in improving overall vehicle EMC performancet.

title: Self-Adaptive Artificial Intelligence Systems for Large-Scale Data-Driven Decision Making
authors: Md Jubayar Hossain, Mohammed Majid Bakhsh, Mohammad Abdus Sami, Md. Ishtiaque Alam, Md Mehedi Hassan Melon, Mia Md Tofayel Gonee Manik
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The growing magnitude, diversity, and speed of data produced across contemporary cyber-physical, enterprise, and cloud-edge systems has revealed inherent constraints with the inflexible artificial intelligence (AI) models regarding providing real-time choices. Self-adaptive artificial intelligence systems have become an attractive paradigm to overcome these challenges through enabling continuous learning, dynamic model reconfiguration and context-specific optimization in the changing data distributions and operational constraints. In this paper, a detailed self-adaptive AI architecture to serve large-scale decision making with data is provided, which involves online learning, feedback-based adjustment, and automatic policy optimization. The suggested architecture takes advantage of the hybrid intelligence methods merging deep learning, reinforcement learning, and meta-learning to reach strong performance in non-stationary and uncertain environments. Adaptive control loops are incorporated to check data drift, system performance and the consumption of resources and recalibration of a model can be done in real time without involving human intervention. The comprehensive test on large-scale datasets indicates that the proposed solution is much more accurate in making decisions, flexible and resilient to systems against the traditional non-adaptable and semi-adaptive AI models. The outcomes confirm the effectiveness of self-adaptive intelligence in aiding scalable, dependable, and explainable decision-making of dynamic data-intensive applications including smart infrastructure, autonomous systems, and intelligent business analytics.

title: Unequal Error Protection Scheme Based on Implicit Partial Product-LDPC Codes
authors: Xuemei Zhu, Jiawei Duan, Zhicheng Huang, Xiaodong Zhang, Kuan Wu, Qianfan Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This paper proposes an unequal error protection (UEP) scheme based on implicit partial product-LDPC codes. All rows of the source data array are encoded by a low-density parity-check (LDPC) code, while only a prescribed subset of information columns is further protected by a Bose–Chaudhuri–Hocquenghem (BCH) code. The check information generated by the column code is not transmitted explicitly. Instead, it is superimposed on the row LDPC codewords through a free-ride coding mechanism. In this way, the selected columns, referred to as the most important data (MID), are protected by both the row code and the column code, whereas the remaining columns, referred to as the less important data (LID), are protected only by the row code. A turbo-like iterative decoding algorithm is then adopted for the proposed scheme, consisting of free-ride decoding, row decoding, column decoding, and row re-decoding. We further present an approximate performance characterization for the doubly protected MID and a genie-aided lower bound for the singly protected LID. Simulation results show that the proposed scheme provides a substantial reliability gain for the MID, while the LID performance is not sacrificed and can even outperform that of standalone LDPC coding in the medium-to-high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) region.

title: A Multi-Stage BERT-Based Cognitive Framework for Conversational Business Intelligence in SMEs
authors: Dipali Baviskar, Harsh Halwai, Anand Patekar, Anoushka Kachewar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Social media is becoming a more important tool for small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs) to watch competitors, evaluate customer sentiment, and identify new market opportunities. However, it is challenging to convert raw signals into understandable, actionable insights due to the unstructured, fast-moving nature of cross-platform data. A conversational business intelligence layer and a multi-platform trend analytics pipeline were established in earlier work on this project. The multi-stage family of BERT-based models that serve as that system’s cognitive foundation is the particular subject of this research. The approach uses Sentiment-BERT for polarity-sensitive refinement, NER-BERT for business-entity extraction, SBERT for semantic retrieval over trend-aware embeddings, and Intent-BERT for query categorization. Within a single pipeline, each model maintains compatibility while addressing a different reasoning layer. We explain how these models are modified for SMB use scenarios, how they work together to facilitate end-to-end conversational intelligence, and how they open up new avenues for real-time decision support, low-resource fine-tuning, and domain adaptability. This paper focuses specifically on the cognitive foundation of that system, presenting a unified multistage architecture built on BERT-based models.

title: Comprehensive Performance and Risk Profiling of ERIDS: A Resilient Ensemble-Based Intrusion Detection Framework
authors: Niyaz Hussain AMJ, Naveen Kilari, Seeniappan Kaliappan, Ramya M
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Modern networks face increasingly sophisticated threats that outpace signature-based defences. This paper presents ERIDS, a resilient ensemble intrusion-detection system engineered to boost detection accuracy, reliability, and resistance to adversarial manipulation. ERIDS fuses heterogeneous base detectors classical classifiers (SVM, RF), deep temporal models (CNN, LSTM, Transformer), and reconstruction autoencoders using calibrated stacking and an out-of-fold meta-learner, and augments alerts with a structured asset-impact risk-profiling module. The Evaluation of ERIDS on CIC-IDS2017, UNSW-NB15, and proprietary zero-day traces, including tests with adversarial perturbations, measuring detection performance, latency, and resilience. ERIDS achieves F1 ≈ 0.96, AUC ≈ 0.992, latency ≈ 3.2 ms/flow, and robustness ≈ 0.89, outperforming single models and naïve ensembles. Our results indicate that ensemble diversity combined with calibrated risk scoring provides actionable decisions for real-time SOC operations, while highlighting the necessity of continuous calibration and drift monitoring. Researcher also report compute-cost trade-offs and demonstrate improved operational triage in realistic SOC deployment scenarios.

title: Content Aware Community Detection Under Simulated Attacks
authors: Yasasree Lasya Annambhotla, Asi Kuushalie, C V Sree Pranavi, Velumury Varshita, Niharika Panda
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The emergence of the massive social sites has led to more connectivity than ever, yet has set the stage of organized adversarial activities like the bot networks, astroturfing activists, and synthetic communities designed to influence the masses. The condition is problematic to traditional community detection algorithms, in which structural connectivity is the primary factor, since artificial nodes and edges can be used to inflate cluster boundaries. This means that there is a significant gap that is revealed by this vulnerability, most clustering methods work on clean graphs, and social graphs in reality are not clean, are noisy, and actively manipulated. The project directly responds to such robustness gap with the assistance of a multi-view fusion framework where structural, temporal and content-based information is merged into a deeper, more reliable representation of user interactions. To take robustness to a greater extent, we add trust propagation based on behavior interpretation of anomalies. Community detection is carried out by down-weighting suspicious connections by assigning trust scores to nodes and to edges, depending on the posting entropy, burstiness, semantic diversity, and bot-likelihood. It is then followed by adversarial simulation and different degrees of synthetic attacks are employed to test the system. The suggested strategy generates stable communities with aggressive manipulation,s creating greater modularity, lowering trust drift, enhancing bot isolation, and stable community assignments between attack intensities. In general, the framework provides a multi-signal defense against adversarial community manipulation, which is quantifiable.

title: Adaptive Spatial-Temporal Feature Fusion Network for Multilingual Text Recognition
authors: Praetawan Jarutan, Sirawan Phiphitphatphaisit, Zagon Bussabong, Artitayaporn Rojarath, Olarik Surinta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Multilingual scene text recognition presents significant technical complexity due to diverse character structures, varying scripts, background clutter, and contextual dependencies among character sequences. This study proposes an Adaptive Spatial-Temporal Feature Fusion Network (ASTFF-Net) for multilingual scene text recognition. The framework incorporates a multi-layer adaptive spatialtemporal feature fusion architecture into a sequence-based text recognition, integrating a spatiotemporal feature extractor with a Connectionist Temporal Classification (CTC) decoder. A deep ResNet-50 backbone captures hierarchical spatial representations, which are reshaped into ordered sequences to preserve horizontal structural information. Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) layers model contextual dependencies across adjacent character regions, while a one-dimensional convolution enhances temporal consistency. An adaptive fusion mechanism combines spatial and temporal features to generate discriminative representations that encode both visual structure and contextual continuity. The end-to-end training strategy with the CTC objective supports alignment-free sequence learning without character-level annotation. Experimental evaluation on the Multi-language Video Subtitle (MLVS) and Thai-English Multi-script Text Image (MSTI) datasets show that the proposed method consistently outperforms convolutional-recurrent and attention-based baselines. The proposed ASTFF-Net achieves competitive character error rates, yielding 1.63 % on MLVS and 13.22 % on the challenging MSTI dataset, while exhibiting stable convergence and robust generalization.

title: A Secure Intrusion Detection System Against DDoS Attacks Using Machine Learning
authors: M.R.O Aswath Prakash, R Gayathri, M Braveen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: The key benefit of cloud computing lies in its flexibility to adapt to diverse environments, allowing systems to scale up or down based on demand. However, this flexibility also necessitates robust protection against Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attacks to minimise downtime and its associated impacts. This study presents a hybrid machine learning framework that combines all three: K-Nearest Neighbours (KNN), Naive Bayes (NB), and Support Vector Machine (SVM), along with a Random Forest ensemble classifier, for the detection and classification of DDOS attack types. By combining a variety of algorithms within the proposed system, it successfully complements their nature to improve detection results and is more robust to evolving attack patterns, achieving higher accuracy. The framework utilises both a public dataset and a generated dataset to enhance generalisation and real-world applicability. It employs a hybrid feature selection method combining a genetic algorithm and chi-square to select the most relevant features. Integrated with ThingSpeak, an IoT analytics platform, the system enables continuous, real-time visualisation of key performance metrics. Additionally, adaptive thresholding mechanisms are incorporated to dynamically adjust detection sensitivity in response to varying traffic conditions and attack intensities. This approach demonstrates strong capability in identifying DDoS threats while maintaining scalability, making it suitable for large-scale deployment.

title: Exploring Generative Pretrained Language Models for Opinion Mining in Large-Scale Twitter Social Media Data
authors: Siddhesh Amrale
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Opinion mining has gained a significant role in the last few years for the text data analysis coming from various sources like Twitter, Facebook, Google, and Amazon. It enables companies to enhance their business strategies in a proactive manner. The term "sentiment analysis" refers to the practice of collecting and categorizing customer feedback according to their positive, neutral, or negative feelings using specialized software. This paper article presents an enhanced sentiment analysis framework, which relies on the transformer-based language model to classify and study social media data. The proposed approach delineates the textual context and the semantic relations very clearly, therefore, gives better precision than the traditional machine learning models. The model uses the preprocessing, tokenization, and fine-tuning methods to deal with a variety of linguistic expressions and noisy user-generated text. Experimental findings show that the proposed GPT and ETC model attained 99.23% and 96.10 accuracy, which is much higher than the current models including Naive Bayes (NB), CNN-BiLSTM, SVC (Support Vector Classifier), Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) and Random Forest (RF). The general result is that transformer-based architecture, especially GPT, is capable of reaching out to long-range textual dependencies and emotional context which leads to higher performance in the classification. These results confirm GPT’s robustness, adaptability, and high generalization potential for sentiment analysis across complex and noisy social media data.

title: Research on Network Anomaly Detection Based on Improved SMOTE and XGBoost: Addressing Class Imbalance and Missing Data
authors: Hanzhou Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of network technologies and the escalating sophistication of cyberattacks, network anomaly detection has become a pivotal cornerstone of modern cybersecurity defense. However, it is plagued by two persistent bottlenecks: class imbalance (where abnormal samples account for only a tiny fraction of datasets, leading traditional models to favor majority classes) and missing data (which erodes feature integrity and distorts detection accuracy). To resolve these issues, this study proposes a hybrid anomaly detection framework integrating Improved SMOTE (ISMOTE), Multiple Imputation by Chained Equations (MICE), and XGBoost: MICE preserves feature reliability while handling missing data, ISMOTE mitigates class imbalance without excessive noise, and XGBoost enables efficient, robust detection. Evaluated on CSE-CIC-IDS2018 and UNSWNB15 datasets, the framework boosts abnormal sample detection rates by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 12 \%$</tex> compared to baseline methods (e.g., Traditional SMOTE+XGBoost) while maintaining high precision, verifying its effectiveness in addressing both core challenges and offering practical value for real-world cybersecurity scenarios.

title: Self-Optimizing Automated Learning Pipeline for Big Data Exploration and Predictive Intelligence Applications
authors: Hrushikesh Panchabudhe, Nikhil Mangrulkar, Rohit Lanjewar, Shekhar Nipane, Aayush Borkar, Tanmay Zalke
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The rapid increase in big data, which is characterized by volume, velocity, variety, veracity, and value, has placed a highly urgent need on scalable, secure, and automated machine learning (ML) solutions. The traditional ML pipelines may require a lot of manual tuning, do not operate with efficiency in terms of data management and are scaled in a distributed environment. In this paper, the Self-Optimizing Automated Learning Pipeline towards Big Data Exploration and Predictive Intelligence Applications is introduced, which is an end-to-end framework that comprises secure data ingestion, MinIO-based distributed storage, and Parquet-optimized data handling to improve its functionality and efficiency. To reduce human effort, the proposed system is an automated preprocessing, feature engineering model selection, and containerized deployment system based on Python modules. The interface is configurable, with a number of checkboxes, and is capable of supporting heterogeneous datasets and workflows which can be customized. The pipeline supports various ML models, such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Transformer networks, and classic algorithms, such as XGBoost and Random Forest, that allow it to be flexible to wide range of analytical tasks. Experimental results using large-scale datasets reveal significant performance improvements - Transformer model takes 32% of the time and has 28% better storage efficiency as well as up to 94.8% accuracy and the Transformer modeling model is faster than any other predictive model. Besides, user-owned MinIO buckets improve the privacy of data and facilitate distributed processing that is scaled. On the whole, the suggested system provides an efficient, safe, and high-performance system to big data analytics, setting the base to further developments in federated learning and real-time analytics and autonomy in governing the model.

title: Designing Secure Cryptographic Communication Protocols for Wireless Control of Medical Devices in Smart Healthcare Systems and IoT-Enabled Smart Hospitals
authors: Vandana Roy, Nishi Jain, Disha Maity, Vivek Kushwaha, Swathi Pacha, Suman Punia
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Medical device communication with control servers should be secured, as the failures in the flourishing environment of smart healthcare systems and IOT-enabled hospitals may lead to patient harm and data loss. Conventional cryptography solutions are usually unable to accommodate the various harsh resource and latency requirements of a medical IoT setting. To overcome all these challenges, an innovative solution called Secure Lightweight Cryptographic Protocol for Medical IoT (SLCP-MIoT) is presented. The particular protocol, which is formulated to facilitate the use of wireless communication that is authenticated, confidential and also integrity assured, is targeted to operate within the dynamic and heterogeneous network comprising of wearable and implantable medical devices. The suggested solution introduces some key exchange techniques that are efficient, light-weight encryption, and integrity verification techniques to provide end-to-end security without imposing heavy computational overheads. Its hierarchical design allows real-time user communications, periodic session key handshaking and replay resistance without running afoul of performance. Moreover, the protocol involves secure identity provisioning and mutual authentication process that ensures that devices trust the hospital infrastructure. Adopting a two-pronged approach to security and efficiency, SLCP-MIoT is efficient in solving the problematic healthcare application requirements, when secure and reliable control of healthcare devices is required in real-time. The SLCP-MIoT technique achieved an overall accuracy of 98.7% in safe interaction and authentication of devices in smart medical environments.

title: Source Enumeration by Integrating Toeplitz Reconstruction with Elbow-based Method
authors: Xueqin Song, Xiaogang Liu, Bingbing Qi, Desheng Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: An improved Toeplitz-constrained elbow-based source enumeration (EBSE) algorithm is proposed for robust estimation of the number of sources in adverse environments. First, the Toeplitz structure is exploited to modify the covariance of the received data; subsequently, eigenvalue decomposition is performed to extract the eigenvalues, which serve as the input to the ensuing EBSE algorithm. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves stable source enumeration under conditions of low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), small snapshots, and coherent sources, significantly enhancing performance at only a modest computational cost.

title: A Secure and Energy-Efficient Cyber-Physical System Framework for Next-Generation Industrial Automation
authors: Divyashree H S, VidyaSagar Adabala, Kasi Viswanathan, Vinay Kulkarni, A Mutharasan, Hazari Naresh
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The dynamic increase of on-line physical connections of the next-generation industrial automation demands a safe and energy efficient system to boost the potentialities of real time operation, predictive analytic and high control capabilities. The world that is already industrialized should not only possess the relevant systems which will keep watch over the communications routes, but also possess systems which will be able to optimize the use of the energy besides being dependable alongside the manner in which the networked devices interact with the networks. The model proposes an adjustable structure that can be utilised in achieving confidentiality, integrity, and availability of industrial information using artificial intelligence, blockchain-linked trusts, and spindly packages of cryptography. It will consume less electric power and will show fewer delays at the end in case you collocate energy conscious technologies as well as smart technologies at the border. An emergency countermeasures approach confronts the occurrence of immature threats and intelligent surveillance targets aberration accomplishment. Moreover, the framework concerns the interaction of the heterogeneous hardware with the infrastructures of the clouds computing, and the increase in the outlay at the needs of users on the very large scale of industries. Energy optimization is achieved in regards to dynamic load balancing, and dynamically based choices. The model can help to intimately automate significant infrastructure because security and efficiency form two basic constituents. The plan forms an avenue to the strengthening, intelligent, and nomadic industrial systems according to the objectives of industry 5.0. The high criteria required were successfully met by the proposed system with 96% data integrity of security and 95% policy flexibility, which showed utmost reliability.

title: Rolling Bearing Fault Diagnosis Based on RBMO-VMD and Multi-Scale Deep Attention Network
authors: Pengyuan Zou
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This paper addresses the issue that convolutional neural networks (CNNs) compromise signal temporal characteristics during feature extraction. It proposes a rolling bearing fault diagnosis method combining Red-billed Blue Magpie Optimisation Variational Modal Decomposition (RBMO-VMD) with a Multi-scale Deep Attention Network (MDANet). Firstly, the RBMO algorithm adaptively optimises key VMD parameters, effectively preventing modal aliasing and achieving precise vibration signal decomposition. Building upon this, fourteen dimensionless and dimension-dependent features are extracted to form a feature set. For fault classification, a multiscale deep attention network is designed. This architecture captures features across different temporal scales through parallel multiscale convolutional layers, while incorporating an attention mechanism to adaptively enhance key information. Combined with deep fully-connected layers and regularisation techniques, this enhances the model's generalisation capability. Experiments on the CWRU bearing dataset demonstrate that this approach achieves an average diagnostic accuracy of 99.87% for ten fault states, validating the proposed method's effectiveness and robustness in feature extraction and fault classification.

title: Impact of Deployment Strategies on Wireless Sensor Networks Performance under Security Threats
authors: Aina Mehta, Jasminder Kaur Sandhu, Harshal Patil
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Due to their decentralized architecture, Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are particularly vulnerable to attacks targeting the network layer. This paper investigates the impact of three deployment strategies—Random, Grid, and Hierarchical—on WSN performance under sinkhole attack scenarios. Simulation-based analysis is conducted to evaluate key performance metrics, including E2E, throughput, and packet delivery ratio (PDR). Results indicate that Grid deployment achieves the highest resilience, with a PDR of 98.1%, while Hierarchical deployment offers balanced performance. In contrast, Random deployment experiences severe degradation, with PDR dropping to 26.8% and delay increasing significantly. These findings underscore the critical role of deployment strategy in ensuring network robustness under adversarial conditions. Future work will explore additional network-layer threats such as wormhole and blackhole attacks and focus on prioritizing the integration of intelligent learning models with simulation tools to enable adaptive detection and mitigation of security threats in dynamic WSN environments.

title: Cryptocurrency Price Prediction Using Deep Learning and Ensemble Methods
authors: Harsh Shivhare, Rekha Kaushik, Uday Deshmukh
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Cryptocurrency markets are characterized by extreme volatility and a decentralized nature, making price predictions very challenging. This work performs a comparative evaluation of a statistical, machine learning, and deep learning methodology for forecasting Bitcoin prices using one year of daily data from March 2024 to February 2025. The models compared are ARIMA, Prophet, XGBoost, LSTM, GRU, Bi-LSTM, and an ensemble LSTM–GRU. Experimental results are presented after comparing machine learning and deep learning models with statistical baselines. It is observed that the best performance is achieved through an LSTM–GRU ensemble with RMSE = 2489.04 and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.533. The results emphasize the efficacy of hybrid neural models in capturing nonlinear dynamics in cryptocurrency markets and present future opportunities in Transformer-based forecasting models.

title: Smart Dietary Swaps: Machine Learning for Nutrient-Aware Food Substitution
authors: Mulakala Vrisheeka, Malladi Nanda Gopal, Suresh Anand M
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This study introduces Smart Dietary Swaps, a machine-learning–driven, nutrient-aware food substitution system that makes recommendations to improve diet while taking into account user health conditions and preferences. Foods were categorized into healthy, moderate, and unhealthy food classes based on nutritional variables including calories, protein, carbohydrate, fat, fiber, sodium, micronutrients, and food type. Two datasets used for training were introduced: nutrient profiles and a large, comprehensive recipe dataset, and four methods were trained, including KNN, RF, a PCA-based MLP, and GNN. Each method was then evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score variables. The GNN outperformed the other methods for both datasets, achieving accuracy scores ranging from 87 to 89% and an F1 score of 95% that remained stable, further suggesting that the GNN generalized well and was balanced in its classification scheme. The PCA-based MLP performed well with accuracy scores of 86-85% and an F1 score of 93%, while KNN and RF achieved moderate results. The system captures user-centric health indicators (i.e., BMI, allergies, age-based nutrient requirements, etc.) and biochemical markers (i.e., iron, zinc, cholesterol, glucose), providing flexibility and personalized food substitution recommendations. In summary, the findings of this study suggest that graph-based learning, particularly the GNN approach, can be used as a reliable and effective basis for developing intelligent, data-driven personalized dietary recommendation systems.

title: Feature-Optimized Blood Glucose Level Prediction with Explainable AI (XAI)
authors: Aditya Das Gupta, Md. Nahin Irtiza, Shihab-Al-Rashid
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Diabetes Mellitus is a global health problem where precise blood glucose prediction is crucial for patient well-being. Although machine learning (ML) models have shown promise, their “black-box” nature often hinders clinical use due to lack of transparency. This study offers a clear and accessible way to develop a blood glucose prediction model that is both accurate and reliable. This research introduces a step-by-step process with a comparison of different feature optimization techniques, and demonstrates that removing outliers can greatly enhance the model’s performance. The study uses a public dataset of 16,969 physiological records to train and validate the models, and shows that removing outliers can significantly improve the model’s performance. The enhanced LightGBM model achieved a Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of $15.57 \mathrm{mg} / \mathrm{dL}$ on the test set. Importantly, our work includes modern Explainable AI (XAI) methods, such as SHAP and LIME, to make the model’s decision process fully transparent. This work helps bridge the gap between predictive accuracy and clinical trust, offering a strong framework for creating trustworthy AI in diabetes management.

title: Polynomial Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks for Fault Detection and Diagnosis in Industrial Systems
authors: Lazhar Khriji, Majdi Mansouri, Khadija Attouri, Abdelmalek Kouadri
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Fault Detection and Diagnosis (FDD) is critical for ensuring safety, reliability, and efficiency in modern industrial systems. Data-driven approaches have gained prominence due to their ability to handle nonlinear processes without relying on exact physical models. However, many machine learning methods lack interpretability, limiting their deployment in safety-critical environments. This paper proposes the use of Polynomial Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (PolyKAN) for FDD in nonlinear industrial processes. PolyKAN employs polynomial basis functions in a Kolmogorov-Arnold Network framework, enabling transparent and structured nonlinear modeling. The approach is validated on a real cement rotary kiln process under healthy and faulty conditions. Experimental results demonstrate high fault classification accuracy with improved interpretability compared to conventional deep learning models. PolyKAN provides a practical and explainable framework for industrial fault diagnosis.

title: Leveraging Texture Learning Bias for Image Super-Resolution
authors: Heng Bai, Jungang Yang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Deep learning methods have significantly advanced image super-resolution (SR) techniques, but still face challenges in texture-dense regions. Existing works focus on enhancing the ability to extract and leverage texture information, overlooking the relationships among different textures. In this work, we reveal the texture learning bias phenomenon in image SR. Through a cross-texture correspondence analysis, we harvest some unique insights, based on which we propose a method to explicitly utilize the texture learning bias for image SR. In our method, we design multiple expert modules to obtain specific texture learning bias, and design a routing module for expert selection. Experimental results demonstrate that our method can be applied to different networks to achieve promising performance in recovering different texture details.

title: Research on Coordination Technology between Large and Small Models in Transmission Line Defect Detection
authors: Qingping Zhang, Shuang Zhang, Zhenhua Yan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: To address the issue in traditional intelligent detection of transmission line defects where small models have limited recognition capabilities for complex defects, while large models consume significant computational resources and have slow inference speeds, this paper proposes a collaborative technology combining “fast screening with small models, deep analysis with large models, and global decision optimization”. By constructing a reasonable collaborative architecture, this technology integrates the high-precision advantage of large models with the high-efficiency characteristics of small models, aiming to achieve rapid and accurate detection of transmission line defects. The article first analyzes the design ideas and execution processes of three core collaborative architectures: series, parallel, and hybrid. Subsequently, it studies data collaboration, training collaboration, and inference collaboration technologies to address issues such as uneven data distribution, model parameter mismatch, and inference decision conflicts during the collaborative process. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed technology is analyzed through typical application cases. The analysis shows that the collaboration of large and small models in transmission line defect detection can effectively resolve the contradiction between real-time performance and accuracy in defect detection, thereby improving detection efficiency.

title: Machine Learning-Based Early Risk Stratification Framework for Chronic Kidney Disease Progression Using Comprehensive Multi-Domain Clinical and Biochemical Data
authors: Md. Maniruzzaman, Naima Najam Nejum, Musfika Jannat Mamata, Hasebul Hassan Chowdhury, Fahim Ahamed Romit, Sudoy Kumer Ghosh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Chronic kidney disease (CKD) is a major health problem in the world today with the need to ensure that risks are stratified at an early age to avoid an irreversible loss of renal functions. The paper provides a machine learning architecture that combines extensive demographic, lifestyle, comorbidity, laboratory biomarker, medication, and environmental exposure information to predict CKD progression. Additional processing included median imputation, categorical encoding, outlier suppression and filtering use of variance. Dimensionality reduction used ANOVA F-test and principal component analysis in order to determine discriminative features such as serum creatinine, serum glomerular filtration rate, albumin-creatinine ratio, blood pressure indices, and electrolyte markers. Four algorithms, including the ridge regression, support vector machines, random forest, and gradient boosting were assessed by means of repeated stratified cross-validation. The gradient boosting had a better performance with an area under curve 0.867, accuracy 93.1, F1-score 96.3 and Brier score 0.048, which shows the best discrimination and calibration. The framework allows specific risk measurement that facilitates timely nephrology referral, individualized therapeutic intervention and evidence-based disease management planning in clinical practice.

title: Gait-Driven Pneumatic Pressure Accumulation Utilizing Multi-Stage Compression
authors: Taiki Yamaguchi, Kazuki Higashi, Kensuke Harada
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper proposes a pneumatic generation mechanism that enables compact and self-contained air compression based on a multi-stage accumulation process. The proposed method employs a serially connected multi-cylinder configuration, in which each stage operates with a deliberate phase difference to efficiently accumulate compressed air within a limited volume. Furthermore, by incorporating an intermittent knock-cam mechanism, the multi-stage compression process can be mechanically driven by periodic external forces such as human gait. In this study, the proposed mechanism was implemented as a shoe-mounted device, where the knock action is switched at every step, thereby realizing stepwise pressure accumulation through walking. This structure enables high-pressure air generation up to approximately 1 MPa in a fully mechanical manner without relying on electrical or chemical energy sources. Both simulation and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed mechanism achieves stepwise compression and functions as a compact, high-pressure, and power-free pneumatic source that has been difficult to realize with existing wearable pneumatic generation approaches.

title: LLM-Augmented Stiefel Graph Neural Networks for Zero-Shot Spatio-temporal Forecasting_supp1-3689935.pdf
authors: Liang Xie
publish: N/A
abstract: Spatio-temporal time series (STTS) play a crucial role in domains such as traffic forecasting, energy scheduling, and financial analysis. However, accurate and efficient prediction remains challenging due to the complex dynamic dependencies across temporal and spatial dimensions. Existing Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) often struggle to balance effectiveness and efficiency when modeling dynamic spatio-temporal relations. Meanwhile, Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit strong capabilities in modeling long-range dependencies and generalization under few-shot and zero-shot conditions, yet their ability to capture spatio-temporal structures remains limited. To address this, we propose LAD-SGNN, a unified framework that integrates the advantages of graph-based and language-based modeling. Specifically, LAD-SGNN employs Spectral Graph Convolution on the Stiefel manifold (SGSC) together with Linear Dynamic Graph Optimization (LDGOSM) to efficiently extract dynamic spatio-temporal features with nearly linear complexity. Structured prompts and a spatio-temporal alignment mechanism are then designed to fuse the extracted dynamic spectral information with task semantics, which are fed into a lightweight LLM to achieve unified modeling of spatio-temporal structures and semantic reasoning. In this framework, SGSC efficiently represents dynamic spatial dependencies in the spectral domain, while the prompt-based alignment strategy explicitly injects spatio-temporal information into the LLM, thereby enhancing generalization across datasets. Extensive experiments on multiple real-world spatio-temporal datasets demonstrate that LAD-SGNN significantly improves prediction accuracy in both zero-shot and few-shot scenarios.

title: AdaTracker: Learning Adaptive In-Context Policy for Cross-Embodiment Active Visual Tracking_supp1-3692075.mp4
authors: Fangwei Zhong
publish: N/A
abstract: Realizing active visual tracking with a single unified model across diverse robots is challenging, as the physical constraints and motion dynamics vary drastically from one platform to another. Existing approaches typically train separate models for each embodiment, leading to poor scalability and limited generalization. To address this, we propose AdaTracker, an adaptive in-context policy learning framework that robustly tracks targets on diverse robot morphologies. Our key insight is to explicitly model embodiment-specific constraints through an Embodiment Context Encoder, which infers embodiment-specific constraints from history. This contextual representation dynamically modulates a Context-Aware Policy, enabling it to infer optimal control actions for unseen embodiments in a zero-shot manner. To enhance robustness, we introduce two auxiliary objectives to ensure accurate context identification and temporal consistency. Experiments in both simulation and the real world demonstrate that AdaTracker significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in cross-embodiment generalization, sample efficiency, and zero-shot adaptation.

title: Intelligent Policy Analyzer for Privacy and Legal Documents Using Machine Learning and Intelligent System
authors: R Akiladevi, Anand S, Barathraj V L
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Privacy policies, terms of service, cookie policies, software licenses, and employment contracts are critical documents that regulate online services and workplace agreements. However, their extensive length, dense legal language, and intricate structures often render them inaccessible to ordinary users, resulting in low readership and a lack of true consent or awareness. This creates tangible risks, including data misuse, lost rights, privacy violations, and unexpected liabilities, highlighting the pressing need for an accessible solution. The principal objective of this project is to develop an intelligent analyzer that leverages machine learning and advanced natural language processing techniques to automatically interpret, segment, and summarize complex privacy and legal documents. Users can interact with the system through a conversational interface, enabling them to obtain plain-language explanations, receive answers to specific queries, and access key insights about their rights, obligations, and data protection practices. The scope of this work encompasses a broad spectrum of legal and policy documents, not limited to privacy policies but also covering terms of service, software licenses, and employment contracts. While the tool provides comprehensible summaries and interactive explanations, it serves as an assistive platform rather than a replacement for professional legal counsel. The methodology integrates state-of-the-art machine learning models such as BERT, GPT, and domain-adapted Legal-BERT, utilizing labeled datasets like OPP-115, APP-350, and PolicyQA for robust performance. The system workflow includes document ingestion, clause segmentation, context-aware summarization, and chatbot-driven Q&A. Emphasis is placed on usability, accuracy, and data privacy throughout the design. The anticipated outcome is a user centric, time-saving application that democratizes access to legal understanding and policy transparency. This tool has direct applications for individuals, employees, corporate users, compliance managers, and researchers in need of rapid, reliable insights into legal documentation.

title: Image Dehazing of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles Based on Physics-Guided Diffusion Model
authors: Yuxin Zhu, Jun Li, Qinghong Sheng, Xiao Ling, Bo Wang, Fan Gao, Piji Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Drone vision is often degraded by cloud occlusion, which causes blurring or loss of surface observation information and significantly hinders subsequent image processing and interpretation tasks. Notably, fine details of ground objects can sometimes remain partially visible beneath thin clouds. Meanwhile, recent advances in diffusion models have demonstrated strong generative capabilities, making them promising candidates for addressing thin cloud occlusion. In this paper, we propose a physics-guided image dehazing framework for drone vision based on diffusion model. The framework operates in two stages. In the first stage, a multi-scale non-local U-Net, guided by the atmospheric scattering model, is designed to estimate physical parameters such as the transmittance map and global atmospheric light. In the second stage, the strong generative capacity of the diffusion model is integrated with the physical priors obtained in the first stage, endowing the diffusion process with physical awareness to better compensate for information loss caused by clouds.Extensive experiments on both synthetic and real-world datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art approaches.

title: Logarithmic Diffusion Model with Structure-Aware Guidance for SAR Image Synthesis
authors: Xinlong Liu, Ting Chen, Xiangli Yang, Hai Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Generative models offer a promising solution for synthetic aperture radar (SAR) image generation to address the few-shot learning problem in automatic target recognition (ATR). Recently, diffusion models have emerged as a powerful class of generative models, which generate images by iteratively reversing a predefined forward diffusion process. However, applying diffusion models to SAR image generation remains challenging, due to the in herent speckle noise and non-Gaussian distribution of SAR images. To address these challenges, we propose SarLogDiff: a diffusion based framework that explicitly adapts to SAR images through logarithmic diffusion and structure-aware guidance. SarLogDiff defines its forward and reverse processes using the logarithmic diffusion, and generates samples conditioned on the structure-aware guidance. The logarithmic diffusion enables SAR image distribution to approach a Gaussian distribution, while the structure-aware guidance preserves the high-level semantics from conditional input (e.g., a reference image) during generation. Extensive experiments demon strate that SarLogDiff can generate high-quality SAR images, and the synthetic samples significantly enhance the ATR performance. Code is available at https://github.com/Kong-CT/SarLogDiff.

title: Hierarchical Agentic RAG: A Cloud-Agnostic Orchestration Protocol for Multi-Hop Reasoning Over Heterogeneous Enterprise Data
authors: Abhijit Ubale, Siva Kantha Rao Vanama, Rama Krishna Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The organizations working across multiple cloud services encounter many difficulties that impact the integration and reasoning of different sources of heterogeneous data. These hurdles include the interoperability, scalability, and compliance of these systems. To improve upon the current limitations, this research proposes a cloud-independent orchestration protocol based on a Hierarchical Agentic RAG Framework. The framework includes a multi-layered agent structure consisting of agents such as decomposing agents, retrieval agents, and synthesis agents, which work together to enable a more efficient method for reasoning through multiple hops of data regardless of the source (i.e., structured, semi-structured, or unstructured) from within the organization. The proposed protocol allows for the decoupling of the reasoning workflow from the dependencies on any specific cloud platform, which allows for flexibility in the ongoing changes that may happen with any of the service providers that an organization may utilize. The experimental evaluation presented demonstrates that the framework has an accuracy of 93% while outperforming traditional RAG models and existing, cloud-dependent orchestration protocols, in terms of precision, recall, and F1 Score. Furthermore, the system has demonstrated strong levels of scalability, interoperability, and adaptability within a very complex enterprise environment. The proposed protocol is especially applicable in operations/organizations where data governance and cross-cloud intelligence will be necessary; such as finance, healthcare, and government. Collectively, this research provides a reliable, scalable, and efficient means for enterprise knowledge orchestration and accelerating reasoning processes throughout the distributed cloud ecosystem.

title: Machine Learning-based Crop Recommendation Systems: A Comprehensive Survey and Future Research Directions
authors: PalaPala SivaGanesh, Tirunagari MohanKrishna, J. Vijitha Ananthi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Crop analysis and prediction is an increasing and significant factor that enhances agricultural practice globally. Crop recommendation assists farmers to choose the most suitable crops that suit their soil type and the climate in the area. Previously, farmers used to depend on professional advice or family-inherited knowledge, which required considerable time and individual efforts and, in many instances, made mistakes. As the global population is projected to reach nearly 9.7 billion by 2050, the demand for sustainable food production that preserves soil health has become increasingly critical. Machine learning approaches can be used as a solution to the problem by automating crop recommendations and detecting the first symptoms of pests or diseases. This assists farmers to raise their yields, maintain land soils, and make the land to remain productive over a long period. This review work focuses on seven popular machine learning algorithms with a large historical dataset of soil nutrient (nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium), pH value, temperature, humidity, and rainfall. The future direction gives explicit crop recommendations that suit every location enabling farmers to both increase production, employ sustainable techniques, make greater profits, make better choices and require professional assistance significantly less often. This approach supports sustainable agriculture and helps address future food demands.

title: EERA: A Hybrid Ensemble Regression Model for Accurate Time Series Prediction in IoT Applications
authors: Ajit Patil, Amol Potgantwar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: IoT systems generate continuous sensor streams that often contain irregular fluctuations, noise, and nonlinear behaviours, making accurate forecasting a persistent challenge. Traditional regression models capture specific characteristics of such data but frequently show unstable performance when evaluated across different IoT environments. To address this limitation, this paper introduces EERA+, a hybrid ensemble regression framework that combines REPTree, SMOreg, and a Multi-Layer Perceptron through a two-stage fusion process involving error-based weighting and a stacking meta-regressor. This design enables the ensemble to balance short-term variability with long-range trends, resulting in predictions that are both accurate and consistent.EERA+ is tested on three real IoT datasets—the UCI Appliances Energy dataset, the Intel Berkeley Laboratory sensor dataset, and the Household Power Consumption dataset—representing smart-home energy usage, environmental sensing, and large-scale power monitoring. Across the datasets, the proposed ensemble records clear improvements over individual models and standard regressors. On the Appliances dataset, EERA+ achieves an MSE of 0.118, RMSE of 0.343, and an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.986, improving accuracy by 8–32% compared to the base models. On the Intel sensor data, the ensemble reduces MAE by 29% and MAPE by 34%, demonstrating stability under noisy readings and sensor drift. For the power-consumption dataset, EERA+ reaches an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.972, outperforming all baseline regressors while maintaining consistent error reductions across different time windows.These results confirm that combining diverse learning behaviours through a structured ensemble can improve forecasting reliability in practical IoT settings. The study also shows that a single unified model can generalize across distinct IoT domains, highlighting its suitability for real-time monitoring and decision support.

title: SmartData Interlock: An Access Control Architecture and Data Exchange Model for Secure Data Ecosystem in Smart Buildings
authors: Ravi Sharma, Avinash Ramachandruni, Ekaterina Petrova, Pieter Pauwels
publish: IEEE Transactions on Privacy
abstract: Smart buildings increasingly rely on heterogeneous data sources, including real-time Internet of Things (IoT) streams, point cloud scans, and Building Information Modelling (BIM), to optimise energy consumption, safety, and operational efficiency. However, the integration of these data sources introduces significant challenges, particularly in terms of ensuring user privacy and data sovereignty across distributed systems. Building operators, tenants, service providers, and emergency responders often require access to overlapping datasets with different legal, organisational, and contextual constraints. Without appropriate governance mechanisms, data sharing risks violating privacy regulations and exposing sensitive information. To address these challenges, this paper first discusses the development of Personalised Data Hubs (PDHs) based on the International Data Spaces (IDS) concept, which integrates multiple data sets into a unified, compliant framework. Building on PDHs, we present the SmartData Interlock (SDI) model, a graph-based, sovereignty-aware access control and data exchange architecture that enables controlled data sharing, allows users to define their own access policies, and ensures regulatory compliance. SDI extends Category Based Data Access (CBDA) models with purpose binding, delegation semantics, contextual constraint enforcement, and executable policy graphs for lifecycle-aware smart building governance. Using a realistic smart building policy graph, this work evaluates SDI performance and shows that secure data exchange can be achieved with bounded computational overhead, confirming its practical viability in smart building applications and urban planning.

title: PSO-Optimized Deep Learning Model for ECG-Based Arrhythmia Detection in Wearable Devices
authors: Maheswari. S, M. Venith Vijay, Umadevi. G, N. Hemavathy, Ganapathy. G, Praveen Kumar. A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The identification of heart diseases is an acute issue because the patterns of ECG signals are difficult to interpret, and traditional diagnostic frameworks are constrained. To overcome this, a Walrus Optimization-Assisted LSTM-based model is developed to enhance predictive precision by utilizing clinical and ECG data. It combines the learning of temporal features with LSTM and the adaptive hyperparameter optimization with the Walrus Optimization Algorithm, which guarantees stable convergence and a better model of the cardiac abnormalities. The overall accuracy of experimental assessment shows 98.42%, the sensitivity of 97.85%, the specificity of 98.11%, the F1-score of 98.27%, and the reduction of false positives by 16% relative to conventional deep-learning models. Also, the optimized model has a convergence rate that is 21% faster and is more resistant to noise and data imbalance. These findings validate the ability of the proposed framework to provide reliable predictive heart disease information in real-time, which has high clinical potential for deployment in automated diagnostic and monitoring systems.

title: Parametric Modeling and GPR Imaging of Planetary Lava Tubes Constrained by Terrestrial Analogs
authors: Chunyang Zhao, Ling Zhang, Zhijun Huo, Shuo Han, Rui Gao
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Lava tubes are priority subsurface targets on basaltic planetary bodies, and Ground Penetrating Radar (GPR) is a primary detection method. However, interpretation is often limited by forward models relying on idealized cavities, which underestimate scattering effects caused by geometric asymmetry and boundary roughness. We introduce a parametric modeling and imaging workflow constrained by terrestrial analogs. Using measured cross-sections from the Pyroduct database, we embedded realistic cavity geometries into a lunar stratigraphic model defined by correlated random permittivity. Finite-Difference Time-Domain (FDTD) simulations were performed with a 60 MHz source, followed by Kirchhoff pre-stack time migration. Unlike ideal cavities, analog-constrained models produce asymmetric diffractions and local phase discontinuities. Quantitative analysis shows that realistic morphological complexity severely degrades the signal-to-clutter ratio (SCR) of ceiling reflections, which drops from 80.67 dB in the idealized model to 25.19 dB in the analog-constrained model. Migration analysis suggests that while ceiling location and lateral extent are reliably constrained, the tube floor remains difficult to resolve. Field GPR data from a terrestrial lava tube show similar ceiling-dominant responses, validating this geometric prior model for evaluating performance in future planetary missions.

title: Dynamic Policy Learning for Legged Robot with Simplified Model Pretraining and Model-Homotopy-Inspired Transfer_supp1-3692082.mp4
authors: Hae-Won Park
publish: N/A
abstract: Generating dynamic motions for legged robots remains a challenging problem. While reinforcement learning has achieved notable success in various legged locomotion tasks, producing highly dynamic behaviors often requires extensive reward tuning or high-quality demonstrations. Leveraging reduced-order models can help mitigate these challenges. However, the model discrepancy poses a significant challenge when transferring policies to full-body dynamics environments. In this work, we introduce a continuation-based learning framework that combines simplified model pretraining and model-homotopy-inspired transfer to efficiently generate and refine complex dynamic behaviors. First, we pretrain the policy using a single rigid body model to capture core motion patterns in a simplified environment. Next, we employ a continuation strategy to progressively transfer the policy to the full-body environment, minimizing performance loss. To define the continuation path, we introduce a parametric transition path from the single rigid body model to the full-body model by gradually redistributing mass and inertia between the trunk and legs. The proposed method achieves faster convergence and demonstrates superior stability during the transfer process compared to baseline methods. Our framework is validated on a range of dynamic tasks, including flips and wall-assisted maneuvers, and is successfully deployed on a real quadrupedal robot.

title: Multi-Adapter LLMs: Dynamic Mixture of LoRAs for Scalable Personalization
authors: Varun Kotte, Rohit Joshi, Supratim Dutta, Ravindra Rajasekhar Kavuru
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable generalization and reasoning capabilities, but their performance in domain-specific or user-personalized contexts remains limited by the inefficiency of fine-tuning full model parameters. This paper explores a modular approach using multiadapter Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) techniques, dynamically mixed at inference time to achieve scalable personalization across diverse tasks. The study examines hybrid adaptation strategies for optimizing latency, parameter efficiency, and model interpretability within large-scale environments. A proposed architecture integrates dynamic adapter selection, prompt-based routing, and attention reweighting mechanisms that enable flexible, task-aware adaptation. The work concludes with case studies and evaluations highlighting substantial gains in personalization quality and compute efficiency across multiple benchmarks.

title: Kidney Tumor Detection in CT Scans: Combining 3D CNN and Transformer Networks
authors: B. Venkatesu Goud, P. Phanindra Kumar Reddy, P. Sai Varshitha, T. Sruthi, N. Lakshmi Narayana Reddy, K. Harsha Vardhan Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: A novel hybrid deep learning algorithm is proposed to detect kidney tumors on CT scans, which is a combination of 3D convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and transformer networks capability. In the framework, volumetric processing consists of sequence modeling and thus, the framework is useful in the representation of local texture as well as the global contextual relationship among the multi-slice CT volumes. The model employs the ConvNeXt-inspired 3D backbone to preserve the spatial relations among slices, and then, the transformer encoder is introduced that predicts the long-range dependencies among slices with attention mechanisms, and enhances the accuracy of the slices detection. It has such innovations as a depth-sensitive patch embedding layer that maintains the inter-slice interconnections of 3D convolutions and 2D attention maps, adaptive positional encodings to handle variable slice spacing in CT scans, and a multi-scale feature fusion module, which combines complementary information between the CNN and transformer pathways. The advances improve high sensitivity and specificity of the model on detection of malignant lesions, cysts and kidney stones. Performance of the model training on large-scale data of kidney CT volumes with normal and pathological cases is very high, and inferencing time is just okay in clinical processes. The hybrid methodology is favorable (in comparison with traditional 3D CNNs and pure transformer models) in terms of its capacity to handle slice-to-slice variations without the need to lose fine spatial details. The study shows that hybrid architectures will be able to expedite volumetric medical imaging to result into a major solution that would advance diagnostic AI within the radiology discipline.

title: Mathematical Framework for Harmonic Analysis of Cluster Stream Buffer Switching in MMC HVDC: Chebyshev-Based Approach and Insulation
authors: Chan-Ki Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
abstract: This paper presents a novel analytical framework for harmonic characterization of the Cluster Stream Buffer (CSB) switching method in Modular Multilevel Converter (MMC)-based Voltage Source Converters (VSCs). Due to the nonlinear nature of Nearest Level Control (NLC), conventional Fourier-based approaches are not well suited for harmonic analysis of CSB. To address this limitation, a Chebyshev polynomial-based method is proposed, enabling a closed-form representation of harmonic components as functions of the modulation index and switching level. The proposed approach provides improved analytical insight and computational efficiency compared to conventional methods. Furthermore, a design methodology integrating harmonic analysis with dielectric stress evaluation is introduced for optimal switching level selection. The results show that a minimum of 33 levels is required under typical conditions, while higher levels (e.g., 49 levels) may be necessary under increased harmonic stress.
