The analysis shows that the most severe causes of perceptual audio degradation are atmospheric turbulence, beam wander, attenuation due to fog and pointing instability, which result in amplitude fading, jitter, and dropout. Modulation schemes and signal-processing methods currently used are not designed to be optimized to meet the continuity and latency requirements of audio signals, even at high rates. Pointing, acquisition and tracking limits are other limitations that limit stability, particularly in mobile or non-terrestrial platforms. Nevertheless, the development of adaptive optics, multimodal FSO, optical computing, and quantum communication can present effective efforts to enhance audio fidelity. On the whole, the review indicates the necessity of audio-focused FSO research and suggests specific future directions, namely, perceptual assessment, low-latency modulation design, sophisticated PAT systems, and safe optical audio systems.

title: VISTA-LoRa: Task-Oriented Semantic Visual Transmission for Ultra-Low-Bitrate LoRa
authors: Muhammad Toaha Raza Khan
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Long Range (LoRa)’s ultra-low bitrate and strict payload constraints make conventional visual transmission infeasible for embedded Internet of Things (IoT) nodes. This letter presents VISTA-LoRa, a fully deterministic and training-free task-oriented semantic framework that transmits only clarity-selected and morphology-adapted visual regions required for a downstream detector. The VISTA-LoRa pipeline integrates clarity-aware spatio-temporal Region of Interest (ROI) selection, an airtime-normalised morphology-variant optimiser, and a Low-Frequency/High-Frequency (LF/HF)-weighted Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) encoder with Reed-Solomon (RS) protection, followed by multi-scale semantic verification at the receiver. Experiments on real tracked frames show that VISTA-LoRa achieves the highest semantic reliability among the evaluated schemes, obtaining a mean receiver-side confidence of 0.78 and a 4.5–6.4× payload reduction while preserving perfect structural consistency. Such a payload-efficient, high-confidence semantic uplink is particularly valuable for delay-tolerant visual IoT applications such as remote inspection, environmental monitoring, and low-power autonomous systems that must operate within the severe energy, bandwidth, and duty-cycle restrictions of LoRa-class networks. Despite using stronger quantisation and spatial reduction, VISTA-LoRa maintains 91.7% of the original semantic confidence compared to 75.0% for baseline approaches under identical constraints demonstrating its suitability for visual reporting over ultra-low-bitrate LoRa links.

title: HMEPR: A Hierarchical Multimodal Emotion Recognition Framework with Redundancy-Aware Feature Refinement
authors: Bo Zhong, Jia Li, Yeling Xu, Aoduo Li, Rongpei Zhang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Multimodal emotion recognition (MER) aims to infer affective states from heterogeneous inputs such as text, audio, and visual signals. In practice, however, redundant and conflicting information across modalities can weaken cross-modal alignment and impair downstream fusion, especially when emotionally irrelevant tokens or noisy modality-specific patterns are present. To address this problem, we propose HMEPR, a hierarchical multimodal emotion recognition framework built on an alignment-refinement-fusion paradigm. The framework first employs a Cross-Modal Interaction Alignment (CMIA) module to enhance semantic consistency through bidirectional interaction and gated alignment. It then applies a Redundancy-Aware Feature Refinement (RFR) module based on Cross-Modal Native Sparse Attention (CNSA) to suppress redundant non-text information by means of text-guided sparse connectivity, semantic hash grouping, and dynamic routing, while preserving emotionally salient cues. Finally, a Multi-Scale Multimodal Feature Fusion (MMFF) module aggregates the refined representations through multi-scale context modeling and adaptive channel-spatial weighting. Experiments on CMU-MOSI and CMU-MOSEI show that HMEPR achieves competitive performance against representative baselines. Ablation studies and additional analyses confirm the contribution of each component, particularly the role of the redundancy-aware refinement strategy in improving alignment quality and fine-grained affect modeling. Overall, the results indicate that explicitly inserting a refinement stage between alignment and fusion improves robustness to redundant multimodal cues.

title: AI-Enhanced Career Guidance System for Personalized Career Pathways
authors: Ramchandra. H. V, Pragathi M, Srishti P Desai, T Mythri, Chittiboina Sai Niharika Yadav, Gagana V
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The Artificial Intelligence (AI) idea is changing education and the work sphere, and thus the career guidance system needs to be developed on a high level. The traditional methods of counseling that can be subjective and have a limited scope are incapable of fitting the range of available academic, behavioral, and labor-market data. We conduct studies on the AI-supported methods of career advice, such as rule-based, machine learning models, and deep learning architectures as well as hybrid ML-NLP frameworks. These systems are based on structured and unstructured data in production of personalized recommendations. Nevertheless, there are still limitations which include limitations in interpretability, data set(s) variety, semantic reasoning, and adaptive feedback systems. The emerging career guidance systems are to have multimodal inputs, semantics as well modeling and the application of iterative learning to suit the changes in the skill requirements.

title: A Hybrid CNN-LSTM Model for Multimodal Biometric Authentication via ECG and Respiration
authors: Ganeshan R, M.Neha, P.Vasudev
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Biometric authentication systems have mostly used physical traits like fingerprints, facial recognition, or iris patterns. But these techniques can be spoofed and attacked by the environment. In order to overcome these problems, physiological indicators like Electrocardiogram (ECG) and respiratory rates have been used as trustworthy and dependable biometrics in tackling these problems. We create a hybrid CNN-LSTM-based deep learning model in this work to identify the person by using a set of ECG and respiration signals. We remove noise and baseline drift with a frequency range of 0.540 Hz, and we remove real breathing cycles with a frequency range of 0.10.35 Hz. The two signals are standardized and divided into 5 seconds windows and then fed into the model. The CNN derives spatial relationships on the signal morphology whereas LSTM derives temporal relationships resulting in robust feature learning. The accuracy and stability of the experimental results is high among different individuals and this confirms that ECG and respiration patterns are distinguishable, just like fingerprints. This paper highlights the opportunities of physiological signal-based multimodal biometrics as a real-time authentication system to be incorporated with hardware in healthcare and security services.

title: Effect of HMD Frame Rate Reduction on Attentional Orienting in a Driving Simulator
authors: Sora Maehara, Wataru HASHIMOTO, Satoshi NISHIGUCHI, Yasuharu MIZUTANI
publish: 2026 IIEEJ International Conference on Image Electronics and Visual Computing (IEVC)
abstract: This study examines whether drivers' attention in a simulated driving environment can be directed by temporarily reducing the frame rate of the head-mounted display (HMD) when the participant gazes at specific objects. In our experiment, the frame rate of HMD was dynamically reduced based on gaze fixation, and we assessed its impact by measuring participants' accelerator-release reaction times in response to the sudden appearance of target objects. The results demonstrated that reaction times were significantly reduced when the target appeared within the HMD field of view under reduced frame rate conditions, suggesting that transient frame rate reduction can function as an effective attentional cue without the user consciously noticing it. These findings point to a practical approach for designing VR training scenarios that aim to direct user attention while maintaining a high level of immersion, and they highlight the importance of further research into optimal parameter settings and individual variability.

title: Real-Time Fake Profile Detection using Behavioral Biometrics
authors: T.Sangeetha, N. Deepak, S. Dhanapal, A. Kishore Aravind
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Beyond single identifying factors, the system compares a wide variety of profile features to match user profiles and detect fraudulent accounts. provides for several similarity measures and attribute priority, which improves the accuracy of fake account detection and profile matching. Profile photos, lengths of full names and descriptions, numerical ratios in usernames and full names, external URLs, privacy settings, and social activity metrics (posts, followers, and following) are some of the features that use Logistic Regression and Exploratory Data Analysis (LEDA) algorithms to match and identify user profiles and distinguish between genuine and fraudulent accounts. The framework proposes a feasible solution for intra-social network operations such as data integration, enrichment, information retrieval, and false account detection. The experimental results indicate that the framework performs better than current solutions.

title: High-Precision Stereo Matching Based on Selective Attention and Residual Cost Aggregation for Real-Time Autonomous Driving on Edge Devices
authors: Bifa Liang, Yichao Wang, Ziyang Hu, Zhicong Huang, Haifeng Hu, Jianming Xu, Dihu Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Stereo matching is essential for depth estimation in autonomous driving, often balancing accuracy and efficiency, especially on resource-constrained edge devices. High-precision networks demand heavy computation, while speed-optimized ones compromise accuracy. This work presents SARNet, a real-time, high-precision stereo matching network for autonomous driving, designed for efficient deployment on the edge device NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin. We develop SAFE, a Selective Attention Feature Extractor (SAFE) that integrates patch layers, convolutions, and attention mechanisms for stereo feature extraction. To generate high-quality disparity candidates, we employ a Group-wise L2 Distance to construct a full-range yet compact cost volume. Additionally, the Enhanced Residual Cost Aggregation (ERCA) module enhances cost aggregation accuracy while maintaining efficiency. To further improve precision, we propose a Confidence-weighted Residual Refinement (CWRR) post-processing module that jointly predicts residuals and per pixel confidence, weights residual updates by confidence to suppress erroneous corrections in low-confidence regions, and incorporates a lightweight channel–spatial attention to boost representational capacity. SARNet employs a four-stage selective attention-based residual framework, using a coarse-to-fine strategy and residual computation to balance accuracy and speed. Optimized with TensorRT, SARNet exceeds real-time requirements, making it ideal for autonomous depth perception. Extensive experiments confirm its superior speed-accuracy trade off over existing stereo matching networks.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning–based Adaptive Routing for VANETs with Real-Time Congestion Awareness
authors: P. Janani Durga, V.Mani, R.Suresh, S.Arivumani Samson, S.Saranya, S. Manibharathi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Vehicular Ad-Hoc Networks (VANETs) experience dynamical changes in topology as well as traffic jams, which significantly deteriorates the routing performance. To address this dilemma, the current paper presents a deep learning-based adaptive routing architecture that dynamically chooses the routing paths by exploiting real-time congestion information. The routing problem is modeled in terms of sequential decision making, where the learning agent monitors values of the traffic density, the vehicle speed and the congestion states so as to maximize the routing actions. Realistic congestion patterns have been included in order to add flexibility to the different traffic conditions. As has been shown by the experimental evaluation, the proposed approach achieves a routing accuracy of 95.6%, which is 19.3% points higher than the conventional approaches to routing involving the use of the static routing algorithm. In addition, the model promotes congestion -state discrimination with an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.964 thus suggesting effective decision making process in dynamic settings. These findings verify that incorporation of congestion-aware learning significantly enhances the efficiency of routing, dependability and resiliency in the VANET environment that is highly dynamic.

title: Real-Time Intrusion Detection in IIoT/OT Networks Via Traffic-Feature Forecasting: A Testbed Study
authors: Mhamad Bakro, Andrea Marotta, Walter Tiberti, Ottorino Odoardi, Ivano Salvatore, Piergiuseppe Di Marco
publish: 2026 IEEE 22nd International Conference on Factory Communication Systems (WFCS)
abstract: Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) and operational technology (OT) networks are increasingly integrated with physical processes, enabling real-time sensing and actuation while widening the cyber-physical attack surface. Enterprise intrusion detection systems (IDS) often rely on payload inspection, signatures, or high-dimensional engineered features that are difficult to deploy in OT and may yield high false-positive rates while missing some anomalies. This work presents a lightweight IDS for IIoT/OT environments that relies only on passive, per-second traffic telemetry and residual-based forecasting. From mirrored traffic, we extract low-cost statistical features, forming a dataset representation without payload parsing. We emulate denial-ofservice (DoS) flooding and injection/replay manipulations, and use simple rule-based thresholds to label traffic and construct a supervised benchmark dataset. We evaluate two forecastingbased detectors: (i) a supervised Random Forest (RF) forecaster trained on rule-labeled data, and (ii) a one-class Long ShortTerm Memory (LSTM) forecaster trained exclusively on normal traffic (label-free). Real-world experiments on a prototype PLC-to-motor-drive control loop with an external attack platform show that an LSTM-based, residual-driven temporal forecasting approach enables a robust and scalable IDS suitable for timingconstrained IIoT/OT deployments, achieving 100% detection with zero false alarms in the evaluated scenarios.

title: Scaled Output Consensus for Euler-Bernoulli Beam Networks under Dual Communication Graphs
authors: Bao-Zhu Guo, Qing-Qing Hu, Feng-Fei Jin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control of Network Systems
abstract: This paper studies scaled output consensus for a network of Euler-Bernoulli beam systems subject to external disturbances acting through all channels. The boundary feedback in slope and angular velocity is transmitted over two distinct communication graphs. A virtual disturbance aggregator and an active disturbance rejection control (ADRC)-based scheme are developed to estimate and reject the total disturbance entering the control channel, while a virtual leader reformulates the objective as leader-follower synchronization. Exponential stability of the closed-loop error system is proved mainly using the Huang-Prüss criterion, yielding scaled output consensus. Numerical experiments validate the proposed approach.

title: SCZ-HA: A Seamless Cross-Zone Handover Authentication Scheme for V2I Communication
authors: Xia Feng, Ruomeng Lin, Kaiping Cui, Liangmin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Vehicle-to-infrastructure (V2I) communication connects vehicles and transportation infrastructures (e.g., road-side units, 5G base stations), facilitating bidirectional information exchanges. As a matter of fact, V2I communication is constrained by the limited wireless coverage, which forces vehicles to undergo cross-zone handovers frequently. Existing protocols for cross-zone handover either incur high overhead or neglect the risks of attacks during service disconnections. To tackle these concerns, we propose a seamless cross-zone authentication scheme tailored for V2I communication, called SCZ-HA. Drawing inspiration from the zone encryption scheme (EuroS&P 2020), we integrate long-term and short-term credentials to optimize efficiency and enhance security. When entering a new zone, vehicles use their valid long-term credentials to request short-term credentials from the local infrastructure. These short-term credentials are then encapsulated and distributed to neighboring zones using Shamir's secret sharing, thereby reducing communication overhead. During cross-zone handovers, vehicles only present encapsulations for verification by the target infrastructure, thus eliminating the need to validate full credential contents. Unlike previous schemes, SCZ-HA avoids redundant verifications, enabling millisecond-level handovers and supporting traceability of malicious vehicles. Experimental results show that our scheme achieves a substantial reduction in authentication latency, with an average improvement of 70.54% compared to state-of-the-art schemes.

title: Numerical Simulation of Transmission Tower Collapse Caused by Galloping of Iced Conductors
authors: Junbo Liu, Ji Wang, Zhaoqing Chen, He Liu, Hongda Dong, Jingyao Luan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Power Electronics and Electric Drives (PEED)
abstract: In this study, a tower-line system model was established in nonlinear finite-element software. Aerodynamic forces considering variations in wind attack angles were applied to simulate the galloping process of the tower-line system. The extreme stress and displacement responses of the tower at different locations before and after bolt loosening under galloping loads were compared. Finally, the collapse mechanism of the transmission tower caused by bolt loosening during ice-coated galloping was studied. Studies show that without considering bolt loosening, transmission towers can withstand galloping loads induced by ice accretion equal to the conductor’s diameter. After bolt loosening occurs, the internal forces inside the tower undergo redistribution, leading to a steep rise in the stress of neighboring components and a marked enlargement of the tower top displacement. The collapse process follows a chain-like path: single-point bolt, loosening, stress redistribution, aggravated load on adjacent joints, multi-point bolts loosening, structural system instability.

title: Strategies in Managing Online Learning Assignments
authors: Shiyrah Theosebes Sela, Christine Paulina, Djuria Suprato
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Education and E-Learning
abstract: None

title: Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE Workshop on Microelectronics and Electron Devices (WMED)

title: OCR Model Comparison: EasyOCR vs. Keras-OCR on TextOCR Dataset
authors: Prashant Singh Sangroya, Nishant, Deepika
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This study compares two popular open-source Optical Character Recognition (OCR) frameworks, EasyOCR and Keras-OCR, using the challenging TextOCR dataset. A clear process involving data preparation, model setup, and cloud-based inference execution ensures fair and repeatable testing. The evaluation looks at important metrics like precision, recall, character error rate (CER), and word error rate (WER). Results show that EasyOCR, with its transformer-based design, performs well in multilingual and low-resolution situations. Meanwhile, Keras-OCR provides faster inference and works better with clean, structured text that includes stylized fonts. A significant contribution of this work is its direct comparison of these two models in real-world scenarios, such as dealing with curved, noisy, and cluttered text, which fills a gap in current research. The findings give useful advice for choosing OCR tools for document digitization, mobile apps, and edge deployment. Future research will focus on creating hybrid models that balance accuracy and processing efficiency across different use cases.

title: AI-Based Multi-Class Classification of Mood Disorders Using Psychological and Behavioral Features
authors: Patatri Acharya, Sudipta Acharya
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Automated detection and classification of mood disorders can play a critical role in supporting clinical diagnosis and treatment. This study employs supervised machine learning techniques to determine the presence or absence of mood disorders based on behavioral and psychological features. Individuals are first classified as either having a mood disorder or being in a Normal (non-disorder) state. If a mood disorder is detected, the model further classifies the condition into one of three clinically significant categories: bipolar I disorder, bipolar II disorder, or major depressive disorder. This specific task has not been explored in prior research. A dataset with mental health assessment data of 120 patients was obtained from the Kaggle website, having features like mood swing, sadness, optimism, sleep disorder, suicidal thoughts, etc. Data preprocessing was done using Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique(SMOTE) for class balancing, manual encoding for handling categorical values and feature normalization. Twelve classification models were evaluated: Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Naive Bayes, Decision Tree, Random Forest, XGBoost, AdaBoost, Gradient Boosting, CatBoost, LightGBM, and a multi-layer perceptron(MLP). Experiments were conducted using both 80:20 and 70:30 train–test splits. The CatBoost model achieved the highest performance, giving an accuracy of 87.5% with a 80:20 split and an accuracy of 86.11% with a 70:30 split. The KNN model achieved the lowest performance, giving an accuracy of 63.89% with a 70:30 split and an accuracy of 54.17% with 80:20 split. These results demonstrate the potential of machine learning-based frameworks in aiding clinical diagnosis and treatment.

title: Sleep-Wake Estimation using Frequency Analysis of Electroencephalogram Signals
authors: Effat Mahajabin Ahona, Afifa Halim Prapti, Maysha Rahman Trisha, Md. Bashir Uddin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Diagnosis of sleep diseases like sleep apnea and insomnia requires a precise estimation of wake and sleep states. Using a single-channel electroencephalogram (EEG) signal, this work proposes an automated algorithm for sleep-wake segment estimation and determination of sleep parameters. 200 polysomnography (PSG) records along with the related demographics and manual scorings (annotations) from the sleep heart health study (SHHS) database were collected at random for this study. EEG signals were extracted from the PSG records and separated into development and validation. After preprocessing, EEG signal was divided into 30 -second epochs. The continuous wavelet transform (CWT) was used to estimate the frequency components from each epoch of EEG signal. To separate sleep segments from wake segments, a rule-based thresholding technique was used. The proposed technique produced the output in form of total sleep time (TST), total wake time (TWT), and sleep efficiency (SE). The total amount of time spent in each sleep stage is known as TST, whereas the sum of all wake episodes recorded during the total recording time (TRT) is defined as TWT. SE is defined as the percentage of bedtime (total hours in bed) that is spent asleep. From the epoch-based analysis, the algorithm obtained 87.79% accuracy, 91.67% sensitivity, 75.14% specificity, and an $F 1$ score of 0.91. Strong agreement between the algorithm and annotation was achieved by Bland-Altman plots. The mean bias for TST, TWT, and SE was resulted in -0.04 hours, -0.05 hours, and -0.32 %, respectively. The results indicate that the proposed automated technique has the potential to be incorporated into wearable and home-based sleep monitoring systems.

title: A Deep Neural Network Approach to Model-Based Engineering and Resource Scheduling in Industrial IoT (IIoT) Environments
authors: Swati, Arvind Pujari, Archana Mishra, M. Suhasini, Shalini Vaishya, V. Divya
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This paper aims to present the use of a DNN-based Model-Based Engineering technique for improving resource scheduling in Industrial IoT systems. Cloud computing with IoT has offered strategic improvements to industrial automation through dynamics such as connectivity. The proposed DNN-MBE work builds an improved machine learning technique to manage the difficulties in process control like energy control and shelling, and different tasks like operation of energy controlling pump voltage employing deep learning mathematics. Accomplishments include a coherent approach to combining DNN with MBE processes, thus providing immediate optimization of reference signals and power. The validation of the framework was established in a stepwise manner and exemplified by evaluating the mean square error, energy consumption and computational costs which was lower than those associated with conventional MBE strategies. Furthermore, the DNN-MBE approach provides IoT devices and cloud structure a method to have large and reliable interaction toward applying Industry 4.0 paradigm. This paper shows that the integration of DNN to MBE has the capability to revolutionize IIoT applications to enhance large-scale efficiency, lower latency, and controllable cost for industrial automation systems.

title: IoT-Enabled Predictive Maintenance for Gas Flow Meters in Urban Gas Networks: A Case Study on Intelligent Service Systems
authors: Kang Xia
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: The rapid urbanization in China has amplified demands on urban gas networks, where gas flow meters are essential for safe and efficient distribution yet vulnerable to failures like corrosion and flow anomalies. This study presents an IoT-enabled predictive maintenance framework, drawing from a case of a Chinese gas company managing 2,206 non-residential clients. Traditional maintenance relies on periodic inspections, leading to inefficiencies amid increasing client volumes from policies such as bottle-to-pipe conversions and biannual inspection mandates. The proposed system integrates ultrasonic sensors, AI-driven analytics, and real-time monitoring to detect anomalies, automate alerts, and facilitate closed-loop hazard management. A mixed-methods approach analyzes quantitative data from 390 inspections in 2023 and 270 in 2024, revealing 101 and 228 rectified hazards, respectively, alongside qualitative insights into operational challenges. Results indicate a 20-30% reduction in maintenance costs (15% in inspection labor, 8% in parts replacement, 12% in emergency repairs), enhanced rectification rates to 100%, and improved safety in high-density areas. The framework advances energy security by addressing gaps in reactive practices, offering scalable solutions for urban infrastructures. Such advancements not only mitigate environmental risks from gas leaks but also foster economic efficiency in energy distribution, with potential applications extending to smart grid management and renewable integration. Future work explores blockchain for data integrity and multi-city validations to broaden global adoption.

title: Artificial Intelligence–Assisted Budget Forecasting and Operations Management for Strategic Decision-Making
authors: Halaswamy D, Lakshmi Kantha, Giridhara Naidu B, Rakesh N, K.Binith Muthukrishnan, Revanayya Kantayya
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Artificial intelligence has become a revolution in terms of improving financial and operational approaches within the corporate ecosystems. Financial forecasts and operations oversight that use state-of-the-art machines training and statistical analysis allow businesses to plan, minimize waste, and invest money where it will do the best. Better and more current knowledge on changes to budgets and operational risks might be made available to executives with the use of current data pipelines, machine learning, and adapting techniques. In addition to improving the cost framework and efficiency assessment, this approach may also make businesses less expensive and adaptable to changes in competition. Fiscal decisions that are in line with the company's strategic objectives will be consistent and transparent thanks to the combining of artificially intelligent forecasting apps with enterprise resource planning programs. Possible anomaly identification, operational streamlining, and data-based capital prioritization all add to the evident increase of correctness and robustness. Applying AI to spending planning and operational oversight procedures lays the groundwork for intentional decline, which in turn enables pre-emptive and long-range decision-making, delivers robust profitability, and offers sustained development in the dynamic corporate environment. With a Technical Accessibility Factor of 96.4%, the suggested solution outperformed all others.

title: Automatic Modulation Recognition of 24 Modulation Types Based on CNN and CGDNN
authors: Pengzhe Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of wireless communication, there is an urgent need for the recognition of complex signals. Traditional methods often suffer from performance degradation under low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions. Deep learning, particularly hybrid models that integrate Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for spatial feature extraction with Gated Recurrent Units (GRU) for temporal modeling (e.g., the Convolutional Gated Recurrent Deep Neural Network, CGDNN)—offers a novel solution for comprehensively capturing signal characteristics. However, research on unified recognition of up to 24 modulation types remains insufficient and warrants further exploration. To comprehensively capture signal characteristics, this study constructs and validates a hybrid Convolutional Gated Recurrent Deep Neural Network (CGDNN), integrating a CNN front-end for spatial feature extraction with a GRU back-end for temporal modeling, to address the challenging unified recognition of up to 24 modulation types. Using the RadioML 2018.01A dataset segmented by SNR levels, the model was designed, trained, and optimized. The results indicate that both CNN and CGDNN achieve accuracy rates above 80% at SNRs of 8 dB and above. The CGDNN attains a peak accuracy of 91.5% at 30 dB. Furthermore, it is observed that fundamental modulation formats are learned effectively by the model.

title: D-FINE-DSM: Infrared Image Detection for Power Equipment Based on Dual-domain Selection
authors: Mengyao Xu, Xinnan Fan, Jiawei Chen, Pengfei Shi, Lingquan Shi
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: To address the pronounced thermal noise and background heat source interference in infrared images of power transmission lines, an infrared transmission-line detection method based on dual-domain selection is proposed. First, Efficient Channel Attention Network (ECA-Net) is introduced to adaptively enhance target features without increasing model complexity, and introduced a dual-domain selection mechanism (DSM) to dynamically couple spatial-domain and frequency-domain features for background suppression. Coordinate Attention Dilated Residual Block (CADRB) is proposed. It enlarges the receptive field and fuses contextual information via multi-scale dilated convolutions, and introduces coordinate attention at the end to preserve directional positional cues and highlight key regions. Experimental results showed the proposed method achieves 85.7% mAP@0.5 and 48.4% mAP@0.5:0.95. Compared with the baseline D-FINE model, it yields accuracy gains of 6.5% and 3.2%, respectively, achieving a balance between accuracy and efficiency in challenging infrared scenarios.

title: Functional Observer of Stringing Machine: Bond Graph Modeling, Simulation and Practice
authors: Rim Attafi, Sami Souissi, Habib Dallagi
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: A functional observers design by Bond Graph (BG) approach is presented in this paper. Taking into account its structural and causal properties, the bond graph model is used for modeling, simulation and practice purposes. The paper also highlights and clarifies the need for the BG method, to outline the stability performances. Simulation and experimental results on the stringing machine are provided to show the dynamic behavior of system variables and to evaluate the performance of the observer for modelling and estimation tasks.

title: Improved AVR Performance Using a Combined ANFIS and Fractional-Order PID Controller
authors: Krishnaveni Srinivasan, R. Muniraj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: The frequency and voltage stabilisation issues commonly occur in complex electric power systems. The proposed research article primarily focuses on regulating the voltage of an AVR system to enhance stability. A novel Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS) is preferred in this article to control the AVR system. Instead of Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID), the Fractional Order Proportional Integral Derivative Controller (FOPID) is suggested in this research work to provide the data set for ANFIS to train the fuzzy inferential system. The FOPID controller consists of five parameters, such as derivative gain constant (KD), integral gain constant (KI), proportional gain constant (KP), order of derivative $(\mu)$, and order of integrator $(\lambda)$, which are optimised. The suitability of a designed ANFIS-FOPID controller in a real-time environment is examined in this article through three fundamental analyses: transient analysis, stability analysis, and robustness analysis. In transient and robustness analysis, key output voltage metrics such as settling time (ST), steady-state error (SSE), maximum peak (MP), percentage overshoot (PO), and Rise Time (RT) are evaluated under fixed load perturbations. The stability of the AVR system is also ensured through pole-zero identification using the FOMCON tool in MATLAB. The dominance of the ANFIS-FOPID in the AVR system is also demonstrated in this article by comparing the results with the Fuzzy controller, sine-cosine algorithm-based FOPID controller (SCA-FOPID), sine-cosine algorithm-based PID controller (SCA-PID), and ANFIS-PID controller. All simulations were conducted in MATLAB R2022a using the FOMCON toolbox for developing fractional-order controllers.

title: Eye On Violence: Real-Time Violence Detection in Surveillance Video Using a CNN–LSTM Spatiotemporal Framework
authors: Mayuri Barapatre, Akshay Padia, Mayank Mokhere, Vaishvik Jaiswal, Pravin Sonsare
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Growing public safety concerns and the expansion of CCTV infrastructures have made violence detection in video surveillance streams an important area of study and research. When violent situations occur, manual monitoring of such systems is ineffective, error-prone, and often fails to deliver timely alerts. To address this challenge, this study proposes a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network–Long Short-Term Memory (CNN–LSTM) model capable of distinguishing violent from non-violent events by jointly capturing spatial cues from individual frames and temporal motion patterns across sequences. The pipeline includes frame extraction, fixed-length sequence generation, spatial feature encoding using a CNN backbone, and temporal modeling using LSTMs. The model is evaluated on the RWF-2000 dataset, a real-world collection of surveillance videos containing both violent and non-violent scenes. Experimental results demonstrate strong performance, achieving an overall accuracy of 68%, indicating the model’s suitability for near real-time, safety-critical surveillance applications. This study discusses dataset preparation, model design, training methodology, and evaluation, offering a practical framework for automated violence detection.

title: Optuna-Tuned Hybrid A*–DWA Planner for 3D Quadcopter Navigation in Dynamic Environments
authors: Nada Braiek, Imen Saidi
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Hybrid global-local planning architectures are commonly used for quadcopter navigation to balance global path efficiency with local reactivity in the presence of obstacles. However, the performance of such hybrid planners is strongly influenced by the selection of internal parameters, which are often tuned manually and in an ad hoc manner. This paper presents a hybrid 3D path planning framework that combines an energyaware A* global planner with a 3D Dynamic Window Approach (DWA) local controller for quadcopter navigation in environments containing static and dynamic obstacles. Energy considerations are incorporated through a configurable energy proxy that penalizes altitude changes and aggressive velocity profiles. To improve planner performance and robustness, Bayesian optimization using Optuna is employed offline to tune key planning parameters. The proposed approach is evaluated through a comparative study between a default hybrid planner and its Optuna-tuned counterpart across multiple randomized scenarios. Experimental results show that parameter tuning leads to higher success rates and improved trajectory efficiency, highlighting the practical benefits of systematic parameter optimization for hybrid planning architectures in aerial robotics.

title: Miniaturized Dual-Port THz MIMO Antenna with Enhanced Isolation and Diversity for 6G Networks
authors: Arafat Uddin, Nirzar Barua, Shanchayan Barua, Sakhawat Hossen Sun, Mirajul Islam, Md. Ashadul Islam, Riadul Hoque
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: This work presents a compact dual-port MIMO antenna designed for terahertz (THz) applications, particularly targeting next-generation 6 G systems. The proposed antenna features a dual microstrip patch structure on a quartz substrate with copper metallization, occupying a small footprint of $95 \times 55 \mu \mathbf{m}^{2}$ and a thickness of $1.6 \mu \mathrm{~m}$. A honeycomb-inspired slot array is etched within and around the patch elements to enhance surface current distribution, thereby improving bandwidth and radiation performance. Additionally, the extended upper patch arrays help reduce mutual coupling and maintain polarization purity without the need for complex decoupling networks. Fullwave simulations in CST Microwave Studio confirm dual-band operation at 6.9 THz and 8.9 THz, achieving return losses of -58 dB and -35 dB, respectively. The antenna exhibits gains of 5.9 dBi and 9.9 dBi, with impedance bandwidths of 420 GHz and 250 GHz, and good VSWR characteristics. The MIMO configuration provides isolation of about 35 dB and an envelope correlation coefficient below 0.0015. These results indicate that the proposed honeycomb-slotted dual-patch antenna is a promising candidate for high-speed THz communication and sensing systems.

title: A Lightweight STFT-Free Human Activity Recognition Framework Using IR-UWB Radar and Transfer Learning
authors: Xiunan Hou, Jianfeng Zhang, Yibo Ren, Jiaxiao Du, Xin Ning, Zhaoxia Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Continuous indoor human activity recognition is required for healthcare and smart home applications. In current radar systems, a high computational overhead is inevitably generated by traditional time-frequency transformations like the Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT). Additionally, severe overfitting is often caused when complex neural networks are trained on small-scale datasets. To address these specific bottlenecks, a lightweight classification system is developed using a PulsON 440 IR-UWB radar. The STFT is completely bypassed in this work. Instead, static background clutter is suppressed by a double-delay MTI filter, and the raw echoes are converted directly into 2D Range-Time maps to capture macroscopic displacement. For feature extraction, an ImageNet pre-trained ResNet-18 architecture is employed through a transfer learning strategy. A dataset consisting of 2,750 samples was collected to evaluate the model. It encompasses five physical actions: bend, fall, sit, squat, and walk. The data was strictly partitioned into three independent test tiers following a cross-scenario protocol to evaluate generalization. The system achieved a 99.33% accuracy in LOS environments and a 100% Fall Recall under dynamic interference, demonstrating superior stability compared to complex attention-based architectures.

title: Feature-Level Debiasing for Robust Visual Model Training
authors: Zhuoxuan Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This work addresses two critical limitations in debiasing research for image recognition models: the information bottleneck between bias capture and debiasing training, and the lack of explicit guidance for model feature learning during debiasing. We propose a novel feature-level debiasing framework for robust visual model training, which consists of three core modules. Specifically, the bias capture and representation module leverages Partial Least Squares (PLS) with supervision from model performance metrics to extract latent biased subgroups and construct multi-dimensional bias representations for each sample. The visual feature disentanglement module reverses the time steps of diffusion models to integrate hierarchical disentanglement learning of visual features into the classification training pipeline. The biased feature filtering module calculates the bias degree of each feature segment via sample pairs to achieve accurate filtering of biased features. Compared with state-of-the-art debiasing methods, our approach achieves superior performance on unbiased testsets and strong robustness under distribution shifts.

title: Ranging Technology for UAV Nodes Based on Integration of Communication and Navigation
authors: Zhengdong Wang, Haoyu Nie, Wei Cao, Jiamin Qi, Yihui Wang, Hangcheng Han
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: UAV swarms demand reliable inter-node ranging, yet GNSS-based methods are interference-prone, and independent communication-navigation systems suffer from spectrum conflicts and inflexibility. This paper proposes a communication-navigation integrated ranging technique that enables relative distance measurement by reusing communication link data, decoupling high-speed data transmission from precision ranging. A TOA estimation method combining pseudo-code regeneration and sinc operator is introduced: the sinc operator models the band-limited distorted correlation function, and Gauss-Newton iteration performs high-precision peak localization. Simulations show the ranging accuracy asymptotically approaches the CRLB, remains independent of specific modulation/coding, and offers strong versatility for inter-UAV ranging.

title: Zero-Trust Security Models for Cloud, Edge, and IoT Environments
authors: Emran Hossain, Shuchona Malek Orthi, Md Abubokor Siam, Foysal Mahmud, Mohammad Abdul Goffer, Jahid Hassan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The scalding increase of cloud computing, edge intelligence, and Internet of Things (IoT) ecologies has tremendously enlarged the frontier of cyber-attacks, and hence, the traditional perimeter-based protection strategies cannot be employed anymore. Zero-Trust Security (ZTS) has already emerged as a useful paradigm that demands an on-the-fly verification, the least-privilege access and extreme identity-centric controls, irrespective of network location. This paper is a critical analysis of the Zero-Trust Security Models, which are specifically formulated to fit within cloud, edge, and IoT environments because they are heterogeneous resources with limited resources and dynamic threat environments. According to this framework, it assumes the combination of identity and access control, validation of device trust, micro- segmentation, policies, and continual behaviour monitoring in distributed computing layers. The priority is concentrated on providing east-west traffic assurance, insider threat minimization, and adaptive trust analysis of latency-sensitive edge and IoT deployments. Besides, the paper discusses the issues of architectural design, deployment issues and performance trade-offs including scalability, latency overhead, and interoperability. The suggested solution will enhance the capability of responding to advanced persistent threats and unlicensed access by executing the concepts of Zero-Trust in cloud-edge-IoT continuums, which will guarantee the efficiency of operations. The findings indicate that in order to build secure, scale-able and future-oriented distributed systems, Zero-Trust-based security designs were the key.

title: Application of AI to Identify Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) Abnormalities: Combining Intelligent Sensors for Predictive Industrial Inspection
authors: J Seethalakshmi, T. Kamaleshwar, V. Pushpa, V Haritha, Murthoty Satheesh Babu, s Dharanika
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: The IIoT establishes connections across various industrial assets, allowing for automated decision-making and smart production through the use of operational data streams in real-time. With the proliferation of sensor networks, it becomes more challenging to detect machine irregularities, forecast equipment failures, and guarantee operational continuity. Dynamic and nonlinear organizational dynamics make conventional rule-based techniques ineffective. Through the use of smart sensors and advanced anomaly detection algorithms, this study explores the application of artificial intelligence (AI) to detect anomalies in IIoT systems. Helping with predictive industrial inspection is the aim. An accurate, real-time monitoring of industrial surroundings is achieved by the suggested system through the combination of multimodal fusion with machine learning and deep learning approaches. Smart sensors measure things like environmental factors, current variations, temperature changes, audio emissions, and vibration signatures; data is collected by an integrated data-processing pipeline. A range of architectures, such as GNNs, autoencoders, LSTM networks, and hybrid CNN-LSTM designs, are utilized to detect early-stage machine degeneration. Subtle shifts in time-series patterns can be captured by these designs. By teaching the system to behave normally and identify abnormalities, unsupervised learning methods solve the prevalent problem of sparsely fault-labeled datasets. The IIoT generates massive amounts of data, and this study suggests a distributed edge-cloud architecture to deal with them. The core idea is that edge devices can do fast inferencing, while cloud platforms can handle large-scale model training.

title: Adaptive IoT Framework: A Modular Approach for Smart Device Integration
authors: Aravind Narayanan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This paper presents a modular, open-source IoT framework designed to address interoperability, scalability, and security challenges in heterogeneous smart device environments. The framework comprises three core components: a Rust-based device library for performance and reliability, a microservices backend for scalable device management, and a web-based dashboard for real-time monitoring. Emphasis is placed on modularity to enable seamless integration of new devices without system disruption. Security is implemented through layered mechanisms to ensure data protection and regulatory compliance. Experimental evaluation demonstrates 18% reduction in communication latency, support for 50,000 concurrent device connections with less than 5% performance degradation, and 27% lower response time compared to monolithic architectures. Case studies in insurance applications show improved risk assessment accuracy and fraud detection capabilities. The framework provides a foundation for adaptive IoT deployments across various domains.

title: Self-ATDDQN: a Self-Attention Integrated Double Deep Q-Network for Safe and Efficient Highway Driving
authors: Xinyu Cai
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) offers an effective approach for autonomous driving decision-making, but existing methods still suffer from issues such as low learning efficiency and insufficient decision safety in highway scenarios. This paper proposes a Self-Attention Integrated Double Deep QNetwork (Self-ATDDQN) algorithm. This algorithm embeds a self-attention module into the Q-network, and by explicitly modeling the dynamic interaction relationship between vehicles, the model can adaptively focus on key traffic participants. Finally, it combines the double deep Q-network to complete the driving decision output. Experimental results show that Self-ATDDQN is significantly better than the baseline algorithms in key metrics such as average driving distance, collision rate and others.

title: Removal of Baseline wander from ECG signals using Generalized Moreau Envelope Total Variation Filter
authors: Ch Kantharao Sarihaddu, I Madhava, K.M.V Lakshmi Manasa, V Sivaprasad, Ch Ratna Raju, E Narasamma
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) is a crucial bio-signal utilized for the diagnosis of cardiovascular diseases. Noise frequently occurs during the acquisition of ECG data in healthcare settings. Baseline wander (B<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">w</inf>) represents a form of noise that may distort the ECG signal, leading to inaccurate interpretations. B<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">w</inf> denotes low-frequency shifts in baseline of ECG signals, potentially obscuring essential physiological details. Consequently, significant focus has been directed towards ECG denoising to ensure precise diagnosis and analysis. This study employed generalized moreau envelope total variation (GMUETAV) filter to eliminate B<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">w</inf> from the electrocardiogram (ECG). The openly accessible MIT-BIH Arrhythmia dataset is employed to substantiate suggested method. The simulation outcomes demonstrate that proposed strategy surpasses other advanced ECG filtering methods, as assessed by performance metrics such as correlation coefficient (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">e</inf>) alongside output signal-to-noise ratio (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">o</inf>) across different input signal-to-noise ratios (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</inf>).

title: Ethical AI Audits: Preventing Algorithmic Discrimination in Substance Abuse Referrals
authors: Shalmali Joshi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The choices people make regarding health can be dictated by certain programs rather than by people. It can make some people not have good care. The EAIA framework proposed in this essay assesses the bias of clinical referral predictive machine learning models and also helps mitigate it. The framework uses 120,000 synthetic patient profiles and a public anonymized dataset of addiction-treatment intakes to compare differences across protected attributes of race, sex, income, age. Four different models were built, including a Logistic Regression, Random Forest and Gradient Boosting, and a Deep Neural Network to evaluate baseline gaps and fairness interventions. The findings indicate that the EAIA framework effectively diminishes discrepancies in false negative rates and statistical parity. It is able to achieve these results without sacrificing or with marginal sacrifices on the performance metrics. By visually inspecting the error distribution plots, ROC curves and calibration plots, it appears that our predictions are more stable as well as interpretable and more clinically trustworthy. The results indicate that audits based on monitoring and mediation can support the development of ethical, high-performance, responsible AI. The larger framework may enable a more generalized approach to the incorporation of fairness and accountability into healthcare ML pipeline.

title: Univariate Forecasting of EV Power Consumption Using LSTM Networks: A Deep Learning Approach
authors: T Puvirajan, S. Amosedinakaran, P.Babu Rao, A Bhuvanesh, S.Vinoth John Prakash, B Vinod
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Electric vehicle retailing is increasing day by day worldwide, and power consumption is rising accordingly. A power system planner has to predict the required power consumption well in advance. So, a power system planner has to understand how actual demand is rising and know techniques to predict it. Better forecasting of the future is essential for medium-term planning. Deep learning techniques, such as LSTM, are more essential models for forecasting demand for the medium-term planning of this study. An LSTM time-series model has been adopted and has provided a good, optimal solution for this study. This is a historical, demand-based future demand prediction conducted in this research. Tamil Nadu is taken as a case study, and data from January 2022 to the present, including monthly consumption data, are available. In which one-fourth of the data and three-fourths of the data have been adopted for testing and training the LSTM model. The proposed model has given good predictions after training and validation with an RMSE of 0.844. Based on testing, forecasting of EV power consumption trends until July 2026 has been done. The proposed LSTM model has provided insights into predictable future consumption results. The conclusions have shown that LSTM univariate models could help utilities, regulators, and power system planners anticipate EV-based demand changes and deal with the structure of the plant.

title: Estimation of Lower Limb Trajectory From Foot Measurements
authors: Vijay Bhaskar Semwal, Ruchika Sharma, Neha Gaud, Yadunath Pathak, Abhishek Sharma, Pranay Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: In this research we develop a deep neural network model to forecast lower limb movements based on foot measurements. The model uses autoencoder and decoders to learn the relationship between foot morphology and lower limb kinematics, which includes joint angles and segment orientations. The model takes in a set of foot measurements as input, including foot length, width, arch height, and other parameters. These measurements are preprocessed , extract features from the input data and learn to model the relationship between foot morphology and lower limb kinematics. The output of the model is a set of predicted joint angles (hip, knee and ankle) and segment orientations for the lower limb, which can be used to analyze gait patterns, diagnose musculoskeletal disorders, and inform treatment and rehabilitation plans. During the experiment, the Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) consistently falls within a certain range of (1.48<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">◦</sup>, 1.38<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">◦</sup>). The development of the model represents an important step towards improving our understanding of the complex relationship between foot morphology and lower limb kinematics, and could have significant implications for the diagnosis and treatment of lower limb injuries and disorders.

title: Spatial-Temporal Intelligence for Environmental IoT Monitoring via a CNN-LSTM Hybrid Deep Learning Framework
authors: Vignesh Ramamoorthy H, Sathish Krishna Anumula, Prashanth Kumar Ravula, Sushmitha B H, Shaik Yacoob, S. Kaliappan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Internet of Things (IoT) technologies have grown rapidly, and it has become possible to continuously monitor the environmental conditions due to the deployment of large quantities of sensors, which provide massive amounts of multivariate time-series data that need to be analyzed intelligently and reliably. Conventional machine learning systems can be difficult to use to capture complex spatial interactions and long-term temporal relationships captive to environmental IoT sensor data. To overcome these shortcomings, this paper will suggest hybrid CNN-LSTM deep learning model to provide accurate analysis of environmental sensors data in smart environments. The convolutional layers are used to learn correlation of local features of both the environmental parameters and the LSTM aspect is used to learn the long term temporal dynamics. The environmental sensor dataset of the proposed model is assessed on a big data of real-life sensors of temperature, humidity, air quality, and pressure. Wide-scale experimental testing proves that the presented CNN-LSTM model has high classification precision of 98%, high precision, recall, and F1-scores. Further tests based on ROC-AUC, Precision-Recall, and low-noisy sensor conditions robustness as well as statistical validation ensure the model stability and its generality capacity. The findings indicate the appropriateness of the suggested solution in terms of effective and scalable environmental monitoring systems using the IoT.

title: FASR: A Future-Aware and Smooth Rhythm-Aware Time-Gated GRU for Sequential Recommendation
authors: Peihua Zou, Jingshu Shi, Xingjun Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Sequential recommendation systems, despite their progress, still struggle with two core challenges: perceiving complex temporal dynamics in user behavior and modeling users' diverse interests. To address these limitations, this paper proposes FASR, a novel Future-Aware and Smooth Rhythm-aware Time-Gated GRU Recommender. At the core of our model is the innovative SRT-GRU encoder, which features a dual temporal gating mechanism composed of a rhythm-aware short-term gate and a smooth rhythm-aware session gate. This allows SRT-GRU to deeply understand behavior intervals, density, and rhythm, encoding this understanding into context-aware states enriched with model “confidence”. Building on this, we employ a dualstream architecture derived from users' similar interactions. A Historical Stream encodes the user's own history, while a Future Stream encodes “future behavior” sequences mined from neighbors, achieving “future-awareness”. Subsequently, a Capsule Network with dynamic routing acts on these timeaware contextual sequences to disentangle diverse historical interests and future trends. Finally, a target-aware readout and fusion mechanism generates a final user representation that simultaneously captures past preferences, potential future trends, and multiple interests for recommendation. Extensive quantitative and qualitative experiments on three real-world datasets (MovieLens-1M, Movies&TV, Movies&T, VOD) demonstrate that our model significantly outperforms state-of-the-art sequential recommenders on various metrics, while also providing complementary and novel recommendations. Ablation studies further confirm the effectiveness of each temporal component within our proposed SRT-GRU encoder.

title: Velocity-Driven Fall Detection: A Drift-Corrected Feature Extraction Methodology for Wearable IMU Systems
authors: Vandana Shakya, Soni Changlani
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Falls are a serious and growing concern for older adults, yet many wearable fall-detection systems still struggle because they rely mostly on acceleration data, which doesn’t fully describe how a fall actually unfolds. One important but often overlooked indicator is velocity, since it reflects the real movement and energy of the body. The challenge is that raw IMU data makes velocity hard to use due to drift that builds up during integration. In this work, we introduce a multi-stage processing pipeline that removes sensor noise, gravity effects, and drift to produce clean, reliable velocity signals. Using a public dataset, we show that velocity-based features significantly improve the performance of machine-learning models compared to traditional acceleration-only approaches. These results suggest that incorporating velocity can make wearable fall-detection systems more accurate, more dependable, and ultimately safer for the people who rely on them

title: Intelligent Swarm Algorithms for Intensify Blockchain-Cloud Ecosystem Security
authors: Ganji Ramanjaiah, Balajee Maram, Komanduri Venkata Sesha Sai Ramakrishna, Tummala Srinivasa Ravi Kiran
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Blockchain and cloud computing are two domains that can provide a wide range of applications, which offer high security for cloud data. Combining these two domains creates a fusion-based model that addresses new security challenges, including data theft, abnormal nodes, and invalid nodes. Cloud computing primarily provides online services, including data storage, security, task scheduling, and resource allocation. In this paper, Intelligent Swarm Algorithms (ISA) are introduced as a Novel Optimization-Driven Security System (NODSS) for blockchain–cloud ecosystems (BCE) to address several issues. The ISA model uses the Quantum-Integrated Swarm Optimization (QISO) for sustainable cloud operations. It is also helpful in improving blockchain speed by validating blocks in the cloud network and effectively processing scheduling in the cloud. It combines the principles of quantum computing with swarm intelligence to create a robust intrusion detection system (IDS). Experimental analysis demonstrates that the proposed approach, when applied to high-dimensional datasets, effectively utilizes cloud resource scheduling. Finally, the proposed approach demonstrates high performance in terms of security, speed, and efficiency.

title: Design of a Dynamic Correlation-Aware Prefetching Mechanism for Back-End Parallelism
authors: Meng Luo, Xiaokun Zhu, Yang Zhang, Guojun Han
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Although solid state drive (SSD) caching can effectively mitigate the flash access bottleneck, further improving read performance under highly concurrent and fragmented workloads remains challenging. Most existing prefetching strategies rely on short-term statistics or instantaneous correlations for prediction. When workload phases shift or noise is present, they struggle to balance prediction accuracy against system overhead. In addition, traditional prefetching schemes rarely consider SSD back end parallelism explicitly, which limits read performance gains. To address these issues, we propose Back-end Parallelism-Aware Dynamic Correlation Prefetching (BPDCP). With a fixed request window, BPDCP learns correlation patterns and preserves a stable model across windows via two-level filtering and recency-aware decay. On this model, it issues request level predictions and progressively weakens outdated correlations. At the execution level, BPDCP incorporates back-end resource status and applies a parallelism aware scheduling strategy, allowing prefetch requests to utilize idle parallelism across channels, LUNs, and planes, reducing interference with foreground I/O. Simulation results on FEMU with real world workloads show that, compared with representative prefetching methods, BPDCP reduces total I/O latency and average read response time while keeping the overhead on the write path under control.

title: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence in Digital Marketing and Social Media Integration: A Case Study of Reliance Jio’s AI-Driven Customer Engagement Strategies
authors: Minakshi Nag, Ashutosh Shukla, Sayali Kharpate, Upma Tripathi, Neha Singh, Suruchi Tripathi, Shreya Sheel, Shweta Dwivedi, Mohd Talha
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The use of new technologies has altered the interaction between customers and businesses, where the Artificial Intelligence (AI) has been instrumental in establishing new marketing and social media interactions. This paper will focus on how Reliance Jio, a leading Indian telecommunications firm, has used AI to improve customer relations, personalize the communication, and streamline the marketing campaigns. The research problem will be to understand how effectively AI will develop and sustain customer engagement and loyalty to the brand in a competitive telecom market. The paper will use a case study methodology to study the use of AI chatbots, predictive analytics, recommendation system and social media sentiment analysis in Reliance Jio. The findings show that Jio has used AI in the customer service to anticipate the customer needs, provide real-time services and move customer service responsive to proactive services. The research restates the importance of AI in social media and marketing when the integration of data and real-time analytics can create responsive customer relations. Jio is the first in the telecom services industry to adopt AI in marketing the services to customers to enhance their satisfaction and support marketing decisions. The research can provide the marketing community with the insights on the integration of AI, and the academic community with the use of technology in marketing and consumer interaction.

title: A Compact Modified Inverted F Antenna with Enhanced Performance for IoT Devices in 2.45 GHz ISM Band
authors: Md. Arifuzzaman, Md. Masud Rana
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: The unprecedented needs and expansion of the Internet of Things (IoT) have increased the demand for compact, highly efficient antennas ensuring reliable connectivity within the 2.45 GHz Industry, Scientific and Medical (ISM) band. A compact and optimized miniaturized modified inverted-F antenna (IFA) for Internet of Things (IoT) applications in the 2.45 GHz ISM band is presented in this study. The proposed antenna uses a parasitic slot and ground-plane changes to obtain a small dimension of $40 \times 25 \times 1.6 \mathrm{~mm}^{3}$ to improve impedance matching, bandwidth and radiation performance. An FR-4 substrate with a dielectric permittivity of 4.4, a substrate thickness of 1.6 mm and an input impedance of 50 ohms are used to design the antenna to guarantee correct matching. With a fractional improved bandwidth of 22.85%, a VSWR of 1.24 and a return loss of -19.16 dB, the antenna operates efficiently at 2.45 GHz. Furthermore, it provides a gain of 1. 10 dBi with a radiation efficiency of 71.58% and an omnidirectional radiation pattern suitable for dense IoT environments. The integration of Defective Ground Structure (DGS) and parasitic slotting demonstrates significant performance improvements compared to conventional IFA designs while maintaining a low profile and simple structure. Due to its compact configuration, wide bandwidth, and stable radiation characteristics, the proposed antenna is a promising candidate for miniaturized IoT devices requiring reliable 2.45 GHz connectivity.

title: X-ForensicNet: An Explainable Offline LLM Framework for Automated Windows Digital Forensics with Neuro-Symbolic Validation
authors: Radharaman Tiwari, Manisha J. Nene
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Digital forensics investigation of Windows systems is traditionally manual, time-consuming, and demands expert analysis of vast forensic artifacts. Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) show significant promise in automating forensic interpretation but relying on cloud services threatens data privacy and lacks explainability required for legal admissibility. We propose X-ForensicNet, a fully offline forensic framework integrating local LLM inference, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and neuro-symbolic rule-based validation within a multi-agent architecture. The system automatically extracts Windows artifacts from forensic disk images, structures them into JSON datasets, semantically analyzes them with a local LLM enhanced by symbolic forensic rules, and generates transparent, explainable forensic reports. Our system accelerates forensic investigations by 2.8× while improving accuracy to 96.3%, supports complete offline operation for privacy-sensitive environments, and ensures legal defensibility through explainable reasoning trails. Experimental evaluation on real and synthetic forensic datasets demonstrates significant improvements over traditional manual analysis and cloud-based approaches.

title: A Dynamic Multi-Objective Cost Function for Personalized and Accessible Urban Pathfinding
authors: Aayam Bajaj, Atharva Dhokte, Yuvrajsing Bahure, Hitendra Chavan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Standard urban navigation systems often fail to meet the needs of individuals with mobility impairments. These systems focus on optimizing the shortest path, ignoring critical accessibility factors like surface quality, dynamic real-time obstacles, and sidewalk slopes, thereby creating an informational gap for users. In order to address these shortcomings, this paper introduces a pathfinding framework, by enhancing the A* search algorithm using a dynamic, multi-objective cost function. With calculation of the traversal cost for every path segment, our methodology provides personalized and context-aware routing. The derivation of this cost happens through the integration of multiple layers of data, including: (1) Personalized user profiles, (2) Community-sourced data on obstructions and temporary obstacles, (3) Machine learning model for predictive risk assessment, and (4) Live weather updates. This results in generation of routes that consider user safety and comfort instead of just distance. Hence this system offers a reliable and accessible navigation experience.

title: LLM-Enabled Cognitive Search for Context-Aware Knowledge Retrieval from Heterogeneous Data Sources
authors: Mallikarjun Gouru, Tanvi Mittal, Ashraf Syed, Subrat Kumar Prasad, Sreepal Reddy Bolla, Revanth Reddy Bandaru
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: The successive increase in the heterogeneous data in structured databases, semi-structured documents, and unstructured text repositories have rendered context-conscious knowledge retrieval a significant concern in the modern information systems. Sometimes, traditional methods of search such as keywords, rules, and other systems do not have the capability of reflecting the intent of the semantics, dependencies between contexts and cross-source relations leading to a common output of fragmented and low-precision retrieval. In an effort to overcome these shortcomings, the paper at hand suggests an LLM-based cognitive search model that would be used to achieve context-based knowledge retrieval in heterogeneous data sources. The suggested method consists of the combination of large language models (LLMs) and semantic embedding algorithms, contextual reasoning, and adaptive query orchestration to allow the deep interpretation of the user intent and data semantics. This structure integrates multi-modal and multi-source data with a semantic indexing layer and uses dynamically refined and ranked queries and synthesized knowledge with LLM-based reasoning. The proposed model in contrast to traditional retrieval systems enables contextual memory, conversational interaction, and domain-aware inference, which enable it to provide accurate, explainable and personalized search results. Experimental analysis on the variety of enterprise-scale datasets proves that the retrieval accuracy, contextual relevance, and response consistency are improved significantly in comparison with traditional and neural information retrieval benchmarks. The findings suggest that there is a next-generation of knowledge management, decision support system and enterprise information retrieval system which involves the LLM-enabled cognitive search as a scalable and intelligent solution.

title: AIML-Enabled Body-Bias Management to Minimise Leakage and PDP in 65 nm Domino Logic
authors: C. Somasundar Reddy, Kathi Venkataramana, Seeniappan Kaliappan, Ramya M
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Domino logic is still popular in data paths that need latency, but at the 65nm node the increasing subthreshold leakage and narrowing energy-delay ratios makes it difficult to maintain performance and energy efficiency. This paper offered an AIML-enabled per-block body-bias management system which predicted and regulated both the uncertainty-aware surrogates and constrained optimisation predictions. The proposed system controls the static leakage, propagation delay and switching energy to permit safe, fine grained VB V tuning with PVT and aging. Models like XGBoost ensembles and Gaussian processes were trained on a reproducible PTM 65-nm SPICE dataset generated with Monte-Carlo PVT sweeps and aging perturbations. Constrained Bayesian optimisation and a reinforcement-learning variant were employed to propose bias settings that are subjected to probabilistic timing and SNM constraints. In SPICE-validated experiments the AIML controller achieved substantial gains over static baselines at an average leakage reduction of 42% and PDP improvement of 18% while keeping timing-violation probability within designer thresholds. This analysis additionally quantified sensor/ADC resolution, controller area/power overhead and interactions with DVFS and power-gating. Overall, the proposed framework offered a practical, low-overhead path to adaptive, reliability-aware power management for 65-nm domino logic.

title: Hybrid Reputation Routing for Decentralized Networks: A Blockchain-Enabled Secure and Scalable Protocol with On-Chain/Off-Chain Partitioning
authors: Ramkinker Singh, Om Narayan, Praveen Baskar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Our suggestion is a hybrid reputation-based routing protocol in the context of decentralized routing. networks which partitions trust measurements to on-chain immutable logs and off-chain dynamic computations to attain security and scalability. The protocol incorporates a layer of sharded blockchain to store critical. trust data and local reputation model to do real-time updates, enabling routing choices that cannot be tampered with. performance. The trust ledger which operated on-chain kept the records of the impartiality of. Historical performance and node identities, whereas the off-chain reputation engine uses graph neural network to calculate dynamic. real-time scores like packet delivery rate to be trusted. and latency. Additionally, it uses rollup-based batches of synchronization off-chain. optimistic updates to zero-knowledge proofs, making on-chain efficient. checking with minimum overhead. The suggested approach replaces conventional network discovery and forwarding modules using trust-based adjacency lists and trust-based pathing, and thus enhancing routing consistency over adversarial settings. Moreover, the structure integrates Hyperledger Fabric with. Graph Attention Network-based high-throughput sharded ledger operations. to update reputation in a privacy-preserving manner, proving to be linear. network size scalability. The experimental findings indicate that the system supports 10,000 transactions per shard and produces. Under 100 ms per ZK-Rollup proof, which is appropriate to large-scale IoT. and DeFi deployments. This publication fills the gap between pure on-chain. and off-chain reputation systems, which provides a viable solution to scalable and secure decentralized routing.

title: AudX: A CNN–LSTM-Based Spectrogram Framework for Speech Enhancement and ASR Improvement in Noisy Environments
authors: Bollimuntha Kavya Sai, Garapati Mohitha, M. Chandralekha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Background noise interrupts speech signals that researchers record in actual settings, which results in dual problems because both speech quality and automatic speech recognition accuracy become worse. The paper introduces AudX as a speech enhancement framework that uses deep learning to achieve noise reduction and improved transcription results through its spectrogram-based design. The system uses a hybrid structure that combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) for spectral feature extraction and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for temporal modelling. The VoiceBank-DEMAND dataset serves as the model’s training data, and the model uses supervised learning.
