title: Implementation of ML based Routing Algorithms for 5G Wireless Networks Architectures
authors: Kandala Kalyana Srinivas, K Jyostna, Chevella Anil Kumar, G Varshini, D Karthik, G L V Ramakrishna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Performance is based on routing efficiency and adaptability regardless of the system architecture.In this paper we are going to compare three broadly known routing algorithms: Enhanced Opportunistic Routing with Learning (EORL), Open Shortest Path First (OSPF), Software Defined Networking with Q-learning (SDN-Q).The parameters used for testing are throughput ,packet delivery ratio, performance benchmarks, and overall scalability. This paper provides through insights into each algorithm’s advantages and disadvantages in contemporary networks focusing on adaptability and autonomous smart-decision making system. These findings are useful for tailoring methods for effective routing in the networks ranging from legacy systems to AI-controlled and programmable networks.

title: AI-based Noise Cancellation and Speech Enhancement for Early Parkinson’s Disease Detection
authors: Kavitha K, Pyreddy Bhagirath Reddy, Kathri Gujarathi Arun, Putluru Sashi Vardhan Reddy, Pavuluru Muni Venkata Joshith
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Non-invasive diagnosis of Parkinson's disease (PD) has been proposed as one of the most promising methods of conducting speech analysis to detect the disease early. nevertheless, acoustic heterogeneity in reality and external noise has a strong negative impact on classification effects. The paper is an attempt at developing a strong noise-resistant system incorporating signal improvement, acoustic feature derivation, and machine learning in order to enhance PD detection. A spectral subtraction and Wiener filtering with spectral enhancement using short time fourier transform (STFT) to minimize additive noise and preserve pathological speech characteristics are proposed. Using the refined cues, Mel-frequency cepstral coefficients (MFCCs) and features of phonatory significance to a clinician like jitter, shimmer, fundamental frequency, and harmonic to noise ratio are derived and used to categorize a support vector machine (SVM). The literature reviews two benchmark datasets (UCI Parkinson and PC-GITA corpus) on the conditions of controlled noise. Experimental findings show that the enhancement strategy suggested is more effective than noisy speech inputs in enhancing classification strength and has an average accuracy of 96.4, sensitivity, specificity, and area under the ROC curve. The results have shown that noise suppression is a potent tool in increasing the discriminative ability of acoustic biomarkers to detect PD. This paper shows the relevance of speech improvement methods to automated diagnostics pipelines and demonstrates that the noise-conscious preprocessing can enhance reliability in the field of practical screening. The suggested framework provides a computationally effective and interpretable approach to creating user-friendly, speech-based, and initial detection equipment of Parkinson disease.

title: High speed Semiconductor based control circuit for Battery Equalization used in Electric vehicle Battery management system
authors: Femy P. H., Jayakumar J.
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Devices, Circuits, and Systems (ICDCS)
abstract: This paper details the development of a high speed control circuit based on semiconductors for active battery equalization in electric vehicle (EV) battery management systems. The proposed method combines an inductor-based battery equalization topology with a VLSI control circuit for real-time state-of-charge (SoC) monitoring and fast switching. The control mechanism considers SoC deviation as the basic parameter for equalization decision criteria. It applies high-frequency PWM technique to ensure controlled inductor current and efficient energy transfer between cells. The proposed method gives emphasis on low switching losses, reduced device stress, and scalability for large battery packs. The VLSI controller provides rapid equalization response compared to conventional microcontroller-based systems. Simulation results show higher balancing speed, better current regulation, and high equalization efficiency under varying load conditions. The proposed system provides an energy-efficient, and scalable solution for next-generation integrated EV battery management systems.

title: Low-Light Medical Image Enhancement Using GANs with Clinical Constraints
authors: Sakthivel S, S. Dinakar Jose, V. Keerthiga, K. Balasubramanian, Pooja Vasant Koli, Sajiv G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: The loss of visibility in crucial anatomical structures during low-light medical imaging remains a persistent challenge in ultrasound, endoscopy, and low-dose X-ray imaging, negatively impacting interpretive confidence and increasing the chances of errors. Available low-light image enhancement techniques improve global brightness of the images but virtually do not improve the utility of the images in diagnostics since the clinical structures are blurred or noise is amplified. This paper introduces a CC-GAN model that enhances images captured in low-light conditions while retaining anatomical integrity using anatomical prior information and constraints bounded by a radiologist. The model employs attention encoders, U-Net, PatchGAN discriminators, and clinical constraints modules dedicated to the imaging integration. The CC-GAN relies on the illumination-aware encodings to impose structural and edge accuracy decomposition. Evaluation of the model using a mixed-modality dataset containing 6240 low-light images yields a PSNR of dB 32.81, SSIM of 0.962, LPIPS of 0.082, and FID of 21.7 while outperforming 3 techniques in the domain. The radiologists reporting on the model noted a 28% improvement in the visibility of the lesion boundary and 22% reduction in artifact amplification relative to baseline GAN techniques. The CC-GAN model exhibited accurate anatomical reconstruction of the images through low-light enhancement. Thus, it can be embedded with image and CAD systems.

title: Simulation of a Dual-Parameter Optical Fiber Sensor Based on OptiSystem Modeling
authors: Anis Farisha Alias, Nur Najahatul Huda Saris, Azura Hamzah, Nurul Ashikin Daud, Nadiatulhuda Zulkifli, Marinah Othman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Robotics and Technologies for Industrial Automation (ROBOTHIA)
abstract: This paper presents the modeling of a Fiber Bragg Grating (FBG)-based sensing system for dual-parameter temperature and strain measurement using OptiSystem simulation. Conventional FBG sensors are commonly limited to single-parameter sensing, resulting in cross-sensitivity issues in applications that require simultaneous temperature and strain monitoring. The proposed model analyzes wavelength shift responses under varying strain and temperature conditions. Optimization is carried out by adjusting the grating period, thermal expansion coefficient, and grating length, followed by the selection of optimal values based on reliability analysis. Performance evaluation using coefficient of variation indicates values exceeding 10 %, suggesting instability under the selected configuration. Linearity assessment conducted by independently varying strain and temperature under the optimized setup yields linearity values of 0.4495 and 0.2272, respectively. These results indicate that further refinement of structural parameters is necessary to improve the reliability and linearity of the proposed dual-parameter FBG sensing system.

title: Design and Simulation of a Compact Axial and Radial Corrugated Horn Antenna for Ku-Band Defense and Aerospace Applications
authors: Rakshitha M, Monikka P, Mrunal A Marihal, P. Raja Ramana Rao, Subrata Kumar Datta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a compact axial and radial corrugated horn antenna for Ku-Band defense and aerospace applications. The antenna employs a combination of axial and radial corrugations to achieve efficient mode conversion into the hybrid HE<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf> mode, resulting in improved beam quality with low cross-polarization and reduced sidelobe levels. An axially corrugated profile shortens the horn length while maintaining high gain and radiation efficiency, making it suitable for defense and aerospace systems where size and performance are critical. The antenna was designed and analyzed using CST Microwave Studio. Simulation results show that the antenna achieves gain above 20 dBi with low sidelobe levels across the Ku-Band, demonstrating its potential for next-generation defense and aerospace platforms.

title: SHCF: Synergistic Hybrid Clustering Framework for Personalized Nutrition Recommendation and Endocrine Homeostasis using K-Means and Gaussian Mixture Models
authors: S Akshaya, S Jeevitha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: The methodologies of nutrition recommendation over the past years have more or less centered on generalized eating habits with little regard to personal health conditions and hormonal levels. The nutritional value of foods is a major criterion in most of the existing methods of categorizing foods despite the fact that important factors like age, gender, and other physiological changes like puberty, pre and post menopause and andropause are not well considered. An intelligent nutrition recommendation system is suggested to classify people according to their age, gender, hormonal levels, and health issues like diabetes, high blood pressure, and cholesterol level levels using unsupervised machine learning methods. The suggested Synergistic Hybrid Clustering Framework (SHCF) is based on K-Means clustering, and Gaussian Mixture Models (GMM) to represent hard and probabilistic cluster boundaries. The data is preprocessed with encoding of labels, normalization of data and dimensionality reduction through Principal Component Analysis (PCA) keeping 95 percent of the data variance. Four clusters are distinguished with the help of both the individual models and the hybrid approach that are connected with various hormonal stages. The hybrid model incorporates the good features of K-Means and GMM, enhancing the stability of clusters, the accuracy and that which represents integration of nutritional patterns. According to the end cluster work, this system can create individual nutrition plans based on hormonal age and health history. The SHCF framework has better personalized and clustered performance in comparison to a single model. The findings and results show that combining hormonal and health determinants with hybrid machine learning methods is effective in intelligent data-based nutrition planning.

title: Wildfire Occurrence Analysis using Meteorological Data: A Comparative Study of Statistical Methods and State-of-the-Art Learning Models
authors: Anil Kumar, Soham Ghosh, Pooja Gowda
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Inventive Computation Technologies (ICICT)
abstract: Wildfires are a serious environmental and social issue that is highly affected by the meteorological fluctuation and fuel state. Models capable of capturing long-term temporal patterns and short-term nonlinear relationships are therefore necessary for accurate wildfire detection. This study is a comparative analysis of statistical time-series modeling and the state-of-the-art learning models to analyze the occurrence of wildfires using multi-source meteorological and fuel related variables. First, ARIMA and SARIMA models are used to describe the high-risk periods over time and identify the periods with statistically significant high-risk of wildfires. Then, event-level wildfire occurrence classification on classical machine learning and deep learning models, such as LSTM, Bi-LSTM, and GRU are used. Accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC -AUC are the metrics of model performance, and the recall-based thresholding is prioritized in order to minimize false wildfires. Experiments indicate that deep learning-based models surpass statistical and classical methods, and the GRU model has a test ROC -AUC of 0.796. Its findings indicate the trade-off between false alarms and detection sensitivity as well as the significance of threshold-aware assessment in wildfire early-warning systems.

title: Attention-Guided Hybrid Learning for Tree Species Classification in Mining Restoration Areas Using UAV Hyperspectral and LiDAR Data
authors: Guo Wang, Cheng Wang, Yuncheng Deng, Jinliang Wang
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Accurate tree species classification in open-pit mining regions is crucial for assessing ecological restoration effectiveness. However, it remains challenging due to extreme surface reflectance, dust contamination, and spectral whitening effects prevalent. To address these challenges, this letter proposes an Attention-Guided Misclassification-Aware Hybrid Learning (AG-MAHL) framework for tree species classification. Unlike existing fusion methods that simply concatenate features or ignore hard samples, our framework introduces (i) an attention-guided fusion strategy to align spectral-structural features under high-reflectance conditions, and (ii) a misclassification-aware learning mechanism that dynamically reweights difficult samples. Experiments conducted at the Haikou Phosphogypsum Tailings Reservoir in Kunming, China, demonstrate that the proposed AG-MAHL framework achieves an overall accuracy (OA) of 0.8571, a weighted F1-score of 0.8423, and a balanced accuracy of 0.8402. This performance surpasses that of conventional machine learning methods and representative deep learning baselines. The results indicate that the proposed attention-guided hyperspectral–LiDAR fusion, coupled with misclassification-aware learning, offers an effective solution for tree species classification in spectrally challenging open-pit mining restoration environments.

title: Integrated Motor Drives: A Review of Recent Developments, Enabling Technologies and Future Research Directions
authors: Xu Deng, Barrie Mecrow, Thomas M. Jahns, Lee Empringham, Liliana De Lillo, Nick Simpson, Andrew Hopkins
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: The integration of electric machines, power electronics and controls into a single unit with common cooling and packaging is termed an Integrated Motor Drive (IMD). Integration with the load is also becoming increasingly common in many applications so, for example, the fan blades in a fan drive become integrated with the electric drive. Research into integrated drives and enabling technologies is both extensive and growing. Six years have passed since the last major review paper on the topic was published, and many significant new products have been introduced during this time in a wide variety of fields incorporating IMD-based configurations. Simultaneously, there have been major advances in materials, semiconductor technology, manufacturing methods, and cooling technologies, which have opened up many new opportunities for future IMD improvements. This paper reviews these advances and discusses the impact they will have upon new IMD systems. It also looks forward and presents a longer-term vision of how IMDs will further evolve during coming decades.

title: Two-Stage Hierarchical Near-Field Beam Training for Extremely Large-Scale RIS
authors: Dingcheng Yang, Zhaoqi Zhang, Yu Xu, Fahui Wu, Changsheng You
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: The extremely large-scale reconfigurable intelligent surface (XL-RIS) is a highly promising technology for the sixth-generation (6G) wireless communications. However, the conventional near-field beam training (NFBT) for XL-RIS is intractable and incurs excessively high beam training overhead. To solve this problem, we propose a two-stage hierarchical NFBT scheme for XL-RIS. In the first stage, we generate a hierarchical codebook based on the proposed 3D binary-tree search strategy, which can iteratively and evenly divide the beam search region into sub-regions, wherein the sampling point pair formed by the center points of the BS sub-region and the user sub-region corresponds to a codeword in the codebook. Then, we perform NFBT on this hierarchical codebook to achieve coarse beam alignment in the downlink. In the second stage, we perform NFBT based on the globally optimal codeword of the first stage to achieve fine-grained beam alignment. Numerical results show that the proposed two-stage hierarchical NFBT scheme can significantly reduce the beam training overhead while achieving high rate.

title: Experimental Study on Failure Mechanism of 4H-SiC MOSFETs Under Unclamped Inductive Switching (UIS) Stress Conditions
authors: Amir Shahzad, Na Ren, Liang Qiao, Xinke Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Device and Materials Reliability
abstract: This paper discusses a comprehensive reliability evaluation of a 1200-V 4H-SiC planar MOSFET fabricated in-house for research purposes, subjected to Unclamped Inductive Switching (UIS) stress under a wide temperature range from 25°C to 175°C. Avalanche current, breakdown voltage, and avalanche energy were recorded in a systematic way to measure temperature-dependent ruggedness. The examination of post-stress failure by ultrasonic scanning, decapsulation, OBIRCH/EMMI, and FIB-SEM showed that a series of degradation processes occur, starting with early charge trapping and slight shifts of leakage, then through the formation of localized hot-spots, weakening of the gate-oxide, and the erosion of metallization. Current crowding around the source region linked by a single bond wire was the cause of catastrophic failures, which caused a short between gate and source and between drain and source once thermal runaway and parasitic BJT activation had taken place. It was demonstrated that temperature is a strong accelerator of degradation, decreasing the avalanche capability and increasing structural damage. The SEM cross-section shows that the hotspot that develops at the corner of the p+ region where the highest accumulation of electric field takes place, causes p-well degradation during early failure. These results provide new insight into the thermal-electrical overstress limits of SiC MOSFETs and highlight the critical influence of packaging-dependent current distribution on avalanche reliability.

title: X-IONet: Cross-Platform Inertial Odometry Network for Pedestrian and Legged Robot
authors: Dehan Shen, Changhao Chen
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Learning-based inertial odometry has achieved remarkable progress in pedestrian navigation. However, extending these methods to quadruped robots remains challenging due to their distinct and highly dynamic motion patterns. Models that perform well on pedestrian data often experience severe degradation when deployed on legged platforms. To tackle this challenge, we introduce X-IONet, a cross-platform inertial odometry framework that operates solely using a single Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU). X-IONet incorporates a rule-based expert selection module to classify motion platforms and route IMU sequences to platform-specific expert networks. The displacement prediction network features a dual-stage attention architecture that jointly models long-range temporal dependencies and inter-axis correlations, enabling accurate motion representation. It outputs both displacement and associated uncertainty, which are further fused through an Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) for robust state estimation. Extensive experiments on the public RoNIN pedestrian dataset, the GrandTour quadruped dataset, and a self-collected Go2 quadruped dataset demonstrate that X-IONet achieves state-of-the-art performance, reducing ATE and RTE by 14.3% and 11.4% on RoNIN, 11.8% and 9.7% on GrandTour, and 52.8% and 41.3% on Go2. These results highlight X-IONet's effectiveness for accurate and robust inertial navigation across both human and legged robot platforms.

title: Simulation and Modeling of Biodegradable Capacitor
authors: Anjana J.G, Abhinav K.L, Ananthu A.C, B S Vishnu Chasanth, Dheeraj M, Unnikrishnan Jayan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Futuristic Technology
abstract: Biodegradable capacitors (also referred to as green capacitors) are a trend to replace regular components in short-lived and disposable electronics. In this work, the TCAD was used to simulate the shapes of a MOS, MSM, and parallel plate capacitor, verify their electrical behavior with silicon dioxide, zinc oxide, and hafnium oxide materials and palladium, silver, or titanium electrodes. Depletion and Schottky barriers contributed to the strong variation in capacitance of MOS and MSM capacitors when voltage varied such that capacitors are useful in sensing but not in long-term storage. The parallel plate capacitors exhibited minimal variations to bias and remained constant thus they can be used in biodegradable capacitor designs. Such findings provide a starting point for selecting the most appropriate dielectrics and shapes of devices to be made in the future.

title: GKNet: Graph Kalman Filtering and Model Inference via Model-based Deep Learning
authors: Mohammad Sabbaqi, Riccardo Taormina, Elvin Isufi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract: Inference tasks involving time series over graphs are of importance in applications such as urban water networks, economics, and networked neuroscience. Addressing these tasks typically relies on identifying a computationally affordable model that jointly captures the graph-temporal patterns of the data. In this work, we propose a graph-aware state space model for graph time series, where both the latent state and the observation equation are parametric graph-induced models. More specifically, we consider the state equation to follow a stochastic partial differential equation driven by noise over the edges accounting not only for potential uncertainties thereof, but also for increasing the degrees of freedom in a tractable manner. The graph structure conditioning of the noise dispersion allows the state variable to deviate from the stochastic process in certain neighborhoods. The observation model is a sampled and graphfiltered version of the state capturing multi-hop neighboring influence. The goal is to learn the parameters in both state and observation models from the partially observed data for downstream tasks such as prediction and imputation. The model is inferred first through a maximum likelihood approach that provides theoretical tractability but is limited in expressivity and scalability. To improve on the latter, we use the state space formulation to build a principled deep learning architecture that jointly learns the parameters and tracks the state in an endto- end manner in the spirit of Kalman neural networks. The proposed models are evaluated on controlled and four real-world scenarios, showing the effectiveness of the proposed approach when the model lacks training data. Finally, we conduct a case study on water networks, where the proposed model is used to predict the water levels in the network when the data is extremely scarce.

title: Contextual Bandit-Based AMC for Quasi-Stationary Wireless Backhaul Links on SDR Platforms
authors: Diego Gabriel Soares Pivoto, Juliano Silveira Ferreira, Matheus Sêda Borsato Cunha, Felipe A. P. De Figueiredo, Luciano Leonel Mendes
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Selecting the Modulation and Coding Scheme (MCS) from the measured Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) is commonly implemented via precomputed Look-Up Tables (LUTs) derived from analytical models, link-level simulations under idealized assumptions, or empirical Bit Error Rate (BER)/Block Error Rate (BLER) thresholds. In operational Software-Defined Radio (SDR) platforms, however, non-ideal channel estimation, pilot-grid interpolation, equalizer noise enhancement, decoder non-idealities, and Radio Frequency (RF) front-end impairments distort this mapping, causing conventional tables to miss the expected goodput. This paper proposes a measurement-driven Adaptive Modulation and Coding (AMC) strategy based on Contextual Bandit (CB), which learns a stationary SNR→MCS policy directly from link measurements by maximizing downlink Medium Access Control (MAC)-layer goodput. The target application is a TV White Space (TVWS) backhaul in the Ultra High Frequency (UHF) band, designed to provide Internet connectivity to remote areas: both the Base Station (BS) and the remote terminal are fixed, typically under Line-of-Sight (LoS), yielding quasi-stationary channel conditions over many transmission intervals. We implement and evaluate the proposed approach on an SDR-based radio access system in two representative deployments: (i) an RF wireless link that exercises the full Physical Layer (PHY) stack under regulatory transmit-power constraints, and (ii) a Radio over Fiber (RoF) extension enabling tens-of-kilometers reach. The context is the measured SNR (discretized into bins), the action is the MCS index, and the reward is the delivered goodput; transmission errors and retransmissions are naturally captured through reduced goodput. For controlled experimentation and reproducible operating regimes, we constrain the effective SNR within a desired interval by programmatically adjusting the transmit-chain gain. As baselines, we consider as references the link adaptation based on an Inner Loop Link Adaptation (ILLA) obtained from laboratory calibration, an Outer Loop Link Adaptation (OLLA) that periodically adjusts the selected MCS using a BER target, and a supervised Histogram-Based Gradient Boosting Regressor (HGBR) baseline, trained offline from measured samples to approximate the goodput-maximizing SNR→MCS mapping. Experimental results show that the learned CB policy improves the area under the goodput–SNR curve and the mean Spectral Efficiency (SE) over both the baselines within the operational SNR range, while satisfying the BER target. In addition, by directly learning the optimal SNR→MCS mapping from interaction with the real environment, the proposed approach avoids the approximation stage required by the offline HGBR model and eliminates the transient convergence period required by the BER-driven OLLA to adapt from the initial ILLA table toward its steady-state operating point, thereby enabling immediate near-optimal performance in quasi-stationary wireless backhaul links.

title: RGPO: Ranking-Guided Preference Optimization for Reliable Clinical Reasoning_supp1-3688221.pdf
authors: Fang-Ming Hung
publish: N/A
abstract: Medical question answering in healthcare systems requires advanced reasoning that integrates domain knowledge with clinical workflows. However, existing large language models (LLMs) often generate reasoning chains that lack factual accuracy and clinical reliability. We propose Ranking-Guided Preference Optimization for Reliable Clinical Reasoning (RGPO), a framework that combines reinforcement learning with preference-driven reasoning refinement to enhance clinical chain-of-thought performance in LLM-based healthcare systems. RGPO employs task-adaptive reasoning templates aligned with clinical protocols and a probabilistic evaluation mechanism that integrates with healthcare information systems, including EHRs and clinical documentation, while automatically identifying and correcting low-quality reasoning chains. Unlike traditional pairwise preference methods, RGPO introduces groupwise ranking optimization based on the Bradley-Terry model with KL-divergence regularization for stable clinical deployment. The framework addresses key challenges, including multimodal data integration, clinical validation, and real-world interoperability. Experiments on PubMedQA, MedQA-USMLE, and real-world validation at Far Eastern Memorial Hospital (FEMH) demonstrate consistent improvements over strong baselines. Our 2B-parameter model outperforms larger 7B-20B models, including medical-specialized variants, while maintaining computational efficiency for healthcare deployment. These results demonstrate that combining preference optimization with quality-driven refinement provides a scalable, clinically validated approach to building reliable medical LLMs for practical healthcare applications.

title: AI Data Center Load Forecasting: A Liquid Neural Network Approach
authors: Mariam Mughees, Yuzhuo Li, Zhiheng Lin, Yize Chen, Yunwei Ryan Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: Unprecedented growth of AI data centers exhibit highly stochastic and bursty electricity demand profiles, with load ramps of several megawatts within seconds, posing significant new challenges for smart grid stability and operation. Such events can destabilize power systems if not properly addressed, under-scoring the need for forecasting methods tailored to emerging loads. Accurate short-term power forecasting provides great potential to enable grid operators to dynamically allocate resources, maintain frequency stability, and reduce reserve requirements in smart grid with emerging large loads. To reliably interconnect growing demand of AI compute into power grids, there is a strong need for accurately forecasting AI data center power demand. In this paper, we propose a short-term, data center-scale power forecasting framework for AI training and inference using Liquid Neural Networks (LNN). Evaluated on large language models’ power traces, our approach achieves higher accuracy with fewer neurons compared to classical deep learning models, offering a scalable, efficient, and utility-scale forecasting solution. This highlights the potential of LNN as lightweight, adaptive models to capture the dynamics of emerging large loads more effectively, thereby enabling grid operators to maintain reliability under increasing AI data center demands.

title: Push Smarter, Not Harder: Hierarchical RL-Diffusion Policy for Efficient Nonprehensile Manipulation
authors: Steven Caro, Stephen L. Smith
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Nonprehensile manipulation, such as pushing objects across cluttered environments, presents a challenging control problem due to complex contact dynamics and long-horizon planning requirements. In this work, we propose HeRD, a hierarchical reinforcement learning-diffusion policy that decomposes pushing tasks into two levels: high-level goal selection and low-level trajectory generation. We employ a high-level reinforcement learning (RL) agent to select intermediate spatial goals, and a low-level goal-conditioned diffusion model to generate feasible, efficient trajectories to reach them. This architecture combines the long-term reward maximizing behaviour of RL with the generative capabilities of diffusion models. We evaluate our method in a 2D simulation environment and show that it outperforms the state-of-the-art baseline in success rate, path efficiency, and generalization across multiple environment configurations. Our results suggest that hierarchical control with generative low-level planning is a promising direction for scalable, goal-directed nonprehensile manipulation. Code, documentation, and trained models are available: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/carosteven/HeRD</uri>.

title: A Strong Misalignment Tolerance and Low Stray Magnetic Fields Wireless Power Transfer System for Planar Type AUV with Hybrid Magnetic Coupler
authors: Haibing Wen, Xiaolong Zhou, Min Guo, Jie Wen, Kehan Zhang, Zhengchao Yan, Lei Yang, Yaopeng Zhao, Xiangqian Tong, Baowei Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: The influence of multi-dimensional misalignments on the output characteristics and stray magnetic fields interference present critical challenges to the wireless power transfer (WPT) systems for planar-type underwater vehicles. To address the aforementioned issues, this article proposes a dual-channel magnetic coupling mechanism. The newly developed stepped bipolar four-coil (SBF) transmitter structure, integrated by bipolar four-coil units and stepped coils, effectively suppresses the stray magnetic fields while simultaneously generating a uniform magnetic field distribution. Furthermore, a rectangular cross-shaped solenoid pad (RCSP) receiver featuring a structural compatibility is put forward. The complement effect of rectangular and cross-shaped solenoid coils is employed to ensure the pick-up of both vertical and horizontal magnetic flux, thus, a nearly constant mutual inductance between the proposed SBF-RCSP magnetic coupler is achieved. A 500W experimental prototype is constructed to validate the proposed WPT system. Within a lateral misalignment range of 100 mm, full 360° rotational offset, and 15° tilt angle, the output power fluctuation does not exceed 9.06%, and the transmission efficiency variation remains within 0.88%. Stray magnetic field in the evaluation region is reduced by 72.83% compared with that of a rectangular coil with the same self-inductance.

title: Reference-Signal-Based Multi-Degree-of-Freedom Modulation for Power-Signal Dual Modulation in DC-DC Converters
authors: Minxia Tan, Tianqu Hao, Jiazheng Huang, Yitong Liu, Zheng Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: The trade-off between modulation complexity and bitrate in power-signal dual modulation technique is an essential design task that largely determines availability of such technique in a specific application. This paper proposes a novel reference-signal-based multi-degree-of-freedom modulation (RSMDoF) scheme. Its core principle is to simultaneously modulate data into the phase, frequency, and amplitude of a square wave, which is then superimposed on the reference signal to achieve multi-degree-of-freedom encoding. The scheme expands the modulation degrees of freedom and relaxes the constraint that the data-signal frequency is much lower than the switching frequency. Consequently, compared with existing schemes, the RS-MDoF scheme improves bitrate without increasing the complexity of system hardware and software. This paper presents a detailed theoretical analysis of the principle. Experimentally, a parallel dual-boost converter platform implementing the RSMDoF scheme achieves a 25 kbps bitrate with basic demodulation, which verifies the scheme's advantage of higher bitrate without added complexity.

title: Switching-Type Gain-Scheduling for Mining Truck Active Suspension Systems under Fading Channels: Time-Sequence Enhancement and Experimental Validation
authors: Penghua Bai, Xia Zhou, Yang Liu, Xiangpeng Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Addressing the dual challenges of complex road conditions in mine tunnels and adverse communication environments, this article proposes a reliable gain-scheduling control for industrial mining truck active suspension systems (MTASSs) under fading channels. First, by integrating interval type-2 (IT-2) fuzzy models and Markov processes, a dynamic model is constructed that captures system intrinsic uncertainties and external time-varying disturbances. Second, a simulated fading channel is established using prior statistical information to quantify signal attenuation, and a measurement censoring mechanism integrating dynamic monitoring and adaptive filtering is designed to enhance transmission reliability. On this basis, a time-sequence enhancement switching-type gain-scheduling (TESG) observer-assisted fuzzy control law with homogeneous polynomially parameter-dependent properties is developed. It incorporates a time-sequence enhancement using normalized fuzzy weighted membership functions (NFWMFs) from both current and historical moments, alongside a switching mechanism that exploits the implicit algebraic properties of these NFWMFs for more flexible control gain allocation, thereby reducing design conservatism. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach improves the system’s anti-vibration capability and saves energy.

title: Multiscale Physics-Embedded Automated Modeling with an Application to Photovoltaic Forecasting
authors: Yuan Yan, Shiqi Wang, Jiang Xiao, Hui Deng, Changjiang Ma, Jianwei Zhao, Yun Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: While there exist photovoltaic (PV) forecasting methods, persistent challenges remain, such as dynamically changing working conditions, nonlinearities, limited interpretability, and dependency on quality datasets. To address these issues, this article proposes a multiscale-dynamic and neural-tuned physics-formulated PV forecasting method, and thus develops a physics-embedded automated modeling (PEAM) framework for engineering systems. Different from a physics-informed neural network (PINN), PEAM explicitly integrates first-principle derived physical equations directly into the model for interpretability, with neural networks exploiting their nonlinear fitting and adaptation capabilities. Here, Bayesian optimization is utilized systematically to address hyperparameter sensitivity and significance analysis, thus automatically identifying critical environmental impact factors, focusing on the most influential input variables, and providing effacacy for data usage. Comprehensive experimental validation conducted on three different prediction steps, ten testing datasets, and one extreme weather test set from two real-world solar stations shows that PEAM consistently outperforms existing methods such as PINN. Overall, the PEAM framework enhances the predictive accuracy, interpretability, and robustness for practical deployment of PV forecasting systems.

title: Advancing Autonomous BVR Air Combat: Integrated Strategy Optimization and Adaptive Learning
authors: Wenfei Wang, Le Ru, Maolong Lv, Hailong Xi, Li Mo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: In modern air combat, the complexity of beyond visual range (BVR) engagements stems from the dynamic nature of the game environment, numerous confrontation factors, and continually evolving strategies. These challenges underscore the limitations of traditional algorithms in addressing BVR decision-making problems. To overcome these issues, this paper proposes a decision-making method that combines strategy reuse with demonstration guidance, aiming to enhance the learning efficiency and adaptability of agents in complex environments. Additionally, a role-based agent framework and a collaborative learning mechanism are introduced to diversify strategies and promote group cooperation. By incorporating a strategy training approach driven by strategy entropy, the proposed method further improves the adaptability and robustness of agents in complex BVR air combat scenarios. Simulation results validate the effectiveness and superiority of the approach in BVR air combat scenarios, highlighting its efficiency and stability in the decision-making process.

title: Design of an embedded hardware platform for cell-level diagnostics in commercial battery modules
authors: Gabriele Marini, Alessandro Colombo, Andrea Lanubile, William A. Paxton, Simona Onori
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: While battery aging is commonly studied at the cell-level, evaluating aging and performance within battery modules remains a critical challenge. Testing cells within fully assembled modules requires hardware solutions to access cell-level information without compromising module integrity. In this paper, we design and develop a hardware testing platform to monitor and control the internal cells of battery modules contained in the Audi e-tron battery pack. The testing is performed across all 36 modules of the pack. The platform integrates voltage sensors, balancing circuitry, and a micro-controller to enable safe, simultaneous cell screening without disassembling the modules. Using the proposed testing platform, cell voltage imbalances within each module are constrained to a defined reference value, and cell signals can be safely accessed, enabling accurate and non-invasive cell-level state-of-health assessments. On a broader scale, our solution allows for the quantification of internal heterogeneity within modules, providing valuable insights for both first- and second-life applications and supporting efficient battery pack maintenance and repurposing.

title: Exploring the Application of AI-Assisted Blended Model in Educational Research Methods Course
authors: Xiaoying Xia
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 9th International Conference on Education and E-Learning
abstract: None

title: Real-Time Product Dispensing System with FPGA
authors: Ch. Babaiah, P Somasundri, J Palanimeera, Rakesh Kumar, Binod Kumar, Shrikant Upadhyay
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This paper presents the RTL design logic of vending machine and its implementation into FPGA zed board. FPGA technology enables faster processing, reconfigurability, and better resource efficiency for vending control. The FPGA-based approach provides a modern, adaptable, and high-performance solution for vending machine automation. The Control logic in this proposed model of vending machine includes insertion of amount, cancel operation and dispensing of candy. The proposed design is tested and simulated using Xilinx Vivado 2024 version, achieving a 69.3% of power optimization than the previous model, with further power consumption and timing analysis performed across different RTL coding styles (if-else, switch case, and while loop implementations).

title: Hybridisation of Quantum computing Methods in logistics optimization through Machine learning
authors: Ritu Aggarwal, Eshaan Aggarwal
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The intersection of quantum computing with machine learning transforms the various effective technologies. It works with the integration of quantum computing into logistics through supply chain management to solve and use optimization problems. These technologies have the capability of data pre-processing and analysing logistics through various machine learning models. This chapter explores the various quantum computing principles that are used to solve complex problems. This chapter discussed quantum annealing, quantum applications, and quantum optimisation algorithms by key factor algorithms based on the different ML approaches used. It is based on fleet maintenance, loading cargo, and prediction with scheduling algorithms. Quantum computing, when hybridized with ML and logistic optimisation, could solve the problems related to classical computing problems in this chapter. In this proposed work, the hybrid approach with ML and quantum computing with enhanced industry-specific solutions in different aspects of quantum computing in sectors such as cybersecurity. In logistics, the optimization concepts help to define the problems through a hybridization method with their applications.

title: On Amount of Fading and Ergodic Channel Capacity of Terahertz Communications Using Compound Channel Model
authors: Tingguang Gan, Shuping Dang, Xiangcheng Li, Hongliu Wei, Zhenrong Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: In this paper, we study terahertz (THz) communication systems using a compound channel model that captures the effects of multi-path fading, dynamic shadowing, molecular absorption loss, and propagation loss. Based on the estimates of the channel parameters under consideration, we derive the exact expressions of a probability density function (PDF) and a cumulative distribution function (CDF) for the end-to-end signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of the THz communication system. Afterwards, we use the obtained PDF and CDF to derive closedform expressions of amount of fading (AoF) and ergodic channel capacity (ECC). Moreover, the asymptotic analytical expression of ECC is also derived to discover more useful information. The correctness of the derived expressions is verified by numerical simulations through Monte Carlo simulations. Through the analytical and numerical results presented in this paper, we provide different perspectives on analyzing THz communication systems using the compound channel model, which are expected to expedite the theoretic research of THz wireless communications.

title: Control and Detection of Epileptic Seizure Using Machine Learning Techniques: K-Means Clustering Application
authors: Houda Ghidhaoui, Wahid Souhail, Hedi Khammari
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: In this study, a new method for real-time prediction of epileptic seizures is proposed that uses single-channel EEG recordings from FP1-F7 electrode using spectral analysis in sliding windows to detect preictal changes in the EEG as an early warning signs of seizures, and uses empirical mode decomposition (IMF1 and IMF3) to capture fine frequency and amplitude variations. Relevant features, such as amplitude fluctuations, frequency shifts, and spike counts are extracted, and a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier is employed to differentiate seizure-related and non-seizure states, which shows the efficacy of combining sophisticated signal processing with machine learning for seizure forecasting.

title: Speaker Identification from Bone-Conducted Speech via Dual Attention-Transformer Model: A Study with EmoBone Dataset
authors: Md. Ibne ShihabShad, Sayeeda Khan, Md. Sarwar Hosain, Akif Mahdi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Speaker identification using bone conducted (BC) speech is challenging due to limited data availability. A dual attention based CNN Transformer hybrid model is proposed and evaluated on the EmoBone dataset, which comprises 14,354 samples from 28 speakers across different regions. Experiments were conducted in python within a GPU-accelerated setup. The feature extraction process fused local acoustic descriptors, such as MFCCs and mel spectrograms, with global prosodic cues to characterize speaker identity under varying emotional conditions. The model architecture combines convolutional layers for capturing fine grained temporal frequency patterns with transformer layers employing attention to model long range contextual dependencies. The model was trained using the adam optimizer, incorporating early stopping and learning rate scheduling to promote stable convergence, without employing class weights. It attained a final training accuracy of 99% with a loss of 0.8272, and a validation accuracy of 99.36% with a loss of 0.8026. The model exhibited excellent generalization and robustness, as reflected by classwise precision, recall, and f1-scores mostly above 0.98, an IoU of 0.9717, balanced accuracy of 0.9985, and strong AUC metrics. These results highlight the potential of BC speech for speaker identification and provide a reliable foundation for future studies and practical implementations.

title: ECG Signal Acquisition Module:Innovative Solutions for Cardiac Monitoring
authors: Asif Ahmed Rafi, Rahul Biswas, Md Moznuzzaman
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) signal acquisition is fundamental to cardiac health monitoring and diagnosis. This paper presents the design and implementation of an innovative ECG signal acquisition module optimized for real-time, low-noise, and high-fidelity cardiac monitoring applications. The proposed system integrates advanced analog front-end circuitry with efficient digital signal processing techniques to ensure accurate detection of subtle cardiac events. A low-power microcontroller based interface that enables portability and seamless data transmission for remote healthcare solutions. Extensive testing on simulated and real patient data demonstrates the module’s reliability, noise immunity and compliance with biomedical signal standards. This work aims to contribute to the development of compact, cost-effective and scalable ECG monitoring systems for modern healthcare environments.

title: Electricity Market Price Prediction Using a BP Neural Network Model Evolved by Genetic Algorithm
authors: Kate Yan
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: The recent fast restructuring of the world energy industry because of the low carbonization, alongside the increase in the portion of energy generation in the power system, has made it extremely crucial to forecast the cost of electricity depending on the form of the power source. Prices in electricity markets have had a long history of influences of numerous factors, some of which have been nonlinearly or nonlinearly related to one another. Price forecasting of electricity adopted in this paper relies on back propagation neural network (GA-BP) whose optimization is achieved by genetic algorithm. This model will make use of power generation figures of different sources of energy to forecast the behavior of electricity prices in the market. To solve the commonly known problem of local optima of conventional artificial neural networks, genetic algorithms may be used to explore all of the viable space and locate the best possible starting point of neural networks and thus ensure increased predictive accuracy. It is a present study of the research where the data related to the generation of electrical energy and electricity price have been obtained and analyzed using the ENTSO-E official website and Spain TSO company named Red Elèetrica de Espana respectively. The error comparison method of mean squared error and L2 regularization was used to determine the structure of the Backpropagation Neural Network, which was subsequently optimized through the Genetic Algorithm. Using various errors as compared with GA-BP model is more efficient when it comes to making predictions about test set with various errors and in terms of predictive capacity, the exactness of electricity pricing prediction.

title: A Predictive IoT Framework for Continuous Vehicle Emission Monitoring Using Ensemble CatBoost and XGBoost Learning
authors: Sk Kamal Sundar, Ra Kaushik, Akhilesh Shukla, R Srinikesh, P Jayanthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Traditional vehicle emission monitoring systems are limited by their reactive, single-point approach, which does not account for real-world factors like weather and traffic that affect vehicle emissions and undermine proactive policy and maintenance. In this paper, we propose GreenPulse AI, an IoT-based Smart Emission Monitoring System that uses predictive Machine Learning (ML) methods to mitigate those limitations. GreenPulse AI continuously collects data to compute an adaptable a multifactor Green Emission Score and leverages accurate ML model predictions to forecast emission levels for the next hour. The significant contributions of this work include a multi-factor scoring algorithm that incorporates all emissions contributors, ML to enable accuracy in short-term predictions, and a timely, multi-factor framework that collects data continuously for further preventive maintenance and proactive environmental policy reasoning.

title: CarryMe: A Mobile-Based Structured Purchase-Request Management System for Enhancing Usability and Digital Consumer Experience
authors: Pisit Chansuek, Sarinthip Phaenthong, May Boondee, Thanakrit Janchidfah, Pirat Worasiri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: In the era of social commerce, informal purchase-request activities are commonly managed through chat-based platforms, leading to fragmented information, miscommunication, and inefficient coordination. This study proposes CarryMe, a mobile-based application designed to structure and manage purchase-request activities using a cloud-based architecture. The system enables real-time tracking, centralized data management, and standardized workflows. The application was developed and evaluated with 114 participants using a structured usability questionnaire. Descriptive statistics and inferential analysis were applied, including mean, standard deviation, and independent t-tests. The results indicate a high level of user satisfaction (Mean <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$= 4.25, \text{SD}=0.58$</tex>), particularly in ease of use and process clarity. Statistical analysis revealed no significant difference across gender groups <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{p}>0.05)$</tex>, indicating consistent usability. The findings demonstrate that transforming informal coordination into structured systems significantly enhances usability and reduces operational ambiguity. This study contributes to mobile application design in social commerce environments.

title: Multimodal Interest-Tree Based Negative Enhancement for Recommendation
authors: Doudou Si, Lexin Song
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Multimodal recommender systems often suffer from “semantic distortion” when constructing item graphs via modality similarity, misaligning feature proximity with user intent. Existing behavior-based denoising methods remain limited by shallow high-order modeling or noise introduction. We propose MINERec, which introduces Interest-Tree-Guided Representation Purification. MINERec first constructs a Behavior-Aware Interest Tree (BAIT) based on item co-occurrence in user interaction histories to anchor authentic user intents. By cross-validating modality similarity against this co-occurrence structure, we identify items with “high modality similarity but zero co-occurrence” as hard negatives that represent semantic traps. Coupled with Structural-Semantic Adaptive Contrastive Learning (SACL), our method dynamically repels these negatives to filter noise at the representation level prior to graph reconstruction. Experiments on three datasets show MINERec outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, achieving an average improvement of 3.16%.

title: Predictive Control of a Hybrid PEM Electrolyzer-Fuel Cell System for Frequency and Voltage Regulation in High PV Penetration Grids
authors: El Manaa Barhoumi, Sulaiman Z. Almutairi, Kamel Sayahi, Abdel Aziz Raslan, Slah Farhani, Faouzi Bacha
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: The widespread development of grid-connected electrolyzers for green hydrogen production has brought new opportunities and challenges to modern power systems. Electrolyzers facilitate flexible usage of excess renewable power, however their dynamic interaction with the power grid can have significant impacts on voltage stability, frequency support and power quality. In this paper, the role of PEM electrolyzers and FCs in improving voltage and frequency stability in PV integrated energy systems is analyzed. The surplus electricity of PV system is fed to a 50 MW PEM electrolyzer to produce green hydrogen, which can be recirculated to fuel cells as an energy vector during periods with low solar radiation and high load demand. An energy management system formulation is adopted to ensure system-level coordination. The optimization is performed every 5 minutes using a prediction horizon of 1 hour. Optimization variables are the Electrolyzer and fuel cell power trajectories. The objective is the minimization of the overall cost of grid import, frequency error, hydrogen deficit and actuation, subject to operating limits and rate bounds. The simulations show that in a system combining 100 MWp of PV with the 50 MW Electrolyser, grid stability is enhanced, and the effects of PV intermittency are dampened. The results demonstrate the capability of predictive control-based coordination for increasing reliability and enabling hydrogen-based flexibility in future smart and sustainable energy systems.

title: Improve Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) Brain-Computer Interfaces: Wearable Sensor and Deep Network Integration for Better EEG Signal Classification
authors: J. Bala Samuvel, Hebatullah Awwad, M Monisha, K. Saranya, Ramesh Banoth, L Manimegalai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: One exciting development in brain-to-device communication is the use of brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) based on electroencephalogram (EEG) signals. Classifying EEG signals is challenging due to the non-stationarity of brain waves, poor signal-to-noise ratio, and large inter-subject variability. The automatic extraction of discriminative features from raw EEG data using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) has demonstrated promising results; nevertheless, their efficiency can be hindered by sensor constraints and a lack of communication between wearable devices and deep learning architectures. To enhance the accuracy and robustness of EEG signal classification, this research proposes a CNN-based BCI framework that integrates state-of-the-art wearable EEG sensor designs with optimal deep neural network topologies. The suggested approach processes spatiotemporal EEG patterns captured by high-fidelity wearable sensors using specialised convolutional neural network (CNN) layers, preserving physiological signal properties, all via end-to-end learning. Sensor-aware preprocessing, adaptive convolution kernels, and attentionbased feature fusion all work together to make the model less noise sensitive and more generalizable. This technology is perfect for practical BCI applications, as it employs small, wearable sensors to capture data in real time without restricting the user’s movement or comfort. Compared with BCI models that rely on convolutional neural networks (CNNs), the integrated design shows markedly improved performance in classification tests. The results show that scalable, user-friendly, and reliable brain-computer interface (BCI) systems for healthcare, rehabilitation, and HMI use wearable electroencephalogram (EEG) hardware in conjunction with deep learning architectures.

title: MISTY: Multidimensional Identification of Synthetic Text Yield
authors: Md. Siam Ansary
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: The proliferation of large language models has made distinguishing between human-written and AI-generated text a critical challenge across academic, journalistic, and regulatory domains. In this work, we propose MISTY (Multidimensional Identification of Synthetic Text Yield), a novel hybrid detection framework that integrates supervised contrastive learning with rich linguistic feature engineering to robustly identify AI-generated content. MISTY first encodes input texts using the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 sentence transformer, then optimizes a supervised contrastive projection head to learn semantically separable embeddings. These embeddings are further fused with a comprehensive set of stylistic, syntactic, discourse, and entropy-based features-including perplexity from GPT-2, readability scores, part-of-speech distributions, and lexical entropy-yielding a $\mathbf{1 4 6}$-dimensional feature vector. A neural classifier trained on this enriched representation distinguishes human and AI text with high precision. Our results demonstrate that MISTY is able to achieve excellent outcomes. This framework offers a lightweight, extensible, and interpretable approach for the detection of synthetic text in practical applications.

title: Dual-Channel Contrastive Convolutional Network for Respiratory Disease Prediction and Classification
authors: Laxmibai, Lakshmi Patil, Vinita Patil
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Respiratory diseases such as COVID-19, pneumonia, and tuberculosis continue to pose major global health challenges, demanding rapid and precise diagnosis. Although chest X-rays (CXRs) are widely used for screening, traditional machine learning approaches often fail to handle noisy, low-contrast images, leading to suboptimal classification accuracy. To address this research gap, this study proposes the Dual-Channel Contrastive Convolutional Network (DCC-Net), designed to enhance CXR quality, extract multi-scale features, and provide accurate respiratory disease detection and classification. The objectives include noise removal, contrast improvement, attention-based feature refinement, and efficient prediction using a hybrid classifier. DCC-Net’s architecture integrates a Noise-Contrast Removal Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) (NC-RCNN) for preprocessing, an Enhanced Multi-Scale CNN (EMSC-Net) for feature extraction, and a hybrid classifier for prediction/classification. Extensive experiments on COVID-19 Radiography, Lung Disease, and CXR Image datasets demonstrate high performance, achieving accuracies of 99.5%, 99.95%, and 99.96%, respectively. The results validate the robustness and efficiency of DCC-Net, highlighting its potential for real-world clinical applications.

title: Artificial Intelligence for Interpreting Cognitive and Emotional States in Psychoanalytic Contexts
authors: Kumari Priyanka Sinha, T. Hashni, Ch Madhava Rao, Ramraj B, K.Rani, Ch. Janakamma
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Artificial intelligence has progressed markedly in simulating cognitive and emotional processes; nonetheless, its utilization in psychoanalysis settings is still constrained. This research presents a Hybrid Deep Neuro-Symbolic Psychoanalytic Interpretation Model (HDNS-PI) that amalgamates heterogeneous neural networks with symbolism rationale models to analyse cognition and mental conditions in therapeutic contexts. The technique employs vocal prosody, subtle facial movements, text-independent associations, and physical cues to establish an integrated latent psychoanalysis paradigm that links observed behaviours with core psychoanalysis principles. A transformer-based video encoder utilises methods augmented by semi-supervised contrastive learning, enabling the recognition of defensive mechanisms, emotional fluctuations, and cognitive distortions. The embedded elements are incorporated into a neural processing layer informed by psychoanalytic theory, allowing the model to infer fundamental trends and dynamic conflicts. The system further replicates behavioural state transitions between sessions, offering a comprehensible trajectory regarding mental variations and resistance patterns. The outcome is structured as clinically coherent narratives, providing counsellors with pragmatic insights while preserving interpretive richness. This methodology amalgamates psychoanalytic concepts with AI analysis, linking smart machines and therapeutic practice, so augmenting the precision and ethical oversight of assessments of unconscious processes in medical psychoanalysis. The proposed HDNS-PI method achieved an overall accuracy of 98% in interpreting cognition and emotional conditions.

title: Integrating Ensembled Vision-Based Techniques for Intelligent Path Planning in Agricultural UAVs
authors: Rohan Vishwakarma, Ram Kumar Sahu, Kamlesh Raghuwanshi, Surabhi Karsoliva, Rahul Das Vairagi
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are transforming the area of precision agriculture by allowing effective crop monitoring, crop surveillance and autonomous flight over expansive farmlands. Nevertheless, the implementation of effective and intelligent path planning in dynamic agricultural settings is an important issue because of the uneven terrain, disorganized patterns of crops, and the changing environmental factors. In this paper, the authors suggest a unified platform that builds on ensembled vision-based methods to achieve precise scene perception and smart route planning in UAVs in the agricultural field. The ensemble model combines predictions of multiple deep learning models such as convolutional neural networks and transformer-based segmentation models to improve the accuracy and precision of detecting crop rows, obstacles, and classifying the terrain. After that, the field maps divided into segments are applied to create optimal flight paths through a hybrid planning process that involves heuristic algorithms (A search and Genetic Algorithms) that are combined with Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) solutions. The simulations and real-world data justify the proposed system and prove that the accuracy of the navigation, the efficiency of the coverage, and the effectiveness of the obstacle avoidance is much higher than those achieved using the single-model or conventional planning methods. The findings support the possibility of vision-based ensemble learning and hybrid planning to enhance the intelligent and autonomous UAV operations to support precision agriculture.

title: ACAR-YOLO: Adaptive Context Aggregation and Channel Recalibration for Efficient Object Detection
authors: Junzhe Xu
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This paper proposes ACAR-YOLOv8s, a lightweight object detection model that combines three different enhancement strategies: adaptive sampling, multi dilation context aggregation, and channel-wise recalibration. To address the issue of information loss due to strided downsampling, a light-weight adaptive extraction (LAE) module is added to the model to extract fine-grained local details. A MultiDilation (MD) module is used to extract multi-scale contextual information by performing parallel dilated convolutions with different receptive fields. Additionally, a channel attention (CA) mechanism is added to the model to recalibrate the feature responses and highlight the defect-related cues. Experiment results on the Fabric Defect Computer Vision Dataset show that the proposed model achieves 93.8% mAP@0.5, 50.5% mAP@0.5:0.95, 93.5% Precision, and 83.9% Recall, respectively, which outperforms the YOLOv8s baseline model by 7.3 percentage points in mAP@0.5. Ablation experiments also validate the complementary roles of the three modules, which consistently improve the model performance when combined together.

title: A Hybrid Model for Innovative Brain Stroke Prediction
authors: Sarthak Kharade, Saaem Shaikh, Saeed Sufiyan Shaikh, Mayank Vora, Vaishali Kosamkar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Brain stroke remains a leading cause of long-term mortality and disability worldwide, which requires rapid and accurate diagnostic tools. Medical imaging, particularly Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI), plays a crucial role, but interpretation can be complex and time consuming. Artificial intelligence, combining Deep Learning (DL) for feature extraction and Machine Learning (ML) for classification, offers promising avenues for automated stroke prediction. This paper investigates three hybrid models for brain stroke classification using MRI data: VGG16 pre-trained features with a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), DenseNet121 pre-trained features with a support vector machine (SVM) and fine-tuned EfficientNetV2B2 features with a light gradient boost machine (LightGBM). We utilized a dataset of brain magnetic resonance images classified as ’normal’ or ’Stroke.’ Data augmentation and pre-processing techniques specific to each model were applied. The performance of each hybrid model was evaluated using the accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, and confusion matrices. Experimental results demonstrate that the finetuned EfficientNetV2B2 combined with LightGBM achieved the highest accuracy of approximately 96%, significantly outperforming the VGG16+MLP (93% accuracy) and DenseNet121+SVM (91% accuracy on validation data) approaches. These findings highlight the potential of fine-tuned feature extraction coupled with robust ML classifiers for developing innovative and accurate brain stroke prediction systems.
